/transition-risk-dcf.title: Transition Risk DCF Engine
/transition-risk-dcf.description: DCF impairment engine for 8 portfolio assets under 5 NGFS scenarios. Computes climate-adjusted WACC, carbon cost pass-through in cash flows, NPV impairment waterfall, and stranded year identification.

calculationEngine.methodology: Climate-adjusted DCF with carbon cost overlay
calculationEngine.formula: WACC_adj = WACC_base + β_carbon × P_carbon(t=5)
calculationEngine.brief: Carbon price trajectories under 5 NGFS scenarios feed into DCF cash flow projections. WACC is adjusted by a carbon beta (β_carbon) calibrated from cross-sectional regression of EV/EBITDA on carbon intensity. Carbon cost = Emissions(t) × CarbonPrice(t) × (1-PassThroughRate). Stranded year = first year where cumulative NPV < 0.

dataPoints.name: Carbon Price (NZ2050, 2030)
dataPoints.interpretation: Deterministic carbon price trajectory under Net Zero 2050 scenario

dataPoints.name: WACC Adjustment
dataPoints.interpretation: Additional cost of capital reflecting carbon risk exposure

dataPoints.name: NPV Impairment
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage decline in asset value due to carbon costs and WACC adjustment

dataPoints.name: Stranded Year
dataPoints.interpretation: Year when the asset becomes economically unviable under given scenario

dataPoints.name: Pass-Through Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of carbon costs passed to customers (energy sector: 60-70%)

userInteraction: Select asset and NGFS scenario from dropdowns
Carbon Prices tab shows 5 trajectory curves with interactive horizon
DCF Engine tab computes base vs climate-adjusted NPV with waterfall chart
Portfolio Exposure aggregates impairment across all 8 assets
Stranded CAPEX identifies the year when NPV turns negative
Scenario Comparison shows impairment % side-by-side across all 5 scenarios

/stranded-asset-analyzer.title: Stranded Asset Analyzer
/stranded-asset-analyzer.description: Stranded asset write-down schedule with exponential decay model, residual value curves using half-life decay, 8-sector stranded asset matrix, and remediation pathways for converting stranded assets to productive use.

calculationEngine.formula: WriteDown(t) = InitialValue × StrandedPct × (1 - exp(-λ·t))
calculationEngine.brief: Each sector has a stranded percentage per NGFS scenario. The write-down schedule follows exponential decay with sector-specific lambda (λ). Residual value = InitialValue × exp(-ln(2)/HalfLife × t). Remediation pathways estimate conversion CapEx and IRR for repurposing (e.g., coal→battery storage).

dataPoints.name: Stranded %
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of sector assets at risk of stranding under given scenario

dataPoints.name: Write-Down Half-Life
dataPoints.interpretation: Time for residual value to decline by 50%

dataPoints.name: Remediation IRR
dataPoints.interpretation: Internal rate of return on converting stranded assets to green use

userInteraction: Select sector and scenario to view write-down schedule
Residual Value Curves show half-life decay per sector
Bubble Map visualizes exposure by sector size and stranded percentage
Remediation Pathways show conversion options with CapEx and IRR

/tech-displacement-modeler.title: Technology Displacement Modeler
/tech-displacement-modeler.description: S-curve technology adoption model with Wright's Law learning curves for 6 clean technologies. Includes cost crossover year calculation, job displacement vs. green job creation, and scenario sensitivity.

calculationEngine.formula: f(t) = L / (1 + exp(-k(t - t_mid))); LCOE(t) = LCOE₀ × (1-rate)^t
calculationEngine.brief: Adoption follows a logistic S-curve with technology-specific parameters (L=maximum penetration, k=steepness, t_mid=inflection year). Cost decline follows Wright's Law: each doubling of cumulative production reduces cost by the learning rate (solar 20-25%, wind 15-18%, batteries 18-22%). Crossover year = when new tech LCOE < incumbent.

dataPoints.name: Solar PV Learning Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Cost reduction per doubling of cumulative capacity

dataPoints.name: EV Cost Parity
dataPoints.interpretation: Year when EV total cost of ownership equals internal combustion

dataPoints.name: Green H₂ Parity
dataPoints.interpretation: Year when green hydrogen cost matches gray hydrogen

dataPoints.name: Job Displacement
dataPoints.formula: Green jobs created - fossil jobs lost
dataPoints.interpretation: Net employment impact of technology transition

userInteraction: Select technology from 6 options (Solar, Wind, EV, Heat Pump, Green H₂, DAC)
S-Curve tab shows adoption trajectory under selected scenario
Cost Learning Curves compare LCOE trajectories
Disruption Timeline marks crossover years
Job Transition shows displacement vs creation balance

/sector-transition-scorecard.title: Sector Transition Scorecard
/sector-transition-scorecard.description: PACE 4-pillar sector transition framework (Physical risk, Abatement cost, Carbon cost, Energy price) for 6 GICS sectors. Includes SBTi pathway comparison, marginal abatement cost curves, and emissions trajectories.

calculationEngine.formula: paceComposite(s) = avg(Physical, Abatement, Carbon, Energy)
calculationEngine.brief: Each sector scored on 4 dimensions (0-100): Physical risk exposure, Abatement cost ($/tCO₂ for available decarbonization options), Carbon cost pass-through capacity, and Energy price sensitivity. SBTi Sectoral Decarbonisation Approach (SDA) provides sector-specific emission pathways to 2050.

dataPoints.name: PACE Composite
dataPoints.interpretation: Higher = better transition position; Energy sector typically 35-45, Tech sector 70-78

dataPoints.name: Abatement Cost Curve
dataPoints.interpretation: Cost of emission reduction technologies ranked from cheapest to most expensive

dataPoints.name: SBTi Pathway Gap
dataPoints.interpretation: Deviation of sector emissions from science-based pathway

userInteraction: Review sector scorecards with PACE radar chart
Compare PACE pillars across 6 GICS sectors
SBTi Pathways tab shows actual vs required emissions trajectory
Abatement Cost Curves rank decarbonization options by $/tCO₂
Emissions Trajectories show historical and projected sector emissions

/just-transition-intelligence.title: Just Transition Intelligence
/just-transition-intelligence.description: ILO Just Transition Framework with 5-pillar scoring across 10 global regions. Includes workforce vulnerability analysis, financing gap quantification, green job sector pipeline with 2030/2040 projections.

calculationEngine.formula: JTF_score = 0.25×SocialDialogue + 0.20×Rights + 0.30×Employment + 0.15×SocialProtection + 0.10×Development
calculationEngine.brief: Each region assessed across 5 ILO pillars with weighted composite. Vulnerability combines fossil job dependency, wage gap (fossil vs green), reskilling cost per worker, and institutional capacity. Financing gap = estimated JTF need - available JTF funding.

dataPoints.name: Global JT Finance Gap
dataPoints.interpretation: Gap between adaptation/transition needs and current financial flows

dataPoints.name: Regions Assessed
dataPoints.interpretation: Western/Eastern Europe, N.America, East/South/SE Asia, LatAm, SSA, MENA, Oceania

dataPoints.name: Green Job Pipeline (2030)
dataPoints.interpretation: Projected green jobs by 2030 across 8 clean economy sectors

Vulnerability Matrix ranks regions by fossil dependency and reskilling need
Financing Gap shows JTF need vs available funding by region
ILO JTF Alignment checks each pillar compliance
Green Job Sectors shows 8-sector pipeline with 2030/2040 projections

IRENA Renewable Energy and Jobs Annual Review

/policy-regulatory-impact.title: Policy & Regulatory Impact
/policy-regulatory-impact.description: 6 policy instrument deep-dives: EU ETS price trajectory, CBAM cumulative liability model, UK MEES/EPC building standards, US IRA green tax credits, EU Taxonomy alignment, and ICAO CORSIA aviation offsetting.

calculationEngine.formula: CBAM_liability = Σ(ImportVolume_i × EmbeddedCarbon_i × (EU_ETS_price - Origin_carbon_price_i))
calculationEngine.brief: Each policy instrument modelled with jurisdiction-specific parameters. EU ETS: allowance supply-demand balance drives price trajectory. CBAM: 6 sectors (steel, cement, aluminium, fertilizers, electricity, hydrogen) with embedded carbon calculation. IRA: tax credits per unit ($0.0275/kWh solar, $3/kg H₂, $7,500/EV, $180/t DAC).

dataPoints.name: EU ETS Price (2030)
dataPoints.interpretation: Projected EU ETS allowance price based on Market Stability Reserve

dataPoints.name: CBAM Liability (portfolio)
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual CBAM cost for portfolio companies importing into EU

dataPoints.name: IRA Solar Credit
dataPoints.interpretation: Per-kWh tax credit for solar electricity generation

dataPoints.name: UK MEES Risk
dataPoints.interpretation: Properties at risk of non-compliance with minimum energy standards

/portfolio-transition-alignment.title: Portfolio Transition Alignment
/portfolio-transition-alignment.description: Portfolio-level transition alignment analysis across ITR (Implied Temperature Rise), GFANZ commitment, TPT 3-pillar framework, and PACTA methodology. Includes engagement & escalation register.

calculationEngine.formula: ITR = T_budget / (Σ Projected_Emissions_t / Allocated_Budget_t)
calculationEngine.brief: ITR quantifies the implied global temperature outcome if all companies emitted at the same rate as the portfolio. GFANZ: checks commitment status across NZAM/NZAOA/NZBA alliances. TPT: 3-pillar assessment (Ambition, Action, Accountability). PACTA: technology-level alignment for high-emitting sectors.

dataPoints.name: Portfolio ITR
dataPoints.interpretation: Current portfolio implies 2.4°C warming vs 1.5°C Paris target

dataPoints.name: GFANZ Alignment
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of portfolio by AUM with GFANZ-aligned commitments

dataPoints.name: TPT Readiness
dataPoints.interpretation: Transition plan readiness score across Ambition, Action, Accountability

userInteraction: ITR Overview shows portfolio temperature trajectory and 2030 target

/financed-emissions-attributor.title: Financed Emissions Attributor
/financed-emissions-attributor.description: PCAF 5 asset class financed emissions attribution engine. Computes attribution factors, data quality scoring (1-5), WACI benchmarking, and Scope 3 Category 15 quantification with company drill-down.

calculationEngine.formula: FinancedEmissions = AF_i × (Scope1 + Scope2 + Scope3); AF_equity = Investment / EVIC
calculationEngine.brief: Attribution factors differ by asset class: Listed Equity/Bonds use EVIC (Enterprise Value Including Cash), Project Finance uses 100% attribution, Commercial RE and Mortgages use loan-to-value. WACI = Σ(w_i × Emissions_i / Revenue_i). Data quality 1 (audited) to 5 (asset-class proxy).

dataPoints.name: WACI
dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio-weighted average carbon intensity relative to revenue

dataPoints.name: Financed Emissions
dataPoints.interpretation: Total attributed GHG emissions from portfolio investments

dataPoints.name: Data Quality Avg
dataPoints.interpretation: Average PCAF data quality score (1=best, 5=worst)

dataPoints.name: PCAF Classes Covered
dataPoints.interpretation: Listed Equity (1), Corp Bonds (2), Project Finance (3), Commercial RE (4), Mortgages (6)

userInteraction: PCAF Dashboard shows total financed emissions with asset class breakdown

/transition-finance-screener.title: Transition Finance Screener
/transition-finance-screener.description: 8 green/sustainability/SLB instruments screened against ICMA Principles, EU Taxonomy alignment, greenium analysis, DNSH assessment across 6 environmental objectives, and SLB KPI tracking with step-up mechanism.

calculationEngine.formula: Greenium = YTM_green - YTM_comparable_conventional (bps)
calculationEngine.brief: Each instrument screened against: (1) ICMA framework alignment (GBP for green bonds, SBP for social, SLB Principles for sustainability-linked), (2) EU Taxonomy substantial contribution to 1+ of 6 environmental objectives + DNSH for remaining 5, (3) Greenium quantification (negative spread = investor pays premium for green). SLB KPI tracking monitors whether issuer meets sustainability targets.

dataPoints.name: Green Bond Greenium
dataPoints.interpretation: Green bonds trade at lower yield (premium) vs conventional

dataPoints.name: Taxonomy Aligned %
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of bond proceeds meeting EU Taxonomy criteria

dataPoints.name: DNSH Pass Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Do No Significant Harm assessment across all 6 EU objectives

dataPoints.name: SLB Step-Up
dataPoints.interpretation: Coupon increase if issuer fails to meet sustainability KPI

/multi-dim-transition-scorer.title: Multi-Dim Transition Scorer
/multi-dim-transition-scorer.description: 6-pillar multi-dimensional transition risk scorer with public and proprietary data tiers. Scores 6 companies (Shell, Vestas, BASF, RWE, Lufthansa, BlackRock) with A-E rating, news signal feed, and universe ranking.

calculationEngine.formula: Score = 0.22×Carbon + 0.18×Technology + 0.20×Policy + 0.18×Market + 0.12×Capital + 0.10×Social
calculationEngine.brief: Six pillars: Carbon Exposure (22%), Technology Readiness (18%), Policy Risk (20%), Market Dynamics (18%), Capital Access (12%), Social License (10%). Public tier uses CDP, SBTi, Bloomberg ESG. Proprietary tier adds supply chain intelligence, facility-level data, lobbying analysis. Rating: A(≥75), B(≥60), C(≥45), D(≥30), E(<30).

dataPoints.name: Carbon Exposure
dataPoints.interpretation: Direct and indirect carbon exposure including Scope 3

dataPoints.name: Technology Readiness
dataPoints.interpretation: Investment in clean technology and innovation pipeline

dataPoints.name: Policy Risk
dataPoints.interpretation: Exposure to carbon pricing and climate regulation

dataPoints.name: Public vs Proprietary Delta
dataPoints.interpretation: Difference between public data tier and enhanced proprietary scoring

/transition-risk-heatmap.title: Transition Risk Heatmap
/transition-risk-heatmap.description: 10 sectors × 5 geographies × 3 NGFS scenarios risk matrix. 50-cell color-coded heatmap with sector and geographic averages, scenario sensitivity analysis.

calculationEngine.formula: risk(sector, geo, scenario) = baseScore(sector, geo) × scenarioMultiplier
calculationEngine.brief: Base scores for each sector-geography cell calibrated from IPCC AR6 and NGFS data. Scenario multipliers: Current Policies (0.8x), Below 2°C (1.0x), Net Zero 2050 (1.35x). Risk bands: CRITICAL(≥75, red), HIGH(≥55, orange), MEDIUM(≥35, amber), LOW(≥20, green), MINIMAL(<20, teal).

dataPoints.name: Sectors
dataPoints.interpretation: Energy, Materials, Industrials, Utilities, Consumer Disc, Consumer Staples, Healthcare, Technology, Financials, Real Estate

dataPoints.name: Geographies
dataPoints.interpretation: North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Emerging Markets, Middle East

dataPoints.name: Scenario Multiplier (NZ)
dataPoints.interpretation: Base scores scaled by 1.35 under Net Zero 2050 scenario

userInteraction: Risk Heatmap shows 10×5 color-coded matrix — click any cell for detail
Sector Analysis shows average risk score per sector across geographies

/carbon-footprint-intelligence.title: Carbon Footprint Intelligence
/carbon-footprint-intelligence.description: GHG Protocol-aligned Scope 1/2/3 emissions analysis for 4 companies (Microsoft, BP, Apple, Unilever). Scope 3 category drill-down, carbon intensity benchmarking, SBTi trajectory comparison.

calculationEngine.formula: Total = Scope1 + Scope2(market) + Scope3; Intensity = Total / Revenue
calculationEngine.brief: Scope 1: direct emissions from owned/controlled sources. Scope 2: indirect from purchased energy (market-based and location-based). Scope 3: 15 value chain categories with Cat 11 (Use of Sold Products) often dominant for fossil fuel companies. Carbon intensity = tCO₂e / $M revenue for peer benchmarking.

dataPoints.name: Scope 1
dataPoints.interpretation: Emissions from company-owned sources

dataPoints.name: Scope 2 (market)
dataPoints.interpretation: Indirect emissions from purchased electricity, adjusted for renewable procurement

dataPoints.name: Scope 3 Cat 11
dataPoints.interpretation: Emissions from customer use of sold products (dominant for O&G companies)

dataPoints.name: Carbon Intensity
dataPoints.interpretation: Normalised metric for cross-company and cross-sector comparison

/climate-var-engine.title: Climate Value-at-Risk Engine
/climate-var-engine.description: Climate VaR engine decomposing risk into transition + physical + interaction components under 5 NGFS scenarios. Interactive AUM/horizon/confidence controls, loss distribution, Delta CoVaR, and 5×7 stress test matrix.

calculationEngine.formula: CVaR = CVaR_trans + CVaR_phys + ρ · √(CVaR_trans × CVaR_phys) × corrFactor
calculationEngine.brief: Transition VaR = AUM × transRate × √(T/10). Physical VaR = AUM × physRate × √(T/10). Interaction term captures compound effects where transition and physical risks amplify each other. Horizon scaling uses √(T/10) for multi-year extension. Delta CoVaR measures each sector's marginal contribution to systemic risk.

dataPoints.name: Total CVaR
dataPoints.interpretation: Total portfolio climate loss at 95% confidence, 10yr horizon, NZ2050

dataPoints.name: Transition VaR
dataPoints.interpretation: Loss from carbon pricing, policy, technology disruption

dataPoints.name: Physical VaR
dataPoints.interpretation: Loss from acute and chronic physical climate hazards

dataPoints.name: Interaction VaR
dataPoints.interpretation: Compound effect amplification

dataPoints.name: Delta CoVaR
dataPoints.interpretation: Energy sector typically contributes 25-35% of systemic climate risk

userInteraction: Use AUM slider ($1B-$50B) to set portfolio size
Select NGFS scenario from 5 options
Adjust horizon (1-30yr) and confidence (90-99%)

Loss Distribution shows probability density with VaR marker

Stress Test Matrix shows all 5 scenarios × 7 horizons

/transition-risk-dashboard.title: Transition Risk Dashboard
/transition-risk-dashboard.description: Executive command centre with 6 KPI cards, sector heatmap, holdings monitor with CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW/LEADER flags, regulatory readiness tracker, and engagement pipeline with escalation framework.

calculationEngine.formula: Portfolio_Score = AUM_weighted_avg(entity_scores); Green_Bond_Pass_Rate = Pass_Count / Total_Count
calculationEngine.brief: Aggregates outputs from all Sprint CA-CE modules into 6 executive KPIs. CVaR from EP-CE1, ITR from EP-CC1, WACI from EP-CC2, Stranded from EP-CA2, GFANZ from EP-CC1, Green Bond from EP-CC3. Holdings flagged: CRITICAL (score<35), HIGH (35-50), MEDIUM (50-65), LOW (65-80), LEADER (>80).

dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio-level climate value-at-risk under NZ2050

dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio implied temperature rise

dataPoints.name: WACI

dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio percentage aligned with GFANZ commitments

dataPoints.name: Regulatory Readiness

/transition-reg-reporting.title: Transition Regulatory Reporting
/transition-reg-reporting.description: TCFD 4-pillar disclosure suite with 11 requirements, ISSB S2 compliance tracker (Para 10-39), CSRD ESRS E1 gap analysis, scenario board narratives, metrics register with source module traceability, and multi-format export centre.

calculationEngine.formula: Completeness = Σ(completed_disclosures) / Σ(required_disclosures) per framework
calculationEngine.brief: TCFD: 4 pillars (Governance G1-G2, Strategy S1-S3, Risk Management R1-R3, Metrics & Targets M1-M3) = 11 required disclosures. ISSB S2: 9 paragraph groups (Para 10-39). CSRD ESRS E1: 6 disclosure requirements (E1-1 through E1-6) mapped to TCFD/ISSB equivalents. Each metric traced to source module (e.g., CVaR from EP-CE1, WACI from EP-CC2).

dataPoints.name: TCFD Completeness
dataPoints.interpretation: Only S3 (scenario resilience) partially complete

dataPoints.name: ISSB S2 Completeness
dataPoints.interpretation: Para 17 (scenario) and Para 33 (Scope 3) in progress

dataPoints.name: CSRD ESRS E1 Gaps
dataPoints.interpretation: Transition plan (E1-1), energy mix (E1-5), and Scope 3 Cat 11 (E1-6)

dataPoints.name: Metrics Register
dataPoints.interpretation: Each metric linked to its computation module (EP-code reference)

/climate-adaptation-pathways.title: Adaptation Pathways Engine
/climate-adaptation-pathways.description: 8 adaptation strategies across 6 sectors with full cost-benefit analysis (BCR 3.8-14x), maladaptation risk assessment, UNEP adaptation finance gap analysis ($124B/yr), and SSP scenario sensitivity.

calculationEngine.formula: BCR = NPV_Benefits / NPV_Costs; SSP_BCR = BCR × scenario_hazard_multiplier
calculationEngine.brief: Each strategy evaluated over 20-year NPV at 5% discount rate. Benefits = avoided physical losses + avoided business interruption + health co-benefits + carbon value. SSP sensitivity: BCR recalculated under SSP1-2.6 through SSP5-8.5 using IPCC hazard intensity multipliers. Maladaptation risk scored 0-100% based on lock-in, distributional equity, emissions impact.

dataPoints.name: Avg BCR
dataPoints.interpretation: Every $1 invested in adaptation returns $5.6 in avoided losses

dataPoints.name: Adaptation Finance Gap
dataPoints.interpretation: Gap between developing country adaptation needs and current flows

dataPoints.name: Maladaptation Risk
dataPoints.interpretation: Risk that adaptation action increases vulnerability or shifts risk

dataPoints.name: NbS BCR
dataPoints.interpretation: Nature-based solutions deliver highest BCR due to co-benefits

/infrastructure-resilience-scorer.title: Infrastructure Resilience Scorer
/infrastructure-resilience-scorer.description: 5-pillar resilience scoring for 10 global infrastructure assets. Includes retrofit prioritisation by BCR, climate haircut valuation (2-33%), and 5-year trend analysis.

calculationEngine.formula: Composite = avg(Structural, Operational, Financial, Environmental, Social)
calculationEngine.brief: Each asset scored 0-100 across 5 pillars. Bands: RESILIENT(≥80), ADEQUATE(≥65), VULNERABLE(≥50), CRITICAL(<50). Climate haircut = f(HazardExposure, Vulnerability, AdaptiveCapacity) calibrated from IPCC damage functions. Retrofit BCR ranks investments by return per dollar of climate-proofing.

/nature-based-adaptation.title: Nature-Based Adaptation Solutions
/nature-based-adaptation.description: 6 NbS projects with protection value, carbon value, biodiversity scoring, community jobs, ecosystem service valuation (Costanza et al.), SDG alignment, and triple-dividend investment case.

calculationEngine.formula: TotalValue = ProtectionValue + CarbonValue + ΣCoBenefits; BCR = TotalValue / Cost
calculationEngine.brief: Protection value from avoided physical losses (e.g., mangroves reduce storm surge 60-80%). Carbon value = sequestration rate × area × carbon price × lifetime. Ecosystem service valuation from Costanza et al. global estimates ($2.8K-$352K/ha/yr by ecosystem type). Triple dividend: avoided losses + carbon revenue + community co-benefits.

dataPoints.interpretation: Highest per-hectare ecosystem service value (fisheries + tourism + coastal protection)

dataPoints.interpretation: Highest BCR of any adaptation strategy on the platform

dataPoints.interpretation: Number of Sustainable Development Goals addressed

/physical-transition-nexus.title: Physical-Transition Nexus
/physical-transition-nexus.description: Combined physical+transition CVaR with dynamic sector-specific correlation and 20 NGFS×SSP scenario combinations.

calculationEngine.formula: IntCVaR = CVaR_trans + CVaR_phys + ρ_dynamic × √(CVaR_trans × CVaR_phys)
calculationEngine.brief: ρ_dynamic varies by sector: Energy has highest interaction (0.35) as both transition policy and physical drought affect operations. Technology has lowest (0.08). Double-hit stress test applies simultaneous worst-case transition + physical scenarios.

dataPoints.name: Integrated CVaR
dataPoints.interpretation: Combined risk higher than sum of parts due to interaction

dataPoints.name: ρ Energy Sector
dataPoints.interpretation: Highest interaction between transition and physical risk

dataPoints.name: Scenario Combinations
dataPoints.interpretation: Full matrix of transition × physical scenarios

/regional-climate-impact.title: Regional Climate Impact Engine
/regional-climate-impact.description: 20 regions × 8 perils × 4 SSP scenarios with GDP shock transmission, sector-specific impacts, and labor productivity loss.

calculationEngine.formula: GDP_impact = DirectLoss + SupplyChainDisruption + InsuranceGap + FiscalCost
calculationEngine.brief: Each region-peril-SSP combination produces a hazard probability and loss estimate. GDP transmission includes indirect effects through supply chains, uninsured losses, and fiscal response costs. Labor productivity loss calculated via WBGT (ISO 7933).

dataPoints.interpretation: Sub-continental resolution

dataPoints.name: Perils

/supply-chain-contagion.title: Supply Chain Climate Contagion
/supply-chain-contagion.description: 15 portfolio companies with Tier 1/2/3 supplier mapping, 5 chokepoint analysis, and cascade simulation.

calculationEngine.formula: Impact = Σ(P(disruption_tier_n) × Revenue_impact × Duration)
calculationEngine.brief: Supply chain modelled as directed acyclic graph. Disruption at Tier 3 propagates upstream with attenuation factor per tier. Chokepoints (Suez, Malacca, Panama, Taiwan Strait, Rhine) create correlated multi-company disruption.

dataPoints.name: Chokepoints

/ml-taxonomy-scoring.title: ML Taxonomy Scoring Engine
/ml-taxonomy-scoring.description: XGBoost ML model using 316 taxonomy features for 12-month forward transition score prediction with SHAP explainability and conformal intervals.

calculationEngine.formula: y_hat = XGBoost(features_316); CI = [y_hat - q_α, y_hat + q_α]

dataPoints.name: R²
dataPoints.interpretation: Model explanatory power

/paris-alignment.title: Paris Alignment Score

/pcaf-financed-emissions.title: PCAF Financed Emissions
/pcaf-financed-emissions.description: Full PCAF Standard implementation for 5 asset classes: listed equity, corporate bonds, project finance, commercial real estate, mortgages. Computes financed emissions with PCAF data quality score 1-5.

calculationEngine.formula: FE = Σ(AF_i × Emissions_i); AF_equity = Investment / EVIC
calculationEngine.brief: Attribution factor (AF) by asset class: listed equity/bonds = Investment/EVIC, project finance = 100%, CRE/mortgages = loan value/property value. WACI = Σ(w_i × Emissions_i/Revenue_i). DQ 1=audited, 5=proxy.

/physical-risk-portfolio.title: Physical Risk Portfolio
/physical-risk-portfolio.description: Asset-level physical climate risk assessment across acute (flood, wildfire, tropical cyclone, storm) and chronic (heat, sea level rise, drought) hazards. Covers 6 SSP scenarios to 2100 with financial loss estimation.

calculationEngine.formula: PhysicalLoss = AssetValue × HazardIntensity × VulnerabilityFactor × ExposureFraction
calculationEngine.brief: Hazard probability from downscaled IPCC AR6 climate projections. Vulnerability functions map hazard intensity (e.g., flood depth, wind speed) to damage fraction. Loss distribution = convolution of hazard probability and damage function.

dataPoints.name: Flood AAL
dataPoints.interpretation: Expected annual flood loss as % of asset value

dataPoints.interpretation: Wet-bulb globe temperature threshold for moderate heat stress

dataPoints.interpretation: Exposure under high-emissions sea level rise scenario

dataPoints.name: PML
dataPoints.interpretation: Loss in an extreme but plausible catastrophe event

calculationEngine.formula: IRO_material = FinancialMateriality OR ImpactMateriality (either triggers)
calculationEngine.brief: Double materiality: (1) Impact materiality = significant actual or potential impact on people/environment, (2) Financial materiality = significant risk or opportunity for company value. Topic triggers disclosure if either test is met. IRO = Impact, Risk, Opportunity.

/carbon-budget.title: Carbon Budget Tracker

calculationEngine.formula: Budget_company = GlobalBudget × (CompanyEmissions / GlobalEmissions)
calculationEngine.brief: Remaining 1.5°C budget (2020 baseline): ~420 GtCO2 (50% probability), ~570 GtCO2 (67%). Current global emissions ~37 GtCO2/yr. Company allocation proportional to current emission share. Budget depletion rate = current pace.

/esg-screener.title: ESG Screener

calculationEngine.formula: Pass = (∀ neg_filter: !triggered) AND (score ≥ min_threshold) AND (∀ norms: compliant)
calculationEngine.brief: Three screening layers: (1) Negative/exclusionary (weapons, tobacco, fossil fuels by revenue threshold), (2) Positive/best-in-class (ESG score > sector median), (3) Norms-based (UN Global Compact, OECD, ILO compliance).

/esg-data-quality.title: ESG Data Quality Dashboard
/esg-data-quality.description: Multi-provider ESG data quality monitoring. Coverage completeness, timeliness scores, provider divergence analysis, and substitution logic for missing values.

calculationEngine.formula: DQ = 0.4×Coverage + 0.3×Timeliness + 0.2×Accuracy + 0.1×Consistency
calculationEngine.brief: Provider divergence: score difference between MSCI and Sustainalytics averages 25-35 points for same company. Substitution logic: if primary provider missing, use secondary → sector median → regional average.

/climate-physical-risk.title: Climate Physical Risk Assessment
/climate-physical-risk.description: Comprehensive physical climate risk assessment covering acute hazards (flood, TC, wildfire) and chronic stressors (heat, SLR, drought) with NatCat loss estimation and TCFD alignment.

calculationEngine.formula: PhysRisk = Hazard × Exposure × Vulnerability; Loss = PhysRisk × AssetValue × DamageFn
calculationEngine.brief: Hazard: climate-modelled intensity (e.g., flood depth, wind speed). Exposure: asset location within hazard footprint. Vulnerability: damage function relating intensity to loss fraction. Output: AAL and loss curve for 10-500yr return periods.

calculationEngine.brief: Event set: 50,000-100,000 stochastic years. OEP (Occurrence Exceedance Probability): largest loss per year. AEP (Aggregate EP): total annual losses. ILW: parametric trigger based on industry loss threshold.

/parametric-insurance.title: Parametric Insurance Analyser
/parametric-insurance.description: Parametric (index-based) insurance product design and analysis. Covers trigger design (rainfall, temperature, wind speed), basis risk quantification, and climate risk transfer pricing.

calculationEngine.methodology: Parametric trigger and basis risk model
calculationEngine.formula: Payout = MaxPayout × max(0, (Trigger-Index)/Trigger); BasisRisk = Var(ActualLoss - Payout)
calculationEngine.brief: Trigger calibration: historical percentile of weather index correlated with agricultural/property losses. Basis risk = variance between parametric payout and actual loss. Lower basis risk = better product but higher trigger cost.

calculationEngine.formula: TreatyLoss = max(0, PortfolioLoss - Retention) × TreatyShare; LossRatio = TreatyLoss / Premium
calculationEngine.brief: Climate adjustment: 1.5°C warming increases Atlantic hurricane losses by 10-15%, flood losses by 15-25% in many regions. Treaty pricing must incorporate forward-looking climate hazard change, not just historical experience.

/stochastic-scenarios.title: Stochastic Scenario Generator
/stochastic-scenarios.description: Economic scenario generation (ESG) for stress testing. Correlated paths for interest rates, GDP, equity, credit spreads, and carbon prices using Vasicek and Cholesky correlated models.

calculationEngine.formula: dX = κ(θ-X)dt + σdW; Correlated paths via Cholesky L decomposition of Σ
calculationEngine.brief: Interest rates: Vasicek/Hull-White mean-reverting model. Equity: geometric Brownian motion with stochastic volatility. Credit: intensity model correlated with macro. Carbon: jumps for policy shock events. All correlated via Cholesky decomposition.

/pandemic-climate-nexus.title: Pandemic-Climate Nexus

/cascading-default-modeler.title: Cascading Default Modeler
/cascading-default-modeler.description: Network-based credit contagion model where one company default triggers counterparty distress. Climate shocks as exogenous triggers with sector correlation matrix.

calculationEngine.formula: P(contagion) = 1 - ∏(1 - P(default_i) × Connectivity_ij)
calculationEngine.brief: Climate trigger: carbon price shock → sector EBITDA collapse → first default → contagion to counterparties via supply chain and financial linkages. Network topology: scale-free distribution means a few hubs drive most contagion.

/vcm-integrity.title: VCM Integrity Analyser

calculationEngine.formula: IntegrityScore = Additionality(25) + Permanence(20) + MRV(20) + Leakage(15) + SDG(10) + Vintage(10)

/carbon-removal.title: Carbon Removal Analytics
/carbon-removal.description: CDR (Carbon Dioxide Removal) portfolio analytics. Covers BECCS, DAC, enhanced weathering, ocean CDR, biochar, and afforestation. Includes permanence, co-benefits, MRV quality, and cost comparison.

calculationEngine.formula: NetRemoval = GrossCapture - StorageLeakage - EnergyEmissions
calculationEngine.brief: Permanence spectrum: geological storage (10,000yr) > biochar (100yr) > afforestation (50-100yr subject to reversal) > enhanced weathering (uncertain). Cost range: afforestation $5-50/tCO2, biochar $60-200, enhanced weathering $100-300, DAC $300-1000+ currently.

/supply-chain-resilience.title: Supply Chain Resilience
/supply-chain-resilience.description: Climate and ESG resilience assessment of supply chains. Covers geographic concentration, single-source dependencies, climate hazard exposure of supplier locations, and near-shoring options.

calculationEngine.formula: Resilience = Diversification × ClimateAdaptation × BusinessContinuity / SupplyConcentration
calculationEngine.brief: Diversification: HHI index of supplier country concentration. Climate adaptation: supplier climate resilience investment. Business continuity: BCP coverage and testing frequency. Concentration: single-source and sole-source dependencies.

dataPoints.interpretation: Supply country concentration (>0.25 = highly concentrated)

dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of supply base in climate-risk locations

/ai-sentiment.title: AI ESG Sentiment Engine
/ai-sentiment.description: Real-time NLP sentiment analysis for ESG news and social media. Covers 10,000+ sources, entity-level sentiment, topic classification, and portfolio momentum signals.

calculationEngine.formula: Sentiment = VADER_score(text) weighted by source_credibility × topic_relevance
calculationEngine.brief: Entity extraction: named entity recognition for company mentions. Topic classification: environment, social, governance sub-topics. Source credibility: tier 1 news > analyst reports > social media. Momentum: change in sentiment vs 30-day average.

/data-quality-dashboard.title: Data Quality Dashboard
/data-quality-dashboard.description: Platform-wide ESG data quality monitoring. Coverage rates, timeliness scores, source reliability, substitution logic audit, and data lineage visualisation.

calculationEngine.formula: DQ_overall = 0.35×Coverage + 0.25×Timeliness + 0.25×Accuracy + 0.15×Consistency
calculationEngine.brief: Coverage: % holdings with primary data per metric. Timeliness: months since last update. Accuracy: provider disagreement rate. Consistency: longitudinal stability. Red/amber/green traffic lights per data dimension.

/esg-backtesting.title: ESG Backtesting Engine
/esg-backtesting.description: Historical ESG factor backtesting for return attribution. Tests ESG tilt strategies, exclusion impact, and engagement alpha across 10-year historical windows.

calculationEngine.formula: Alpha_ESG = R_ESG_portfolio - R_benchmark; Attribution = FF5 + ESG_factor
calculationEngine.brief: ESG factor construction: long top ESG quintile, short bottom quintile. FF5 + ESG regression extracts pure ESG alpha. Exclusion impact: benchmark vs screened index performance attribution. 10-year window covers full market cycle.

/macro-transition.title: Macro Transition Risk
/macro-transition.description: Macroeconomic transition risk analysis. Covers GDP growth impacts under NGFS scenarios, employment effects, sectoral output changes, trade flow shifts, and fiscal implications of carbon pricing.

calculationEngine.formula: GDP_impact = Direct + Indirect + Productivity_loss; Employment = Fossil_lost - Green_gained
calculationEngine.brief: GDP effect: orderly transition = 1-3% GDP gain by 2050 vs baseline (green investment). Disorderly = -1 to -4% GDP disruption. Employment: net positive in most scenarios by 2030 (IRENA data) but regional displacement.

dataPoints.name: GDP Change (Disorderly)
dataPoints.interpretation: Disruption from abrupt policy shock

calculationEngine.formula: PolicyGap = Required_reductions - Policy_reductions; AmbitionGap = NDC_target - Science_pathway
calculationEngine.brief: Two gaps: ambition gap (NDCs insufficient for Paris target) and implementation gap (policies insufficient for NDC targets). CAT rating system: critically insufficient → insufficient → almost sufficient → 1.5°C compatible.

calculationEngine.formula: L&D = Economic_losses + Non_economic_losses attributable to climate change
calculationEngine.brief: Economic L&D: GDP loss, infrastructure damage, crop failure — quantifiable. Non-economic: cultural heritage, biodiversity, human lives — harder to monetise. Attribution science: fraction of extreme event probability attributable to anthropogenic climate change.

/climate-derivatives.title: Climate Derivatives Market
/climate-derivatives.description: Climate financial derivatives overview covering temperature futures (CME Degree Days), precipitation swaps, hurricane index products, and weather risk management for corporates.

calculationEngine.formula: Fair_value = E[Payout(index)] / discount; HDD = max(0, 65-T_mean); CDD = max(0, T_mean-65)
calculationEngine.brief: HDD (Heating Degree Days) and CDD (Cooling Degree Days) futures and options for seasonal temperature risk. Hurricane index products (CHI) provide parametric protection for wind events. Energy companies use these to hedge revenue from weather-dependent demand.

/climate-credit-risk-analytics.title: Climate Credit Risk Analytics
/climate-credit-risk-analytics.description: Climate-adjusted credit risk analytics for loan and bond portfolios. Covers PD/LGD climate overlay, sector-specific scoring adjustments, watchlist integration, and IFRS 9 climate staging.

calculationEngine.formula: PD_climate = PD_base × exp(β × ΔCarbonPrice + γ × HazardIntensity)
calculationEngine.brief: Two channels: (1) Transition channel — carbon price increase → sector EBITDA decline → higher PD, (2) Physical channel — hazard damage → collateral value decline → higher LGD. Combined: multi-factor climate-conditional PD/LGD model.

dataPoints.name: PD Uplift (High Exposure)

/abatement-cost-curve.title: Abatement Cost Curve

calculationEngine.formula: MAC = Annualised_cost_change / Annual_emission_reduction
calculationEngine.brief: Negative cost measures: insulation, LED lighting, fuel switching — save money AND reduce emissions. Positive: CCS, DAC, green hydrogen production — cost money. MACC shows potential vs cost tradeoff across measures.

/insurance-protection-gap.title: Insurance Protection Gap
/insurance-protection-gap.description: Quantifies the gap between total economic losses from natural catastrophe events and the portion covered by insurance, exposing uninsured climate risk. Tracks protection gap trends by peril, region, and sector using sigma and NatCat database methodologies. Supports capital allocation decisions, product development, and public-private partnership structuring for residual risk.

calculationEngine.formula: PGI = (Economic Loss − Insured Loss) / Economic Loss
calculationEngine.brief: Economic losses are sourced from NatCat databases and deflated to constant USD. Insured losses reflect paid and reserved indemnity. The Protection Gap Index ranges 0–1, where values above 0.7 indicate severely underinsured regions requiring public backstop mechanisms.

dataPoints.name: Protection Gap Index
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of economic losses uninsured; higher values flag systemic underinsurance

dataPoints.name: Insured Loss (USD bn)
dataPoints.interpretation: Paid + reserved indemnity from primary and reinsurance markets

dataPoints.name: Economic Loss (USD bn)
dataPoints.interpretation: Total direct damage to assets and infrastructure from catastrophe events

dataPoints.name: Mortality-Adjusted Gap Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Gap index weighted by fatality rate to capture humanitarian severity

userInteraction: Select peril type (flood, windstorm, earthquake, wildfire, drought) and geography to scope the analysis
Review the Protection Gap Index trend chart to identify widening gaps under climate change trajectories
Drill into sector decomposition to assess residential vs. commercial vs. agricultural underinsurance

Export gap quantification to feed reinsurance treaty design or sovereign parametric programme structuring
references: Swiss Re sigma No. 1/2024 â€” Natural Catastrophes
UNDRR Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction 2015–2030

acronyms.def: Ratio of uninsured to total economic loss from a catastrophe event
Extreme natural event causing significant loss of life or economic damage
Framework for layering sovereign and market risk transfer instruments

dataLineage.output: Annual insured and economic loss time series by region

dataLineage.output: Insured loss market share and protection gap by portfolio

dataLineage.output: Forward-looking protection gap under 1.5°C / 2°C / 3°C warming

/internal-carbon-price.title: Internal Carbon Price
/internal-carbon-price.description: Supports the design and calibration of shadow and internal carbon prices aligned with SBTi, TCFD, and IPCC social cost of carbon guidance. Embeds carbon cost into capital allocation, project appraisal, and procurement decisions to accelerate decarbonisation investment. Tracks ICP adoption across portfolio companies and benchmarks against regulatory price floors.

calculationEngine.methodology: Social Cost of Carbon & Shadow Price
calculationEngine.formula: ICPₙᵗ = SCCᵗ × Escalation Factorᵗ + Regulatory Floorᵗ
calculationEngine.brief: The Internal Carbon Price is set as the higher of the jurisdiction regulatory floor and an escalated social cost of carbon. SCC estimates follow the US EPA (2023) central estimate of $190/tCO2e. Escalation of 5–8% per year reflects increasing marginal abatement cost curves consistent with IEA Net Zero by 2050 trajectories.

dataPoints.name: Shadow Carbon Price (USD/tCO2e)
dataPoints.interpretation: Ranges reflect 2030 NZE corridor; below $75 insufficient to drive fuel switching

dataPoints.name: Regulatory Price Floor (USD/tCO2e)
dataPoints.interpretation: Applicable ETS or carbon tax rate in operating jurisdiction

dataPoints.name: Implied Abatement Cost
dataPoints.interpretation: Carbon cost embedded in NPV of low-carbon capex projects

dataPoints.name: ICP Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of Scope 1+2 emissions subject to an active internal carbon price

userInteraction: Set the base year SCC estimate using the EPA central or high-damage scenario toggle
Apply jurisdiction-specific regulatory floor to ensure compliance with local carbon pricing regimes
Define escalation pathway (linear, exponential, step) aligned with SBTi temperature goal
Run project appraisal scenarios to identify capex decisions that flip from negative to positive NPV under ICP
Export ICP assumptions to TCFD scenario analysis disclosures and CDP questionnaire sections C11–C12

CPLC High-Level Commission on Carbon Prices â€” Report 2017

/invoice-parser.title: Invoice Parser
/invoice-parser.description: AI-powered extraction engine that parses utility bills, fuel invoices, and refrigerant purchase records to derive Scope 1 and Scope 2 emission activity data. Applies NLP and document structure recognition to extract quantity, unit, fuel type, and billing period with confidence scoring. Eliminates manual data entry and reduces metering gaps in GHG inventory compilation.

calculationEngine.formula: Eᵢ = Qᵢ × EFᵢ × CVᵢ
calculationEngine.brief: Extracted quantity (Q) in native units is converted using calorific value (CV) where required and multiplied by the applicable IPCC or DEFRA emission factor. Grid electricity factors use location-based eGRID/IEA grid intensity by jurisdiction and year. Confidence scores reflect OCR quality, unit disambiguation accuracy, and emission factor coverage.

dataPoints.name: Extraction Accuracy (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: F1 score on quantity and unit field extraction across invoice types

dataPoints.name: Emission Factor Match Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of line items successfully matched to a published emission factor

dataPoints.name: Confidence Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Per-field extraction confidence; items below 0.7 flagged for manual review

dataPoints.name: Scope Attribution
dataPoints.interpretation: Automatic scope and category assignment based on fuel/utility type

userInteraction: Upload batch of utility bills, fuel receipts, or refrigerant service records in PDF or image format
Review extraction results table with highlighted fields and confidence scores per document
Correct low-confidence extractions using the inline editor before accepting into the inventory
Confirm emission factor assignments and override jurisdiction grid intensity where metered renewable data applies
Export validated activity data to GHG inventory or direct Scope 1/2 reporting engine integration
references: GHG Protocol Corporate Accounting and Reporting Standard 2015
DEFRA UK Government GHG Conversion Factors 2023
US EPA eGRID 2023 Subregion Emission Factors
IPCC AR6 WG3 Annex II â€” Emission Factor Tables 2022

acronyms.def: Technology that converts scanned document images into machine-readable text
Coefficient converting activity data units to CO2-equivalent mass
Energy content per unit mass or volume of a fuel
AI discipline enabling machines to read and interpret human language text

dataLineage.flow: OCR text extraction; layout analysis; field segmentation
dataLineage.output: Structured table of quantity, unit, fuel type, and billing period per line item

dataLineage.flow: Match fuel/utility type and jurisdiction to factor table; apply year-specific values
dataLineage.output: Emission factor per extracted line item with source citation

dataLineage.flow: Aggregate by source, scope, and period; apply confidence threshold filter
dataLineage.output: Scope 1 and Scope 2 activity data file ready for GHG inventory ingestion

/iot-emissions-tracker.title: IoT Emissions Tracker
/iot-emissions-tracker.description: Real-time GHG emission monitoring platform ingesting data from IoT sensors deployed across industrial assets, buildings, and vehicle fleets. Converts raw sensor telemetry (energy, flow, temperature, mass) into continuous Scope 1 and Scope 2 emission streams with sub-hourly granularity. Supports automated CEMS-equivalent reporting and deviation alerting against emission reduction targets.

calculationEngine.formula: Eᵤₜ = Σᵢ (Fᵢₜ × Cᵢₜ × MW_CO₂ / MWᵢ)
calculationEngine.brief: Mass emission is computed from measured flow rate (F) and measured or default concentration (C) for each pollutant stream. For energy-based sensors, stack emission factors replace concentration measurement. Data gaps are filled using EPA-approved substitution algorithms (hourly averages, look-back periods) to maintain reporting continuity.

dataPoints.name: Sensor Update Frequency
dataPoints.interpretation: Sub-hourly telemetry enabling intra-day emission trend detection

dataPoints.name: Data Completeness (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of expected telemetry records received within reporting window

dataPoints.name: Emission Intensity (tCO2e/MWh)
dataPoints.interpretation: Production-normalised emission rate for operational benchmarking

dataPoints.name: Alert Threshold Breach Count
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of periods exceeding set emission rate limits triggering operational review

Set telemetry polling intervals and data quality thresholds for each sensor stream

Schedule automated regulatory reports (daily/monthly) and export CEMS-format data for EPA or environment agency submission

/iris-metrics.title: IRIS+ Metrics
/iris-metrics.description: Comprehensive implementation of the GIIN IRIS+ system for impact measurement and management, covering all five impact dimensions of the Impact Management Project framework. Enables impact investors to select, calculate, and benchmark standardised metrics across thematic areas including climate, agriculture, health, education, and financial inclusion. Supports alignment with SDG targets and generates investor-ready impact reports.

calculationEngine.formula: Impact Scoreᵢ = Σⱼ (wⱼ × Normalised Metricᵢⱼ)
calculationEngine.brief: Portfolio impact is aggregated across IMP’s five dimensions: What, Who, How Much, Contribution, and Risk. Each IRIS+ metric is normalised to a 0–100 scale relative to sector benchmarks and weighted by strategic importance. The composite score enables cross-portfolio comparability while preserving metric-level transparency.

/issb-disclosure.title: ISSB Disclosure Engine
/issb-disclosure.description: End-to-end preparation engine for IFRS S1 general sustainability disclosure and IFRS S2 climate-related disclosure requirements, covering governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics and targets pillars. Automates gap analysis against disclosure requirements, generates tagged XBRL output, and tracks filing readiness across reporting periods. Incorporates TCFD-aligned scenario analysis templates and cross-referencing to ESRS and SEC climate rules.

calculationEngine.formula: DCI = Completed Requirements / Total Applicable Requirements × 100
standards: IFRS S1 General Requirements for Disclosure of Sustainability-related Financial Information
calculationEngine.brief: Applicable requirements are determined by entity size, listing jurisdiction, and transition relief elections. Each requirement is scored as complete, partial, or not addressed. The DCI is computed per pillar and in aggregate, with weighting applied to material requirements identified through double materiality assessment.

dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of S2 paragraphs addressed in current draft disclosure

dataPoints.interpretation: Whether transition plan is disclosed under Strategy pillar paragraph 14(b), not 14(c)
