dataLineage.flow: Cross-reference climate-related financial impacts to P&L/balance sheet notes; validate consistency
dataLineage.output: Auditable evidence package for external assurance provider

/issb-disclosure.valueSummary: Enables listed companies and large entities to achieve compliant, consistent IFRS S2 disclosure aligned with ISSB requirements, reducing regulatory risk and meeting investor expectations for comparable climate-related financial information.

/just-transition.title: Just Transition Analytics
/just-transition.description: Analyses the distributional impacts of energy transition policies on workers, communities, and vulnerable populations using the ILO Just Transition Framework and Climate Policy Initiative methodologies. Quantifies job displacement, wage impacts, and community income loss in fossil fuel-dependent regions alongside green job creation estimates. Supports transition planning, social impact assessment, and JTWG-aligned reporting for policymakers and investors.

calculationEngine.formula: JTISᵣ = α×JobLossᵣ + β×WageGapᵣ + γ×CommunityIncomeᵣ
standards: ILO Guidelines for a Just Transition 2015
Climate Policy Initiative Just Transition Finance Framework
calculationEngine.brief: Regional impact scores aggregate employment, wage, and community fiscal impacts weighted by population vulnerability index. Job displacement uses ILO sector employment multipliers applied to planned coal/oil/gas capacity retirements. Green job creation credits use IRENA regional coefficient tables for renewable energy, efficiency, and grid modernisation sectors.

dataPoints.name: Jobs at Risk (direct)
dataPoints.source: ILO employment multipliers / national labour data
dataPoints.interpretation: Direct fossil fuel sector jobs exposed to transition-driven displacement in the analysis region

dataPoints.name: Green Job Creation Ratio
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated new green economy jobs per displaced fossil fuel job by region and scenario

dataPoints.name: Wage Replacement Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Average wage of new green jobs vs. displaced fossil fuel wages; gap indicates retraining need

dataPoints.name: Community Fiscal Impact (USD)
dataPoints.interpretation: Lost tax base from fossil asset retirement per affected municipality

userInteraction: Select transition scenario (NZE, APS, STEPS) and time horizon to define the pace of fossil fuel phase-out
Map affected regions and identify coal, oil, and gas employment concentrations using the asset-to-community overlay
Review job displacement estimates against green job creation by sector and region
Assess wage replacement rate gaps to identify skills retraining investment needs and timeline
Generate JTWG-aligned transition plan summary for policy engagement or investor social bond reporting
references: ILO Guidelines for a Just Transition towards Environmentally Sustainable Economies 2015
IRENA World Energy Transitions Outlook 2023 â€” 1.5°C Pathway
WRI Creating a Just Transition for All 2020
IEA Special Report on Clean Energy Jobs 2022

acronyms.def: Composite regional score quantifying worker and community exposure to energy transition disruption
UN-convened body producing guidance on equitable climate transition policy and finance
IEA scenario reflecting current national climate commitments and announced policies
Intergovernmental body supporting countries in renewable energy transition

dataLineage.flow: Disaggregate employment by sector, fossil fuel dependency, and region; apply ILO multipliers
dataLineage.output: Job displacement risk map by region and phase-out timeline

dataLineage.flow: Apply to planned renewable capacity additions by technology and region
dataLineage.output: Green job creation forecast by year, region, and sector

dataLineage.flow: Model tax base loss from retiring fossil assets; estimate transfer dependency uplift
dataLineage.output: Community fiscal impact assessment by municipality

/just-transition.valueSummary: Gives policymakers, development banks, and ESG investors a rigorous quantitative basis for designing and financing just transition interventions that address worker displacement, wage gaps, and community income loss in fossil fuel-dependent regions.

/just-transition-finance.title: Just Transition Finance
/just-transition-finance.description: Analyses financing mechanisms for inclusive and equitable climate transition, covering social bonds, sustainability-linked bonds with social KPIs, blended finance structures, and just transition-labelled green bonds. Quantifies capital mobilisation toward affected workers and communities and tracks additionality relative to business-as-usual social spending. Supports ICMA Social Bond Principles and CBI Just Transition Criteria compliance.

calculationEngine.formula: SAI = (JT Financeₜ − BAU Social Spendₜ) / Target Populationₜ
standards: Climate Bonds Initiative Just Transition Criteria 2023
CPI Global Landscape of Climate Finance 2023
IFC Operating Principles for Impact Management
calculationEngine.brief: Additionality measures capital directed to just transition activities above baseline social expenditure. Target population denominates benefits per affected worker or community member. SAI captures whether labelled instruments represent genuine incremental support or reclassification of existing expenditure.

dataPoints.name: JT Finance Mobilised (USD bn)
dataPoints.interpretation: Total capital labelled or aligned with just transition objectives in the analysis portfolio

dataPoints.name: Social KPI Achievement Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of SLB social KPIs achieved at observation dates, avoiding penalty coupon step-up

dataPoints.name: Blended Finance Leverage Ratio
dataPoints.interpretation: Private capital mobilised per dollar of concessional public capital deployed

dataPoints.name: Beneficiary Reach (workers/communities)
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of transition-affected individuals supported through financed programmes

userInteraction: Define the just transition finance mandate by selecting eligible use-of-proceeds categories (retraining, community infrastructure, SME finance)
Review labelled bond and SLB issuance pipeline against CBI Just Transition Criteria eligibility screens
Model blended finance structures using concessional capital tranches and risk sharing agreements
Track SLB social KPI performance against observation dates and assess coupon step-up risk
Generate ICMA Social Bond Principles annual impact report with beneficiary and additionality metrics
references: Climate Bonds Initiative Just Transition Criteria Discussion Paper 2023
CPI Global Landscape of Climate Finance 2023
OECD Blended Finance Principles for Unlocking Commercial Finance for the SDGs
IFC Just Transition Finance Guidance Note 2022

acronyms.def: Measure of incremental capital directed to transition-affected populations above baseline expenditure
Bond with financial characteristics tied to issuer achievement of sustainability KPIs
NGO certifying green and transition bonds against sector-specific science-based criteria
Baseline scenario reflecting continuation of current policies and expenditure patterns

dataLineage.flow: Screen against CBI/ICMA eligibility criteria; tag by just transition category
dataLineage.output: Eligible JT finance deal database with category, size, and tenor

dataLineage.flow: Extract social KPI targets and observation dates; track reported performance
dataLineage.output: KPI achievement status and coupon step-up exposure per bond

dataLineage.flow: Aggregate worker and community reach; compute per-capita capital deployed
dataLineage.output: Beneficiary impact report for ICMA Social Bond Principles annual disclosure

/just-transition-finance.valueSummary: Helps development banks, sovereign issuers, and fund managers structure, monitor, and report credible just transition finance instruments that deliver measurable social benefits to transition-affected workers and communities.

/land-use-carbon.title: Land Use Carbon Analytics
/land-use-carbon.description: Implements IPCC 2006 LULUCF accounting methodologies to quantify carbon stocks and fluxes from land use change, forestry, agriculture, and wetland restoration activities. Supports Article 6 land sector credit origination, REDD+ programme monitoring, and Scope 3 Category 1 land-use emissions attribution for agricultural commodity supply chains. Integrates satellite-derived biomass estimates with national forest inventories.

calculationEngine.formula: ΔC = (Cₜ − Cₜ₋₁) × CF× 44/12
standards: IPCC 2006 Guidelines Vol. 4 Agriculture, Forestry and Other Land Use
GHG Protocol Land Sector & Removals Guidance 2022
calculationEngine.brief: Carbon stock change is computed for five pools: above-ground biomass, below-ground biomass, dead wood, litter, and soil organic carbon. Stocks are derived from Tier 2 biomass expansion factors or Tier 3 allometric equations. The 44/12 conversion converts carbon mass to CO2-equivalent. Permanence and leakage buffers follow Verra or Gold Standard registry requirements.

dataPoints.name: Above-Ground Biomass Density (tC/ha)
dataPoints.source: ESA CCI Biomass / national forest inventory
dataPoints.interpretation: Carbon stock in live above-ground tree biomass per hectare of forest land

dataPoints.name: Soil Organic Carbon (tC/ha)
dataPoints.interpretation: Carbon stored in mineral and organic soils to 30 cm depth

dataPoints.name: Annual Net Flux (MtCO2e/yr)
dataPoints.source: IPCC LULUCF accounts / satellite change detection
dataPoints.interpretation: Net land sector emission or removal in the analysis area per year

dataPoints.name: Permanence Buffer (% of credits)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of earned credits held in risk buffer account against reversal events

userInteraction: Define land use categories and boundaries using the spatial file uploader or jurisdiction selector
Import or connect satellite biomass estimates and cross-validate against national forest inventory reference levels
Run LULUCF carbon stock change calculation across all five IPCC carbon pools
Apply permanence and leakage adjustments for credit origination or voluntary market issuance
Export land sector GHG accounts in UNFCCC common reporting format or Verra registry submission format
references: IPCC 2006 Guidelines for National GHG Inventories â€” Volume 4 AFOLU
Verra VM0007 REDD+ Methodology Framework v1.6
GHG Protocol Land Sector and Removals Guidance 2022

acronyms.full: Land Use, Land-Use Change, and Forestry
acronyms.def: IPCC sector covering carbon fluxes from terrestrial ecosystems and land management

acronyms.full: Reducing Emissions from Deforestation and Forest Degradation
acronyms.def: UNFCCC mechanism for results-based payments for forest conservation in developing countries
Total live organic matter in trees, shrubs, and ground cover above the soil surface
Carbon stored in organic matter within the soil profile

dataLineage.source: ESA CCI / Copernicus satellite biomass products
dataLineage.flow: Mosaic to analysis boundary; validate against field plots; apply uncertainty envelope
dataLineage.output: Spatially explicit biomass density map in tC/ha

dataLineage.flow: Extract species-specific allometric equations; compute per-plot biomass and extrapolate
dataLineage.output: Tier 2 biomass expansion factors by land use category and region

dataLineage.flow: Apply transition matrices to compute area converted per land use class pair per year
dataLineage.output: Annual land use change area matrix for LULUCF flux calculation

/land-use-carbon.valueSummary: Provides forest developers, agri-commodity traders, and land sector financiers with IPCC-consistent carbon accounting tools for REDD+ project development, supply chain deforestation attribution, and voluntary carbon market credit origination.

/land-use-deforestation.title: Land Use & Deforestation
/land-use-deforestation.description: Satellite-based deforestation monitoring and commodity-linked deforestation risk analysis, supporting compliance with the EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) and financial sector due diligence under TNFD and financial institution frameworks. Tracks forest cover loss by commodity supply chain, country, and operator using Global Forest Watch and Copernicus data. Assesses portfolio exposure to deforestation-linked commodities at counterparty level.

calculationEngine.formula: DRSᵢ = ForestLossᵢ × CommodityLinkᵢ × JurisdictionRiskᵢ
calculationEngine.brief: Forest loss area (ha/yr) from Hansen/GFW analysis is linked to commodity production areas via supply-shed mapping. Jurisdiction risk reflects FAO Forest Area percentage, governance index, and zero-deforestation commitment status. The composite DRS enables counterparty ranking and EUDR high-risk country flagging.

dataPoints.name: Forest Cover Loss (ha/yr)
dataPoints.source: Hansen/GFW annual tree cover loss alerts
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual deforested area within commodity supply-shed of the assessed operator

dataPoints.name: Commodity Deforestation Linkage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of forest loss attributable to the specific commodity in the supply-shed

dataPoints.name: EUDR High-Risk Flag
dataPoints.interpretation: Country classification under EUDR Article 29 benchmarking system

dataPoints.name: Zero-Deforestation Commitment Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of traded volume covered by credible zero-deforestation commitments

userInteraction: Upload operator or counterparty list with commodity type, sourcing country, and coordinates of production facilities
Review satellite deforestation alerts overlaid on supply-shed polygons for the past 12 months
Assess EUDR compliance status per operator: high-risk, standard, or low-risk country classification
Score counterparties on the Deforestation Risk Score and prioritise for enhanced due diligence
Generate EUDR due diligence statement data package and export for operator registry submission
references: EU Deforestation Regulation No. 2023/1115 â€” Official Journal
Hansen et al. High-Resolution Global Maps of 21st-Century Forest Cover Change â€” Science 2013
GFW Pro Deforestation Risk Monitoring Methodology
Trase Supply Chain Transparency Platform Documentation

acronyms.def: EU law requiring operators to ensure goods placed on the EU market are not linked to deforestation
WRI platform providing satellite-based near real-time deforestation monitoring data
Composite score quantifying an operator’s supply chain exposure to forest cover loss
Framework for reporting on nature-related risks and opportunities, including biodiversity

dataLineage.source: Hansen / GFW tree cover loss rasters (annual)
dataLineage.flow: Clip to commodity supply-shed polygons; compute ha lost per operator per year
dataLineage.output: Annual deforestation alert area by operator and commodity

dataLineage.flow: Match sourcing countries to high/standard/low classification; flag high-risk operators
dataLineage.output: EUDR compliance status and enhanced due diligence trigger list

dataLineage.flow: Link operator production volumes to forest loss area via supply-shed attribution
dataLineage.output: Commodity-level deforestation linkage proportion per operator

/land-use-deforestation.valueSummary: Enables financial institutions and commodity traders to fulfil EUDR due diligence obligations and TNFD nature disclosure requirements by providing satellite-backed, counterparty-level deforestation risk assessment with audit trail.

/lifecycle-assessment.title: Lifecycle Assessment
/lifecycle-assessment.description: ISO 14040/14044-compliant LCA engine for calculating product carbon footprints and environmental impact across the full value chain from raw material extraction to end-of-life disposal. Supports Scope 3 Category 1 (purchased goods) and Category 11 (use of sold products) quantification and product environmental footprint (PEF) Category Rules compliance for EU Green Claims and CSRD-aligned product disclosures.

calculationEngine.formula: PCF = Σᵢ (Qᵢ × EFᵢ)
standards: EC Product Environmental Footprint Category Rules
calculationEngine.brief: Activity quantities (Q) for each lifecycle stage are multiplied by background emission factors (EF) from ecoinvent or GaBi databases. System boundary follows ISO 14044 cradle-to-grave scope. Allocation follows economic value by default with sensitivity to physical allocation. Uncertainty is propagated using Monte Carlo with 1,000 iterations per product system.

dataPoints.name: Global Warming Potential (kgCO2e/FU)
dataPoints.interpretation: Climate change impact per functional unit across the full product lifecycle

dataPoints.name: Hotspot Stage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Lifecycle stage (material, manufacture, transport, use, EoL) contributing the highest share of PCF

dataPoints.name: Data Quality Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite reliability score across technology, time, geography, completeness, and method representativeness

dataPoints.name: Allocation Sensitivity (Δ%)
dataPoints.interpretation: PCF change when switching from economic to physical mass allocation

userInteraction: Define product system, functional unit, and system boundary in the LCA configuration wizard
Build the process tree by adding lifecycle stages and linking material and energy flows
Select background emission factor sources (ecoinvent v3.10, GaBi, or custom) and apply appropriate cut-off criteria
Run GWP100 calculation and review stage contribution analysis to identify decarbonisation hotspots
Validate data quality using the pedigree matrix and export ISO 14044-conformant LCA report and PCF declaration
references: ISO 14040:2006 â€” Environmental Management â€” Life Cycle Assessment â€” Principles and Framework
ISO 14044:2006 â€” Environmental Management â€” Life Cycle Assessment â€” Requirements and Guidelines
European Commission PEF Category Rules Guidance 2021
GHG Protocol Product Life Cycle Accounting and Reporting Standard 2011

acronyms.def: Total lifecycle GHG emissions of a product expressed per functional unit
Reference unit quantifying the function of the product system studied in LCA
EC methodology for measuring and communicating product lifecycle environmental performance
Systematic compilation of environmental inputs and outputs associated with a product system throughout its lifecycle

dataLineage.source: Bill of materials and supplier data
dataLineage.flow: Match materials to ecoinvent processes; apply geographic and technology proxies; score data quality
dataLineage.output: Process tree with linked background emission factors per material and energy flow

dataLineage.flow: Convert utilities to emission equivalents; account for waste treatment and recycling credits
dataLineage.output: Manufacturing stage GWP contribution per product unit

dataLineage.flow: Model recycling, landfill, incineration routes; apply avoided burden credits per ISO 14044
dataLineage.output: End-of-life stage GWP including recycling credits and disposal impacts

/lifecycle-assessment.valueSummary: Equips product managers and sustainability analysts with ISO-compliant LCA capabilities to quantify PCFs, identify decarbonisation priorities, and prepare product-level disclosures required under EU Green Claims regulation and CSRD product sustainability requirements.

/live-carbon-price-monitor.title: Live Carbon Price Monitor
/live-carbon-price-monitor.description: Real-time monitoring of global carbon market prices across EU ETS (EUA), California Cap-and-Trade (CCA), RGGI, UK ETS, and voluntary carbon markets (CBL, Xpansiv). Aggregates exchange feeds, news sentiment, and regulatory event calendars to contextualise price movements. Provides historical price series, volatility analytics, and price forward curve visualisation for carbon risk management.

calculationEngine.formula: σ_c = √(252) × σ(ln(Pₜ/Pₜ₋₁))
standards: ICAP Emissions Trading Worldwide Status Report 2024
Refinitiv Carbon Research â€” EUA Price Drivers
calculationEngine.brief: Annualised price volatility is computed from daily log returns over a rolling 30-day window, scaled by √252 trading days. Forward curve estimation uses interpolation across exchange-traded futures contracts. Price correlation between compliance markets and voluntary credits is tracked using rolling 90-day Pearson correlation.

dataPoints.name: EUA Spot Price (€/tCO2e)
dataPoints.interpretation: Current EU ETS Phase 4 allowance spot price; key reference for European carbon cost modelling

dataPoints.name: EUA 30-day Volatility (%)
dataPoints.source: Computed from ICE daily settlement prices
dataPoints.interpretation: Annualised price volatility; above 40% indicates elevated carbon market risk

dataPoints.name: CCA Spot Price (USD/tCO2e)
dataPoints.interpretation: California Carbon Allowance current price; benchmark for US West Coast carbon exposure

dataPoints.name: VCM Nature-Based Credit Price (USD/tCO2e)
dataPoints.interpretation: Indicative voluntary market price for REDD+ and afforestation credits

userInteraction: Select compliance markets and voluntary registries to display on the multi-market price dashboard
Review real-time price ticker, 24-hour change, and intraday price chart for each selected market
Analyse 30-day and 90-day price volatility trends and correlation heatmap across markets
Cross-reference price movements with the regulatory event calendar (auctions, MRR deadlines, policy decisions)
Export historical price series and forward curve data for use in carbon cost model and TCFD scenario analysis
references: ICAP Emissions Trading Worldwide Status Report 2024
European Commission EU ETS Directive 2003/87/EC consolidated 2023
World Bank State and Trends of Carbon Pricing Report 2023

acronyms.def: EU ETS compliance unit representing the right to emit one tonne of CO2-equivalent
Compliance unit in California’s Cap-and-Trade Program covering major GHG emitters
US Northeast multi-state cap-and-trade programme for power sector CO2 emissions
Market where entities purchase carbon credits voluntarily to offset emissions outside compliance obligations

dataLineage.flow: Ingest real-time and end-of-day settlement data; clean and normalise to USD/tCO2e
dataLineage.output: Live price ticker and historical daily close series per market

dataLineage.flow: Scrape EC, ARB, RGGI event schedules; tag auction dates, MRR deadlines, policy consultations
dataLineage.output: Event overlay on price chart with annotation and impact flags

dataLineage.flow: Parse carbon market news headlines; apply sentiment classifier; correlate to price moves
dataLineage.output: Sentiment score time series and flagged high-impact news events

/live-carbon-price-monitor.valueSummary: Provides carbon risk managers, treasury teams, and sustainability officers with a real-time, multi-market carbon price intelligence hub to inform hedging decisions, internal carbon price calibration, and TCFD physical and transition risk scenario inputs.

/live-feed-manager.title: Live Feed Manager
/live-feed-manager.description: Central management console for financial and ESG data feed ingestion, covering WebSocket real-time streams, REST API polling, SFTP batch delivery, and third-party vendor feed configurations. Monitors feed health, data latency, field completeness, and schema drift with automated alerting. Supports feed prioritisation, fallback routing, and audit logging for platform data governance.

calculationEngine.formula: FQS = w₁×Completeness + w₂×Timeliness + w₃×Accuracy
standards: DAMA Data Management Body of Knowledge v2
calculationEngine.brief: Completeness measures field population rate against schema. Timeliness measures median delivery latency versus contractual SLA. Accuracy scores anomaly detection flag rate using z-score outlier detection on numeric fields. Weighted composite FQS drives automated feed health traffic-light dashboard and vendor SLA breach alerting.

dataPoints.name: Feed Latency (ms)
dataPoints.source: Platform ingestion timestamp vs. vendor publish timestamp
dataPoints.interpretation: End-to-end delay from data source to platform availability; >500ms triggers SLA alert

dataPoints.name: Field Completeness (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of expected fields populated per inbound record

dataPoints.name: Daily Record Volume
dataPoints.interpretation: Total records ingested per feed per day; drops >20% trigger volume anomaly alert

dataPoints.name: Schema Drift Events (30d)
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of unexpected field additions, removals, or type changes detected in the past 30 days

userInteraction: Review the feed health dashboard to identify feeds with degraded FQS, high latency, or completeness gaps
Drill into a specific feed to view latency distribution, field-level completeness heatmap, and recent schema changes
Configure fallback routing rules to switch to backup vendors when primary feed quality drops below threshold
Set up alerting rules for latency SLA breach, record volume drop, and schema drift events
Access full audit log of ingestion events, quality scores, and manual overrides for data governance reporting
references: DAMA International Data Management Body of Knowledge v2 2017
ISO/IEC 25012:2008 â€” Software Engineering Data Quality Model
IOSCO Principles for Financial Benchmarks Final Report 2013
GLEIF LEI Data Quality Management Framework 2021

acronyms.def: Composite metric assessing completeness, timeliness, and accuracy of an inbound data feed
Contractual commitment defining expected data delivery latency and completeness standards
Encrypted file transfer protocol used for batch ESG data file delivery
OMG standard ontology for financial concepts enabling semantic data interoperability

dataLineage.flow: Ingest real-time or polled records; timestamp on receipt; validate against schema
dataLineage.output: Raw inbound record stream with ingestion metadata

dataLineage.flow: Compare inbound field set to registered schema version; detect drift; log changes
dataLineage.output: Schema conformance report and drift event log

dataLineage.flow: Compute FQS components per feed per hour; persist to time-series store
dataLineage.output: Feed health dashboard and SLA breach alert queue

/live-feed-manager.valueSummary: Gives data engineering and governance teams full visibility and control over ESG and financial data feed quality, ensuring platform analytics are built on complete, timely, and accurate inputs with a defensible audit trail.

/living-wage-tracker.title: Living Wage Tracker
/living-wage-tracker.description: Global benchmarking platform comparing reported or estimated worker wages to living wage standards from Anker Research Institute, WageIndicator Foundation, and Fair Wage Network across 120+ countries and 400+ regions. Quantifies the living wage gap by sector, country, and occupation group and models the cost impact of wage uplift on issuer financials. Supports CSRD ESRS S1 employee matters disclosure and social bond impact reporting.

calculationEngine.formula: LWGᵢ = max(0, LivingWageᵢ − ActualWageᵢ) / LivingWageᵢ
standards: Anker Research Institute Living Wage Benchmarks 2023
CSRD ESRS S1 Employee Matters Standard 2023
calculationEngine.brief: The Living Wage Gap is computed per country-occupation cell as the shortfall of actual or reported median wage below the reference living wage, normalised to the reference. Cost of closure is estimated as LWG × affected headcount × benchmark wage, expressed as percentage of revenue to assess affordability and flagging risk of wage suppression in supply chains.

dataPoints.name: Living Wage Gap (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage shortfall of actual wages below living wage reference for the assessed geography and occupation

dataPoints.name: Workers Below Living Wage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of workforce in the analysis scope earning below the applicable living wage benchmark

dataPoints.name: Wage Uplift Cost (% of revenue)
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated incremental labour cost to close the living wage gap as a proportion of issuer revenue

dataPoints.name: Fair Wage Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite score across living wage, pay equity, social dialogue, and wage management dimensions

userInteraction: Search or upload the entity list and map each company to its primary operating geographies and workforce composition
Select the living wage benchmark source (Anker, WageIndicator, or national statutory minimum as floor)
Review the living wage gap table by country, occupation group, and worker category
Model wage uplift scenarios to quantify cost impact on EBITDA margin and assess affordability
Export CSRD ESRS S1 disclosure data tables covering median wage, pay ratio, and living wage gap metrics
references: Anker Research Institute Living Wage Reference Values by Country 2023
Fair Wage Network Assessment Framework and Score Methodology
CSRD ESRS S1 â€” Own Workforce Standard 2023
ILO World Employment and Social Outlook 2023

acronyms.def: Proportional shortfall between actual or reported wages and the applicable living wage benchmark
CSRD standard covering reporting on own workforce including wages, working conditions, and social dialogue
UN agency setting international labour standards on wages, working conditions, and rights at work
NGO providing 12-dimension wage assessment methodology and fair wage certification

dataLineage.source: Company HR and wage data / CDP supply chain survey
dataLineage.flow: Extract median wage by country and occupation; validate against minimum wage floors
dataLineage.output: Actual wage data per geography and occupation group

dataLineage.flow: Match to entity operating geography and urban/rural classification; apply year adjustment
dataLineage.output: Living wage reference value per country-occupation cell

dataLineage.source: Headcount by country from company reporting
dataLineage.flow: Combine with LWG to compute total cost of gap closure; express as % of revenue
dataLineage.output: Wage uplift cost model and EBITDA impact estimate

/living-wage-tracker.valueSummary: Enables ESG analysts, social bond issuers, and CSRD-reporting companies to quantify, benchmark, and disclose living wage performance, supporting investor engagement on worker welfare and demonstrating supply chain social risk management.

/llm-esg-extractor.title: LLM ESG Extractor
/llm-esg-extractor.description: Large language model-powered extraction engine that identifies, classifies, and normalises ESG data points from unstructured sources including sustainability reports, proxy statements, press releases, contracts, and regulatory filings. Maps extracted entities to ESRS, SASB, GRI, and SFDR data point taxonomies with confidence scoring and evidence citation. Enables systematic ESG data collection at scale without manual analyst input.

calculationEngine.formula: ECS = P(correct entity) × P(correct value) × P(correct unit)
standards: NLP Confidence Calibration â€” Platt Scaling
calculationEngine.brief: ECS is the joint probability of correct entity identification, value extraction, and unit assignment, calibrated using Platt scaling on a held-out labelled corpus. Extractions below ECS 0.70 are flagged for human review. Named entity recognition uses fine-tuned transformer models on ESG domain training data; value extraction applies regex-anchored span detection.

dataPoints.name: Extraction Recall (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of ground-truth ESG data points successfully extracted from documents

dataPoints.name: Extraction Precision (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of extracted data points that are correct, avoiding false positives

dataPoints.name: ESRS Taxonomy Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of mandatory ESRS data points for which at least one extraction is available

dataPoints.name: Average ECS
dataPoints.interpretation: Mean extraction confidence score across all extracted data points in a batch run

userInteraction: Upload a batch of sustainability reports, integrated reports, or filings in PDF or DOCX format
Select target taxonomies (ESRS, GRI, SASB, SFDR PAI) to focus extraction scope and reduce noise
Review extracted data point table with evidence snippets, confidence scores, and taxonomy mappings
Accept, correct, or reject individual extractions using the inline annotation interface
Export validated ESG data set to the central data repository or structured CSV for downstream analytics
references: CSRD ESRS Data Point Taxonomy EFRAG 2023
Devlin et al. BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers 2019
SEC AI/ML Guidance for Financial Disclosures 2023

acronyms.def: Joint probability score assessing correctness of entity, value, and unit identification by the LLM extractor
NLP technique for identifying and classifying named entities such as metrics, organisations, and quantities in text
AI field enabling machines to understand and extract structured information from human language
Framework for evaluating corporate sustainability and societal impact across three non-financial dimensions

dataLineage.flow: OCR if required; section parser; table and text segmentation
dataLineage.output: Structured document corpus with page, section, and paragraph metadata

dataLineage.flow: Apply NER + span extraction; map to taxonomy identifiers; score confidence
dataLineage.output: Extracted data point table with value, unit, evidence snippet, ECS, and taxonomy mapping

dataLineage.flow: Accept / correct / reject per extraction; retrain model on corrections
dataLineage.output: Validated ESG data set with provenance chain and model improvement feedback

/llm-esg-extractor.valueSummary: Scales ESG data collection by orders of magnitude, enabling investment teams and sustainability officers to extract structured, taxonomy-aligned ESG data from thousands of documents in hours rather than weeks of manual analyst effort.

/lp-reporting.title: LP Reporting
/lp-reporting.description: Standardised ESG reporting module for private equity and real assets fund managers preparing investor (LP) sustainability reports aligned with ILPA ESG Data Convergence Initiative (EDCI), UN PRI, and AIFMD Article 23 requirements. Aggregates portfolio company-level ESG data, computes fund-level KPIs, and generates investor-ready report packs. Supports GHG intensity, diversity, and governance metric benchmarking against EDCI cohort data.

calculationEngine.formula: GHG_fund = Σᵢ (GHGᵢ / Revenueᵢ) × (AUMᵢ / AUM_fund)
calculationEngine.brief: Fund-level GHG intensity is the AUM-weighted average of portfolio company Scope 1+2 intensity (tCO2e per USD million revenue). Missing company data is addressed by applying sector EDCI median estimates with disclosure of imputation rate. Board diversity and health & safety metrics follow EDCI standardised definitions and collection templates.

dataPoints.name: Scope 1+2 GHG Intensity (tCO2e/$M rev)
dataPoints.interpretation: AUM-weighted portfolio GHG intensity; enables LP-to-LP comparability via EDCI cohort

dataPoints.name: EDCI Data Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of portfolio companies reporting all six core EDCI KPIs

dataPoints.name: Board Gender Diversity (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of portfolio company board seats held by women across the fund

dataPoints.name: Lost-Time Injury Rate (per 200k hrs)
dataPoints.interpretation: Employee injury frequency rate normalised to 200,000 working hours across portfolio

userInteraction: Configure fund profile with vintage year, strategy, geography, and AIFMD classification
Upload or connect portfolio company ESG data using the EDCI standardised template
Review fund-level KPI computation with imputation flags for companies with incomplete data
Benchmark fund KPIs against EDCI cohort medians by strategy and vintage year

Trade body representing LP interests and publishing private equity operational standards

/maritime-imo-compliance.title: Maritime IMO Compliance
/maritime-imo-compliance.description: Comprehensive compliance analytics for the IMO 2050 decarbonisation strategy, covering CII (Carbon Intensity Indicator) rating computation, EEXI (Energy Efficiency Existing Ship Index) compliance assessment, and EU MRV regulation CO2 reporting. Tracks fleet-level emission intensity trends against IMO trajectory and identifies vessels requiring technical or operational upgrades. Supports Poseidon Principles portfolio alignment for ship finance.

calculationEngine.formula: CII = CO₂ Emitted (g) / (Capacity × Distance Sailed (nm))
calculationEngine.brief: CII is computed from annual voyage-level fuel consumption data converted to CO2 using IMO fuel-specific conversion factors. Capacity is deadweight tonnage for bulk carriers and tankers, or gross tonnage for passenger ships. The CII rating (A–E) is determined by comparing computed CII against ship-type and size-specific required CII with annual tightening factors through 2030.

dataPoints.name: CII Rating
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual carbon intensity rating; D or E for three consecutive years requires an approved corrective action plan

dataPoints.name: EEXI Compliance Gap (% above limit)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage by which the vessel’s attained EEXI exceeds the required EEXI limit; positive indicates non-compliance

dataPoints.name: AER (g CO2/dwt·nm)
dataPoints.source: EU MRV and IMO DCS reporting
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual Efficiency Ratio; key metric for Poseidon Principles climate alignment scoring

dataPoints.name: IMO 2050 Trajectory Alignment (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Fleet deviation from IMO decarbonisation pathway; positive score means above (worse than) trajectory

userInteraction: Import fleet register with vessel type, size class, flag state, build year, and installed engine specifications
Upload EU MRV or IMO DCS voyage-level fuel consumption data for the reporting year
Review automatically computed CII ratings, EEXI attained values, and trajectory alignment per vessel
Identify vessels rated D/E and model corrective action options (speed reduction, engine power limitation, alternative fuels)
Export Poseidon Principles climate alignment report and EU MRV aggregate CO2 emission disclosure
references: IMO Resolution MEPC.338(76) â€” 2021 Guidelines on the Operational Carbon Intensity Indicator (CII)
IMO Resolution MEPC.333(76) â€” 2021 Guidelines on the Energy Efficiency Existing Ship Index (EEXI)
EU MRV Regulation No. 2015/757 on Monitoring, Reporting and Verification of CO2 Emissions from Maritime Transport

acronyms.def: IMO operational efficiency metric measuring CO2 emitted per unit of transport work annually
IMO technical index measuring design-stage energy efficiency of existing ships against baseline
Metric used by the Poseidon Principles expressing annual CO2 emissions per deadweight tonnage-nautical mile
IMO mandatory system requiring ships above 5,000 GT to collect and report annual fuel consumption data

dataLineage.source: EU MRV / IMO DCS voyage data
dataLineage.flow: Aggregate fuel consumption by voyage; apply IMO CO2 conversion factors; compute transport work
dataLineage.output: Annual CII and AER per vessel

dataLineage.flow: Extract installed power, MCR, and shaft power limitations; apply ship-type EEXI formula
dataLineage.output: Attained EEXI vs. required EEXI compliance status per vessel

/materiality-scenarios.title: Materiality Scenarios
/materiality-scenarios.description: Forward-looking materiality assessment engine that evaluates how ESG topic materiality evolves under different regulatory, market, and climate scenarios. Integrates CSRD double materiality, ISSB financial materiality, and SASB sector standards with scenario-specific impact and likelihood adjustments. Enables dynamic materiality mapping that anticipates regulatory shifts and stakeholder expectation changes over 5–10 year horizons.

calculationEngine.formula: SMSᵢₛ = Materialityᵢ × (1 + ΔLikelihoodᵢₛ + ΔImpactᵢₛ)
calculationEngine.brief: Base materiality scores reflect current impact and financial significance per ESRS 1 and IFRS S1 criteria. Scenario adjustments (Δ) modify likelihood and impact estimates based on regulatory probability, market repricing, and physical hazard intensification in each scenario. Scenario weighting enables probability-adjusted materiality profiles for multi-scenario disclosure.

dataPoints.name: Topics Above Materiality Threshold (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of assessed ESG topics meeting materiality threshold in the analysis scenario

dataPoints.name: Regulatory Scenario Probability (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Assigned probability of the regulatory scenario materialising within the assessment horizon

dataPoints.name: Materiality Score Variance (Δ)
dataPoints.interpretation: Absolute difference in materiality score for a topic between the base and the most adverse scenario

dataPoints.name: Topic Emergence Lead Time (yrs)
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated years until a currently sub-threshold topic crosses the materiality threshold under the trend scenario

userInteraction: Define the materiality assessment scope, sector classification, and value chain boundaries for the entity
Select scenario set: regulatory acceleration, market repricing, physical intensification, or combined stress
Review scenario-adjusted materiality heat map showing score changes per topic per scenario
Identify emerging topics crossing the materiality threshold under adverse scenarios and prioritise for disclosure
Export scenario-adjusted materiality matrix for integration into CSRD DMA documentation and ISSB S1 disclosure
references: CSRD ESRS 1 General Requirements Standard â€” Double Materiality Guidance 2023
ISSB IFRS S1 General Requirements for Disclosure of Sustainability-related Financial Information 2023
TCFD Technical Supplement: The Use of Scenario Analysis in Disclosure 2017
EFRAG Implementation Guidance on Materiality Assessment 2023

acronyms.def: CSRD-required assessment of both impact materiality and financial materiality for each sustainability topic
Materiality score modified for probability and magnitude changes under a specified future scenario
CSRD overarching standard defining reporting principles, entity-specific requirements, and double materiality
CSRD taxonomy for categorising sustainability-related matters as impacts, financial risks, or financial opportunities

dataLineage.flow: Score topics on impact significance and financial significance scales; apply ESRS 1 thresholds
dataLineage.output: Base-year materiality assessment matrix

dataLineage.flow: Assign probability and impact adjustment to each pending regulation per scenario

/materiality-trends.title: Materiality Trends
/materiality-trends.description: Analyses the historical evolution of ESG topic materiality by sector using longitudinal data from SASB standards revisions, investor engagement priorities, regulatory activity, and academic ESG materiality research. Identifies topics experiencing rapid materiality growth (emerging), declining relevance (fading), or structural shifts driven by regulatory catalysts. Informs dynamic materiality assessment and strategic ESG topic prioritisation.

calculationEngine.formula: MTIᵢₜ = (Investor Priority Scoreᵢₜ + Regulatory Activity Scoreᵢₜ + Litigation Risk Scoreᵢₜ) / 3
standards: Harvard Law School Forum on Corporate Governance ESG Survey
calculationEngine.brief: The Materiality Trend Index aggregates three signal dimensions: investor priority (engagement letter frequency and AUM of requesting investors), regulatory activity (volume of new rules and consultation responses), and litigation risk (ESG-related legal action frequency and damages quantum). Monthly scores are smoothed with a 6-month exponential moving average.

dataPoints.name: MTI Score (0–100)
dataPoints.source: Composite of investor, regulatory, and litigation signals
dataPoints.interpretation: Current materiality trend strength; above 70 indicates a rapidly emerging or highly active topic

dataPoints.name: Trend Velocity (MTI/yr)
dataPoints.interpretation: Rate of materiality change; high positive velocity signals accelerating investor and regulatory attention

dataPoints.name: Regulatory Catalyst Events
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of significant regulatory proposals or enactments affecting the topic in the past 12 months

dataPoints.name: Sector Materiality Consensus (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of companies in the sector for which the topic is deemed financially material by SASB

userInteraction: Select sector and geographic scope to filter the materiality trend database to comparable peer universe
Review MTI time series chart for topics of interest to identify inflection points and trend reversals
Rank topics by trend velocity to surface rapidly emerging materiality concerns ahead of mainstream adoption
Cross-reference regulatory catalyst event overlay with MTI spikes to understand materiality drivers
Export trend analysis to support dynamic materiality assessment documentation and investor communications
references: Khan, Serafeim, Yoon â€” Corporate Sustainability: First Evidence on Materiality (2016)
Eccles & Serafeim â€” The Performance Frontier: Innovating for a Sustainable Strategy (2013)
Harvard Law School Forum on Corporate Governance ESG Annual Survey 2023

acronyms.def: Composite monthly score tracking the pace and direction of ESG topic materiality evolution by sector
Standard-setting body publishing industry-specific financially material ESG disclosure standards
Smoothing technique that weights recent observations more heavily to dampen short-term noise in trend series
Framework for evaluating corporate sustainability performance across three non-financial dimensions

/mdb-climate-finance.title: MDB Climate Finance
/mdb-climate-finance.description: Tracks and analyses climate finance flows from multilateral development banks (World Bank, IFC, ADB, AfDB, EBRD, EIB, IADB) against the $100bn collective goal, Paris alignment targets, and COP28 MDB Joint Climate Finance commitments. Quantifies co-financing leverage ratios, grant element, and private capital mobilisation per institution, region, and sector. Supports OECD DAC Rio marker methodology compliance and MDB Joint Climate Finance Tracking methodology.

calculationEngine.formula: LR = (Private + Bilateral + Other) / MDB Core Climate Finance
standards: MDB Joint Climate Finance Tracking Methodology 2023
CPI Global Landscape of Climate Finance 2023
UNFCCC Standing Committee on Finance Biennial Assessment
calculationEngine.brief: Leverage ratio captures total climate finance mobilised per dollar of MDB core concessional or non-concessional climate finance. Private capital mobilisation is attributed per OECD DAC methodological guidance on measuring catalytic effects. The ratio is computed by institution, region (global/developing country), and adaptation/mitigation split.

dataPoints.name: MDB Climate Finance (USD bn)
dataPoints.interpretation: Total commitments from all seven major MDBs classified as climate finance under joint methodology

dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of MDB climate finance directed to adaptation and resilience vs. mitigation

dataPoints.name: Grant Element (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Degree of concessionality in MDB climate instruments; higher grant element indicates greater subsidy for adaptation-critical projects

userInteraction: Select MDB institutions and reporting years to scope the climate finance tracking dashboard
Review climate finance volumes by institution, sector, and developing vs. developed country split
Analyse adaptation/mitigation split against $100bn commitment targets and COP28 goals
Assess private capital mobilisation leverage ratios and identify instruments with highest catalytic effect
Export MDB climate finance data aligned with UNFCCC Standing Committee Biennial Assessment reporting templates
references: MDB Joint Report on Climate Finance 2023
OECD DAC Methodological Note on Measuring Mobilisation of Private Finance 2023
UNFCCC Standing Committee on Finance Biennial Assessment 2022
CPI Global Landscape of Climate Finance 2023
COP28 UAE Consensus â€” New Collective Quantified Goal on Climate Finance

OECD body setting standards for measuring, monitoring, and reporting official development assistance

UNFCCC financial mechanism channelling climate finance to developing countries for mitigation and adaptation

/ml-risk-scorer.title: ML Risk Scorer
/ml-risk-scorer.description: Machine learning-based ESG risk scoring engine that trains, evaluates, and deploys classification and regression models on structured ESG and financial data. Supports XGBoost, random forest, logistic regression, and neural network architectures with full feature importance, SHAP explainability, and model performance monitoring. Applies SR 11-7 and ECB model risk management guidance to document model governance requirements.

calculationEngine.formula: Scoreᵢ = f(β₁X₁ + β₂X₂ + ... + βₙXₙ)
standards: SR 11-7 Supervisory Guidance on Model Risk Management
calculationEngine.brief: The scoring model maps ESG feature vectors to risk scores using gradient-boosted trees by default. Feature importance is computed via SHAP Shapley values, providing additive feature attribution consistent with game-theoretic fairness axioms. Model performance is monitored using Population Stability Index (PSI) to detect distribution shift in production inputs, triggering revalidation workflows.

dataPoints.name: Model AUC-ROC
dataPoints.interpretation: Area under ROC curve; above 0.75 indicates acceptable discrimination for ESG risk classification

dataPoints.name: SHAP Feature Importance (top 3)
dataPoints.interpretation: Top three features by mean absolute SHAP value driving model risk score output

dataPoints.name: Population Stability Index (PSI)
dataPoints.interpretation: Distribution shift metric for model inputs; high PSI triggers mandatory model revalidation

dataPoints.name: Model Accuracy (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of correctly classified ESG risk categories on held-out validation data

userInteraction: Configure the training data set by selecting features from the ESG database and defining the target variable (risk class or score)
Train the selected model architecture and review performance metrics on train/validation/test split
Analyse SHAP feature importance plots to validate that model drivers are economically interpretable
Run PSI monitoring check to assess whether production data distribution has shifted from training sample
Generate model validation documentation aligned with SR 11-7 and ECB model risk management requirements
references: Federal Reserve SR 11-7 Supervisory Guidance on Model Risk Management 2011
ECB Guide on Internal Models â€” Model Risk Management Chapter 2019
Lundberg & Lee â€” A Unified Approach to Interpreting Model Predictions (SHAP) 2017
ISO/IEC 42001:2023 â€” Artificial Intelligence Management System Standard
Chen & Guestrin â€” XGBoost: A Scalable Tree Boosting System 2016

acronyms.def: Framework for computing feature attributions using Shapley values from cooperative game theory
Statistical measure comparing input feature distributions between training and production to detect model drift
ROC curve summary metric measuring model discrimination ability across all classification thresholds
Gradient boosted decision tree algorithm widely used for structured data classification and regression

dataLineage.flow: Extract, clean, and engineer features; handle missing values; normalise scales
dataLineage.output: Training feature matrix with target labels for ML model fitting

dataLineage.flow: Cross-validate on train/val/test splits; compute AUC, F1, and calibration metrics
dataLineage.output: Model performance report and champion model selection

dataLineage.flow: Score new entities via deployed model endpoint; log PSI and score distribution
dataLineage.output: Entity-level ESG risk scores with SHAP explanations and monitoring alerts

/model-governance.title: Model Governance Hub
/model-governance.description: Centralised ESG model risk management platform covering model inventory, validation workflows, documentation standards, approval gating, and ongoing performance monitoring across all quantitative ESG and climate risk models. Implements SR 11-7, ECB, and PRA model risk management supervisory expectations with role-based access control, version management, and independent validation assignment. Supports both in-house and vendor model governance.

calculationEngine.formula: MRR = Materiality × Complexity × (1 + Concentration)
standards: Federal Reserve SR 11-7 Supervisory Guidance on Model Risk Management
PRA SS1/23 Model Risk Management Principles 2023
calculationEngine.brief: Model Risk Rating is the product of materiality score (business impact), complexity score (technical sophistication), and a concentration uplift reflecting the model’s role as a critical single point of failure. High MRR models (above 75th percentile) require annual full validation and independent model risk committee approval. Low MRR models qualify for ongoing monitoring without full revalidation.

dataPoints.name: Models in Inventory
dataPoints.interpretation: Total number of quantitative ESG and climate risk models registered and governed in the platform

dataPoints.name: Models Awaiting Validation (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of registered models past their scheduled validation date without approved revalidation

dataPoints.name: Mean Validation Cycle Time (days)
dataPoints.interpretation: Average elapsed time from model validation initiation to independent validation committee approval

dataPoints.name: Critical Model (High MRR) Count
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of models rated High MRR requiring annual full validation and senior sign-off

userInteraction: Register a new model by completing the model intake form including purpose, scope, data inputs, and output metrics
Assign model risk rating using the materiality, complexity, and concentration scoring rubric
Initiate validation workflow and assign to independent validator; track documentation completeness checklist
Review validation findings, model limitations documentation, and approve or request remediation
Monitor ongoing model performance using the PSI and outcome analysis dashboard; trigger revalidation when thresholds are breached
references: Federal Reserve SR 11-7 Supervisory Guidance on Model Risk Management 2011
ECB Guide on Internal Models 2019 â€” Chapter on Model Risk Management
PRA Supervisory Statement SS1/23 â€” Model Risk Management Principles for Banks 2023
Basel Committee on Banking Supervision BCBS 239 â€” Risk Data Aggregation 2013
ISO/IEC 42001:2023 â€” Artificial Intelligence Management System

acronyms.def: Composite score assessing the significance and complexity of a quantitative model for governance purposes
Framework for identifying, measuring, monitoring, and controlling risks arising from use of quantitative models
Senior governance body responsible for approving high-risk models and overseeing model risk management framework
Statistical measure comparing input feature distributions between model development and production periods

dataLineage.source: Model intake forms and technical documentation
dataLineage.flow: Classify by model type, risk rating, and validation status; register in inventory
dataLineage.output: Structured model registry with metadata, ownership, and governance status

dataLineage.output: Validation report with finding severity classification and remediation requirements

/modern-slavery-intel.title: Modern Slavery Intelligence
/modern-slavery-intel.description: Supply chain forced labour detection and modern slavery compliance platform supporting UK Modern Slavery Act (MSA), Australian Modern Slavery Act, and US UFLPA (Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act) due diligence obligations. Combines AI-powered adverse media screening, supplier risk scoring, and regulatory entity list screening to identify forced labour exposure across Tier 1–3 supply chains. Generates annual modern slavery statement data.

calculationEngine.formula: FLRSᵢ = SectorRiskᵢ + CountryRiskᵢ + AdverseMediaScoreᵢ + RegulatoryListHitᵢ
standards: Global Slavery Index â€” Walk Free Foundation 2023
calculationEngine.brief: Forced Labour Risk Score aggregates sector inherent risk (ILO forced labour sector mapping), country risk (Global Slavery Index national prevalence), adverse media score (AI screen of news for 11 ILO forced labour indicators), and regulatory entity list hit status. Scores above 70 trigger enhanced due diligence workflow requiring supplier questionnaire and independent audit.

Generate UK and Australian MSA annual statement sections from aggregated supply chain risk data
references: UK Modern Slavery Act 2015 â€” Transparency in Supply Chains Provisions (Section 54)
US Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act (UFLPA) 2021
ILO Hard to See, Harder to Count â€” Survey Guidelines on Indicators of Forced Labour 2012
Walk Free Foundation Global Slavery Index 2023
