/monte-carlo-var.title: Monte Carlo VaR
/monte-carlo-var.description: Monte Carlo simulation engine for climate risk Value at Risk (VaR) computation, modelling the distribution of portfolio losses under stochastic climate transition and physical risk scenarios. Supports full revaluation simulation with 10,000+ paths, tail risk analytics (CVaR/Expected Shortfall), and copula-based cross-asset correlation structures. Provides regulatory climate stress testing input for ICAAP and ORSA frameworks.

calculationEngine.formula: CVaRα = E[L | L > VaRα]
standards: EIOPA Climate Change Stress Test Methodology 2022
Glasserman Monte Carlo Methods in Financial Engineering 2003
calculationEngine.brief: Portfolio loss distribution is generated by simulating correlated climate risk factor paths (carbon price, physical hazard frequency, stranded asset write-downs) using Cholesky-decomposed correlated normal variates. Full revaluation of each portfolio position at each simulation path produces the empirical loss distribution. CVaR is the expected loss in the tail beyond the VaRα confidence threshold (typically α = 0.95 or 0.99).

dataPoints.name: Climate VaR (95%, 1-yr, USD)
dataPoints.interpretation: Maximum expected climate-driven portfolio loss not exceeded in 95% of simulated scenarios

dataPoints.name: CVaR/Expected Shortfall (95%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Average loss in the worst 5% of simulated climate scenarios; more sensitive to tail risk than VaR

dataPoints.name: Simulation Paths
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of Monte Carlo paths run; more paths reduce sampling error in tail estimates

dataPoints.name: Carbon Price Shock Contribution (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of total CVaR attributable to carbon price transition risk factor

userInteraction: Define the portfolio positions and select asset-level climate risk factor sensitivities from the risk model library
Configure simulation parameters: number of paths, time horizon, correlation structure, and climate factor volatility
Run simulation and review the loss distribution histogram with VaR and CVaR markers at selected confidence levels
Decompose VaR by climate risk factor (carbon price, physical hazard, policy shock) using factor attribution
Export simulation results for ICAAP climate stress test section and regulatory capital quantification
references: BCBS Principles for the Effective Management and Supervision of Climate-related Financial Risks 2021
ECB Economy-wide Climate Stress Test â€” Methodology and Results 2021
EIOPA Climate Change Stress Testing Insurance Sector 2022
Glasserman â€” Monte Carlo Methods in Financial Engineering (Springer 2003)
NGFS Climate Scenarios for Central Banks and Supervisors 2023

acronyms.def: Expected portfolio loss given that the loss exceeds the VaR threshold; also known as Expected Shortfall
Maximum portfolio loss not exceeded at a given confidence level over a specified time horizon
Insurer self-assessment of risks and capital adequacy under Solvency II Pillar 2
Bank self-assessment of capital adequacy relative to risk profile under Basel Pillar 2

dataLineage.flow: Map positions to climate risk factor sensitivities; assign sector and geography
dataLineage.output: Position-level risk factor loading matrix

dataLineage.flow: Generate correlated factor paths via Cholesky decomposition; apply to factor loadings
dataLineage.output: Simulated loss distribution across all Monte Carlo paths

dataLineage.flow: Align factor volatility and shock distributions to NGFS Disorderly / Hot House scenarios
dataLineage.output: Scenario-conditioned simulation inputs for regulatory stress test submission

/monte-carlo-var.valueSummary: Enables risk managers to quantify the distribution of climate-driven portfolio losses with statistical rigour, supporting ICAAP climate stress test requirements, capital buffer sizing, and investor climate risk disclosures.

/multi-factor-integration.title: Multi-Factor Integration
/multi-factor-integration.description: Simultaneous integration of ESG quality, smart beta (value, momentum, low volatility, quality), and macroeconomic factors in portfolio construction and risk attribution. Uses Barra-style factor model frameworks to decompose portfolio active return and risk across ESG, style, sector, and macro dimensions. Enables ESG-tilted factor portfolios that control for unintended sector and style biases while preserving ESG signal integrity.

calculationEngine.formula: max αᵀw − λ wᵀΣw subject to constraints
standards: MSCI Barra Global Equity Factor Model GEM3 Documentation
Grinold & Kahn â€” Active Portfolio Management 2000
calculationEngine.brief: Portfolio weights (w) are optimised to maximise expected multi-factor alpha (α) minus a risk-aversion-scaled (λ) variance term using the factor covariance matrix (Σ). Factor alphas for ESG, value, momentum, quality, and macro combine via information coefficient-weighted composite signal. Constraints enforce sector, country, and single-stock limits alongside ESG minimum score floors.

dataPoints.name: ESG Factor IC (Information Coefficient)
dataPoints.interpretation: Correlation between ESG signal and forward 12-month returns; positive IC validates ESG as a return factor

dataPoints.name: Active Factor Exposure (β vs. benchmark)
dataPoints.interpretation: Active loading on each factor relative to benchmark; near-zero indicates unintended factor bets are neutralised

dataPoints.name: Factor Diversification Ratio
dataPoints.interpretation: Ratio of weighted average factor volatility to portfolio volatility; higher values indicate better factor diversification

dataPoints.name: Tracking Error (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Expected annualised active return volatility relative to benchmark from multi-factor risk model

userInteraction: Configure the factor model by selecting ESG signal source, smart beta factors, and macro overlay signals
Set factor signal weights using IC-based or equal-weight combination and review composite alpha signal
Run multi-factor optimisation with ESG score floor, sector, and tracking error constraints
Analyse factor contribution to active risk and return using the Barra-style attribution decomposition
Rebalance portfolio quarterly and review factor drift to determine whether reoptimisation is triggered
references: Fama & French â€” Common Risk Factors in the Returns on Stocks and Bonds (1993)
MSCI Barra Global Equity Model GEM3 Research Notes
Grinold & Kahn â€” Active Portfolio Management 2nd Edition 2000
Asness, Frazzini, Pedersen â€” Quality Minus Junk (2019)

acronyms.def: Correlation between a factor signal and subsequent returns; measures signal predictive power
Standard deviation of portfolio active returns relative to benchmark; measures active risk taken
Single-factor asset pricing model relating expected return to systematic market risk beta
Multi-factor asset pricing framework modelling returns as linear combinations of risk factor exposures

dataLineage.source: ESG ratings and raw pillar scores
dataLineage.flow: Z-score by sector to remove sector bias; compute ESG alpha signal
dataLineage.output: Sector-neutralised ESG factor signal per security

dataLineage.flow: Compute smart beta factor scores: B/P, momentum, low vol, profitability, investment
dataLineage.output: Multi-factor alpha signal matrix for optimisation input

dataLineage.flow: Decompose portfolio variance into factor and specific components; compute ex-ante TE
dataLineage.output: Factor attribution: active return and risk decomposition by factor type

/multi-factor-integration.valueSummary: Enables systematic equity managers to build ESG-integrated factor portfolios that capture proven risk premia while maintaining ESG quality standards, with full transparency into factor interaction effects and active risk sources.

/multi-tenancy-audit.title: Multi-Tenancy Audit
/multi-tenancy-audit.description: Enterprise-grade audit and verification module for multi-tenant SaaS deployments, ensuring data isolation, access control integrity, configuration separation, and compliance boundary enforcement across tenant accounts. Validates row-level security policies, API key scoping, audit log completeness, and encryption key segregation. Generates SOC 2 Type II evidence artefacts and ISO 27001 access control audit reports.

calculationEngine.formula: TIS = (Passed Isolation Tests / Total Isolation Tests) × 100
standards: SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria 2017 â€” Logical Access Controls
NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 â€” Access Control and Audit Controls
calculationEngine.brief: Tenant Isolation Score measures the proportion of isolation control tests passing automated verification. Tests cover row-level security bypass attempts, cross-tenant API call simulation, audit log gap analysis, encryption key cross-contamination, and configuration bleed detection. Scores below 95% indicate critical isolation failures requiring immediate remediation.

dataPoints.name: Tenant Isolation Score (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of isolation controls passing verification; below 95% triggers P0 escalation

dataPoints.name: Audit Log Completeness (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of expected user and API events recorded in immutable audit log per tenant

dataPoints.name: RLS Policy Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of data tables with enforced row-level security policies scoped to tenant ID

dataPoints.name: Cross-Tenant Data Leakage Events
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of successful cross-tenant data access events detected in penetration testing

userInteraction: Run automated tenant isolation test suite to verify RLS policies, API key scoping, and session isolation
Review audit log completeness report per tenant and identify any event type gaps
Inspect encryption key segregation status to confirm per-tenant key derivation and storage isolation
Simulate cross-tenant access attempts using the penetration test harness and review detected vs. blocked events
Generate SOC 2 and ISO 27001 evidence packages from test results and audit log samples for external auditor submission
references: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 â€” Information Security Management Systems Requirements
NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 â€” Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems 2020
OWASP Testing Guide v4.2 â€” Business Logic Testing

acronyms.def: Database security feature restricting data row access based on the current user or tenant identity
Percentage of isolation control tests passing automated verification in a multi-tenant deployment
AICPA audit standard assessing security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy controls
CSA framework of cloud security controls mapped to ISO 27001, SOC 2, and NIST standards

dataLineage.source: Database schema and RLS policy definitions
dataLineage.flow: Enumerate all tenant-scoped tables; verify RLS policy presence and correctness via SQL test queries
dataLineage.output: RLS coverage matrix with pass/fail per table and tenant

dataLineage.flow: Parse API requests for cross-tenant key usage; flag any tenant ID mismatch between key and data accessed
dataLineage.output: Cross-tenant access attempt log with blocked/passed status

dataLineage.flow: Compute expected vs. received event counts by type; identify gaps; verify tamper-evidence hashes
dataLineage.output: Audit log completeness report and integrity verification certificate

/multi-tenancy-audit.valueSummary: Gives platform security and compliance teams automated, continuous verification of multi-tenant isolation controls with auditable evidence supporting SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certifications and regulatory data protection obligations.

/natcat-loss-engine.title: NatCat Loss Engine
/natcat-loss-engine.description: Probabilistic natural catastrophe loss modelling engine covering flood, windstorm, earthquake, wildfire, and tropical cyclone perils using industry-standard exceedance probability (EP) curve methodology. Computes average annual loss (AAL), probable maximum loss (PML), and return period loss estimates for property and infrastructure portfolios. Integrates RCP/SSP climate change factors to project loss escalation under warming scenarios.

calculationEngine.formula: PMLᵀ = EP_curve⁻¹(1/T)
standards: IPCC AR6 WG1 Extremes Assessment Chapter 11
calculationEngine.brief: The PML at return period T is read from the site exceedance probability curve, derived by convolving hazard intensity distribution, vulnerability function, and financial exposure. Climate change factors multiply hazard intensity distributions per RCP scenario following IPCC AR6 probabilistic projections for each peril. AAL is the integral of the EP curve across all return periods.

dataPoints.name: Average Annual Loss (USD)
dataPoints.interpretation: Long-run expected annual loss from natural catastrophe events per insured or exposure portfolio

dataPoints.name: PML 250-year (USD)
dataPoints.source: EP curve at T=250 return period
dataPoints.interpretation: Loss expected to be exceeded once in 250 years; typical regulatory and reinsurance treaty reference point

dataPoints.name: Climate Change AAL Uplift (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage increase in AAL under RCP 8.5 2050 climate change relative to current climate baseline

dataPoints.name: Coefficient of Variation (AAL)
dataPoints.interpretation: Ratio of standard deviation to mean of simulated annual losses; higher values indicate greater loss volatility

userInteraction: Upload property or infrastructure exposure file with location, construction type, occupancy, and insured value
Select perils and regional hazard modules and set climate change scenario and projection year
Run probabilistic loss analysis and review EP curve, AAL, and return period loss table
Decompose losses by peril, region, and construction class to identify concentration risk
Export PML table and EP curves for reinsurance treaty pricing, regulatory capital calculation, and TCFD physical risk disclosure
references: AIR Worldwide Touchstone Catastrophe Modelling Platform Documentation
RMS Risk Intelligence NatCat Methodology Guide
IPCC AR6 Working Group I Chapter 11 â€” Weather and Climate Extreme Events 2021
Lloyd’s of London Realistic Disaster Scenarios 2023
EIOPA NatCat Methodology for Solvency II Standard Formula

acronyms.def: Long-run expected value of insured losses from natural catastrophe events per year
Loss expected to be exceeded at a specified return period from natural catastrophe events
Probability that total annual losses exceed a given dollar threshold in any single year
IPCC GHG concentration trajectory used to project future climate hazard intensification

dataLineage.flow: Geocode to peril hazard grid; look up hazard intensity from regional hazard module
dataLineage.output: Site-level hazard intensity distribution per peril

dataLineage.flow: Match construction type and occupancy to damage function; compute mean damage ratio per intensity
dataLineage.output: Site-level loss ratio distribution for convolution with exposure

dataLineage.flow: Simulate 10,000 event loss table years; sort; build exceedance probability curve
dataLineage.output: Portfolio EP curve, AAL, PML table, and return period loss estimates

/natcat-loss-engine.valueSummary: Enables insurers, reinsurers, and real asset managers to quantify, stress test, and disclose natural catastrophe loss exposure with climate change projection, supporting reinsurance structuring, Solvency II SCR computation, and TCFD physical risk quantification.

/nature-hub.title: Nature Risk & Opportunities Hub
/nature-hub.description: Integrated hub for nature-related risk and opportunity assessment following the TNFD LEAP (Locate, Evaluate, Assess, Prepare) framework. Combines biodiversity footprint analysis, ecosystem service dependency mapping, and nature-positive target tracking across portfolio companies. Integrates IBAT biodiversity data, ENCORE ecosystem service database, and TNFD disclosure templates to support A15 nature disclosures and SBTN target alignment.

calculationEngine.formula: BFS = Σᵢ (Impact Driverᵢ × Ecosystem Sensitivityᵢ × Areaᵢ)
calculationEngine.brief: Biodiversity Footprint Score aggregates the product of land-use impact driver intensity, ecosystem sensitivity weight, and affected area across all operational footprints and upstream supply chains. Ecosystem sensitivity weights derive from IUCN threat categories and IBAT critical habitat proximity. The score enables portfolio-level nature impact comparison and SBTN Step 1 scoping.

dataPoints.name: Biodiversity Footprint Score (MSA·km²)
dataPoints.interpretation: Mean Species Abundance impact per square kilometre; higher values indicate greater biodiversity loss contribution

dataPoints.name: Critical Habitat Proximity (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of operational sites within or adjacent to IUCN Key Biodiversity Areas or critical habitats

dataPoints.name: Ecosystem Service Dependency (High/Med/Low)
dataPoints.interpretation: Level of business model dependency on ecosystem services (water, pollination, climate regulation, etc.)

dataPoints.name: Nature-Positive Target Progress (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Progress toward company-level nature-positive commitments relative to SBTN no-net-loss baseline

userInteraction: Complete the TNFD LEAP Locate step by mapping operational and supply chain footprints to biomes and ecosystems
Run the ENCORE dependency screen to identify high and very high ecosystem service dependencies by business segment
Conduct IBAT critical habitat proximity analysis for priority operational sites
Complete the Assess step by quantifying nature-related financial risks (physical, regulatory, reputational) from TNFD risk register
Prepare TNFD disclosure aligned with recommended disclosures A-M across governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics pillars
references: TNFD Recommendations of the Taskforce on Nature-related Financial Disclosures v1.0 2023
SBTN Science-Based Targets for Nature â€” Initial Guidance for Business 2023
ENCORE Exploring Natural Capital Opportunities, Risks and Exposure â€” UNEP-WCMC
IBAT Integrated Biodiversity Assessment Tool â€” IUCN/BirdLife/UNEP-WCMC/CI
GBS Global Biodiversity Score Methodology â€” CDC Biodiversité 2020

acronyms.def: Framework providing guidance on reporting and acting on nature-related risks and opportunities
TNFD’s four-phase process for identifying and assessing nature-related dependencies, impacts, and risks
Initiative developing science-based target frameworks for nature across land, freshwater, ocean, and atmosphere

acronyms.full: Exploring Natural Capital Opportunities Risks and Exposure
acronyms.def: UNEP-WCMC tool mapping business sector dependencies and impacts on ecosystem services

dataLineage.flow: Geocode to biome and ecoregion; overlay IBAT KBA and critical habitat layers
dataLineage.output: Site-level critical habitat proximity flags and biome classification

dataLineage.flow: Match sector to ecosystem service dependency matrix; rank services by dependency level
dataLineage.output: Ecosystem service dependency profile by business segment and sector

dataLineage.flow: Map sourcing geographies to deforestation and ecosystem degradation risk by commodity
dataLineage.output: Upstream nature impact footprint by commodity and sourcing region

/nature-hub.valueSummary: Gives corporate sustainability teams and institutional investors the framework, data, and analytics to identify, assess, and disclose nature-related financial risks and opportunities in line with TNFD recommendations and emerging regulatory requirements.

/nature-loss-risk.title: Nature Loss Risk
/nature-loss-risk.description: Quantifies portfolio exposure to financial risks arising from nature loss and ecosystem service degradation through supply chain dependency mapping and physical nature risk scoring. Assesses how biodiversity decline, deforestation, soil degradation, and freshwater stress translate to revenue disruption, cost escalation, and regulatory liability for investee companies. Supports TNFD Assess pillar and CSRD ESRS E4 biodiversity disclosure.

calculationEngine.formula: NLFRSᵢ = Σⱼ (Dependencyᵢⱼ × ServiceDegradationⱼ × RevenueExposureᵢⱼ)
standards: CSRD ESRS E4 Biodiversity and Ecosystems 2023
IPBES Global Assessment Report on Biodiversity 2019
calculationEngine.brief: Nature Loss Financial Risk Score aggregates revenue exposure-weighted products of ecosystem service dependency level (High=3, Medium=2, Low=1) and ecosystem service degradation trajectory (IPBES degradation index) across all services on which each company’s operations and supply chain depend. Higher scores indicate greater revenue vulnerability to ecosystem decline.

dataPoints.name: NLFRS (0–100)
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite nature loss financial risk score; above 60 indicates material revenue exposure to ecosystem degradation

dataPoints.name: High Dependency Services Count
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of ecosystem services on which the business has high dependency, heightening nature loss exposure

dataPoints.name: Revenue at Risk from Nature Loss (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of business revenue dependent on ecosystem services that are in significant decline globally

dataPoints.name: Freshwater Stress Exposure (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of operations or supply chain sourcing in high or extremely high water stress basins

userInteraction: Select companies or portfolio for nature loss risk screening using the ENCORE sector dependency tool
Map each company to its ecosystem service dependencies and review degradation status per service
Overlay operational and supply chain geographies onto freshwater stress, soil degradation, and deforestation risk maps
Compute NLFRS and identify the ecosystem services driving highest financial risk per company
Prepare CSRD ESRS E4 disclosure data on biodiversity impacts, dependencies, and nature-related financial risks
references: CSRD ESRS E4 â€” Biodiversity and Ecosystems Standard 2023
WEF Nature Risk Rising: Why the Crisis Engulfing Nature Matters for Business and the Economy 2020
IPBES Global Assessment Summary for Policymakers 2019

acronyms.def: Composite score quantifying revenue exposure to financial risk from ecosystem service degradation
CSRD standard requiring disclosure of company impacts and dependencies on biodiversity and ecosystems

acronyms.full: Intergovernmental Science-Policy Platform on Biodiversity and Ecosystem Services
acronyms.def: UN-convened body assessing the state of global biodiversity and ecosystem services
IUCN-designated site critically important for the global persistence of biodiversity

dataLineage.flow: Match company sector to dependency intensity matrix; rank services by dependency level
dataLineage.output: Ecosystem service dependency profile per company and sector

dataLineage.flow: Extract service-specific degradation index by geography; map to company supply chain sourcing regions
dataLineage.output: Ecosystem service degradation trajectory per dependency and geography

dataLineage.flow: Overlay with freshwater stress, soil carbon, and forest cover datasets
dataLineage.output: Nature risk spatial exposure heatmap by site and sourcing region

/nature-loss-risk.valueSummary: Helps portfolio managers and sustainability analysts translate the abstract risk of nature loss into quantified revenue-at-risk estimates, enabling prioritised engagement with high-exposure companies and credible TNFD and ESRS E4 disclosures.

/nature-scenarios.title: Nature Scenarios
/nature-scenarios.description: Scenario analysis platform for assessing portfolio impacts under nature-positive transition pathways drawn from IPBES transformative change scenarios, SBTN nature-positive trajectories, and TNFD illustrative nature scenarios. Models how biodiversity regulation, ecosystem restoration mandates, deforestation bans, and natural capital pricing affect corporate revenues, costs, and asset values across sectors and geographies. Supports TNFD Prepare pillar forward-looking disclosure.

calculationEngine.formula: NSISᵢₛ = Σⱼ (Policy Shockⱼₛ × Sector Sensitivityᵢⱼ × Revenue Exposureᵢⱼ)
calculationEngine.brief: Scenario impact is computed as the sum across nature policy shocks (deforestation bans, ecosystem restoration levies, pollinator protection mandates, natural capital pricing) of the product of policy shock magnitude (% cost uplift or revenue constraint), sector sensitivity (dependency-adjusted exposure), and revenue exposure fraction. Positive scores indicate net revenue risk; negative indicate nature-positive opportunity capture.

dataPoints.name: Nature Scenario Revenue Impact (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated revenue impact range under the nature-positive transition scenario on a 5–10 year horizon

dataPoints.name: Regulatory Cost Uplift (% EBITDA)
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated EBITDA impact from nature-related regulatory costs (deforestation bans, ecosystem levies) in the scenario

dataPoints.name: Nature Opportunity Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite score capturing revenue and competitive advantage opportunities from nature-positive market shifts

dataPoints.name: Scenario Time Horizon
dataPoints.interpretation: Target year for nature policy implementation used in scenario modelling

userInteraction: Select the nature scenario set: TNFD Illustrative Scenarios, IPBES sustainability pathways, or custom policy combinations
Map portfolio companies to sectors with nature-related policy exposure using ENCORE and EUDR/CBAM regulatory pipeline
Run NSIS computation across all scenario-policy combinations and review sector-level impact rankings
Identify companies with highest net nature transition risk and those positioned to benefit from nature-positive market growth
Prepare TNFD Prepare pillar disclosure with scenario narratives, assumptions, and financial impact ranges
references: TNFD Nature-related Risk and Opportunity Assessment â€” Illustrative Scenarios Annex 2023
IPBES Global Assessment Scenarios and Models Chapter 2019
SBTN Science-Based Targets for Nature â€” Step 2 Ambition Guidance 2023
EC Biodiversity Strategy 2030 and Nature Restoration Law 2023

acronyms.def: Composite score quantifying estimated revenue and cost impact of nature-positive transition policies on a company or portfolio
EU regulation mandating restoration of 20% of EU land and sea areas by 2030 with binding national targets
Initiative developing science-based methodologies for corporate nature targets across freshwater, land, ocean, and atmosphere

acronyms.full: Intergovernmental Science-Policy Platform on Biodiversity and Ecosystem Services
acronyms.def: UN-convened body providing assessments and scenarios on global biodiversity status and trends

dataLineage.flow: Extract policy shock magnitudes and timelines by sector and geography; calibrate to EC NRL and SBTN targets
dataLineage.output: Policy shock parameter matrix by scenario, sector, and year

dataLineage.flow: Map sector nature dependencies to policy shock exposure; weight by revenue share
dataLineage.output: Sector sensitivity matrix for NSIS computation

dataLineage.flow: Apply revenue exposure fractions and policy shock magnitudes; compute net EBITDA and revenue impact range
dataLineage.output: Company and portfolio-level nature scenario financial impact output

/nature-scenarios.valueSummary: Enables investors and corporate strategists to quantify the financial implications of nature-positive transition scenarios, identify exposure concentrations, and prepare credible forward-looking TNFD nature scenario disclosures aligned with IPBES and SBTN pathways.

/fixed-income-esg.title: Fixed Income ESG
/fixed-income-esg.description: Integrates ESG factors into corporate bond portfolio construction and monitoring, capturing spread differentials attributable to ESG quality. Provides greenium analytics comparing green bond yields against matched conventional bond curves, enabling attribution of ESG alpha and risk-adjusted performance measurement.

calculationEngine.formula: OAS_esg = OAS_benchmark + β_esg × ESG_score + β_dur × Duration + ε
calculationEngine.brief: Decomposes option-adjusted spread into benchmark, ESG, duration, and idiosyncratic components using multivariate regression across the bond universe. The ESG beta coefficient quantifies the spread compression per unit ESG score improvement, isolating greenium from duration and credit effects.

dataPoints.name: Greenium (bps)
dataPoints.interpretation: Negative greenium indicates green bonds trade richer than conventional peers; values beyond -10 bps suggest supply-demand imbalance.

dataPoints.name: ESG Score (issuer)
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite ESG score reflecting environmental management, social practices, and governance quality; scores above 70 associate with tighter spreads historically.

dataPoints.name: Carbon Intensity (tCO2e/$M revenue)
dataPoints.interpretation: Scope 1+2 intensity normalised by revenue; high-intensity issuers exhibit statistically wider spreads controlling for credit rating.

dataPoints.name: Green Bond Allocation (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of portfolio AUM in labelled green, social, or sustainability bonds; tracks alignment with internal SRI mandates.

userInteraction: Filter the bond universe by credit rating, sector, and ESG score threshold to define the investable set.
Run the OAS attribution model to decompose spread by ESG, duration, and credit quality components.
Review greenium signal tab to identify mispriced green bonds trading wider than the fitted curve.
Use the sector heatmap to identify ESG concentration risk and rebalance toward higher-scoring issuers.
references: Larcker & Watts (2020) â€” Where's the Greenium?
ECB Working Paper: Green Bond Premium (2021)

acronyms.def: Bond yield spread over the risk-free curve after removing embedded option value, used for like-for-like credit comparison.
Yield differential between a labelled green bond and a matched conventional bond from the same issuer or comparable credit.
Bloomberg's evaluated pricing service providing independent fair-value estimates for fixed income securities.

dataLineage.flow: Normalise to 0â€“100 scale, map to ISIN universe
dataLineage.output: ESG-adjusted OAS per bond

dataLineage.flow: Verify use-of-proceeds alignment, match to conventional peer
dataLineage.output: Greenium in basis points

dataLineage.flow: Compute weighted average ESG score and carbon intensity
dataLineage.output: Portfolio-level ESG summary KPIs

/fixed-income-esg.valueSummary: Enables fixed income portfolio managers to quantify the ESG spread premium, screen issuers by carbon intensity, and optimise allocation toward labelled green bonds without sacrificing yield targets. Supports regulatory reporting under SFDR Article 8/9 and EU Green Bond Standard alignment.

/food-supply-chain-emissions.title: Food Supply Chain Emissions
/food-supply-chain-emissions.description: Quantifies agricultural GHG emissions across the food value chain with Scope 3 Category 1 granularity, enabling SBTi FLAG (Forest, Land and Agriculture) target setting and supplier engagement. Covers enteric fermentation, fertiliser application, land-use change, and post-farm processing emissions using commodity-specific emission factors.

calculationEngine.formula: FLAG_intensity = Σ(commodity_i × EF_i × LUC_multiplier_i) / Revenue_USD
calculationEngine.brief: Aggregates land-based emissions across sourced commodities using IPCC Tier 2 emission factors adjusted for land-use change multipliers derived from Pendrill et al. deforestation attribution model. Results are normalised by revenue to produce FLAG intensity metrics comparable to SBTi sector benchmarks.

dataPoints.name: FLAG Intensity (tCO2e/$M revenue)
dataPoints.interpretation: Commodity-weighted land-based emission intensity; food & beverage sector median is approximately 350 tCO2e/$M; values above 600 signal high deforestation-linked commodities.

dataPoints.name: LUC Emissions Share (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of total FLAG emissions attributable to land-use change; soy, palm, and beef supply chains typically exceed 40%.

dataPoints.name: Supplier Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of Tier 1 spend with supplier-reported emissions data; below 60% triggers modelled estimation using spend-based proxies.

dataPoints.name: SBTi FLAG Target Gap (tCO2e/yr)
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual absolute emissions reduction required to achieve the FLAG pathway by 2030 relative to base year.

userInteraction: Upload commodity procurement data by supplier and map each commodity to IPCC Tier 2 emission factor categories.
Apply LUC multipliers for high-deforestation-risk commodities (soy, palm, beef, cocoa, timber).
Compute FLAG intensity and compare against SBTi sector benchmark to determine target gap.
Prioritise supplier engagement actions using the supplier risk matrix ranked by emission contribution and data quality score.
references: Pendrill et al. (2022) â€” Disentangling the Numbers Behind Agriculture-Based Deforestation
IPCC AR6 WG3 Chapter 7 â€” Agriculture, Forestry and Other Land Use

acronyms.def: SBTi guidance category covering land-based emissions from agriculture, forestry, and land-use change.
GHG emissions resulting from conversion of forests, peatlands, or other carbon-rich ecosystems to agricultural land.
IPCC-derived coefficient converting commodity production quantity to CO2-equivalent emissions.

dataLineage.source: Procurement spend data by commodity and supplier
dataLineage.flow: Map to HS codes, assign IPCC Tier 2 EFs, apply LUC multipliers
dataLineage.output: FLAG emissions by commodity and supplier

dataLineage.flow: Validate against third-party deforestation databases (Global Forest Watch)
dataLineage.output: Supplier data quality scores

dataLineage.flow: Compare portfolio FLAG intensity to sector pathway
dataLineage.output: Annual target gap in absolute tCO2e

/food-supply-chain-emissions.valueSummary: Supports food, beverage, and retail companies in setting science-based FLAG targets, prioritising supplier decarbonisation investments, and meeting CSRD ESRS E1/E4 land-use disclosure requirements. Provides the evidentiary basis for CDP forests questionnaire responses and TNFD nature disclosures.

/framework-interop.title: Framework Interoperability
/framework-interop.description: Maps disclosure requirements across TCFD, ISSB IFRS S1/S2, ESRS (CSRD), GRI Standards, and CDP frameworks to identify overlaps, gaps, and sequencing opportunities. Enables organisations to build a single disclosure architecture that satisfies multiple frameworks simultaneously, reducing duplication and compliance cost.

calculationEngine.formula: Coverage_ij = |Requirements_i ∩ Requirements_j| / |Requirements_i ∪ Requirements_j|
calculationEngine.brief: Computes Jaccard similarity between framework requirement sets to quantify inter-framework alignment. Each requirement is tagged by topic (climate, water, biodiversity, governance, social) and disclosure type (governance, strategy, risk management, metrics/targets) enabling structured gap analysis.

dataPoints.name: TCFD-to-ISSB Alignment (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: ISSB IFRS S2 incorporates substantially all TCFD recommendations; residual 13% relates to industry-specific metrics not in TCFD.

dataPoints.name: ESRS-to-ISSB Overlap (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Two-thirds of ISSB S2 datapoints are satisfied by ESRS E1 compliance; the gap relates to transition plan granularity requirements.

dataPoints.name: Unique GRI Requirements
dataPoints.interpretation: GRI covers social and economic topics not addressed by TCFD/ISSB/ESRS, including tax, anti-corruption, and supply chain labour practices.

dataPoints.name: CDP-to-TCFD Alignment (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: CDP questionnaire questions map almost entirely to TCFD pillars; residual items relate to water and forests modules.

userInteraction: Select the frameworks applicable to your organisation based on jurisdiction, listing venue, and stakeholder requirements.
Run the coverage matrix to identify which requirements are satisfied by a single disclosure and which require framework-specific responses.
Use the gap analysis tab to prioritise data collection for requirements unique to each framework.
Export the unified disclosure calendar with framework-by-framework mapping for project planning.
references: EFRAG â€” ESRS Set 1 Final Standards (July 2023)
CDP Technical Note: Reporting on Climate (2023)

acronyms.def: IFRS Foundation body that issues global baseline sustainability disclosure standards IFRS S1 and S2.
EU mandatory disclosure standards issued under CSRD, covering E, S, and G topics.
Global environmental disclosure system used by companies to report on climate, water, and forests.

dataLineage.flow: Tag requirements by topic and disclosure type, compute Jaccard matrix
dataLineage.output: Interoperability coverage heatmap

dataLineage.flow: Map existing disclosures to framework requirements
dataLineage.output: Gap analysis by framework

dataLineage.flow: Sequence requirements by jurisdiction and mandatory vs voluntary status
dataLineage.output: Prioritised disclosure roadmap

/framework-interop.valueSummary: Reduces disclosure duplication by identifying a minimum set of datapoints that simultaneously satisfies TCFD, ISSB, ESRS, GRI, and CDP. Enables sustainability teams to build a single data architecture serving all frameworks and provides a compliance roadmap aligned to regulatory deadlines across jurisdictions.

/fund-of-funds.title: Fund of Funds ESG
/fund-of-funds.description: Aggregates ESG metrics and carbon footprint across fund-of-fund holdings using look-through methodology, penetrating sub-fund layers to compute portfolio-level weighted averages. Supports SFDR Article 8/9 classification at the FoF level and PAI indicator aggregation across underlying funds with varying data availability.

calculationEngine.formula: CF_FoF = Σ_f (w_f × Σ_i (w_fi × EVIC_fi⁻¹ × Emissions_fi))
calculationEngine.brief: Applies PCAF look-through methodology by weighting each underlying fund by its FoF allocation, then weighting each underlying holding by its sub-fund weight, normalising financed emissions by EVIC at each level. Where sub-fund holdings are unavailable, fund-level carbon intensity proxies are applied with a data quality score penalty.

dataPoints.name: Look-Through Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of FoF NAV for which full holding-level data is available; below 70% triggers proxy-based estimation.

dataPoints.name: Weighted Avg Carbon Intensity (tCO2e/$M)
dataPoints.interpretation: Revenue-normalised Scope 1+2 carbon intensity aggregated across all look-through positions.

dataPoints.name: SFDR PAI Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of mandatory PAI indicators computable from available sub-fund disclosures without estimation.

dataPoints.name: Sub-Fund ESG Classification
dataPoints.interpretation: Distribution of sub-funds by SFDR article; FoF Article 9 status requires all sub-funds to be Article 8 or above.

userInteraction: Load sub-fund holdings using available transparency reports and SFDR periodic disclosures.
Apply look-through weights to aggregate holding-level ESG scores and emissions to the FoF level.
Review data quality dashboard to identify sub-funds with low look-through coverage and apply proxy adjustments.
Generate SFDR PAI template with aggregated indicators and data quality notes for annual report disclosure.
references: SFDR Delegated Regulation (EU) 2022/1288 â€” Annex I/II

acronyms.def: Investment vehicle that allocates capital to a portfolio of underlying funds rather than directly to securities.
SFDR mandatory indicators measuring negative sustainability effects of investment decisions.
PCAF-defined denominator for financed emissions calculations: market cap plus total debt plus cash.

dataLineage.source: Sub-fund transparency reports and holdings files
dataLineage.flow: Apply look-through weights, map to ISIN universe
dataLineage.output: Holding-level ESG and emissions data

dataLineage.source: SFDR PAI disclosures from underlying funds
dataLineage.flow: Aggregate PAI indicators weighted by FoF allocation
dataLineage.output: FoF-level PAI summary table

dataLineage.flow: Compute FoF weights, apply PCAF look-through formula
dataLineage.output: Weighted average carbon footprint and intensity

/fund-of-funds.valueSummary: Enables fund-of-fund managers to demonstrate SFDR compliance at the product level despite one or two layers of fund intermediation, produce PCAF-aligned financed emissions disclosures, and identify sub-funds that constrain the FoF ESG profile through low transparency or high carbon intensity.

/geopolitical-ai-gov.title: Geopolitical AI Governance
/geopolitical-ai-gov.description: Provides governance controls and bias-detection analytics for AI models used in geopolitical risk assessment, ensuring model outputs are explainable, auditable, and free from geographic or political bias. Covers model card documentation, fairness metrics, adversarial robustness testing, and regulatory alignment with the EU AI Act and OECD AI Principles.

calculationEngine.formula: DIR = P(ŷ=1 | group=A) / P(ŷ=1 | group=B)
calculationEngine.brief: Computes the ratio of positive prediction rates across geographic and political groupings to detect systematic model bias. A DIR below 0.80 (four-fifths rule) triggers mandatory bias investigation and retraining under EU AI Act Article 10 data governance requirements for high-risk systems.

dataPoints.name: Disparate Impact Ratio
dataPoints.interpretation: Values outside 0.80â€“1.25 range indicate potential geographic bias requiring model audit; values near 1.0 indicate equal treatment across groups.

dataPoints.name: Model Explainability Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite score reflecting proportion of model predictions explainable by identified features; below 0.65 fails EU AI Act transparency requirements.

dataPoints.name: Adversarial Robustness (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of predictions unchanged under structured adversarial perturbation; below 80% indicates fragile geopolitical risk signals.

dataPoints.name: Audit Trail Completeness (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of AI-assisted decisions with full input, output, and model version logged; mandatory for regulatory audit.

userInteraction: Review model card for each active geopolitical AI model, checking training data provenance and known limitations.
Run the disparate impact analysis across geographic regions and political regime groupings to detect systematic bias.
Execute adversarial robustness tests using structured geopolitical scenario perturbations.
Generate the EU AI Act conformity assessment report with SHAP-based feature attribution for high-impact predictions.

Mehrabi et al. (2021) â€” A Survey on Bias and Fairness in Machine Learning

acronyms.def: Statistical fairness metric measuring the ratio of positive outcome rates between demographic or geographic groups.
Game-theoretic approach to explain the output of machine learning models by attributing predictions to input features.
NIST structured approach for managing AI risks across the model lifecycle.

Reduces regulatory and reputational risk from biased or opaque AI-assisted geopolitical risk scores informing investment decisions.

/geopolitical-esg-hub.title: Geopolitical ESG Hub
/geopolitical-esg-hub.description: Analyses the intersection of geopolitical risk events and ESG factor dynamics, quantifying how political instability, sanctions, trade conflicts, and resource nationalism affect portfolio ESG scores and carbon transition risk. Provides scenario-based stress testing of ESG metrics under geopolitical shock scenarios.

calculationEngine.formula: ΔESG_i = α + β_geo × GPR_index + β_sanction × Sanction_dummy + ε
calculationEngine.brief: Estimates portfolio ESG score sensitivity to the Caldara-Iacoviello Geopolitical Risk Index using panel regression across holdings, with sanctions exposure as a binary covariate. The geopolitical beta quantifies ESG score erosion per 100-point GPR index increase, enabling scenario-based ESG impact assessment.

dataPoints.name: Geopolitical Risk Beta (β_geo)
dataPoints.interpretation: Negative beta indicates ESG scores decline with rising geopolitical risk; values below -0.15 signal high exposure to governance and social factor deterioration.

dataPoints.name: Sanctions-Exposed AUM (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio weight in securities from issuers with direct or indirect sanctions exposure; above 5% requires enhanced due diligence.

dataPoints.name: Resource Nationalism Risk Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite score reflecting probability of government expropriation, contract renegotiation, or export restrictions in operating geographies.

dataPoints.name: ESG Score Drawdown under GPR Shock
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated ESG score decline under a 200-point GPR index shock, calibrated to 2022 Russia-Ukraine geopolitical shock.

userInteraction: Select the geopolitical scenario (sanctions escalation, trade war, resource nationalisation) and calibrate the GPR shock magnitude.
Review the portfolio heatmap showing ESG sensitivity by issuer country and sector under the selected scenario.
Analyse the governance factor breakdown to identify which ESG pillars are most exposed to geopolitical deterioration.
Export the scenario stress test results for integration into TCFD risk management disclosures.
references: Caldara & Iacoviello (2022) â€” Measuring Geopolitical Risk
UNPRI â€” ESG Factors in Sovereign Debt Investing (2020)
MSCI â€” Geopolitical Risk and ESG Scores (2023)
World Bank Political Risk Insurance Group â€” Resource Nationalism Monitor

acronyms.def: Caldara-Iacoviello index measuring geopolitical risk based on newspaper coverage of adverse geopolitical events.
US Treasury agency administering economic and trade sanctions.
Three-pillar framework for evaluating non-financial sustainability and governance performance of issuers.

dataLineage.flow: Regress against ESG score panel data by issuer
dataLineage.output: Geopolitical ESG sensitivity betas

dataLineage.output: Sanctions exposure flags by holding

dataLineage.output: Stressed portfolio ESG distribution

/geopolitical-esg-hub.valueSummary: Enables portfolio managers and risk officers to quantify the ESG impact of geopolitical shocks, stress-test governance scores under sanctions or conflict scenarios, and satisfy TCFD risk management disclosure requirements for geopolitical transition risks.

/gfanz-sector-pathways.title: GFANZ Sector Pathways
/gfanz-sector-pathways.description: Provides analytics for assessing portfolio and counterparty alignment with Glasgow Financial Alliance for Net Zero sector decarbonisation pathways, covering power, steel, cement, aviation, shipping, agriculture, and real estate. Enables financial institutions to measure and report on portfolio alignment to sector-specific net-zero pathways as required under GFANZ membership commitments.

calculationEngine.formula: Alignment_s = 1 - (Intensity_portfolio_s - Intensity_pathway_s(t)) / (Intensity_baseline_s - Intensity_pathway_s(t))
standards: GFANZ Guidance on Use of Sectoral Pathways (2022)
calculationEngine.brief: Measures the fractional progress of each sector toward its net-zero pathway target, where 1.0 indicates full alignment and 0.0 indicates no progress from baseline. The pathway intensity at time t is interpolated from the IEA NZE or NGFS 1.5°C-aligned scenario trajectory for each sector.

dataPoints.name: Power Sector Alignment Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Scores above 0.70 indicate the financed power generation mix is consistent with net-zero by 2050; coal-heavy portfolios typically score below 0.30.

dataPoints.name: Steel Sector Carbon Intensity (tCO2/t steel)
dataPoints.interpretation: Global average is 1.85 tCO2/t; NZE pathway target is 0.4 tCO2/t by 2050, requiring ~80% intensity reduction.

dataPoints.name: Aviation Sector SAF Blend (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Sustainable aviation fuel blend rate; GFANZ pathway requires 65% SAF by 2050; current industry average is under 1%.

dataPoints.name: Shipping Alignment (CII Rating)
dataPoints.interpretation: Carbon Intensity Indicator rating; Poseidon Principles alignment target requires fleet average CII rating of A or B by 2030.

userInteraction: Select the sectors present in your portfolio and load issuer-level production and intensity data for each.
Map issuers to GFANZ sector pathway benchmarks using the IEA NZE or NGFS 1.5°C scenario trajectory.
Compute sector alignment scores and identify issuers whose current intensity exceeds the pathway target.
Generate the GFANZ-format sector pathway alignment report for annual climate commitment reporting.
references: GFANZ â€” Guidance on Use of Sectoral Pathways for Financial Institutions (2022)
IEA Net Zero by 2050 â€” A Roadmap for the Global Energy Sector (2021)
Poseidon Principles â€” Climate Alignment Assessment (2023)

acronyms.full: Glasgow Financial Alliance for Net Zero
acronyms.def: Coalition of leading financial institutions committed to accelerating the decarbonisation of the global economy.
IEA scenario consistent with limiting global warming to 1.5°C with no or limited overshoot.
Low-carbon aviation fuel produced from sustainable feedstocks, key to aviation sector decarbonisation.
IMO operational metric rating ship carbon efficiency on an Aâ€“E scale.

dataLineage.source: Issuer production and emissions data (CDP / company reports)
dataLineage.flow: Normalise intensity by sector-specific production metric, compare to pathway

/governance-hub.title: Governance Analytics Hub
/governance-hub.description: Provides comprehensive board composition, audit quality, executive remuneration, and shareholder rights analytics across portfolio holdings, enabling governance factor integration into ESG scoring and active ownership strategies. Covers ISS and Glass Lewis proxy voting alignment, board independence, gender diversity, and remuneration-sustainability linkage metrics.

calculationEngine.formula: GQS = w_1 × BoardInd + w_2 × AuditQuality + w_3 × RemunAlign + w_4 × ShareholderRights
calculationEngine.brief: Constructs a composite governance quality score from four sub-pillars weighted by empirical association with long-run shareholder returns. Board independence and audit quality account for 55% of the composite; remuneration alignment to sustainability targets accounts for 15%, reflecting increasing regulatory emphasis on ESG-linked pay.

dataPoints.name: Board Independence (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of independent non-executive directors; best practice threshold is 50% (UK Code) to 2/3 (ISS policy); below 50% flags governance concern.

dataPoints.name: Gender Diversity (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Female representation on the board; EU gender balance directive targets 40% for listed companies by 2026.

dataPoints.name: ESG Pay Linkage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of executive long-term incentive plan linked to quantified ESG/sustainability metrics; above 25% associated with stronger E&S outcomes.

dataPoints.name: Audit Committee Independence (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Best practice requires fully independent audit committee; deviations require explain-or-comply disclosure.

userInteraction: Filter the portfolio by governance score decile to identify holdings with material governance deficiencies.
Review the board composition tab to assess independence, diversity, and tenure concentration risks.
Analyse remuneration linkage to sustainability KPIs and compare against say-on-pay vote outcomes.
Export engagement priorities ranked by governance score gap and portfolio weight for stewardship team.
references: EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive â€” ESRS G1 (2023)

acronyms.def: Leading proxy advisory firm providing governance research and voting recommendations to institutional investors.
Executive remuneration structure linking pay to multi-year performance targets, increasingly including ESG metrics.
Global membership organisation of institutional investors promoting effective corporate governance standards.

/green-bond-portfolio-analytics.title: Green Bond Portfolio Analytics
/green-bond-portfolio-analytics.description: Provides comprehensive analytics for green, social, sustainability, and sustainability-linked bond (GSS+) portfolios including use-of-proceeds tracking, impact reporting, greenium quantification, and ICMA Principles alignment. Enables portfolio managers to monitor labelled bond allocation, verify impact claims, and produce investor impact reports.

calculationEngine.formula: Greenium_i = ZSpread_conventional_matched - ZSpread_green_i
calculationEngine.brief: Estimates greenium by computing the Z-spread differential between each green bond and a matched conventional bond from the same issuer with equivalent maturity and seniority. Where no exact match exists, a synthetic conventional yield is fitted using a cubic spline on the issuer's conventional bond curve.

dataPoints.name: Portfolio Greenium (bps)
dataPoints.interpretation: Weighted average greenium across GSS+ holdings; negative values indicate green bonds trade richer, reducing yield but reflecting market ESG premium.

dataPoints.name: Use-of-Proceeds Allocation (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of green bond proceeds allocated to eligible green projects per issuer reports; unallocated proceeds above 5% flag monitoring concern.

dataPoints.name: Impact: Renewable Energy Capacity (MW)
dataPoints.interpretation: Aggregate renewable energy capacity financed across portfolio's green bond holdings, reported per €1M invested.

dataPoints.name: CBI Certification Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of GSS+ portfolio holdings carrying third-party CBI certification; higher rates indicate stronger use-of-proceeds integrity.

userInteraction: Load the GSS+ bond portfolio and classify each holding by bond type (green, social, sustainability, SLB) and ICMA Principles version.
Run greenium analysis to compute Z-spread differentials against matched conventional bonds for each holding.
Review the use-of-proceeds tracker to verify project category allocations and identify unallocated or misallocated proceeds.
Generate the investor impact report aggregating financed renewable energy, emissions avoided, and social beneficiaries across all holdings.
references: Bloomberg BNEF â€” Green Bond Market Outlook (2024)

acronyms.def: Collective label for labelled bonds with explicit sustainability use-of-proceeds or performance targets.
Bond with coupon step-up/step-down linked to issuer achievement of predefined sustainability performance targets.
International NGO providing the Climate Bonds Taxonomy and certification scheme for green debt instruments.

dataLineage.flow: Classify by ICMA type, extract use-of-proceeds categories
dataLineage.output: Labelled bond classification database

dataLineage.source: Issuer green bond allocation and impact reports

dataLineage.output: Portfolio impact report

dataLineage.flow: Fit spline, compute Z-spread differential
dataLineage.output: Greenium by holding in basis points

/green-building-cert.title: Green Building Certification
/green-building-cert.description: Tracks BREEAM, LEED, and ENERGY STAR certification status across real estate portfolio assets, computing certification-driven premium analytics and identifying gap-to-certification for uncertified assets. Supports EU Taxonomy real estate technical screening criteria alignment and SFDR PAI 18 (energy inefficient real estate) monitoring.

calculationEngine.formula: EPG = (EUI_actual - EUI_benchmark) / EUI_benchmark × 100
standards: EU Taxonomy Real Estate TSC (Delegated Act)
calculationEngine.brief: Computes the energy use intensity gap between each asset's actual consumption and the certification benchmark, expressed as a percentage deviation. Assets with EPG above +20% are flagged for energy efficiency investment prioritisation; assets achieving EPG below -15% qualify for top-quartile certification labels (BREEAM Excellent / LEED Platinum).

dataPoints.name: BREEAM Certified (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of portfolio floor area with valid BREEAM certification; BREEAM Excellent or Outstanding required for EU Taxonomy substantial contribution.

dataPoints.name: Energy Use Intensity (kWh/m²/yr)
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual energy consumption normalised by gross internal area; EU Taxonomy office threshold is 70 kWh/m²/yr primary energy.

dataPoints.name: Certification Premium (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Rental or capital value premium of certified vs uncertified comparable assets; BREEAM Excellent assets command 4â€“6% rental premium in major European markets.

dataPoints.name: ENERGY STAR Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentile score vs comparable US building stock; score ≥75 qualifies for ENERGY STAR certification.

userInteraction: Upload the real estate asset register with energy performance certificates and existing certification details.
Review the certification gap analysis to identify assets within reach of the next certification tier.
Use the EU Taxonomy screening tab to assess which certified assets meet technical screening criteria for real estate.
Prioritise capex for energy efficiency upgrades based on certification premium uplift vs investment cost.
references: LEED v4.1 Building Operations and Maintenance
EU Taxonomy Delegated Act â€” Real Estate Technical Screening Criteria (2021)
JLL â€” Green Premium in European Real Estate (2022)

acronyms.full: Building Research Establishment Environmental Assessment Method
acronyms.def: UK-originated green building certification system rating buildings across energy, water, materials, transport, and ecology.
Annual energy consumption per unit floor area (kWh/m²/yr), the primary metric for building energy benchmarking.
Quantitative thresholds defining what economic activities substantially contribute to EU environmental objectives.

dataLineage.source: Energy performance certificates and meter data

dataLineage.output: EPG scores by asset

/green-building-certification.title: Green Building Cert Manager
/green-building-certification.description: Manages the end-to-end certification workflow for BREEAM, LEED, WELL, and NABERS across a real estate portfolio, tracking assessment stages, assessor assignments, evidence uploads, and certification renewal deadlines. Provides portfolio-level certification pipeline reporting and automated renewal alerts to prevent certificate lapse.

calculationEngine.formula: CRI = Σ_i (evidence_i × w_i) / Σ_i w_i × (1 - days_to_deadline / 365)
calculationEngine.brief: Weights evidence completion by credit category importance and discounts by time-to-deadline proximity to produce a readiness score between 0 and 1. Assets with CRI above 0.75 are on track for certification; assets below 0.50 with deadlines within 90 days trigger escalation alerts.

dataPoints.name: Certification Pipeline Value (€M)
dataPoints.interpretation: Aggregate GAV of assets currently in active certification process across all schemes; tracks workflow progress by certification stage.

dataPoints.name: Evidence Completion (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Average percentage of required certification evidence items submitted across assets in the pipeline.

dataPoints.name: Renewal Risk Assets
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of certified assets with certificates expiring within 12 months requiring renewal assessment initiation.

dataPoints.name: WELL Feature Compliance (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of WELL health and wellbeing features currently met; 50+ features required for WELL Silver certification.

userInteraction: Add assets to the certification pipeline and assign the target certification scheme, target rating, and assessment deadline.
Upload evidence documents against each credit or feature category and track completion percentage by section.
Assign assessors and review their progress dashboards for credits under assessment.
Monitor the renewal calendar and initiate renewal workflows for certificates approaching expiry.

acronyms.def: Performance-based certification system measuring built environment features affecting human health across air, water, nourishment, light, and mind.

acronyms.def: Australian rating system measuring actual energy, water, waste, and indoor environment performance of buildings.

dataLineage.output: Renewal risk dashboard

/green-central-banking.title: Green Central Banking
/green-central-banking.description: Analyses central bank climate risk frameworks, NGFS membership analytics, green quantitative easing (QE) programme design, and climate-aligned collateral frameworks. Tracks progress of 130+ NGFS member central banks against climate-related financial risk supervisory expectations and green monetary policy tool adoption.

calculationEngine.formula: Tilt_score = (GreenShare_portfolio - GreenShare_market) / GreenShare_market × 100
standards: BIS Working Papers â€” Green QE
calculationEngine.brief: Measures the degree to which a central bank's asset purchase programme overweights green bonds and underweights carbon-intensive issuers relative to the market benchmark. A positive tilt score indicates active green tilting; a score of zero indicates market-neutral (proportional) purchasing as per the ECB pre-2021 approach.

dataPoints.name: NGFS Member Central Banks
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of central banks and supervisors that have joined the Network for Greening the Financial System; covers approximately 90% of global GDP.

dataPoints.name: ECB Green Bond Share (% CSPP)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of ECB Corporate Sector Purchase Programme holdings in labelled green bonds; reflects post-2021 climate tilt in corporate bond purchases.

dataPoints.name: Eligible Green Collateral (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of eligible collateral universe meeting green criteria under expanded central bank collateral eligibility frameworks.

dataPoints.name: Climate Stress Test Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of NGFS member central banks that have conducted or are planning climate stress tests of the banking system.

userInteraction: Select the central bank jurisdiction and review its NGFS commitment level and climate risk framework implementation status.
Analyse the green QE tilting score and collateral eligibility breakdown for the selected central bank.
Compare climate stress test methodologies and scenario assumptions across major central banks.
Review the supervisory expectations tracker for climate-related prudential guidance issued to financial institutions.
references: NGFS â€” Recommendations for Central Banks and Supervisors on Climate-Related Financial Risks (2021)
BIS Working Paper 818 â€” Green Central Banking (2020)
Bank of England â€” Climate Biennial Exploratory Scenario (CBES) 2021

Monetary policy tool involving central bank asset purchases to inject liquidity and lower long-term interest rates.
ECB programme purchasing investment-grade corporate bonds, restructured from 2021 to tilt toward lower-carbon issuers.

/green-securitisation.title: Green Securitisation
/green-securitisation.description: Analyses ESG securitisation structures including green ABS, green RMBS, green CLOs, and sustainability-linked securitisation tranches. Provides use-of-proceeds verification, green tranche isolation analytics, and regulatory alignment with EU Securitisation Regulation and EuGBS applicability to structured products.

calculationEngine.formula: GreenCF_tranche = Σ_i (w_i × GreenAsset_i × CollateralCF_i) / TranchePrincipal
standards: ICMA Green Bond Principles â€” Securitisation Supplement
calculationEngine.brief: Allocates collateral-level green asset cash flows to the designated green tranche using pool weight methodology, ensuring use-of-proceeds ring-fencing at the tranche level. The green asset proportion in the collateral pool must exceed the green tranche size to satisfy over-collateralisation of the green use-of-proceeds claim.

dataPoints.name: Green Pool Proportion (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of ABS collateral pool assets meeting green eligibility criteria; must exceed green tranche size to satisfy ICMA Principles.

dataPoints.name: Weighted Average Green Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite green quality score across pool assets weighted by balance; reflects alignment with taxonomy-based green asset criteria.

dataPoints.name: Green Tranche Greenium (bps)
dataPoints.interpretation: Yield spread differential between green and conventional tranches of same seniority within the structure; larger greenium reflects strong ESG investor demand.

dataPoints.name: EU Taxonomy Alignment (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of collateral assets meeting EU Taxonomy technical screening criteria for substantial contribution to climate mitigation.

userInteraction: Load the securitisation deal structure and collateral pool data, classifying each asset against green eligibility criteria.
Compute the green pool proportion and verify it exceeds the designated green tranche size to confirm use-of-proceeds integrity.
Analyse tranche-level greenium by comparing green tranche yields against equivalent conventional tranches.
Generate the EU Taxonomy alignment report for the collateral pool for investor disclosure.
references: ICMA Green Bond Principles â€” Securitisation Supplement (2022)
Climate Bonds Initiative â€” Securitisation Criteria (2022)
ESAs Joint Opinion on Sustainability Disclosure in the Securitisation Regulation (2021)

ABS collateralised by a pool of residential mortgage loans.
Structured credit vehicle backed by a diversified pool of leveraged loans with tranched credit risk.

/green-taxonomy-navigator.title: Green Taxonomy Navigator
/green-taxonomy-navigator.description: Compares multi-jurisdictional green taxonomy frameworks including EU Taxonomy, UK Green Taxonomy, Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) Taxonomy, and China's Green Bond Catalogue, mapping activity-level alignment and identifying mutual recognition opportunities. Enables cross-border issuers and investors to assess dual-taxonomy compliance.

calculationEngine.formula: CTAI_ij = |Activities_i ∩ Activities_j| / |Activities_i ∪ Activities_j|
calculationEngine.brief: Computes Jaccard similarity between activity-level eligible activity sets of two taxonomies, identifying where the same economic activity qualifies under multiple taxonomies vs where divergence exists. Divergence at the technical screening criteria level is further decomposed by threshold stringency differences.

dataPoints.name: EU-UK Taxonomy Alignment (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Activities eligible under both EU and UK taxonomies; 22% divergence relates to UK nuclear/gas transition provisions and threshold calibration differences.

dataPoints.name: EU-China Taxonomy Overlap (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Lower overlap reflects China's inclusion of clean coal and different threshold stringency for transport and buildings sectors.

dataPoints.name: MAS-EU Common Ground (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Singapore-Asia Taxonomy aligns with EU in climate mitigation but diverges in treatment of natural gas as transition activity.

dataPoints.name: Dual-Taxonomy Eligible Activities
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of EU Taxonomy activities that also qualify under at least one other major green taxonomy.

userInteraction: Select the target jurisdictions relevant to your issuance or investment universe.
Browse the activity library and filter by sector to compare technical screening criteria across selected taxonomies.
Use the gap analysis tab to identify activities that qualify in one taxonomy but not others, and the regulatory reason for divergence.
Export the cross-taxonomy compliance matrix for investor communication or prospectus disclosure.

UK Green Taxonomy â€” Interim Report (HM Treasury 2023)
IPSF â€” Common Ground Taxonomy Report (2022)

Singapore's central bank and financial regulator, developer of the Singapore-Asia Taxonomy.
Quantitative or qualitative thresholds defining whether an activity substantially contributes to an environmental objective.

/greenium-signal.title: Greenium Signal
/greenium-signal.description: Detects and quantifies the green bond premium (greenium) against conventional bond benchmarks using matched-pair analysis, cross-sectional regression, and interpolated yield curve methodologies. Provides real-time greenium signal tracking across currencies, sectors, and maturities to support green bond issuance timing and secondary market trading.

calculationEngine.formula: Greenium = ASW_conventional_matched - ASW_green
calculationEngine.brief: Computes greenium as the asset swap spread differential between a green bond and its closest conventional bond match from the same issuer, controlling for maturity, seniority, currency, and coupon type. Where no direct match exists, the conventional ASW is interpolated from the issuer's ASW curve fitted by cubic spline regression.

dataPoints.name: EUR IG Corporate Greenium (bps)
dataPoints.interpretation: Green bonds in EUR investment-grade corporate universe trade on average 4â€“8 bps richer than matched conventionals; greenium has widened since 2020 EU Taxonomy implementation.

dataPoints.name: Sovereign Greenium (bps)
dataPoints.interpretation: Government green bonds show smaller but persistent greenium vs conventional sovereign curves; German Bund twin methodology provides cleanest matched-pair measurement.

dataPoints.name: Greenium Volatility (σ, bps)
dataPoints.interpretation: Rolling standard deviation of greenium signal; elevated volatility may signal new green bond supply disruption or ESG mandate flows.

dataPoints.name: Greenium by Maturity (bps per year duration)
dataPoints.interpretation: Greenium tends to be larger at longer durations reflecting greater ESG investor demand for longer-dated green paper.

userInteraction: Select the currency, sector, and rating bucket for greenium analysis and set the reference date range.
Review the matched-pair table showing each green bond against its conventional comparator with computed greenium.
Analyse the greenium time series to identify cyclical patterns and issuance timing windows.
Use the cross-sectional regression tab to decompose greenium by maturity, sector, and ESG certification quality.
references: ECB Occasional Paper No. 285 â€” The Greenium Matters (2022)
Larcker & Watts (2020) â€” Where's the Greenium?
Bloomberg Intelligence â€” Green Bond Greenium Tracker (2024)

acronyms.def: Yield spread between a bond and the floating rate swap rate of equivalent maturity, used for cross-bond relative value comparison.
Credit rating category of BBB-/Baa3 or above, covering the bulk of the green bond market by volume.
Government agency responsible for sovereign bond issuance, including sovereign green bond programmes.

dataLineage.output: Matched-pair greenium dataset

dataLineage.flow: Compute ASW for green and conventional bonds, apply cubic spline interpolation
dataLineage.output: Greenium in basis points by bond

dataLineage.flow: Flag new green bond supply events, correlate with greenium compression
dataLineage.output: Greenium signal time series with supply events

/greenium-signal.valueSummary: Provides traders and portfolio managers with a real-time greenium signal to optimise green bond issuance timing, identify secondary market relative value opportunities, and demonstrate the ESG pricing premium achieved by labelled green bonds versus conventional peers.

/greenwashing-detector.title: Greenwashing Detector
/greenwashing-detector.description: Uses NLP and machine learning to detect greenwashing patterns in sustainability disclosures, corporate reports, and marketing materials by comparing qualitative claims against quantitative evidence, forward guidance against historical performance, and disclosure specificity against regulatory standards.

calculationEngine.formula: GWS = w_1 × ClaimEvidenceGap + w_2 × SelectivityIndex + w_3 × VaguenessScore + w_4 × RegulatoryGapScore
calculationEngine.brief: Constructs a composite greenwashing risk score by weighting the claim-evidence gap (unsubstantiated quantitative assertions), selectivity index (cherry-picked metrics omitting negative performance), vagueness score (proportion of non-specific sustainability language), and gap to minimum regulatory disclosure standard.

dataPoints.name: Greenwashing Risk Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite score; above 65 triggers enhanced due diligence; above 80 flags potential regulatory referral risk based on FCA/ESMA enforcement precedents.

dataPoints.name: Claim-Evidence Gap (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of sustainability claims lacking supporting quantitative evidence in the same or prior-year disclosure; sector average is approximately 25%.

dataPoints.name: Vagueness Index
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of sustainability-related sentences containing only vague qualitative language (e.g. 'committed to', 'working towards') without measurable targets.

dataPoints.name: Regulatory Gap Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Deviation of disclosure from minimum regulatory specificity requirements under FCA Sustainability Disclosure Requirements or ESMA greenwashing definition.

userInteraction: Upload the sustainability disclosure or marketing material for analysis and select the applicable regulatory jurisdiction.
Review the claim extraction tab to see each sustainability claim identified by the NLP model and its evidence match status.
Analyse the greenwashing risk score decomposition to identify which components (vagueness, selectivity, evidence gap) drive the overall risk.
Export the detailed finding report with regulatory reference for legal and compliance review.
references: FCA Sustainability Disclosure Requirements and Investment Labels (2024)
EU Commission â€” Study on Greenwashing (2021)

acronyms.def: FCA regulatory framework for sustainability-related product disclosures and labelling in UK financial markets.
Composite model output quantifying the probability of greenwashing based on disclosure characteristics.
AI technique for extracting structured information and measuring linguistic characteristics from unstructured text.

dataLineage.flow: NLP claim extraction, evidence matching, vagueness scoring
dataLineage.output: Per-claim greenwashing risk flags

dataLineage.flow: Compare disclosure specificity against regulatory standard
dataLineage.output: Regulatory gap score by requirement

dataLineage.flow: Compare forward claims against historical metrics
dataLineage.output: Claim-evidence gap quantification

/greenwashing-detector.valueSummary: Enables compliance teams, ESG analysts, and regulators to systematically screen sustainability disclosures for greenwashing indicators, prioritise enhanced due diligence on high-risk issuers, and prepare evidence packages for regulatory engagement under the FCA SDR and EU Green Claims Directive.

/greenwashing-exposure-monitor.title: Greenwashing Exposure Monitor
/greenwashing-exposure-monitor.description: Monitors portfolio exposure to greenwashing risk across holdings by aggregating issuer-level greenwashing scores, tracking regulatory enforcement actions, and quantifying financial contagion risk from greenwashing allegations. Integrates news analytics and regulatory filing analysis to provide early warning of greenwashing-related reputational and financial risk.

calculationEngine.formula: GW_VaR = Σ_i (w_i × P_enforcement_i × FinancialImpact_i)
calculationEngine.brief: Estimates the portfolio-level financial risk from greenwashing enforcement by weighting each holding's enforcement probability by its estimated financial impact (fine + reputational loss = average 3â€“8% market cap impact based on enforcement precedents). The result provides a probability-weighted expected loss from greenwashing exposure.

dataPoints.name: Portfolio Greenwashing VaR (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Probability-weighted expected portfolio loss from greenwashing enforcement actions; calibrated to average 5% market cap impact and enforcement probability by greenwashing risk tier.

dataPoints.name: High-Risk Issuer AUM (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio weight in issuers scoring above the high-risk threshold (GWS >65); concentration above 10% triggers portfolio review.

dataPoints.name: Regulatory Actions (YTD)
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of regulatory enforcement actions, investigations, or fines for greenwashing issued by major financial regulators year-to-date.

dataPoints.name: News Sentiment Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Sentiment of greenwashing-related news coverage across portfolio issuers; strongly negative scores precede enforcement actions in 68% of cases.

userInteraction: Review the portfolio exposure heatmap to identify concentration in high greenwashing risk issuers by sector and geography.
Monitor the regulatory enforcement tracker for new actions against portfolio holdings.
Analyse the news sentiment early warning dashboard for emerging greenwashing allegations.
Run the portfolio greenwashing VaR calculation and compare against internal ESG risk limits.
references: FCA â€” Sustainability Disclosure Requirements Policy Statement PS23/16 (2023)
SEC â€” ESG and Climate Disclosure Enforcement Actions (2024)
KPMG â€” Survey of Sustainability Reporting (2022)

acronyms.def: Statistical measure of the maximum expected loss over a defined period at a given confidence level.
US financial regulator that has pursued greenwashing enforcement actions against fund managers and corporates.
Composite model output quantifying the probability of greenwashing based on disclosure characteristics.

dataLineage.flow: Weight by portfolio allocation, compute portfolio-level exposure distribution
dataLineage.output: Portfolio greenwashing risk heatmap

dataLineage.flow: Match enforcement actions to portfolio ISINs, compute financial impact
dataLineage.output: Enforcement contagion risk by holding

dataLineage.source: News and social media sentiment analytics
dataLineage.flow: Score greenwashing sentiment by issuer, flag trending negative sentiment
dataLineage.output: Early warning signals by issuer

/greenwashing-exposure-monitor.valueSummary: Protects investment portfolios from greenwashing-related regulatory and reputational risk by providing continuous monitoring of issuer greenwashing exposure, regulatory enforcement tracking, and portfolio-level VaR quantification enabling proactive divestment or engagement decisions.

/gri-alignment.title: GRI Alignment Checker
/gri-alignment.description: Automates GRI Standards disclosure compliance checking across GRI 1 (Foundation), GRI 2 (General Disclosures), GRI 3 (Material Topics), and topic-specific standards (GRI 300/400 series). Identifies disclosure gaps, checks required content elements, and maps GRI disclosures to equivalent ISSB, ESRS, and TCFD requirements for dual-reporting efficiency.

calculationEngine.formula: DCS = Σ_k (Complete_k × w_k) / Σ_k w_k × 100
calculationEngine.brief: Assesses completeness of each GRI disclosure by checking mandatory content elements (governance, management approach, metrics, and targets), weighting by materiality tier. A DCS above 85% indicates GRI 'with reference' level compliance; 100% across all material topic standards is required for 'in accordance' with GRI Standards.

dataPoints.name: GRI In-Accordance Compliance (%)
