references: ISAE 3000 Revised – Assurance Engagements Other than Audits

acronyms.def: IAASB standards governing non-financial assurance engagements including sustainability reporting verification
US law mandating internal controls over financial reporting, extended to ESG disclosures by SEC rules

dataLineage.flow: Route to authority matrix reviewers; capture approval/rejection actions with timestamps
dataLineage.output: Immutable approval audit trail per disclosure item

dataLineage.flow: Enforce sign-off sequence; trigger SLA monitoring; auto-escalate on breach
dataLineage.output: SLA compliance reports and pending approval dashboards

/approval-workflows.valueSummary: Structured approval workflows prevent premature disclosure publication and create the documented sign-off chain required for ISAE 3000 limited assurance. By enforcing authority matrices and capturing immutable audit trails, the engine reduces regulatory risk from unauthorised disclosures and supports internal audit reviews of ESG data governance.

/article6-markets.title: Article 6 Markets
/article6-markets.description: Paris Agreement Article 6.2 bilateral trading and Article 6.4 supervisory mechanism analytics, covering ITMO issuance, corresponding adjustment accounting, host country authorisation tracking, and carbon market additionality assessment. Monitors NDC contribution accounting and safeguard compliance for traded mitigation outcomes.

calculationEngine.formula: Net_NDC_contribution = ITMOs_issued – ITMOs_cancelled; Corresponding_adjustment = ITMOs_transferred × GHG_metric_tonne
calculationEngine.brief: Each ITMO transfer requires the host country to apply a corresponding adjustment: removing transferred units from its NDC inventory and having the acquiring country add them. Double-entry accounting ensures no double-claiming. Article 6.4 Supervisory Body validates additionality and baselines before unit issuance.

dataPoints.name: ITMOs Tracked
dataPoints.interpretation: International Transferable Mitigation Outcomes registered and authorised under bilateral agreements

dataPoints.name: Corresponding Adjustment Applied
dataPoints.interpretation: Whether host country has removed transferred units from its national inventory

dataPoints.name: Additionality Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Supervisory body additionality rating for 6.4 mechanism project

userInteraction: ITMOs Registry shows all tracked bilateral agreements and unit balances
Corresponding Adjustments tab verifies double-entry accounting per trade
Host Country Authorisation tab tracks formal government approval status
NDC Contribution tab shows net adjustment to host and acquiring country accounts
Additionality Analysis tab reviews 6.4 project baseline and additionality documentation
Safeguards Compliance tab checks environmental and social safeguard adherence
references: Verra VCS v4 Jurisdictional Nested REDD+

acronyms.def: Unit of emission reduction or removal transferred between countries under Paris Agreement Article 6.2
Each country's self-set emission reduction commitment under the Paris Agreement

dataLineage.flow: Ingest ITMO issuance, transfer, and cancellation records; validate corresponding adjustments
dataLineage.output: Reconciled ITMO balance sheet with double-claiming risk flags

dataLineage.flow: Verify corresponding adjustment entries match transferred volumes
dataLineage.output: Corresponding adjustment compliance status per bilateral agreement

/article6-markets.valueSummary: Article 6 markets represent the next frontier of carbon price discovery and cross-border climate finance, with billions of dollars of ITMOs expected to trade annually. Rigorous corresponding adjustment accounting is non-negotiable to prevent double-claiming between host and acquiring countries, and robust additionality verification protects carbon credit buyers from non-additional supply.

/asset-valuation-engine.title: Asset Valuation Engine
/asset-valuation-engine.description: Climate-adjusted DCF and NAV engine incorporating stranded asset write-downs, transition risk premiums in WACC, and physical risk-adjusted terminal values. Supports 5 NGFS scenarios across 8 asset classes and generates regulatory-grade IFRS 13 fair value disclosures. Integrates carbon price trajectories, energy transition capex, and stranded asset half-life models.

calculationEngine.methodology: Climate-adjusted DCF with transition risk WACC
calculationEngine.formula: NAV_climate = Σ_t[FCF_t / (1+WACC_adj)^t] – StrandedWriteDown; WACC_adj = WACC_base + β_transition × RP_carbon
calculationEngine.brief: Transition risk premium (RP_carbon) is calibrated from market-implied carbon beta (β_transition) using cross-sectional regression of credit spreads on carbon intensity. Physical risk adjusts terminal value via scenario-specific damage function. Stranded write-down follows exponential decay with sector half-life.

dataPoints.name: Climate-Adjusted NAV
dataPoints.interpretation: Net asset value after applying transition risk premium and stranded write-downs

dataPoints.name: Transition Risk Premium
dataPoints.interpretation: Additional WACC component reflecting carbon price and policy risk exposure

dataPoints.name: Physical Risk Terminal Value Haircut
dataPoints.interpretation: Reduction in terminal value due to chronic and acute physical climate risk

userInteraction: Select asset and NGFS scenario from configuration panel
DCF Engine tab shows base vs climate-adjusted cash flow waterfall
WACC Decomposition tab breaks down transition risk premium components
Stranded Asset tab models write-down schedule with half-life decay
Physical Risk tab adjusts terminal value for flood, heat, and storm damage
IFRS 13 Export tab generates fair value hierarchy disclosures

acronyms.def: Total asset value minus liabilities; used for real assets, fund, and infrastructure valuation
Blended cost of debt and equity used as DCF discount rate, adjusted here for climate risk premiums

dataLineage.source: NGFS carbon price and macro scenario data
dataLineage.flow: Overlay carbon cost trajectories on company FCF projections; adjust WACC by carbon beta
dataLineage.output: Climate-adjusted NAV per asset under 5 scenarios with sensitivity ranges

dataLineage.flow: Apply damage functions to terminal value by asset location and hazard type
dataLineage.output: Physical risk terminal value haircuts and scenario-weighted expected NAV

/asset-valuation-engine.valueSummary: Climate-adjusted valuation is increasingly required for IFRS 13 fair value disclosures as regulators expect material climate assumptions to be incorporated into goodwill impairment tests and investment property valuations. The engine provides auditable scenario-weighted NAVs that withstand scrutiny from external auditors and climate disclosure assurance providers.

/audit-trail.title: Audit Trail Viewer
/audit-trail.description: Immutable platform audit log capturing all data changes, user actions, report generations, and access events. Provides full ISAE 3000 and SOX-compliant evidence packs with tamper-evident hash chains, granular search by user, action type, and timestamp. Supports external auditor access for sustainability assurance engagements.

calculationEngine.formula: Hash_n = SHA256(event_n || Hash_{n-1}); Integrity_check = (Computed_chain == Stored_chain)
calculationEngine.brief: Each audit event is appended to a hash chain: the SHA-256 hash of each record includes the previous record's hash, making retrospective modification detectable. Integrity checks run hourly; any chain break triggers an immediate P0 alert. Event schema captures user, action, target entity, old value, new value, and IP.

dataPoints.name: Audit Events (30d)
dataPoints.interpretation: Total platform events captured in the last 30 days across all users and modules

dataPoints.name: Chain Integrity
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of audit log records passing hash chain validation; <100% is a critical alert

dataPoints.name: ISAE 3000 Evidence Pack
dataPoints.interpretation: Structured evidence pack exported for external assurance engagements with full event detail

userInteraction: Search audit log by user, date range, action type, or target entity
Event Detail tab shows full before/after values for any data change
Hash Chain Integrity tab shows last verification status and timestamps
User Activity tab ranks users by action volume for anomaly detection
ISAE 3000 Export generates formatted evidence pack for external auditors
Retention Policy tab configures log archival periods per data classification

acronyms.def: IAASB standards used for limited and reasonable assurance of non-financial information
Cryptographic hash function used to create tamper-evident audit log chains

dataLineage.flow: Append each event to hash chain with SHA-256; store in immutable log partition
dataLineage.output: Tamper-evident audit log with hourly chain integrity verification

dataLineage.flow: Filter log by scope period and entity; format per assurance provider template
dataLineage.output: Structured evidence pack with event details, hash proofs, and user activity summary

/audit-trail.valueSummary: An immutable, hash-chained audit trail is the foundational control that enables external assurance providers to give limited or reasonable assurance on ESG disclosures. Without it, auditors cannot verify that data was not manipulated between measurement and reporting, making ISAE 3000 engagement scope and testing prohibitively expensive.

/aviation-corsia.title: Aviation CORSIA
/aviation-corsia.description: ICAO CORSIA compliance analytics covering emission unit requirement calculation, eligible emission unit (EEU) procurement strategy, sector growth factor monitoring, and airline-level offsetting obligation tracking. Models Phase 1 (2024–2026), Phase 2 (2027–2035) obligations and supports SAF credit integration as a CORSIA compliance pathway.

calculationEngine.formula: Offset_req = (Airline_emissions_yr – Airline_emissions_baseline) × Sector_growth_factor; Baseline = avg(2019,2020) emissions
calculationEngine.brief: CORSIA baseline is the average of 2019 and 2020 industry emissions. Airlines offset the fraction of growth above baseline equal to their share of sector growth. Sector growth factor (SGF) is computed annually from total sector emissions vs baseline. SAF blending reduces offset requirement proportionally via CORSIA SAF Life Cycle Assessment credits.

dataPoints.name: CORSIA Baseline
dataPoints.interpretation: Industry emission baseline against which growth above which must be offset

dataPoints.name: Sector Growth Factor
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of total sector emission growth requiring offsetting; <1 in 2020/2021

dataPoints.name: EEU Price
dataPoints.interpretation: Price of CORSIA-eligible emission units from approved VCS/Gold Standard/REDD+ programmes

userInteraction: Airline Portfolio tab shows each carrier's annual emissions and baseline comparison
Offset Obligation tab calculates EEU requirement under 3 growth scenarios
SAF Integration tab models offset reduction from SAF blending credits
EEU Procurement Strategy tab optimises unit mix by price and eligibility
Phase Timeline shows Phase 1 vs Phase 2 obligation profile to 2035
Compliance Export generates ICAO CORSIA MRV submission data
references: ICAO CORSIA Standards and Recommended Practices (SARPs)
ICAO Doc 9501 Environmental Protection Vol IV
IATA SAF Registry and Tracking System

acronyms.full: Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation
acronyms.def: ICAO market-based measure for international aviation emissions targeting carbon-neutral growth from 2020
Low-carbon alternative to conventional jet fuel; CORSIA-eligible SAF reduces offsetting obligation

dataLineage.source: ICAO CORSIA MRV system (airline emission reports)
dataLineage.flow: Calculate sector growth factor and per-airline offsetting obligation
dataLineage.output: EEU requirement schedule per airline and phase

dataLineage.source: Voluntary carbon market EEU price feeds
dataLineage.flow: Optimise EEU procurement mix for cost minimisation subject to CORSIA eligibility
dataLineage.output: Procurement strategy and total compliance cost estimate

/aviation-corsia.valueSummary: CORSIA is projected to generate demand for 1.5–1.8 billion tonnes of carbon offsets between 2024 and 2035, making it one of the largest compliance-driven carbon market mechanisms. Airlines with proactive SAF blending and EEU procurement strategies can reduce compliance costs materially versus spot-market procurement at the time of obligation.

/avoided-emissions-portfolio.title: Avoided Emissions Portfolio
/avoided-emissions-portfolio.description: Scope 4 avoided emissions tracking engine following GHG Protocol Land Sector and Removal Guidance and WBCSD Avoided Emissions Framework. Quantifies emissions displaced by portfolio clean energy, efficient transport, and land-restoration investments against a counterfactual baseline. Supports green bond and sustainability-linked instrument impact reporting.

calculationEngine.formula: Avoided_emissions = Baseline_scenario_emissions – Project_scenario_emissions; Baseline = BAU counterfactual without investment
calculationEngine.brief: Avoided emissions are not included in Scope 1/2/3 inventory but quantify displacement impact. Baseline is the most credible BAU counterfactual (e.g., grid average emission factor vs solar project). Additionality test ensures project would not have happened without investment. Attribution factor = investment / total project capex.

dataPoints.name: Portfolio Avoided Emissions
dataPoints.interpretation: Total annual emissions displaced by portfolio investments vs BAU counterfactual

dataPoints.name: Attribution Factor
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of avoided emissions attributed to investor based on ownership/financing share

dataPoints.name: Additionality Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Likelihood that avoided emissions would not have occurred without the investment

userInteraction: Upload investment portfolio with clean energy / efficiency / land-use holdings
Baseline Definition tab selects counterfactual scenario per investment type
Avoided Emissions Calculation applies attribution factors and aggregates portfolio total
Additionality Assessment scores each project on financial, regulatory, and common practice tests
Impact Report generates green bond use-of-proceeds avoided emissions disclosure
Comparison tab benchmarks avoided emissions against Scope 1+2+3 footprint
references: GHG Protocol Scope 4 and Land Sector Guidance (Draft)
EU Green Bond Standard Delegated Act

acronyms.def: Counterfactual scenario representing emissions trajectory in the absence of the investment or policy

acronyms.full: World Business Council for Sustainable Development
acronyms.def: CEO-led organisation developing sustainability frameworks including the Avoided Emissions guidance

dataLineage.flow: Select BAU counterfactual; apply emission factors; compute avoided emissions with attribution
dataLineage.output: Per-investment avoided emissions attributed to portfolio with additionality flags

dataLineage.source: Grid emission factor databases (IEA, EPA eGRID)
dataLineage.flow: Match project location to grid emission factor for counterfactual baseline
dataLineage.output: Counterfactual emission baselines for clean energy and efficiency investments

/avoided-emissions-portfolio.valueSummary: Scope 4 avoided emissions provide institutional investors with a positive impact metric that complements the carbon footprint narrative. For green bond issuers, credible avoided emissions calculations backed by GHG Protocol methodology are increasingly required by investors as part of use-of-proceeds substantiation and annual impact reporting.

/battery-ev-analytics.title: Battery & EV Analytics
/battery-ev-analytics.description: Lifecycle emissions, critical mineral supply risk, and battery GHG intensity analytics for EV and energy storage investments. Covers LCA from raw material extraction through end-of-life recycling, cobalt/lithium supply chain resilience scoring, and charging infrastructure carbon intensity. Supports EU Battery Regulation carbon footprint declaration requirements.

calculationEngine.formula: Battery_LCA_gCO2e_per_km = (Production_emissions + Use_phase_emissions + EOL_emissions) / Lifetime_km; Production_emissions = Material_kg × EF_material
calculationEngine.brief: Battery LCA allocates production emissions (mining, processing, cell manufacturing) to kWh capacity, then distributes over vehicle lifetime km driven. Use phase intensity depends on grid emission factor at charging location. End-of-life credit applies recycling recovery rate for cobalt, lithium, nickel.

dataPoints.name: Battery Carbon Intensity
dataPoints.interpretation: Lifecycle GHG intensity per km driven; target <50gCO₂e/km for low-carbon EV

dataPoints.name: Cobalt Supply Risk Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Supply concentration and geopolitical risk for cobalt sourcing from DRC

dataPoints.name: Charging Carbon Intensity
dataPoints.interpretation: Grid emission factor at dominant charging location; determines use-phase emissions

userInteraction: Select EV model or battery type to load LCA parameters
Production Emissions tab breaks down mining, processing, and manufacturing phases
Critical Minerals tab shows supply chain concentration (HHI) and ESG risk per mineral
Use Phase tab models charging carbon intensity by grid region and time-of-day
EU Battery Regulation tab generates carbon footprint declaration per battery model
Portfolio Impact tab aggregates EV fleet LCA across all holdings
references: OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Mineral Supply Chains

acronyms.def: ISO 14040/44 methodology quantifying environmental impacts across a product's full lifecycle from cradle to grave
Market concentration measure used to assess supply chain concentration risk for critical minerals

dataLineage.source: Battery chemistry and material intensity data
dataLineage.flow: Apply emission factors per kg of material; sum production phase LCA
dataLineage.output: Battery production carbon intensity by chemistry type (NMC, LFP, NCA)

dataLineage.flow: Map charging location to grid factor; compute use-phase emissions over lifetime km
dataLineage.output: Full lifecycle GHG profile and EU Battery Regulation carbon footprint declaration

/battery-ev-analytics.valueSummary: Battery LCA and critical mineral risk are central to institutional due diligence on EV and energy storage investments. The EU Battery Regulation mandates carbon footprint declarations for batteries above 2kWh by 2025, making accurate LCA methodology a compliance necessity rather than optional disclosure for manufacturers and investors alike.

/biodiversity-credits.title: Biodiversity Credit Market
/biodiversity-credits.description: Voluntary biodiversity credit pricing, trading analytics, and ecosystem unit metrics covering habitat banking, species biodiversity units (BDUs), and nature-based solution credits. Integrates TNFD LEAP locate-evaluate outputs, IUCN habitat condition scoring, and UK BNG (Biodiversity Net Gain) metric. Tracks market liquidity, price discovery, and jurisdictional legal frameworks.

calculationEngine.formula: BDU = Habitat_area × Condition_score × Distinctiveness_score × Connectivity_multiplier; BNG_net_gain = Post_development_BDU – Pre_development_BDU
calculationEngine.brief: Biodiversity units follow the UK BNG statutory metric which scores habitat parcels on condition (0–1), distinctiveness (0–1), and connectivity. Mandatory 10% BNG requires post-development BDU > pre-development BDU by at least 10%. Credit price is set by supply/demand in registered habitat bank market.

dataPoints.name: Biodiversity Unit (BDU)
dataPoints.interpretation: Standardised measure of biodiversity value per hectare of habitat

dataPoints.name: BNG Credit Price
dataPoints.interpretation: Market price per biodiversity unit in registered UK statutory BNG market

dataPoints.name: Net Gain Achievement
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage biodiversity net gain delivered by development project vs pre-development baseline

userInteraction: Habitat Library shows BDU values for 100+ UK and global habitat types
Project Assessment tab calculates pre- and post-development BDU for a site
BNG Net Gain tab verifies mandatory 10% gain requirement

Portfolio Analytics aggregates biodiversity credit holdings and BDU balance
TNFD D4 Export maps credits to nature-related dependency disclosures
references: UK Environment Act 2021 Biodiversity Net Gain

acronyms.def: UK mandatory requirement under Environment Act 2021 for development to deliver ≥10% BDU gain over pre-development baseline
Standardised unit of habitat value from the UK statutory BNG metric, combining area, condition, distinctiveness, and connectivity

dataLineage.output: Market price analytics and portfolio BDU holding valuation

/biodiversity-credits.valueSummary: Biodiversity credits are transitioning from voluntary to mandatory mechanisms in multiple jurisdictions. UK mandatory BNG has created the first statutory biodiversity credit market; similar frameworks are emerging in Australia (ACCUs), EU (NBSAP obligations), and Singapore. Investors in development assets face growing BDU procurement obligations that directly affect project economics.

/blended-finance.title: Blended Finance Structuring
/blended-finance.description: MDB and DFI risk-layering analytics for blended finance structures covering first-loss tranche sizing, concessional capital IRR subsidy calculations, and commercial co-investor returns across 5 deal archetypes. Applies OECD Blended Finance Principles and DFI Working Group models to structure climate and development finance transactions.

calculationEngine.formula: Commercial_IRR = (Project_FCF – Concessional_coupon × Conc_size) / Commercial_investment; FirstLoss_required = Investment × (1 – Commercial_hurdle_IRR / Project_IRR)
calculationEngine.brief: First-loss size is calibrated to lift commercial tranche IRR to institutional hurdle rate (typically 8–12%). Cascade approach tests whether commercial-only financing is viable before applying concessional instruments. Subsidy efficiency metric = development impact / concessional capital deployed.

dataPoints.name: First-Loss Tranche Size
dataPoints.interpretation: Concessional capital required to achieve commercial hurdle rate for co-investors

dataPoints.name: Commercial Investor IRR
dataPoints.interpretation: Return achieved by commercial co-investors after absorbing first-loss support

dataPoints.name: Subsidy Efficiency
dataPoints.interpretation: Development outcomes (jobs, clean energy capacity) per million dollars of concessional capital

userInteraction: Select deal archetype from 5 templates (climate, health, agri, infrastructure, SME)
Capital Stack tab configures MDB, DFI, concessional, and commercial tranche sizes
IRR Waterfall shows returns per tranche under base and stress scenarios
Cascade Check tests commercial-only viability before applying concessionality
Impact Analytics quantifies development outcomes per $ deployed
Term Sheet Export generates OECD-aligned blended finance summary
references: DFI Working Group on Blended Concessional Finance 2017

acronyms.def: Publicly backed institution providing concessional and risk-mitigation instruments to crowd in private capital
International financial institution providing development loans; includes World Bank, ADB, EBRD, IFC

dataLineage.flow: Apply first-loss model to determine concessional tranche size; compute per-tranche IRR
dataLineage.output: Capital stack configuration with IRR waterfall and subsidy efficiency metrics

dataLineage.flow: Benchmark structure against comparable historical blended finance transactions
dataLineage.output: Peer structure comparison and market-standard concessional ratio benchmarks

/blended-finance.valueSummary: Blended finance is the primary mechanism for mobilising private capital into climate-vulnerable and emerging market projects that cannot meet commercial return thresholds unaided. Rigorous first-loss sizing and cascade discipline ensure that concessional capital is used only where necessary, maximising its leverage effect and preserving DFI financial sustainability.

/blockchain-carbon-registry.title: Blockchain Carbon Registry
/blockchain-carbon-registry.description: Tokenised carbon credit issuance, transfer, and retirement platform using smart contract verification for provenance and chain-of-custody. Supports ERC-1155 and Toucan/Base Carbon Tonne standards. Enables real-time registry balances, on-chain retirement certificates, and double-spend prevention across Verra, Gold Standard, and proprietary offsets.

calculationEngine.formula: Credit_balance = Minted – Retired – Transferred; Retirement_hash = SHA256(credit_id || beneficiary || date || amount)
calculationEngine.brief: Each carbon credit is tokenised as an NFT/semi-fungible token with on-chain metadata linking to underlying Verra/GS serial numbers. Smart contract enforces retirement finality: retired tokens are burned and a cryptographic retirement certificate is emitted. Double-spend prevention is guaranteed by token uniqueness on-chain.

dataPoints.name: Credits On-Chain
dataPoints.interpretation: Total live (unretired) carbon credits held in on-chain registry

dataPoints.name: Retirement Certificates
dataPoints.interpretation: Immutable on-chain retirement events with beneficiary, date, and credit metadata

dataPoints.name: Provenance Verification
dataPoints.interpretation: Verification that token metadata matches underlying registry serial number and project data

Portfolio Wallet shows live credit balance by project type and vintage

Retirement tab initiates on-chain burn with beneficiary details and certificate generation

Double-Spend Audit confirms no credit appears in two portfolios simultaneously
references: Verra Registry Rules and Program Guide
Toucan Protocol Base Carbon Tonne (BCT) Standard

acronyms.def: Unique blockchain token used to represent individual or batched carbon credits with immutable provenance metadata
Toucan Protocol standardised on-chain carbon credit pool combining eligible Verra VCU tokens

dataLineage.output: Tokenised carbon credit portfolio with provenance-verified metadata

dataLineage.flow: Capture burn transactions; generate SHA-256 retirement certificate hash
dataLineage.output: Immutable retirement certificate with on-chain timestamp and beneficiary record

/blockchain-carbon-registry.valueSummary: Blockchain tokenisation addresses the longstanding carbon market problem of double-counting by making credit ownership and retirement provably unique and publicly verifiable. On-chain retirement certificates reduce audit costs for corporate net-zero claims and provide the immutable chain-of-custody documentation required by emerging voluntary carbon market integrity standards.

/board-composition.title: Board Composition Analytics
/board-composition.description: Board skills matrix, tenure analysis, independence assessment, and ISS/Glass Lewis proxy advisory criteria scoring for portfolio company boards. Covers lead independent director requirements, committee independence, overboarding screening, and say-on-climate readiness. Benchmarks board composition against ISS QuickScore and MSCI IVA governance ratings.

calculationEngine.formula: Board_score = 0.30×Independence + 0.25×Skills + 0.25×Diversity + 0.20×Tenure; Independence = Ind_directors / Total_directors × 100
calculationEngine.brief: Independence metric counts directors meeting ISS independence criteria (no employment, material relationship, or family links in last 5 years). Skills matrix scores coverage of 12 competencies (climate, digital, finance, risk, sustainability, operations, legal, international, sector, HR, M&A, stakeholder). Overboarding flags directors with >5 public company seats.

dataPoints.name: Board Independence
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of directors meeting ISS independence criteria; <50% triggers ISS concern

dataPoints.name: Avg Board Tenure
dataPoints.interpretation: Average tenure; >9 years raises ISS independence questions for non-executive directors

dataPoints.name: Skills Matrix Coverage
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of 12 key competencies covered by at least one board member

userInteraction: Board Composition tab shows director-level independence, tenure, and skills tagging
ISS QuickScore tab replicates QS 1–10 governance risk scoring
Committee Independence tab checks audit, remuneration, and nominations committee
Overboarding Screener flags directors with excessive public company commitments
Say-on-Climate Readiness assesses TCFD alignment of climate disclosure for AGM
Peer Benchmark compares board metrics against sector governance standards

acronyms.def: Proxy advisory firm whose QuickScore and voting recommendations influence institutional shareholder votes
Board member without operational management role; central to board independence assessments

dataLineage.source: Company proxy statements and annual reports

dataLineage.output: Per-director independence, tenure, and skills metadata

dataLineage.output: QS scores, overboarding flags, and committee independence assessments

/board-composition.valueSummary: Board composition quality is a leading indicator of governance failures that destroy shareholder value: independence deficits correlate with weak oversight of management, and skills gaps in climate and risk increase ESG exposure. Proactive monitoring of ISS/Glass Lewis criteria enables investors to engage before adverse AGM outcomes materialise.

/board-diversity.title: Board Diversity Metrics
/board-diversity.description: Gender, ethnic, skills, and age diversity analytics for board and executive committee covering 30% Club targets, Hampton-Alexander and Parker Review benchmarks, and EU Gender Balance Directive compliance. Tracks intersectional diversity using a composite diversity index and benchmarks against FTSE All-World peer groups.

calculationEngine.formula: DiversityIndex = 1 – Σ_i(p_i²); Gender_parity_gap = |Female_pct – 0.40|; Parker_score = Ethnic_minority_directors / Total × 100
standards: EU Gender Balance on Corporate Boards Directive
calculationEngine.brief: Diversity index uses Herfindahl-Simpson complement (1 – Σp²) applied separately to gender, ethnicity, age cohort, and nationality. Perfect diversity scores 1.0; total uniformity scores 0. Gender parity gap measures distance from 40% female target under EU Directive.

dataPoints.name: Female Board Representation
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of female directors; EU Directive mandates ≥40% by 2026 for large companies

dataPoints.name: Ethnic Minority Representation
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of ethnic minority directors; Parker Review target is ≥10% for FTSE 250

dataPoints.name: Diversity Index (composite)
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite Herfindahl-Simpson diversity score; higher = more diverse across all dimensions

Gender Analytics tab tracks female representation at board and ExCo levels
Ethnicity Analytics tab shows Parker Review compliance by company and sector
EU Directive Compliance tab flags companies below 40% female threshold
Peer Benchmark compares diversity scores against FTSE index and sector averages
Engagement Priority tab identifies companies with lowest scores for stewardship
references: EU Gender Balance on Corporate Boards Directive (EU) 2022/2381

acronyms.def: Senior management body below the board; diversity targets increasingly include ExCo alongside board
Here used as Herfindahl-Simpson index to measure diversity; complement (1–HHI) measures diversity

dataLineage.output: Per-company diversity metrics with EU Directive and Parker Review compliance flags

dataLineage.output: Diversity ranking with peer percentile positioning and stewardship priority flags

/board-diversity.valueSummary: Board diversity metrics are rapidly moving from voluntary best practice to mandatory disclosure and minimum threshold requirements under the EU Gender Balance Directive and UK Listing Rules. Investors monitoring Hampton-Alexander, Parker Review, and EU Directive compliance simultaneously can identify engagement-priority companies before legal non-compliance triggers regulatory sanctions.

/brsr-bridge.title: BRSR Bridge
/brsr-bridge.description: India SEBI Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting compliance mapping tool covering all 9 BRSR principles, 98 core and leadership indicators, and BRSR Core assurance requirements. Maps BRSR disclosures to GRI, TCFD, and UN SDGs for integrated framework alignment. Tracks year-over-year disclosure progress and BRSR Core KPI trajectories.

calculationEngine.formula: BRSR_score = (Core_disclosed + Leadership_disclosed) / (Core_total + Leadership_total) × 100; Core_completeness = Core_disclosed / 48 × 100
calculationEngine.brief: BRSR has 48 Core indicators mandatory for top 150 listed companies and 50 Leadership indicators for voluntary adoption. BRSR Core (2023) adds 9 Key Performance Indicators requiring limited assurance from FY2024. Mapping matrix cross-references BRSR indicators against GRI topics, TCFD recommendations, and SDG targets.

dataPoints.name: BRSR Core Completeness
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of 9 BRSR Core KPIs fully disclosed with required assurance

dataPoints.name: Principle Coverage
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of 9 BRSR principles with at least one indicator disclosed

dataPoints.name: GRI Cross-reference Count
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of BRSR indicators with GRI, TCFD, or SDG alignment tags applied

/brsr-bridge.valueSummary: BRSR is India's first mandatory, structured ESG reporting framework and positions Indian companies within the global sustainability disclosure ecosystem. The BRSR Core with limited assurance requirements from FY2024 elevates reporting quality materially; the platform bridge to GRI and TCFD enables Indian issuers to satisfy domestic SEBI requirements while simultaneously meeting international investor expectations.

/building-energy-performance.title: Building Energy Performance
/building-energy-performance.description: EPC rating analytics, CRREM (Carbon Risk Real Estate Monitor) pathway alignment, and building-level GHG intensity tracking for commercial and residential real estate portfolios. Identifies stranding risk by comparing current energy performance against CRREM 1.5°C and 2°C pathways by property type and country. Supports EU Taxonomy Article 7 technical screening and TCFD physical risk integration.

calculationEngine.formula: Stranding_year = first t where intensity(t) > CRREM_pathway(t); Intensity = Annual_emissions_kgCO2 / GFA_m²; EPC_score = 0–100 (A–G)
calculationEngine.brief: CRREM pathways define maximum allowable carbon intensity (kgCO₂e/m²/yr) per property type and country to remain on 1.5°C or 2°C trajectory. Stranding year is the first year asset intensity exceeds the pathway. Energy intensity (kWh/m²) feeds into EPBD Nearly Zero Energy Building thresholds.

dataPoints.name: Carbon Intensity
dataPoints.interpretation: Building GHG intensity; CRREM 1.5°C UK office pathway is ~15 kgCO₂e/m²/yr by 2030

dataPoints.name: CRREM Pathway Gap
dataPoints.interpretation: Excess carbon intensity above the 1.5°C/2°C CRREM benchmark for property type

dataPoints.name: EPC Rating
dataPoints.interpretation: Energy Performance Certificate band; EU minimum class E by 2027, D by 2030 under EPBD recast

EU Energy Performance of Buildings Directive Recast 2023

EU directive mandating minimum energy performance standards and EPC ratings for buildings

dataLineage.output: CRREM stranding analysis and EU Taxonomy Article 7 compliance assessment

/building-energy-performance.valueSummary: The EPBD recast and EU Taxonomy Article 7 are accelerating regulatory demand for decarbonised buildings, with minimum EPC standards tightening to class D by 2030 for all commercial real estate. CRREM stranding analysis quantifies the financial risk embedded in underperforming properties and provides the CapEx roadmap needed for green loan and green bond refinancing eligibility.

/calculation-engine-monitor.title: Calculation Engine Monitor
/calculation-engine-monitor.description: Real-time monitoring dashboard for all platform calculation pipelines covering GHG inventory, temperature scoring, PCAF attribution, CRREM pathways, and stress testing engines. Tracks pipeline run status, calculation accuracy KPIs, data freshness, and error rates. Provides operators with a centralised view of engine health and performance SLAs.

calculationEngine.formula: Engine_health = 0.40×Success_rate + 0.30×Freshness_score + 0.30×Accuracy_score; Freshness_score = 1 – (Age_hrs / SLA_hrs)
calculationEngine.brief: Each calculation engine reports success rate (completed runs / scheduled runs), data freshness (hours since last successful run vs SLA threshold), and accuracy score (spot-check results against reference dataset). Composite health score below 80 triggers orange alert; below 60 triggers red.

dataPoints.name: Engine Count Monitored
dataPoints.interpretation: Total calculation engines tracked: GHG, PCAF, CRREM, ITR, stress test, and 13 others

dataPoints.name: Overall Pipeline Health
dataPoints.interpretation: Aggregate health score; green ≥80, amber 60–80, red <60

dataPoints.name: SLA Breach Rate (24h)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of scheduled engine runs missing their SLA in the last 24 hours

userInteraction: Dashboard shows all 18 engines with live health score and RAG status
Engine Detail tab drills into run history, success rate, and error log
Data Freshness tab shows last-run timestamp vs SLA threshold per engine
Accuracy KPIs tab tracks spot-check deltas against reference benchmarks
Alert Configuration sets thresholds and routes notifications to ops team
SLA Report generates monthly engine performance summary

acronyms.def: Performance commitment specifying acceptable latency, error rate, and freshness thresholds for each calculation engine
Three-tier status classification system; red = critical, amber = degraded, green = healthy

dataLineage.source: Calculation engine run logs and result outputs
dataLineage.flow: Aggregate success rates, latency, and freshness; compute composite health score
dataLineage.output: Engine health dashboard with RAG status, SLA breach flags, and error summaries

dataLineage.flow: Run spot-check calculations; compare against engine output; compute accuracy delta
dataLineage.output: Accuracy score per engine with anomaly alerts for significant deviations

/calculation-engine-monitor.valueSummary: Calculation engine monitoring prevents silent failures where pipeline crashes or stale data cause incorrect values to appear in disclosures without triggering visible errors. Centralised health scoring with SLA tracking gives data operations teams the visibility to prioritise remediation, maintain disclosure data quality, and evidence governance controls to assurance providers.

/carbon-accounting-ai.title: Carbon Accounting AI
/carbon-accounting-ai.description: LLM-powered extraction of emissions data from invoices, utility bills, supplier documents, and product datasheets using structured information extraction and confidence scoring. Automates Scope 1, 2, and 3 Category 1 data collection, reducing manual GHG inventory effort by 60–80%. Integrates extracted data directly into the platform GHG calculation engine with data quality flags.

calculationEngine.methodology: LLM structured extraction with confidence scoring
calculationEngine.formula: Extraction_confidence = P(field_correct | extracted_value, source_type); GHG_contribution = Activity_data × EF_IPCC_AR6
calculationEngine.brief: A fine-tuned LLM extracts key fields (energy quantity, unit, fuel type, period, supplier) from unstructured documents. Confidence scoring uses a calibrated uncertainty model trained on human-verified extraction pairs. Low-confidence extractions are routed to human review queue. Activity data is matched to IPCC AR6 emission factors for GHG calculation.

dataPoints.name: Extraction Accuracy
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of LLM-extracted fields matching human-verified ground truth

dataPoints.name: Processing Speed
dataPoints.interpretation: Volume of supplier/utility documents processed per hour by the AI extraction pipeline

dataPoints.name: Scope 3 Cat 1 Coverage
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of purchased goods/services spend with AI-extracted supplier emission data

Audit Trail records each extraction with source, model version, and confidence
references: GHG Protocol Corporate Accounting and Reporting Standard
IPCC AR6 WGI Chapter 7 Emission Factors

acronyms.def: Transformer-based AI model trained on large text corpora; fine-tuned here for structured ESG data extraction
NLP sub-task used to identify and extract specific field types (quantity, unit, date) from documents

dataLineage.source: Supplier invoices, utility bills, product datasheets (PDF/image)
dataLineage.flow: LLM extraction of activity data fields; confidence scoring; route low-confidence to human review
dataLineage.output: Structured emission activity records with confidence flags and source document links

dataLineage.flow: Match extracted fuel/energy type to AR6 factor; compute GHG contribution
dataLineage.output: Per-document GHG contribution integrated into Scope 1/2/3 inventory

/carbon-accounting-ai.valueSummary: AI-powered carbon accounting addresses the primary bottleneck in enterprise Scope 3 data collection: the manual effort of extracting activity data from thousands of unstructured supplier documents. By automating 90%+ of extraction with human-in-the-loop validation for low-confidence cases, the engine accelerates GHG inventory closure from months to days.

/carbon-aware-allocation.title: Carbon-Aware Allocation
/carbon-aware-allocation.description: Portfolio construction engine that tilts toward lower-carbon assets while maintaining factor exposure and tracking error constraints. Supports EU Paris-Aligned Benchmark (PAB) and Climate Transition Benchmark (CTB) compliance, Scope 1+2 carbon intensity minimisation, and SBTi-aligned holding exclusions. Backtests carbon tilt impact on risk-adjusted returns.

calculationEngine.methodology: Quadratic optimisation with carbon intensity constraint
calculationEngine.formula: min wᵀΣw s.t. Σw_i×CI_i ≤ (1−Δ_PAB)×CI_benchmark; |Σw_i−Σw_i^bm| ≤ TE_budget
calculationEngine.brief: Quadratic optimiser minimises portfolio variance subject to: (1) carbon intensity ≤50% below benchmark (PAB) or ≥30% (CTB), (2) annual 7% (PAB) or 5% (CTB) decarbonisation trajectory, (3) sector deviation limits, (4) tracking error budget. Carbon intensity = Scope1+2 emissions / EVIC.

dataPoints.name: Portfolio Carbon Intensity
dataPoints.interpretation: WACI-equivalent metric; PAB requires ≥50% below benchmark intensity

dataPoints.name: PAB/CTB Decarbonisation Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Required annual carbon intensity reduction for benchmark compliance

dataPoints.name: Tracking Error vs Benchmark
dataPoints.interpretation: Annualised active return volatility; managed within TE budget to control performance risk

Optimisation tab runs portfolio construction with carbon constraint and TE budget
Holdings Comparison shows over/underweight vs benchmark by carbon intensity decile
PAB Compliance tab verifies all mandatory exclusions and 50%/7% requirements
Backtest tab shows 5-year risk/return impact of carbon tilt vs benchmark
Attribution tab decomposes active return into carbon tilt vs sector/factor effects

acronyms.def: EU benchmark requiring ≥50% lower carbon intensity than parent index and 7% annual decarbonisation
EU benchmark requiring ≦30% lower carbon intensity than parent index and 5% annual decarbonisation

dataLineage.flow: Input to quadratic optimiser with carbon constraint and sector deviation limits
dataLineage.output: Optimised carbon-aware portfolio weights with PAB/CTB compliance verification

dataLineage.source: Scope 1+2 emissions and EVIC per company
dataLineage.flow: Compute WACI per holding; aggregate portfolio carbon intensity
dataLineage.output: Portfolio carbon intensity vs benchmark and annual decarbonisation trajectory

/carbon-aware-allocation.valueSummary: Carbon-aware allocation enables index-hugging ESG funds to achieve EU PAB/CTB regulatory compliance while preserving risk-adjusted returns within controlled tracking error budgets. Rigorous quadratic optimisation with annual decarbonisation trajectory constraints ensures benchmarks remain on a Paris-consistent glide path over a multi-year investment horizon.

/carbon-calculator.title: Carbon Calculator
/carbon-calculator.description: Scope 1, 2, and 3 GHG inventory calculator from activity data using IPCC AR6 emission factors, IEA country electricity emission factors, and GHG Protocol Scope 3 Category guidance. Covers 15 Scope 3 categories with 100+ sub-category emission factors. Outputs verified GHG inventory with IPCC uncertainty ranges and data quality scores.

calculationEngine.methodology: GHG Protocol activity-based emission factor model
calculationEngine.formula: GHG_i = Activity_data_i × EF_i × GWP_100_AR6; Scope2_market = Σ(Energy_i × EF_supplier_i) or EF_grid_location
calculationEngine.brief: Activity data Ã— emission factor Ã— Global Warming Potential (100-year, AR6) gives CO₂-equivalent emissions per source. Scope 2 supports both market-based (supplier emission factors, RECs, PPAs) and location-based (IEA grid average) methods. Scope 3 uses spend-based or physical activity factors per GHG Protocol category guidance.

dataPoints.name: Scope 1 Direct Emissions
dataPoints.interpretation: Combustion, process, fugitive and agricultural emissions owned/controlled by company

dataPoints.name: Scope 2 (Market-Based)
dataPoints.interpretation: Indirect electricity emissions using contractual instruments: RECs, PPAs, supplier-specific factors

dataPoints.name: Scope 3 Total
dataPoints.interpretation: Value chain emissions from purchased goods (Cat.1) through investments (Cat.15)

Inventory Summary shows Scope 1+2+3 totals with data quality DQ score
Export generates GHG Protocol compliant inventory report and XBRL file
references: GHG Protocol Corporate Accounting and Reporting Standard 2004/2015
IEA CO2 Emissions from Fuel Combustion 2023

acronyms.def: IPCC metric expressing the heat-trapping effect of a GHG relative to CO₂ over 100 years; AR6 values used
Contract for clean energy supply used as a Scope 2 market-based instrument to reduce electricity emission factor

dataLineage.output: Scope 1+2+3 GHG inventory by source category with data quality flags

dataLineage.flow: Apply to location-based Scope 2; compare against market-based to show contractual reduction
dataLineage.output: Dual Scope 2 reporting (location-based and market-based) per GHG Protocol

/carbon-calculator.valueSummary: A rigorous Scope 1/2/3 GHG inventory is the foundation of all climate disclosure: temperature scoring, carbon footprint, TCFD, CSRD ESRS E1, and SBTi all depend on a complete and defensible emissions baseline. Using IPCC AR6 GWP100 values and the latest IEA emission factors ensures inventory accuracy is consistent with current scientific consensus.

/carbon-forward-curve.title: Carbon Forward Curve
/carbon-forward-curve.description: EUA, CCA, ACCU, and NZU carbon futures term structure analytics covering forward price discovery, implied volatility, contango/backwardation analysis, and hedging cost quantification. Integrates real exchange data from ICE/CME for EU ETS and California Carbon markets with NGFS scenario price forecasts for long-dated forward curve extrapolation.

calculationEngine.formula: F(t,T) = S(t) × exp((r + c – y)(T–t)); Convenience_yield y = r + c – ln(F/S)/(T–t)
calculationEngine.brief: Carbon forward curve uses cost-of-carry: forward price = spot Ã— exp[(risk-free rate + storage cost – convenience yield) Ã— time]. Contango occurs when carry costs exceed convenience yield (common in compliance carbon markets). Implied volatility extracted from options on EUA futures using Black-76 model.

dataPoints.name: EUA Front Month
dataPoints.interpretation: EU Allowance spot price; historically ranged €5–95/tCO₂ in Phase IV (2021–2024)

dataPoints.name: EUA Dec-27 Forward
dataPoints.interpretation: Three-year forward EUA price; proxy for corporate carbon price planning horizon

dataPoints.name: Implied Volatility (3M)
dataPoints.interpretation: Three-month at-the-money implied vol; historically 30–60% for EUA options
