/compliance-evidence.title: Compliance Evidence Manager
/compliance-evidence.description: Manages the collection, versioning, and audit trail of evidence artefacts required for regulatory compliance filings across CSRD, SFDR, ISSB, GRI, and FCA climate disclosures. Provides workflow tracking, completeness scoring, and deadline management for evidence submission cycles.

calculationEngine.formula: Completeness = (EvidenceItems_Submitted / EvidenceItems_Required) × Quality_Weight
calculationEngine.brief: Required evidence items are derived from mapping disclosure requirements to data categories per regulation. Each submitted item receives a quality weight (1.0 = primary source, 0.7 = third-party verified, 0.4 = management estimate). The completeness score feeds directly into the disclosure readiness dashboard and flags gaps for remediation.

dataPoints.name: Evidence Completeness
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of required disclosure evidence items submitted with adequate quality rating

dataPoints.name: Outstanding Items
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of evidence items required but not yet submitted or verified

dataPoints.interpretation: Average quality weight across all submitted evidence; target >0.7 for regulator confidence

/comprehensive-reporting.title: Comprehensive Reporting Suite
/comprehensive-reporting.description: Generates end-to-end sustainability reports across CSRD/ESRS, TCFD, ISSB S1/S2, and GRI standards from a single data model, ensuring cross-framework consistency and traceability. Supports double materiality assessment outputs, quantitative KPI tables, and XBRL-tagged digital disclosure packages.

calculationEngine.formula: Disclosure_coverage = Σ(Mapped_datapoints) / Σ(Required_datapoints)
calculationEngine.brief: A shared data ontology maps each underlying metric to all frameworks that require it (e.g., Scope 1 emissions populates ESRS E1-6, ISSB S2 para 29, GRI 305-1, and TCFD Metrics simultaneously). Materiality flags from double materiality assessment gate which ESRS topics require full disclosure vs. omission justification. Quality tiers (measured, estimated, proxy) propagate to disclosure confidence ratings.

/consumer-carbon-hub.title: Consumer Carbon Hub
/consumer-carbon-hub.description: Analyses Scope 3 Category 11 consumer use-phase emissions for product portfolios, computing product lifecycle GHG profiles, use-phase intensity benchmarks, and product-level decarbonisation pathway options. Enables manufacturers and retailers to meet GHG Protocol Category 11 reporting requirements and set science-based product targets.

calculationEngine.formula: Cat11_total = Σ_p (Units_sold_p × UsePhase_EF_p × Lifetime_p)
calculationEngine.brief: Use-phase emission factor (EF) is derived from product energy consumption during typical use (kWh/year or litres/year) multiplied by the relevant grid or combustion emission factor. For electrical products, grid EF follows IEA country-specific factors updated annually. Product lifetime assumptions follow sector conventions (appliances 10–15yr, vehicles 12yr, electronics 5yr). Intensity benchmark: tCO₂e per unit revenue or per unit sold.

dataPoints.name: Category 11 Emissions
dataPoints.interpretation: Total use-phase Scope 3 Category 11 emissions across product portfolio

dataPoints.name: Use-Phase Intensity
dataPoints.interpretation: GHG intensity per unit sold during consumer use phase over product lifetime

dataPoints.interpretation: Country-specific grid emission factor used for electrically powered product use-phase

dataPoints.interpretation: Average assumed useful life of products used in Category 11 calculation

dataPoints.name: Category 11 % of Total Scope 3
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of total Scope 3 footprint attributable to consumer use phase

/controversy-materiality.title: Controversy Materiality
/controversy-materiality.description: Assesses the financial materiality of ESG controversies on equity valuations and credit spreads using event study methodology, SASB materiality mapping, and sector-specific financial impact models. Quantifies the expected financial impact of controversy severity tiers on EV/EBITDA multiples and CDS spreads.

calculationEngine.formula: EV_impact = β_controversy × SeverityScore × PersistenceDecay(t)
calculationEngine.brief: Beta coefficients are calibrated from event study analysis of 2,000+ ESG controversies across GICS sectors (2010–2024). Severity scores (1–5) map to empirically observed multiple compression: Severity 3 = −2.5× EV/EBITDA; Severity 5 = −6.8×. Persistence decay follows exponential function with sector-specific half-life (financial sector: 18 months; energy: 36 months). Credit spread impact calibrated from Barclays ESG Credit study.

dataPoints.name: EV/EBITDA Compression (Sev.4)
dataPoints.interpretation: Multiple compression observed for severity-4 ESG controversies in GICS sector cross-section

dataPoints.name: CDS Spread Widening (Sev.5)
dataPoints.interpretation: Credit default swap spread widening observed following severe ESG controversies

dataPoints.name: Controversy Persistence Half-Life
dataPoints.interpretation: Time for financial impact to decay by 50% post-controversy onset, sector-dependent

dataPoints.name: SASB Materiality Alignment
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of controversies mapped to financially material SASB topics for that sector

dataPoints.name: Financial Impact Range
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated range of equity value impact from ESG controversies by severity tier

/controversy-monitor.title: Controversy Monitor
/controversy-monitor.description: Provides real-time ESG controversy monitoring across investee companies with severity scoring, reputational risk flags, and automated alerting. Aggregates controversy signals from news, NGO publications, regulatory filings, and social media to provide a comprehensive incident tracking dashboard.

calculationEngine.formula: Severity = max(Category_weight × Reach_score × Novelty_factor)
calculationEngine.brief: Category weights reflect ESRS/SASB materiality hierarchy: Environment (E) human rights violations = 5.0, labour rights = 4.5, product safety = 4.0, environmental damage = 3.5–4.8. Reach score (1–3) reflects media coverage breadth: local/national/global. Novelty factor decays over time as controversy becomes priced in. ESG risk index = rolling 24-month peak severity.

dataPoints.name: Active Controversies
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of active (unresolved) ESG controversies per company in the monitoring universe

dataPoints.name: Peak Severity Score

/controversy-rating-impact.title: Controversy Rating Impact
/controversy-rating-impact.description: Quantifies the effect of ESG controversy events on third-party ESG rating movements, modelling rating agency response lags, severity thresholds for downgrade triggers, and the portfolio-level rating drift attributable to controversies. Supports prediction of future rating changes and proactive engagement planning.

calculationEngine.formula: Rating_change = α + β₁×Severity + β₂×Persistence + β₃×Sector_materiality + ε
calculationEngine.brief: OLS regression calibrated on 5,000+ company-controversy-rating events (2015–2024). Response lag modelled as agency-specific: MSCI typical lag 3–6 months, Sustainalytics 2–4 months, S&P CSA 0–2 months (annual update cycle). Severity threshold for downgrade: Severity ≥4 with sector materiality >0.6 triggers >80% probability of at least one notch downgrade within 12 months.

dataPoints.name: Rating Downgrade Probability (Sev.4)
dataPoints.interpretation: Probability of at least one ESG rating notch downgrade within 12 months of severity-4 controversy

dataPoints.name: Agency Response Lag
dataPoints.interpretation: Typical delay between controversy onset and ESG rating agency score adjustment

dataPoints.name: Portfolio Rating Drift

/copula-tail-risk.title: Copula Tail Risk
/copula-tail-risk.description: Models multivariate tail risk for correlated climate losses across asset classes and geographies using copula theory, capturing co-dependence structures that standard correlation matrices miss under extreme climate scenarios. Implements Gaussian, Student-t, Gumbel, and Clayton copulas for asymmetric tail dependence.

calculationEngine.formula: VaR_portfolio = Q⁻¹(α, C(u₁,...,u_n; θ))
calculationEngine.brief: Marginal distributions of individual asset climate losses are fitted independently (GEV or GPD for physical losses, normal for transition). Copula parameters (θ) are calibrated to historical joint loss data augmented with climate scenario co-occurrence probabilities. Tail dependence coefficient λ_U = lim P(U₂>u|U₁>u) as u→1 measures co-crash risk. Student-t copula with low df captures symmetric fat-tail co-dependence; Gumbel captures upper-tail clustering (simultaneous extreme physical events).

dataPoints.name: Portfolio Tail VaR (99.5%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio loss at 99.5th percentile accounting for tail co-dependence between assets

dataPoints.name: Upper Tail Dependence λ_U
dataPoints.interpretation: Probability of joint extreme loss; 0 = independence, 1 = perfect co-crash

dataPoints.name: Student-t Copula df
dataPoints.interpretation: Degrees of freedom parameter; lower values imply heavier joint tails and higher co-crash risk

dataPoints.name: Copula Diversification Benefit
dataPoints.interpretation: Reduction in portfolio VaR from copula model vs simple sum of individual asset VaRs

dataPoints.name: Climate Co-occurrence Multiplier
dataPoints.interpretation: Factor by which climate scenarios amplify co-dependence vs historical data

/corporate-governance.title: Corporate Governance Analytics
/corporate-governance.description: Analyses board quality, audit committee effectiveness, executive remuneration alignment with sustainability targets, and shareholder rights across portfolio companies. Benchmarks governance scores against ICGN principles and supports proxy voting decisions with quantitative governance risk flags.

calculationEngine.formula: GovScore = 0.30×Board + 0.25×Audit + 0.25×Remuneration + 0.20×Shareholder
calculationEngine.brief: Board sub-score incorporates independence ratio, tenure diversity (% with <9yr tenure), gender diversity (target ≥40%), and climate competence (directors with energy/climate background). Audit sub-score covers audit committee independence, non-audit fee ratio (<20% target), and auditor tenure. Remuneration score penalises absence of ESG KPIs in LTI (Long-Term Incentive) plan and excessive CEO pay ratio.

/corporate-nature-strategy.title: Corporate Nature Strategy
/corporate-nature-strategy.description: Assesses corporate nature strategies against TNFD LEAP framework and SBTN corporate targets, mapping business dependencies and impacts on nature across freshwater, terrestrial, marine, and atmospheric systems. Provides gap analysis against nature-positive commitments and biodiversity target pathways.

calculationEngine.formula: Nature_exposure = Σ_i (Dependency_i × Ecosystem_condition_i × Location_sensitivity_i)
calculationEngine.brief: LEAP (Locate, Evaluate, Assess, Prepare) methodology structures the nature risk process. Dependency scores assess materiality of each ecosystem service to business operations (water provision, pollination, climate regulation, soil quality). Ecosystem condition sourced from WWF Living Planet Index and IBAT biodiversity sensitivity layers. Location sensitivity weights dependencies by proximity to high-biodiversity-value areas.

/credit-risk-analytics.title: Credit Risk Analytics
/credit-risk-analytics.description: Computes climate-adjusted probability of default (PD), loss given default (LGD), and exposure at default (EAD) for corporate and sovereign credit portfolios under NGFS transition and physical risk scenarios. Integrates climate risk into Basel III-compliant credit risk capital calculations.

calculationEngine.formula: PD_adj = PD_base × exp(β_transition × TransitionShock + β_physical × PhysicalShock)
calculationEngine.brief: Transition shock is measured as carbon cost as a fraction of EBITDA margin, sector-specific. Physical shock quantifies revenue/collateral impairment from climate hazard exposure of business locations. Beta coefficients calibrated from ECB 2022 climate stress test sectoral analysis. LGD adjustment: collateral value haircut applies physical risk discount on real estate and commodity-backed lending. EAD: credit line drawdown probability elevated under climate stress.

dataPoints.name: Climate PD Uplift (Hot House World)
dataPoints.interpretation: Basis point increase in probability of default for high-carbon sectors under Hot House World scenario

dataPoints.name: LGD Physical Risk Haircut
dataPoints.interpretation: Reduction in collateral recovery value due to physical climate damage to pledged assets

dataPoints.name: Climate-Adjusted EL
dataPoints.interpretation: Expected loss multiplier under climate scenarios relative to base case

dataPoints.interpretation: Projected PD for high-carbon sectors under current policies scenario by 2035

dataPoints.name: Climate RWA Add-on
dataPoints.interpretation: Additional risk-weighted assets required under Pillar 2 climate add-on approaches

/critical-mineral-geopolitics.title: Critical Mineral Geopolitics
/critical-mineral-geopolitics.description: Analyses supply chain concentration risk for critical transition minerals including lithium, cobalt, nickel, rare earth elements, and copper across extraction, processing, and refining stages. Computes geopolitical supply risk scores, chokepoint exposure, and portfolio vulnerability to supply disruption.

calculationEngine.formula: GSRI = Σ_i (HHI_i × Governance_Risk_i × Trade_Concentration_i) / n
calculationEngine.brief: HHI (Herfindahl-Hirschman Index) measures production concentration across supplying countries. Governance risk weights use World Bank Worldwide Governance Indicators (WGI) â€” rule of law and political stability dimensions. Trade concentration multiplier reflects reliance on a single processing country (e.g., China processes >80% of global rare earths). Score >2500 = highly concentrated; >4000 = critical chokepoint.

/critical-minerals.title: Critical Minerals Analytics
/critical-minerals.description: Projects clean energy critical mineral demand through 2050 under IEA and BNEF transition scenarios, tracks recycling rate evolution and secondary supply contributions, and analyses investment gaps in mining and processing capacity required to meet energy transition demand.

calculationEngine.formula: Demand(t) = Σ_tech (Deployment(t) × MineralIntensity(tech) × (1 - RecyclingRate(t)))
calculationEngine.brief: Technology deployment follows IEA scenario pathways (NPS, SDS, NZE). Mineral intensity per technology uses IEA material composition data (e.g., NMC811 battery: 8.8kg/kWh nickel, 1.1kg/kWh cobalt, 0.9kg/kWh lithium). Recycling rate increases over time as end-of-life battery and solar panel volumes grow (battery recycling: 5% in 2025 to 40% in 2040 under SDS). Supply gap = projected demand minus committed mine supply.

/cross-asset-contagion.title: Cross-Asset Contagion
/cross-asset-contagion.description: Models systemic climate risk propagation across asset classes and geographies, capturing second-round contagion effects through financial network linkages, collateral spirals, and macro-financial feedback loops. Supports macro-prudential stress testing and systemic risk assessment for climate scenarios.

calculationEngine.formula: Contagion_i(t) = Direct_shock_i + Σ_j (w_ij × Contagion_j(t-1))
calculationEngine.brief: Network adjacency matrix w_ij encodes cross-exposure links: bank-to-bank lending, common asset holdings, reinsurance chains, and sovereign-bank nexus. Direct shocks initialised from NGFS transition and physical risk scenarios. Contagion propagates iteratively until steady state, with amplification from fire-sale dynamics (asset sales depress prices, triggering margin calls on other holders) and credit tightening spirals.

dataPoints.name: Contagion Amplification Factor
dataPoints.interpretation: Ratio of total system loss (including contagion) to initial direct shock loss

dataPoints.name: System-Wide Climate VaR (99%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Systemic financial loss as percent of GDP under severe climate scenario with contagion

dataPoints.name: Bank Interconnectedness (HHI)
dataPoints.interpretation: Concentration of interbank exposures; high HHI amplifies contagion speed

dataPoints.name: Sovereign-Bank Nexus Exposure
dataPoints.interpretation: Bank holdings of sovereign debt from climate-vulnerable sovereigns

dataPoints.name: Contagion Network Diameter
dataPoints.interpretation: Average shortest path for climate shock to propagate across the financial system network

/crrem.title: CRREM Pathway Analytics
/crrem.description: Analyses real estate portfolio decarbonisation against Carbon Risk Real Estate Monitor (CRREM) stranding pathways, computing asset-level stranding years, retrofit investment requirements, and portfolio-level alignment with 1.5°C and 2°C real estate pathways.

calculationEngine.formula: StrandingYear = min{t : EUI(t) > CRREM_pathway(t, asset_type, country)}
calculationEngine.brief: Energy Use Intensity (EUI, kWh/m²/yr) is compared annually against CRREM country and property-type specific decarbonisation pathways. Assets with EUI above the pathway in a given year are classified as stranded in that year. CRREM pathways are derived from IPCC 1.5°C and 2°C carbon budgets allocated to the building sector using a sectoral convergence approach. Retrofit capex is estimated using BREEAM-calibrated benchmarks by property type.

/crypto-climate.title: Crypto Climate Risk
/crypto-climate.description: Quantifies cryptocurrency mining energy consumption and GHG intensity, benchmarks proof-of-work versus proof-of-stake consensus mechanisms, and assesses portfolio-level exposure to regulatory and reputational climate risk from digital asset holdings.

calculationEngine.formula: GHG_crypto = TotalHashrate × EnergyPerHash × GridEF_weighted
calculationEngine.brief: Total Bitcoin network hashrate (EH/s) is multiplied by ASIC efficiency (J/TH) to compute network energy consumption. Grid emission factor is weighted by geographic distribution of mining activity (CCAF miner survey). Ethereum (PoS post-Merge) GHG estimated at <0.002 TWh/yr vs Bitcoin ~130 TWh/yr. Portfolio exposure = holding value in PoW assets × GHG intensity per $M invested.

dataPoints.interpretation: Annualised network energy consumption comparable to Poland’s total electricity use

/csddd-compliance.title: CSDDD Compliance
/csddd-compliance.description: Assesses corporate compliance with the EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive, covering adverse impact identification, prioritisation, prevention, and remediation across direct and indirect supply chain tiers. Tracks remediation plan completion and generates CSDDD annual report content.

calculationEngine.formula: Priority_score = Severity × Likelihood × Breadth / RemediationCapacity
standards: UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights
calculationEngine.brief: Severity is assessed across three dimensions per UNGP: gravity (how serious), reversibility (how recoverable), breadth (how many affected). Likelihood weights supplier-country risk (ITUC Global Rights Index, WBI governance scores) with sector-specific impact probability. Prioritisation matrix ranks adverse impacts to guide resource allocation where all cannot be addressed simultaneously per CSDDD Article 8.

dataPoints.name: In-Scope Companies (EU)
dataPoints.interpretation: Companies subject to CSDDD: EU companies with >1,000 employees and €450M global turnover

dataPoints.name: Supply Chain Tier Coverage
dataPoints.interpretation: Due diligence coverage spans direct (Tier 1) and indirect suppliers to the extent feasible

dataPoints.name: Adverse Impact Categories
dataPoints.interpretation: CSDDD covers environmental impacts (Annex Part II) and human rights impacts (Annex Part I)

dataPoints.name: Remediation Plan Completion
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of identified adverse impacts with completed or on-track remediation plans

dataPoints.name: Grievance Mechanism Coverage
dataPoints.interpretation: Whether accessible grievance mechanisms exist for affected workers and communities

/csddd-engine.title: CSDDD Engine
/csddd-engine.description: Automates the CSDDD due diligence workflow with supplier risk assessment, impact screening questionnaires, prevention and mitigation action tracking, and grievance case management. Integrates supplier onboarding with automated risk-flagging and escalation workflows.

calculationEngine.formula: SupplierRisk = f(Country_risk × Sector_risk × Spend_materiality × Audit_history)
standards: OECD Guidance on Sector-Specific Due Diligence
calculationEngine.brief: Country risk combines WBI governance scores, ITUC Global Rights Index, and INFORM risk index for humanitarian crisis exposure. Sector risk maps to OECD sector-specific due diligence guidance (minerals, garments, agriculture, finance). Spend materiality ensures proportionate focus on high-value relationships. Audit history adjusts risk down for SMETA-audited or SA8000-certified suppliers.

/csrd-esrs-automation.title: CSRD ESRS Automation
/csrd-esrs-automation.description: Automates CSRD data collection, validation, and ESRS disclosure generation across all 12 ESRS topical standards, using AI-assisted data extraction, cross-datapoint consistency checks, and XBRL tagging. Integrates with internal systems to minimise manual data entry and accelerate reporting cycles.

calculationEngine.formula: Coverage = Σ(Datapoints_collected × Quality_weight) / Σ(Datapoints_required)
calculationEngine.brief: Required data points are derived from ESRS materiality gating: ESRS 2 is mandatory for all; topical ESRS apply only where material per DMA. Quality weights: audited data = 1.0, management estimate = 0.6, proxy/analogy = 0.3. Cross-consistency check verifies that same metric (e.g., Scope 1 GHG) populates all referencing datapoints consistently across ESRS E1, ESRS 2 and transition plan.

dataPoints.name: ESRS Data Points (mandatory)
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of mandatory disclosure data points across ESRS 2 and mandatory elements of topical standards

dataPoints.interpretation: Topics assessed as material under impact or financial materiality, driving topical ESRS disclosure

dataPoints.name: Data Collection Automation Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of required data points auto-populated from connected internal systems

dataPoints.name: XBRL Tag Coverage
dataPoints.interpretation: Quantitative data points with valid ESRS XBRL taxonomy tags for digital filing

dataPoints.name: Cross-Consistency Errors
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of inconsistencies detected between same metric appearing in multiple ESRS locations

/csrd-ixbrl.title: CSRD iXBRL Tagger
/csrd-ixbrl.description: Applies ESRS XBRL taxonomy tags to quantitative and qualitative CSRD disclosures, validates tags against EFRAG taxonomy schema, and generates machine-readable iXBRL files for submission to European Single Access Point (ESAP). Supports multi-language tagging for multinational reporters.

calculationEngine.formula: Tag_coverage = Matched_datapoints / Total_quantitative_datapoints × 100%
calculationEngine.brief: ESRS taxonomy elements are matched to disclosure data points using a three-layer matching process: (1) exact datapoint ID match, (2) semantic similarity matching for narrative elements, (3) extension taxonomy for non-standard items. Validation checks cover: tag-value consistency, mandatory extension metadata, period and unit annotations, and schema conformance. Invalid or unmapped tags generate actionable error messages with EFRAG guidance references.

/damage-function-calculator.title: Damage Function Calculator
/damage-function-calculator.description: Models climate economic damage functions from the DICE, PAGE, and FUND Integrated Assessment Models, computing GDP loss under different warming trajectories and allowing comparison of damage function parameterisations, discount rates, and socioeconomic scenarios.

calculationEngine.formula: D(T) = 1 - 1/(1 + α₁T + α₂T²)
calculationEngine.brief: Nordhaus DICE quadratic damage function: D(T) = α₂T² where α₂ = 0.00236 (calibrated to 2.5°C = 1.3% GDP loss). PAGE09 uses probabilistic damage with mean=D(T)+ε and fat right tail. FUND applies sector-disaggregated (agriculture, coastal, health, energy, ecosystems) damage with regional heterogeneity. Burke et al. empirical nonlinear GDP-temperature function shows 6.7% permanent GDP loss per 1°C above historical optimum.

dataPoints.name: Global Damage at 2°C (DICE)
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated annual GDP loss at 2°C warming; considered an underestimate by many economists

dataPoints.name: Global Damage at 4°C (Burke)
dataPoints.interpretation: Empirical damage estimate at 4°C using GDP-temperature panel regression with country fixed effects

dataPoints.name: Social Cost of Carbon (DICE, 3% disc.)
dataPoints.interpretation: Present value of future GDP damages from one additional tonne of CO₂ emissions

dataPoints.name: Fat-Tail Probability (PAGE)
dataPoints.interpretation: Probability of catastrophic damage scenarios (>20% GDP loss) under PAGE probabilistic model

dataPoints.name: Regional Heterogeneity
dataPoints.interpretation: Tropical developing regions experience 2–5× higher per-capita damage than global average

userInteraction: Select IAM damage function (DICE/PAGE/FUND/Burke) from the model picker

Model Comparison tab overlays all four damage functions to illustrate parameterisation uncertainty
references: Burke, Hsiang & Miguel (2015) Global Non-linear Effect of Temperature on Economic Production

acronyms.def: Model integrating climate science and economic analysis to evaluate climate policy impacts and the social cost of carbon
Monetary estimate of the long-term damage from one additional tonne of CO₂ emissions, used in policy cost-benefit analysis
Nobel-winning IAM by William Nordhaus linking GHG emissions, temperature, and economic damage in a single optimisation framework

/data-governance.title: Data Governance Hub
/data-governance.description: Manages ESG data governance policies, stewardship workflows, data quality controls, and metadata standards across the sustainability data platform. Tracks data ownership, access controls, quality KPIs, and policy compliance to ensure reliable, auditable sustainability disclosures.

calculationEngine.formula: DQ_score = 0.30×Completeness + 0.25×Accuracy + 0.25×Timeliness + 0.20×Consistency
calculationEngine.brief: Completeness: proportion of required fields populated. Accuracy: deviation from source verification checks and plausibility bounds. Timeliness: data age relative to reporting period (penalty for >6 months lag). Consistency: cross-system agreement for same metric (e.g., Scope 1 in ERP vs sustainability system). Scores below 70 trigger mandatory data steward review before disclosure sign-off.

dataPoints.interpretation: Overall ESG data quality score across all data categories and sources

/data-hub-ingester.title: Data Hub Ingester
/data-hub-ingester.description: Manages multi-source ESG data ingestion pipelines with normalisation, deduplication, and quality validation for provider data from MSCI, Refinitiv, CDP, Bloomberg, and proprietary sources. Supports scheduled and event-driven ingestion with conflict resolution and provenance tracking.

calculationEngine.formula: Fused_value = Σ_p (w_p × Value_p) / Σ_p w_p
calculationEngine.brief: Provider weights (w_p) are assigned based on source quality tier: primary measured data = 1.0, third-party audited = 0.85, modelled/estimated = 0.5. Conflict resolution applies: if max(Value_p) - min(Value_p) > threshold, flag for steward review rather than auto-fuse. Deduplication uses entity resolution matching on LEI, ISIN, SEDOL, and Bloomberg ID to prevent double-counting across providers.

/data-infra-hub.title: Data Infrastructure Hub
/data-infra-hub.description: Monitors platform data infrastructure performance including storage utilisation, query performance, pipeline health, database replication, and API availability. Provides operational intelligence for platform administrators ensuring sustainability data platform uptime and performance SLAs.

calculationEngine.formula: HealthScore = 0.35×Uptime + 0.30×QueryPerf + 0.20×PipelineHealth + 0.15×StorageStatus
calculationEngine.brief: Uptime measured as 30-day rolling availability percentage (target >99.9%). Query performance scored by P95 query latency vs SLA threshold (target P95 <500ms for standard queries). Pipeline health combines success rate and latency across all active ETL pipelines. Storage status monitors utilisation growth rate and projected capacity headroom (target >30% headroom).

/data-lineage.title: Data Lineage Tracker
/data-lineage.description: Provides end-to-end data lineage mapping from raw source data through transformation pipelines to disclosed sustainability metrics, enabling full traceability for regulatory assurance, audit purposes, and data quality root-cause analysis. Visualises lineage as an interactive directed acyclic graph.

calculationEngine.formula: Lineage_coverage = Metrics_with_full_lineage / Total_disclosed_metrics × 100%
calculationEngine.brief: Full lineage requires documentation of: (1) original source system and record identifier, (2) all transformation steps with logic descriptions, (3) quality checks applied, (4) aggregation methodology, and (5) final disclosure mapping with period and unit. Partial lineage flags metrics where any stage is undocumented. Lineage completeness is a key external assurance readiness indicator under CSRD limited assurance requirements.

dataPoints.name: Assurance-Ready Metrics
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of key disclosed metrics meeting full lineage documentation for limited assurance

/em-debt-climate-risk.title: EM Debt Climate Risk
/em-debt-climate-risk.description: Prices climate transition and physical risk into emerging market sovereign and corporate debt spreads. Integrates NGFS scenario pathways with country-level vulnerability indices and issuer-level carbon intensity to derive climate-adjusted credit spreads. Supports portfolio construction, relative value analysis, and regulatory stress testing across EM fixed income universes.

calculationEngine.formula: OAS_climate = OAS_base + β_trans × TransitionRisk + β_phys × PhysicalRisk
calculationEngine.brief: Decomposes observed OAS into a base credit component and two additive climate risk premia. Transition beta is calibrated from sector carbon intensity relative to national NDC targets. Physical beta incorporates ND-GAIN country vulnerability scores and asset-level hazard exposure.

dataPoints.name: Climate Risk Premium (bps)
dataPoints.interpretation: Incremental spread attributable to unpriced climate risk; high values signal mispriced sovereign paper.

dataPoints.name: NDC Alignment Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Measures issuer decarbonisation trajectory vs. Paris-compatible pathway; below 40 indicates high transition risk.

dataPoints.name: Physical Vulnerability Index
dataPoints.interpretation: Country-level composite of exposure, sensitivity, and adaptive capacity; above 0.7 triggers spread overlay.

dataPoints.name: Carbon Intensity (tCO2e/$M Rev)
dataPoints.interpretation: Corporate issuer emission intensity; benchmarked against sector median and 1.5Â°C budget ceiling.

Choose NGFS scenario (Orderly/Disorderly/Hot House) and time horizon (2030/2040/2050).
Review climate-adjusted OAS decomposition and identify issuers with largest unpriced risk premium.
Run stress scenario to project spread widening under tail physical risk event and export to portfolio risk system.
references: IMF WP/23/145 â€” Climate and Sovereign Risk
BIS Working Paper 1089 â€” Climate Risk and Bond Spreads

acronyms.def: Credit spread stripped of embedded optionality, used as basis for climate premium overlay.
Country-level Paris Agreement emissions reduction commitment submitted to UNFCCC.
Country vulnerability and readiness index used as physical risk proxy.

/embodied-carbon.title: Embodied Carbon Analytics
/embodied-carbon.description: Tracks and analyses lifecycle embodied carbon across construction materials, building projects, and manufactured products using Environmental Product Declaration data. Covers upfront carbon (Modules A1â€“A5), use-stage emissions (B1â€“B7), and end-of-life impacts (C1â€“C4) in accordance with EN 15978 and ISO 14044. Supports whole-life carbon optimisation, EPD benchmarking, and supply chain material substitution analysis.

calculationEngine.formula: WLC = Σ(EC_i × Q_i) + OC_use + EoL_carbon
calculationEngine.brief: Sums embodied carbon contributions of each material quantity multiplied by EPD-sourced carbon factor. Operational carbon from energy use over the reference study period is added, plus end-of-life demolition and disposal emissions. All values expressed in kgCO2e per functional unit or per m² GIA.

dataPoints.name: Upfront Embodied Carbon (kgCO2e/m²)
dataPoints.interpretation: A1-A5 emissions intensity; LETI 2030 target for offices is <300 kgCO2e/m² GIA.

dataPoints.name: Global Warming Potential (kgCO2e)
dataPoints.interpretation: Product-level GWP100 from EPD; primary indicator for material comparison and substitution.

dataPoints.name: Biogenic Carbon (kgCO2e)
dataPoints.interpretation: Separately reported biogenic carbon stored in timber and bio-based products; must not net against fossil GWP.

dataPoints.name: Carbon Hotspot Ratio (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of WLC attributable to top-3 material categories; drives substitution prioritisation.

userInteraction: Upload bill of materials or link to project model to auto-populate material quantities.
Match each material to an EPD record or default ICE v3 factor; review EPD coverage score.
Review A1â€“A5 carbon hotspot chart and apply material substitution scenarios (e.g., timber vs. concrete frame).
Export whole-life carbon certificate and RICS-format report for planning submission or green finance compliance.
references: EN 15978:2011 â€” Sustainability of Construction Works
ICE Database v3.0, University of Bath 2019
RICS Whole Life Carbon Assessment 2nd Ed. 2023
ISO 14044:2006 â€” Life Cycle Assessment Requirements

acronyms.def: ISO 14025-compliant third-party verified document reporting a product lifecycle environmental impacts.
Total carbon emissions across all lifecycle stages from cradle to grave per EN 15978.
CO2-equivalent impact of a substance over 100-year horizon; primary EPD climate indicator.
Total floor area measured internally; standard denominator for embodied carbon intensity benchmarks.

dataLineage.flow: Parse GWP100 values by lifecycle module and material category
dataLineage.output: Material-level embodied carbon factor (kgCO2e/kg or m²)

dataLineage.flow: Quantity take-off matched to EPD records; gaps filled with ICE v3 defaults
dataLineage.output: Project total WLC by lifecycle stage

dataLineage.flow: Compare project intensity to sector percentile bands
dataLineage.output: Carbon performance rating and gap-to-target

/embodied-carbon.valueSummary: Supports architects, developers, and lenders in achieving net-zero whole-life carbon targets, meeting LETI/RIBA 2030 benchmarks, and satisfying green loan eligibility criteria requiring embodied carbon disclosure.

/employee-wellbeing.title: Employee Wellbeing Analytics
/employee-wellbeing.description: Aggregates and analyses workforce health, engagement, and mental wellbeing metrics to support S-pillar ESG disclosure and strategic people risk management. Integrates HR system data on absenteeism, turnover, employee assistance programme utilisation, and engagement survey scores. Aligns output with GRI 401/403, SASB Human Capital standards, and the emerging SEC human capital disclosure rules.

calculationEngine.formula: WBI = w₁×Engagement + w₂×(1−AbsenteeismRate) + w₃×(1−TurnoverRate) + w₄×MHUtilisation
calculationEngine.brief: Composite index weighting four normalised sub-dimensions, each scored 0â€“100. Default weights (0.35/0.25/0.25/0.15) can be adjusted by users. Absenteeism and turnover are inverse-scored so that higher values represent better outcomes. Mental health utilisation is scored relative to benchmark EAP participation rate.

dataPoints.name: Absenteeism Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Unplanned absence days as % of scheduled working days; WHO-linked productivity loss above 3.5%.

dataPoints.name: Engagement Score (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Favourable response rate to core engagement questions; Gallup global median 23% engaged.

dataPoints.name: EAP Utilisation Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of employees accessing mental health support; below 3% may indicate stigma or awareness gap.

dataPoints.name: Voluntary Turnover Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Annualised voluntary leavers as % of average headcount; elevated rates signal retention and culture risk.

userInteraction: Connect HR data source or upload workforce metrics export for the reporting period.
Configure Wellbeing Index weights to reflect organisational priorities and sector benchmarks.
Drill into departmental or geographic breakdowns to identify workforce wellbeing hotspots.
Generate GRI 403 and SASB HC disclosure tables and export for integrated report or ESG data room.
references: GRI 403:2018 â€” Occupational Health and Safety
ISO 45003:2021 â€” Psychological Health and Safety at Work
WHO Mental Health at Work Policy Brief 2022
Gallup State of the Global Workplace 2024

acronyms.def: Composite platform score aggregating engagement, absenteeism, turnover, and mental health utilisation.
Employer-funded confidential counselling and mental health support service.
Core HR database from which headcount, turnover, and absence data are sourced.
International sustainability reporting standard; GRI 403 covers occupational health and safety disclosures.

dataLineage.flow: Aggregate to monthly cohorts; normalise by FTE and region
dataLineage.output: Absenteeism and turnover KPIs by business unit

dataLineage.flow: Extract question-level scores; compute favourable response rate
dataLineage.output: Engagement score with trend vs. prior period

dataLineage.flow: Normalise by total eligible employees; segment by service type
dataLineage.output: EAP utilisation rate and presenting issue breakdown

/employee-wellbeing.valueSummary: Enables HR leaders and ESG teams to monitor workforce health risks, benchmark against sector peers, and produce audit-ready S-pillar disclosures that satisfy GRI 403, SASB HC, and investor human capital questionnaires.

/enablement-methodology.title: Enablement Methodology
/enablement-methodology.description: Implements the PCAF Scope 3 Category 15 investment enablement methodology for financed emissions attribution across asset classes. Quantifies the proportion of an investee's emissions enabled or avoided through financing activity, applying PCAF data quality scoring and economic attribution logic. Supports alignment with PCAF Standard Part A and Part B for banks, asset managers, and insurers.

calculationEngine.formula: EE = (Financing / EVIC) × Investee_GHG
calculationEngine.brief: Attributable financed emissions are computed by multiplying the outstanding financing amount divided by Enterprise Value Including Cash by the investee's total GHG inventory (Scope 1+2, Scope 3 where available). EVIC serves as the economic attribution denominator per PCAF Part A. Avoided emissions from enabling renewable energy projects are reported separately as positive impact.

dataPoints.name: Attribution Factor (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Financing share of EVIC; determines proportion of investee GHG inventory attributed to the financier.

dataPoints.name: Financed Emissions (tCO2e)
dataPoints.interpretation: Absolute attributed emissions from lending or investment activity; primary PCAF reporting metric.

dataPoints.name: PCAF Data Quality Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Score 1 = highest quality (audited Scope 1+2); Score 5 = proxy/estimated; affects disclosure confidence band.

dataPoints.name: Avoided Emissions (tCO2e)
dataPoints.interpretation: Emissions avoided by enabling clean energy or efficiency projects; reduces net portfolio footprint.

userInteraction: Load portfolio holdings with outstanding loan or investment balance and investee EVIC data.
Map each position to the PCAF asset class category (listed equity, corporate bonds, loans, project finance, etc.).
Review attribution factors and verify GHG inventory data quality scores; flag Score 4â€“5 positions for improvement.
Export PCAF-format financed emissions report with data quality distribution and avoided emissions annex.
references: PCAF Global GHG Accounting and Reporting Standard for the Financial Industry 2022
GHG Protocol Corporate Value Chain (Scope 3) Standard 2011
TCFD Guidance on Metrics, Targets and Transition Plans 2021
SBTi Financial Sector Science-Based Targets Guidance 2024

acronyms.def: Global partnership providing standardised methodology for measuring financed and facilitated emissions.
Market cap plus total debt plus minority interest plus preferred equity; PCAF economic attribution denominator.
PCAF five-level scoring system assessing reliability of GHG data used in financed emissions calculation.
GHG Protocol Scope 3 category covering investments; the primary scope for financial sector financed emissions.

dataLineage.flow: Match to PCAF asset class; compute EVIC-based attribution factor
dataLineage.output: Attribution factor per position (%)

dataLineage.flow: Assign PCAF DQ score 1â€“5; apply sector proxy where actuals unavailable
dataLineage.output: Financed emissions per investee (tCO2e)

dataLineage.flow: Calculate avoided emissions vs. grid displacement factor
dataLineage.output: Avoided emissions portfolio total (tCO2e)

/enablement-methodology.valueSummary: Provides financial institutions with a regulatory-grade financed emissions calculation engine that satisfies PCAF Standard requirements, supports SBTi FI target-setting, and generates audit-ready Cat.15 disclosure tables for TCFD and CDP responses.

/energy-security-transition.title: Energy Security & Transition
/energy-security-transition.description: Analyses the tension between national energy security objectives and the pace of low-carbon transition, integrating geopolitical energy dependency scores with NDC ambition and fossil fuel import exposure. Produces country-level energy trilemma assessments covering security, equity, and sustainability dimensions. Informs sovereign risk analysis, infrastructure investment screening, and policy scenario planning.

calculationEngine.formula: ETS = w₁×Security + w₂×Equity + w₃×Sustainability
calculationEngine.brief: Composite score aggregating three normalised pillars each scored 0â€“100. Security pillar incorporates import dependency ratio, diversity of supply (HHI), and strategic reserve adequacy. Equity pillar covers affordability and access rates. Sustainability pillar uses carbon intensity of the energy mix and renewable share trajectory.

dataPoints.name: Import Dependency Ratio (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Net energy imports as % of gross inland consumption; >70% flags high geopolitical exposure.

dataPoints.name: Supply Diversity (HHI)
dataPoints.interpretation: Herfindahl-Hirschman Index of energy source and supplier concentration; HHI >2,500 indicates high concentration risk.

dataPoints.name: Renewable Share of Power Mix (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of variable renewables and hydro in electricity generation; key sustainability pillar driver.

dataPoints.name: Energy Trilemma Score (0â€“100)
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite country score; higher = better balance across security, equity, sustainability dimensions.

userInteraction: Select countries or custom regional grouping for comparative trilemma assessment.
Apply NGFS or IEA NZE scenario to project energy mix evolution and recalculate future trilemma scores.
Review security vs. sustainability trade-off scatter plot to identify countries at highest transition-security conflict.
Export country profiles for sovereign debt risk analysis or infrastructure investment screening report.
references: IMF Working Paper â€” Energy Security and Climate Policy Trade-offs 2023

acronyms.def: Market concentration measure; applied to energy supply diversity â€” higher values indicate greater supplier concentration.
Paris Agreement country-level emission reduction commitment; drives sustainability pillar trajectory.
International energy body publishing annual Trilemma Index ranking 130+ countries on energy balance.
IEA flagship scenario achieving global net zero CO2 emissions by 2050; used as sustainability benchmark.

dataLineage.source: IEA energy statistics (production, imports, consumption by source)
dataLineage.flow: Compute import dependency and HHI by country and fuel type
dataLineage.output: Security sub-score per country

dataLineage.source: IRENA renewable capacity and generation data
dataLineage.flow: Calculate renewable share and year-on-year trajectory
dataLineage.output: Sustainability sub-score with NZE gap

dataLineage.source: World Bank energy access and affordability data
dataLineage.flow: Normalise access rate and household energy burden
dataLineage.output: Equity sub-score for trilemma composite

/energy-security-transition.valueSummary: Enables sovereign analysts, infrastructure investors, and policy advisors to quantify the energy security-transition trade-off, identify countries where transition acceleration could destabilise energy supply, and structure resilient green infrastructure investment theses.

/energy-transition-analytics.title: Energy Transition Analytics
/energy-transition-analytics.description: Tracks global and regional renewable energy build-out trajectories against IEA and IRENA decarbonisation pathways, monitoring grid decarbonisation rates and the financial exposure of stranded generation assets. Integrates capacity addition data, power purchase agreement analytics, and utility-level carbon intensity trends. Supports asset allocation, utility equity analysis, and infrastructure debt underwriting.

calculationEngine.formula: CI_t = CI_0 × (1 − r)^t
calculationEngine.brief: Projects grid emission intensity (gCO2e/kWh) using a compound annual decarbonisation rate calibrated to NZE pathway benchmarks by region. Stranded asset value is computed as the NPV of foregone revenue from fossil generation capacity retired ahead of end-of-technical-life, discounted at the utility WACC plus a stranding risk premium.

dataPoints.name: Grid Carbon Intensity (gCO2e/kWh)
dataPoints.interpretation: Emission intensity of national electricity grid; IEA NZE 2050 global target <50 gCO2e/kWh by 2035.

dataPoints.name: Renewable Capacity Addition (GW/year)
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual new renewable capacity commissioned; IEA NZE requires 1,000 GW/year globally by 2030.

dataPoints.name: Stranded Asset Exposure ($Bn)
dataPoints.interpretation: NPV of unrecoverable capital in fossil generation assets facing early retirement under transition scenarios.

dataPoints.name: PPA Weighted Average Price ($/MWh)
dataPoints.interpretation: Corporate PPA clearing price by technology and region; benchmark for renewable procurement cost competitiveness.

userInteraction: Select region and technology focus (solar PV, onshore wind, offshore wind, storage, gas peakers).
Choose IEA scenario (NZE/APS/STEPS) and project grid carbon intensity trajectory to 2050.
Identify utilities or generators with highest stranded asset exposure under selected scenario and review asset-level detail.
Export capacity build-out gap analysis and stranded asset report for investment committee or regulatory submission.
references: Carbon Tracker â€” The Future's Not in Plastics 2023
SBTi Power Sector Science-Based Target Guidance

acronyms.def: IEA scenario achieving net zero CO2 emissions from the energy sector by 2050; primary decarbonisation benchmark.
Long-term contract between electricity generator and buyer; key instrument for corporate renewable procurement.
IEA scenario reflecting full implementation of all government net zero pledges; intermediate ambition pathway.
Lifetime cost of generation per MWh including capex, opex, and financing; primary renewable competitiveness metric.

dataLineage.flow: Aggregate by technology and region; compute annual capacity additions vs. NZE pathway
dataLineage.output: Renewable build-out gap by region and technology (GW)

dataLineage.source: Utility regulatory filings and asset registries
dataLineage.flow: Map plant-level capacity to fuel type and remaining life; apply stranding discount rate
dataLineage.output: Stranded asset exposure by utility and scenario ($Bn)

dataLineage.flow: Normalise by technology, tenor, and market structure
dataLineage.output: Regional PPA price index and trend ($/MWh)

/energy-transition-analytics.valueSummary: Provides power sector analysts and infrastructure investors with a real-time view of transition velocity, enabling identification of under-valued renewable developers, over-exposed fossil generators, and grid decarbonisation financing opportunities across 50+ markets.

/enterprise-climate-risk.title: Enterprise Climate Risk
/enterprise-climate-risk.description: Delivers a board-level enterprise climate risk dashboard integrating physical and transition risk exposures across all business divisions, assets, and supply chains. Aggregates risk scores into a CEO/CFO-ready climate risk register aligned with TCFD recommendations and emerging ISSB IFRS S2 mandatory disclosure requirements. Provides scenario-based financial impact quantification for strategic planning and regulatory submission.

calculationEngine.formula: ECRS = Σ(w_d × (PhysRisk_d + TransRisk_d)) / Σw_d
calculationEngine.brief: Division-weighted average of standardised physical and transition risk sub-scores (each 0â€“100). Physical risk sub-score aggregates acute and chronic hazard exposures across asset footprint. Transition risk sub-score covers regulatory, technology, market, and reputational dimensions. Enterprise score is value-at-risk calibrated under 1.5°C, 2°C, and 3°C scenarios.

dataPoints.name: Enterprise Climate VaR (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated EBITDA-at-risk from climate factors under tail scenario; above 10% triggers board-level escalation.

dataPoints.name: Physical Risk Hotspot Count
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of owned or leased assets above critical hazard threshold in current and 2050 climate.

dataPoints.name: Transition Risk Exposure ($M)
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated NPV impact of carbon pricing, stranded assets, and revenue shift under 1.5°C scenario.

dataPoints.name: TCFD Disclosure Readiness Score (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of TCFD recommended disclosures populated with quantified data vs. qualitative-only or absent.

userInteraction: Map all business divisions, major assets, and key supply chain nodes to geographic coordinates and sector classification.
Assign NGFS or custom scenario set and time horizons for short (2025), medium (2030), and long-term (2050) assessments.
Review enterprise risk register with financial impact ranges and confidence bands for each risk category.
Generate TCFD/IFRS S2 aligned board report with governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics & targets sections.
references: IPCC AR6 WGII Chapter 16 â€” Key Risks

acronyms.def: Platform composite score aggregating division-level physical and transition risk, weighted by asset value.
Statistical measure of potential financial loss at a given confidence level; applied here to climate scenarios.
ISSB standard mandating climate-related financial disclosures; effective from January 2024 in adopting jurisdictions.
Nature risk framework complementary to TCFD; v1.0 published September 2023.

dataLineage.source: Asset registry (GIS coordinates, book value, sector)
dataLineage.flow: Overlay with physical hazard maps (flood, heat, drought, sea-level rise)
dataLineage.output: Asset-level physical risk score and financial exposure

dataLineage.source: Revenue and cost data by division
dataLineage.flow: Apply scenario-specific carbon price, policy cost, and demand shift factors
dataLineage.output: Division-level transition risk impact ($M)

dataLineage.flow: Gap-assess against 11 recommended disclosures; score quantification maturity
dataLineage.output: TCFD readiness score with disclosure gap register

/enterprise-climate-risk.valueSummary: Equips CFOs, Chief Risk Officers, and sustainability teams with a single authoritative climate risk view that satisfies TCFD, IFRS S2, and emerging mandatory disclosure regimes while providing the financial quantification needed for strategic capital allocation and board-level decision-making.

/entity-360.title: Entity 360 View
/entity-360.description: Provides a comprehensive 360-degree ESG, climate, and financial profile for any corporate entity, aggregating data across emissions, ratings, controversies, governance, supply chain, and regulatory filings into a unified analytical workspace. Designed for credit analysts, ESG researchers, and investment teams requiring rapid entity due diligence. Integrates with all platform modules to surface cross-module entity-level insights.

calculationEngine.formula: Entity_ESG = w_E × E_score + w_S × S_score + w_G × G_score
calculationEngine.brief: Pillar scores are computed from normalised underlying KPIs weighted by SASB industry materiality. Environmental score aggregates carbon intensity, water stress exposure, and waste intensity. Social covers workforce safety, labour practices, and supply chain human rights. Governance incorporates board independence, pay alignment, and anti-corruption controls. Provider consensus uses equal-weighted MSCI/Sustainalytics/ISS blend.

dataPoints.name: Provider Consensus ESG Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Equal-weighted blend of three leading provider scores; high dispersion flags ratings disagreement requiring deeper review.

dataPoints.name: Scope 1+2 Intensity (tCO2e/$M Rev)
dataPoints.interpretation: Carbon intensity normalised by revenue; benchmarked to sector SBTi-aligned budget.

dataPoints.name: Controversy Severity (1â€“5)
dataPoints.interpretation: Most severe ESG controversy in trailing 24 months; Level 4â€“5 triggers enhanced due diligence flag.

dataPoints.name: TCFD Disclosure Score (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of TCFD-recommended disclosures present and quantified in public filings.

userInteraction: Search for entity by name, ISIN, LEI, or ticker to load the 360 profile.
Review summary dashboard spanning emissions, ratings consensus, governance flags, and supply chain risk heat map.
Drill into any sub-module (e.g., ESG Ratings, Physical Risk, Litigation) for detailed analysis within entity context.
Generate entity due diligence pack with all cross-module findings formatted for investment committee or credit committee.
references: LEI Data â€” Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation

acronyms.def: 20-character ISO 17442 code uniquely identifying legal entities participating in financial transactions.
12-character code identifying a specific security issue globally; primary entity lookup key for listed issuers.
Quantitative metric used to assess ESG performance; aggregated into pillar scores within the 360 view.
ESG intelligence firm tracking corporate controversies from 100,000+ public sources in 20 languages.

dataLineage.source: Multi-provider ESG data feeds (MSCI, Sustainalytics, ISS)
dataLineage.flow: Normalise to 0â€“100 scale; compute consensus and inter-provider dispersion
dataLineage.output: Provider consensus score with dispersion band

dataLineage.flow: Reconcile Scope 1/2/3 across sources; flag self-reported vs. estimated
dataLineage.output: Carbon intensity KPIs with data quality indicator

dataLineage.flow: Classify by pillar, severity, and recency; map to entity LEI
dataLineage.output: Controversy severity score and incident log

/entity-360.valueSummary: Accelerates entity-level ESG due diligence from days to minutes, enabling investment teams to make fully informed engagement, exclusion, and credit decisions backed by a comprehensive, multi-source evidence base in a single integrated view.

/epd-lca-database.title: EPD & LCA Database
/epd-lca-database.description: Manages and queries a curated database of Environmental Product Declarations and lifecycle assessment records for construction materials, manufactured goods, and industrial products. Supports EPD search, comparison, version control, and gap analysis against ISO 14025 and EN 15804+A2 requirements. Enables systematic material substitution analysis, EPD data quality auditing, and green procurement workflows.

calculationEngine.formula: CF_delta = (CF_incumbent − CF_alternative) / CF_incumbent × 100%
calculationEngine.brief: Computes percentage carbon factor reduction between incumbent and alternative materials on a per-functional-unit basis, normalising for declared unit differences. GWP100 values are extracted from A1â€“A3 modules for product comparisons. Where EPD scope includes A4â€“A5, the full cradle-to-practical-completion carbon factor is used for embodied carbon calculations.

dataPoints.name: GWP100 A1â€“A3 (kgCO2e/declared unit)
dataPoints.interpretation: Product stage embodied carbon; primary metric for material comparison; must be verified EPD (not self-declared).

dataPoints.name: EPD Validity Status
dataPoints.interpretation: EPDs expire after 5 years; validity flag alerts users to expired records requiring renewal before use in compliance filings.

dataPoints.name: PCR Version
dataPoints.interpretation: Product Category Rule version underpinning the EPD; different PCR versions may use different system boundaries, affecting comparability.

dataPoints.name: Third-Party Verifier
dataPoints.interpretation: Independent verifier name; critical for LEED v4, BREEAM, and green bond compliance requiring Type III EPDs.

userInteraction: Search EPD database by material category, manufacturer, PCR, or declared GWP range.
Compare up to five products on GWP100, acidification, eutrophication, and resource depletion indicators side-by-side.
Upload project bill of materials to auto-match materials to EPD records and flag missing or expired EPDs.
Export EPD citations and GWP values in RICS, LEED v4, or BREEAM credits format for certification submission.
references: ISO 14025:2006 â€” Environmental Labels and Declarations Type III
EN 15804+A2:2022 â€” Core PCR for Construction Products
ISO 14044:2006 â€” Life Cycle Assessment Requirements
LEED v4.1 MRc Building Product Disclosure Credit

acronyms.def: ISO 14025 Type III verified declaration of a product's lifecycle environmental impacts.
ISO 14040/44 methodology quantifying environmental impacts across all lifecycle stages.
Standardised rules defining system boundaries and methodology for EPDs within a product category.
CO2-equivalent climate impact over 100-year horizon; primary EPD indicator for embodied carbon tracking.

dataLineage.source: EPD programme operator databases (ECO Platform, EPD International, IBU, BRANZ)
dataLineage.flow: Parse XML/PDF EPD documents; extract GWP100 by lifecycle module and validate verifier signature
dataLineage.output: Structured EPD record with GWP, validity, PCR version

dataLineage.flow: Fuzzy-match material descriptions to EPD product names and declared units
dataLineage.output: Matched EPD coverage rate and unmatched material list

dataLineage.flow: Filter EPD records by programme compliance criteria (Type III, third-party verified, within validity)
dataLineage.output: Compliant EPD count and certification gap analysis

/epd-lca-database.valueSummary: Streamlines the EPD procurement and verification workflow for project teams, dramatically reducing the time to build LEED v4 MRc credit submissions and RICS whole-life carbon calculations while maintaining a living database of product-level embodied carbon benchmarks.

/equator-principles.title: Equator Principles
/equator-principles.description: Provides a structured compliance workflow for EP IV (Equator Principles fourth edition) environmental and social due diligence in project finance transactions. Supports category assignment, ESAP tracking, stakeholder consultation documentation, and independent monitoring. Covers IFC Performance Standards PS1â€“PS8 and Equator Principles 1â€“10 for projects above the financial threshold in all industry sectors.

calculationEngine.formula: ESAP_score = (Closed_Actions / Total_Actions) × 100 − Σ(Severity_i × Overdue_i)
calculationEngine.brief: Tracks the ratio of closed to total Environmental and Social Action Plan items, penalised by a weighted overdue factor where high-severity outstanding actions carry greater weight. Scoring aligns with the independent environmental and social consultant reporting cycle. Category A projects require formal ESIA; Category B require limited assessment; Category C require minimal assessment.

dataPoints.name: EP Category (A/B/C)
dataPoints.interpretation: Project risk category; Category A = significant adverse E&S impacts; drives assessment scope and disclosure requirements.

dataPoints.name: ESAP Closure Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of ESAP action items closed vs. total; below 80% at financial close raises covenant breach risk.

dataPoints.name: Stakeholder Consultation Events
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of documented free, prior, and informed consultation events; mandatory for affected communities in Category A/B projects.

dataPoints.name: IFC PS Compliance Score (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of applicable PS sub-requirements rated Compliant or Minor Non-Conformance in ESIA review.

userInteraction: Create new project record and classify into EP category using the guided impact screening questionnaire.
Upload ESIA documentation and map findings to IFC PS1â€“PS8 compliance matrix.
Enter ESAP action items with owners, deadlines, and severity ratings; configure monitoring milestone alerts.
Generate EP10 reporting package for annual disclosure and independent consultant sign-off workflow.
references: IFC Performance Standards on Environmental and Social Sustainability 2012
IFC Environmental, Health, and Safety General Guidelines 2019

acronyms.def: Risk management framework adopted by financial institutions for determining, assessing, and managing environmental and social risk in project finance.
Legally binding annex to loan agreement specifying environmental and social mitigation measures and timelines.
Comprehensive assessment of a project's potential environmental and social impacts and proposed mitigations.
IFC PS7 requirement for consultation and consent with indigenous peoples before project activities affecting their rights.

dataLineage.source: Project information memorandum and ESIA documents
dataLineage.flow: Extract and categorise E&S impacts against IFC PS framework; auto-classify EP category
dataLineage.output: EP category assignment and PS compliance gap matrix

dataLineage.flow: Monitor completion status; flag overdue high-severity items and generate lender alerts
dataLineage.output: ESAP closure rate and covenant compliance dashboard

dataLineage.flow: Ingest semi-annual reports; update PS compliance scores and ESAP status
dataLineage.output: Compliance trend and EP10 annual disclosure package

/equator-principles.valueSummary: Enables project finance lenders and EPFIs to manage EP IV compliance workflows across complex multi-lender transactions, reduce reputational and covenant risk from ESAP non-compliance, and satisfy EP10 public reporting obligations with minimal administrative overhead.

/equitable-earth-methodologies.title: Equitable Earth Methodologies
/equitable-earth-methodologies.description: Implements justice-based climate transition frameworks incorporating indigenous rights, community free prior informed consent, gender equity, and distributive justice principles into ESG and climate analytics. Aligns with the Equitable Earth principles, UNDRIP, and emerging just transition taxonomies from ILO, OECD, and the LSE Grantham Institute. Provides scoring, documentation, and narrative analytics for impact-first investors and development finance institutions.

calculationEngine.formula: JTS = w_r × Rights + w_e × Equity + w_c × Community + w_g × Governance
calculationEngine.brief: Composite score across four justice dimensions each scored 0â€“100. Rights dimension covers FPIC documentation, indigenous land recognition, and legal remedy access. Equity dimension assesses distribution of transition costs and benefits across income quintiles. Community dimension tracks local employment, procurement, and benefit-sharing ratios. Governance dimension scores participatory decision-making processes.

dataPoints.name: FPIC Documentation Score (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Completeness of free, prior, and informed consent documentation; 100% required for projects affecting indigenous territories.

dataPoints.name: Local Employment Ratio (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of project jobs filled by local and affected community workers; key equity and community benefit metric.

dataPoints.name: Benefit-Sharing Rate (% of revenue)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of project revenue shared with host communities through direct payments, infrastructure, or equity stakes.

dataPoints.name: Gender Equity Index (0â€“100)
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite measuring women's participation in employment, leadership, and benefit-sharing within the transition project.

userInteraction: Activate the Equitable Earth methodology module and select applicable justice frameworks (UNDRIP, ILO, LSE).
Complete the project justice assessment questionnaire covering rights, equity, community, and governance dimensions.
Review JTS dashboard with pillar breakdown and compare against peer projects and sector benchmarks.
Generate just transition narrative report for DFI board approval, green/social bond framework, or investor ESG questionnaire.
references: UN Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples (UNDRIP) 2007
ILO Guidelines for a Just Transition 2015
LSE Grantham Institute Just Transition Finance Framework 2023
World Bank Environmental and Social Framework 2018
Climate Investment Funds Just Transition Methodology 2023

acronyms.def: Platform composite score measuring justice and equity dimensions of climate transition projects across four pillars.
UNDRIP and IFC PS7 requirement for genuine consultation and consent with indigenous and affected communities.

acronyms.full: UN Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples
acronyms.def: 2007 UN declaration establishing minimum rights standards for indigenous peoples; basis for FPIC requirements.
Bilateral or multilateral institution financing development projects; primary audience for just transition frameworks.

dataLineage.source: Project FPIC documentation and community consultation records
dataLineage.flow: Classify against UNDRIP article checklist; score completeness and authenticity
dataLineage.output: FPIC compliance score and documentation gap log

dataLineage.flow: Disaggregate by gender, community origin, and income quintile
dataLineage.output: Local employment ratio and gender equity index

dataLineage.flow: Map contractual commitments to Equitable Earth benefit-sharing tiers
dataLineage.output: Benefit-sharing rate and community revenue flow ($)

/equitable-earth-methodologies.valueSummary: Enables impact investors, DFIs, and project developers to demonstrate and document genuine justice-based transition practices, satisfy emerging just transition bond frameworks, and build social licence that reduces project delay and reputational risk.

/esg-factor-alpha.title: ESG Factor Alpha
/esg-factor-alpha.description: Quantifies and decomposes alpha generation from ESG factor exposures in equity portfolios using systematic factor modelling. Integrates ESG scores as systematic factors alongside traditional risk factors (market, size, value, momentum) in a multi-factor return attribution framework. Supports quantitative ESG strategy development, factor tilting, and portfolio construction for ESG-integrated equity funds.

calculationEngine.formula: r_i = α + β_mkt×MKT + β_esg×ESG + β_mom×MOM + β_val×VAL + ε_i
calculationEngine.brief: Panel regression of security excess returns on ESG factor and traditional systematic factors. ESG factor constructed as long-short portfolio ranked on provider consensus score within each GICS sector to neutralise sector bias. α (intercept) measures pure ESG alpha after controlling for known risk factors. Factor exposures (β) are estimated via rolling 36-month windows.

dataPoints.name: ESG Factor Loading (β_esg)
dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio sensitivity to the long-short ESG factor; positive β indicates ESG tilt contributing positively when ESG factor is in favour.

dataPoints.name: Annualised ESG Alpha (bps)
dataPoints.interpretation: Excess return attributable to ESG exposure after stripping traditional factor premia; statistically significant at 95% CI.

dataPoints.name: ESG Factor Information Ratio
dataPoints.interpretation: ESG factor return divided by tracking error of ESG factor portfolio; IR >0.3 indicates exploitable systematic signal.

dataPoints.name: ESG Factor Sharpe Ratio
dataPoints.interpretation: Risk-adjusted return of long-short ESG factor portfolio; benchmark against size, value, and momentum factor Sharpe ratios.

userInteraction: Load portfolio holdings and benchmark weights; connect to ESG score provider feed.
Construct sector-neutral long-short ESG factor and run panel regression against the selected factor model.
Review ESG factor loading, t-statistic, and rolling alpha chart to assess signal persistence and regime sensitivity.
Run factor tilt optimisation to maximise ESG factor exposure within tracking error and turnover constraints.
references: Fama & French â€” A Five-Factor Asset Pricing Model (2015)
Nagy et al. â€” Can ESG Add Alpha? MSCI Research 2016
Barra Global Equity Model GEM4 Handbook 2023
Friede et al. â€” ESG and Financial Performance: Aggregated Evidence (2015)

acronyms.def: Active return divided by tracking error; primary measure of alpha generation efficiency per unit of active risk.
Three non-financial dimensions used to assess corporate sustainability and ethical impact.
MSCI Barra's flagship multi-factor model covering 70+ countries; includes explicit ESG and controversy factors.
MSCI/S&P sector taxonomy; used for sector-neutralisation of ESG factor to remove sector bias from signal.

dataLineage.flow: Construct sector-neutral cross-sectional Z-score within each GICS sub-industry
dataLineage.output: Normalised ESG factor scores per security

dataLineage.flow: Align monthly excess returns with factor exposures; run rolling 36-month panel regression
dataLineage.output: ESG factor loading (β), t-stat, and annualised alpha by period

dataLineage.flow: Strip traditional factor contributions (mkt, size, val, mom, quality) from gross alpha
dataLineage.output: Pure ESG alpha after multi-factor adjustment

/esg-factor-alpha.valueSummary: Enables quantitative portfolio managers to build evidence-based ESG factor strategies, demonstrate that ESG integration generates statistically significant alpha in relevant market regimes, and satisfy client mandates requiring both ESG alignment and competitive risk-adjusted returns.

/esg-factor-attribution.title: ESG Factor Attribution
/esg-factor-attribution.description: Decomposes portfolio active returns into ESG factor contributions alongside traditional Brinson-Hood-Beebower and multi-factor attribution components. Identifies whether outperformance or underperformance is driven by ESG tilts, sector allocation effects, or security selection within ESG-screened universes. Supports performance reporting to ESG-mandated clients and strategy governance committees.

calculationEngine.formula: Active_Return = Allocation + Selection + ESG_Tilt + Interaction
calculationEngine.brief: Extends the classic three-effect BHB model with an explicit ESG tilt effect measuring the return contribution of over/under-weighting high vs. low ESG-scored securities within each sector. ESG tilt effect = Σ_s (w_ps − w_bs) × (ΔESG_s × β_esg). Residual interaction effect captures joint allocation-ESG signal.

dataPoints.name: ESG Tilt Contribution (bps)
dataPoints.interpretation: Return contribution attributable to the portfolio's ESG score tilt vs. benchmark; positive = ESG tilt added value.

dataPoints.name: Allocation Effect (bps)
dataPoints.interpretation: Return from over/under-weighting sectors vs. benchmark, independent of security selection within sectors.

dataPoints.name: Selection Effect (bps)
dataPoints.interpretation: Return from security selection within sectors; includes the ESG quality effect within sector weights.

dataPoints.name: ESG Factor R² (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of active return variance explained by the ESG factor; high R² validates ESG as primary active return driver.

userInteraction: Select portfolio, benchmark, and attribution period; load ESG scores and security weights.
Run BHB + ESG tilt attribution decomposition and review waterfall chart of return effects.
Identify sectors or periods where ESG tilt detracted from performance and review score dynamics.
Export client-ready attribution report in GIPS-compliant format with ESG tilt commentary narrative.
references: Brinson, Hood & Beebower â€” Determinants of Portfolio Performance (1986)
CFA Institute â€” ESG Attestation for Portfolio Managers 2021
GIPS 2020 Standards â€” Performance Attribution Guidance

acronyms.def: Classic three-effect performance attribution model decomposing active returns into allocation, selection, and interaction.
CFA Institute performance reporting standards; required for institutional asset manager performance presentations.
Annualised standard deviation of active returns vs. benchmark; primary measure of active risk budget utilisation.
Sector taxonomy used for BHB sector-level attribution groupings.

dataLineage.source: Portfolio management system (daily holdings and weights)
dataLineage.flow: Compute active weights vs. benchmark; align with ESG score time series at month-end
dataLineage.output: Active weight and ESG score differential by security and sector

dataLineage.flow: Normalise scores cross-sectionally; compute score-weighted sector averages
dataLineage.output: Sector ESG score differential (portfolio vs. benchmark)

dataLineage.flow: Apply BHB decomposition with ESG tilt extension; sum to portfolio level
dataLineage.output: Attribution waterfall: allocation, selection, ESG tilt, interaction (bps)

/esg-factor-attribution.valueSummary: Provides portfolio managers and client-facing teams with transparent, defensible attribution showing precisely how ESG integration has contributed to or detracted from performance, supporting client retention, regulatory disclosure, and strategy refinement.

/esg-momentum-scanner.title: ESG Momentum Scanner
/esg-momentum-scanner.description: Detects ESG rating upgrade and downgrade momentum signals across equity and credit universes by analysing rating trajectory, revision frequency, and leading indicator dynamics. Combines provider rating change histories with underlying KPI trend data to generate early-warning signals ahead of formal rating revisions. Supports alpha generation, risk monitoring, and engagement prioritisation for active ESG investors.

calculationEngine.formula: ESG_Mom = Σ(w_p × ΔScore_p) / σ_hist
calculationEngine.brief: Computes a provider-weighted ESG score change velocity normalised by historical score volatility. Positive signals (>+1σ) indicate upgrade momentum; negative (<−1σ) indicate downgrade risk. Leading indicators â€” CDP disclosure score changes, controversy frequency, board composition shifts â€” are incorporated as forward-looking adjustment factors applied to trailing momentum signal.

dataPoints.name: ESG Momentum Z-Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Standardised momentum signal; |Z|>1.5 constitutes actionable signal for engagement or portfolio adjustment.

dataPoints.name: Rating Change Frequency (events/year)
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of formal ESG rating revisions in trailing 12 months; high frequency issuers have less signal value due to noise.

dataPoints.name: Leading Indicator Composite Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Forward-looking adjustment factor based on CDP disclosure quality change, controversy events, and governance changes.

dataPoints.name: Signal Accuracy Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Historical accuracy of momentum Z>1.5 signals in predicting formal rating upgrade within 12 months; benchmark for signal efficacy.

userInteraction: Set universe (equity index, credit benchmark, or custom watchlist) and connect ESG rating provider feeds.
Configure momentum window (3/6/12 months), signal threshold (σ level), and leading indicator weights.
Review momentum scanner output sorted by signal strength; filter by sector, region, or current rating tier.
Set alert thresholds for real-time monitoring and integrate upgrade/downgrade signals into portfolio review workflow.
references: Verheyden et al. â€” ESG for All? The Impact of ESG Screening on Return, Risk, and Diversification (2016)
Friede et al. â€” ESG and Financial Performance Meta-Analysis 2015

acronyms.def: Non-profit running the global environmental disclosure system; CDP score changes are leading ESG momentum indicators.
Underlying ESG metric (e.g., LTIR, water intensity, board diversity) driving rating agency score changes.
Workforce safety KPI frequently flagged as material leading indicator for E or S rating movements.
Proxy advisory and ESG rating firm; ISS QualityScore changes used as governance momentum signal.

dataLineage.flow: Compute rolling Δscore at 3, 6, 12-month windows; normalise by historical score volatility
dataLineage.output: ESG momentum Z-score per issuer and window

dataLineage.flow: Detect year-on-year disclosure quality improvement or decline
dataLineage.output: Leading indicator adjustment factor (+/− 0â€“20 points)

dataLineage.flow: Flag ESG controversy peaks preceding rating downgrades; train logistic signal model
dataLineage.output: Controversy-informed downgrade probability (%)

/esg-momentum-scanner.valueSummary: Enables active ESG investors to systematically identify rating inflection points before the market prices them in, generating alpha from ESG momentum while simultaneously improving engagement prioritisation and portfolio risk management.

/esg-narrative-intelligence.title: ESG Narrative Intelligence
/esg-narrative-intelligence.description: Applies NLP and large language model analysis to corporate ESG narratives in earnings call transcripts, annual reports, sustainability reports, and regulatory filings to extract quantitative ESG signals. Detects greenwashing language patterns, commitment specificity, sentiment trajectory, and KPI disclosure density. Supports fundamental ESG research, controversy monitoring, and regulatory materiality assessment.

calculationEngine.formula: NQS = w_s × Specificity + w_c × Consistency + w_q × QuantDensity + w_t × Sentiment
calculationEngine.brief: Composite scoring model applied to parsed text. Specificity measures percentage of ESG commitments with quantified targets and timelines vs. vague aspirational language.
