Consistency tracks alignment between current and prior period ESG claims (contradiction detection). Quantitative KPI Density counts disclosed numeric ESG metrics per 1,000 words. Sentiment is scored on −1 to +1 scale using a finance-domain ESG sentiment lexicon.

dataPoints.name: Narrative Specificity Score (0â€“100)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of ESG commitments with concrete targets, timelines, and accountability owners; below 40 flags greenwashing risk.

dataPoints.name: ESG KPI Density (metrics/1k words)
dataPoints.interpretation: Quantitative ESG disclosures per 1,000 words of sustainability text; above 8 indicates disclosure-rich reporting.

dataPoints.name: Sentiment Trajectory (quarter-on-quarter)
dataPoints.interpretation: Direction of ESG sentiment change across 8 quarters; sustained decline may precede rating downgrade.

dataPoints.name: Greenwash Risk Flag (1â€“5)
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite greenwashing risk rating; Level 4â€“5 = high specificity-sentiment divergence (positive language, low targets).

userInteraction: Load corporate filings (PDF, HTML, or transcript) or connect to EDGAR/Refinitiv document feed.
Run NLP pipeline to extract ESG mentions, commitments, KPIs, and sentiment by pillar and topic.
Review narrative quality scorecard and greenwashing risk flags; drill into specific text passages triggering high-risk signals.
Export ESG narrative intelligence report for research note, engagement letter, or regulatory filing review.
references: GRI Universal Standards 2021 â€” Disclosure Quality Principles
Loughran & McDonald â€” Textual Analysis in Accounting and Finance (2016)
InfluenceMap â€” Corporate Climate Policy Engagement Tracker 2024

acronyms.def: AI technique for automated analysis and interpretation of human language; applied here to ESG document mining.
Neural language model (e.g., GPT-class) used for ESG narrative extraction, summarisation, and commitment classification.
Platform composite measure of ESG narrative specificity, consistency, quantitative density, and sentiment.

acronyms.full: Electronic Data Gathering, Analysis, and Retrieval
acronyms.def: SEC public filing database; primary source of 10-K, 10-Q, and proxy statement ESG narratives for US issuers.

dataLineage.source: Corporate sustainability reports and annual reports (PDF/HTML)
dataLineage.flow: OCR and section extraction; classify by GRI/ESRS topic; parse ESG commitment statements
dataLineage.output: Commitment inventory with specificity tag and target year

dataLineage.flow: Sentence-level ESG topic classification; compute sentiment per pillar per quarter
dataLineage.output: Sentiment time series by E/S/G pillar

dataLineage.flow: Compare current vs. prior commitments; flag contradictions and retractions
dataLineage.output: Consistency score and contradiction log

/esg-narrative-intelligence.valueSummary: Transforms qualitative ESG narratives into systematic, comparable signals that complement quantitative rating data â€” enabling analysts to identify greenwashing risks, track commitment follow-through, and detect early-warning signals of ESG quality deterioration or improvement.

/esg-portfolio-optimizer.title: ESG Portfolio Optimizer
/esg-portfolio-optimizer.description: Constructs optimised equity portfolios maximising expected return per unit of risk subject to ESG score constraints, carbon budget limits, and climate tilt requirements using mean-variance optimisation. Integrates ESG scores, carbon intensity data, and factor model covariance matrices to generate efficient frontiers with and without ESG constraints. Supports passive ESG index replication, active ESG tilting, and transition pathway portfolio construction.

calculationEngine.formula: max wᵀμ − (λ/2)wᵀΣw, s.t. wᵀ×ESG ≥ ESG_min, wᵀ×CI ≤ CI_max, Σw_i = 1
calculationEngine.brief: Solves quadratic programme maximising utility (return minus risk penalty) subject to minimum portfolio-weighted ESG score, maximum portfolio carbon intensity, sector deviation constraints, and full-investment constraint. The ESG-constrained efficient frontier is derived by parametrically varying λ from 0 to ∞, quantifying the return cost of ESG constraints.

dataPoints.name: Portfolio ESG Score (weighted avg)
dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio-level ESG score computed as holdings-weighted average; minimum constraint typically set at index + 10 points.

dataPoints.name: Carbon Intensity (tCO2e/$M Rev)
dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio-weighted carbon intensity; EU PAB requires minimum 50% reduction vs. parent index at launch.

dataPoints.name: ESG Constraint Cost (bps p.a.)
dataPoints.interpretation: Reduction in expected return from imposing ESG/carbon constraints; typically 5â€“30 bps for broad equity universes.

dataPoints.name: Active Share (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of portfolio differing from benchmark; key metric for ESG active portfolio differentiation.

userInteraction: Load benchmark universe, return forecasts, and factor covariance matrix; connect ESG score and carbon intensity feeds.
Set ESG constraints (minimum score, carbon intensity ceiling, exclusion list) and risk aversion parameter λ.
Review ESG-constrained efficient frontier vs. unconstrained frontier to quantify ESG constraint cost.
Run portfolio rebalancing optimisation with turnover and liquidity constraints; export trade list.
references: EU Delegated Regulation 2020/1818 â€” Paris-Aligned and Climate Transition Benchmarks
Cremers & Petajisto â€” How Active is Your Fund Manager? (2009)

acronyms.def: Markowitz framework maximising expected portfolio return for a given level of variance; foundational portfolio construction method.
EU regulatory benchmark requiring 50% carbon intensity reduction vs. parent index and 7% annual decarbonisation.
Portfolio-weighted carbon emissions per unit of revenue; primary climate constraint metric in ESG optimisation.
Annualised standard deviation of active returns; used as secondary constraint in ESG optimisation alongside ESG score.

dataLineage.flow: Update monthly; validate factor structure stability across ESG-constrained subsets
dataLineage.output: Asset covariance matrix Σ for optimiser input

dataLineage.source: Return forecasts (alpha signals from quant models)
dataLineage.flow: Align signal universe with ESG score coverage; apply shrinkage to mitigate estimation error
dataLineage.output: Expected return vector μ per security

dataLineage.flow: Normalise and align with portfolio universe; construct constraint coefficient vectors
dataLineage.output: ESG score and CI constraint rows for QP solver

/esg-portfolio-optimizer.valueSummary: Delivers institutional-grade ESG portfolio construction combining rigorous quantitative optimisation with integrated ESG and climate constraints, enabling fund managers to launch Paris-aligned products, rebalance existing mandates, and demonstrate systematic ESG integration to clients and regulators.

/esg-ratings-comparator.title: ESG Ratings Comparator
/esg-ratings-comparator.description: Enables systematic cross-provider ESG rating comparison across MSCI, Sustainalytics, ISS, Bloomberg, and Refinitiv for individual issuers and portfolio universes. Quantifies inter-provider rating disagreement, identifies divergence drivers, and produces consensus scores with confidence intervals. Informs provider selection, due diligence processes, and client disclosure on data sources used for ESG integration.

calculationEngine.formula: DI = σ(Score_1, Score_2, ..., Score_N) / μ(Score_1, ..., Score_N)
standards: Berg et al. â€” Aggregate Confusion 2022
FCA ESG Data and Ratings Code 2023
calculationEngine.brief: Coefficient of variation across normalised provider scores measures rating disagreement. High DI (>0.25) indicates material provider disagreement requiring analyst review rather than mechanical reliance on any single provider. Berg et al. attribute divergence to scope, measurement, and weight differences; the comparator diagnoses the dominant divergence source for each issuer.

dataPoints.name: Consensus ESG Score (0â€“100)
dataPoints.interpretation: Equal-weighted average of all available provider scores on common 0â€“100 scale; primary summary metric.

dataPoints.name: Divergence Index (CoV)
dataPoints.interpretation: Coefficient of variation across providers; above 0.25 indicates high disagreement warranting manual analyst override.

dataPoints.name: Score Range (max−min)
dataPoints.interpretation: Difference between highest and lowest provider score for issuer; context metric for rating disagreement magnitude.

dataPoints.name: Provider Coverage Count (1â€“5)
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of providers rating the issuer; partial coverage is itself a data quality risk signal, especially for EM issuers.

userInteraction: Search for issuer by name or ISIN and load all available provider ESG ratings.
Review consensus score, divergence index, and pillar-level heatmap showing agreement and disagreement by E, S, G dimension.
Drill into divergence diagnosis: scope differences, measurement approach, or weighting â€” using the Berg et al. framework.
Export provider comparison matrix for investment committee ESG data source disclosure or manager selection due diligence.
references: Berg, Koelbel & Rigobon â€” Aggregate Confusion: The Divergence of ESG Ratings (2022)
ESMA Consultation on ESG Ratings Regulation 2023
FCA ESG Data and Ratings Code of Conduct 2023
Chatterji et al. â€” Do Ratings of Firms Converge? (2016)
IOSCO Report on ESG Ratings and Data Providers 2021

acronyms.def: Standard deviation divided by mean; used as the normalised inter-provider divergence measure.
EU financial markets regulator proposing mandatory authorisation and conduct rules for ESG rating providers.
UK financial regulator; published voluntary ESG data and ratings code of conduct in 2023.
US-headquartered proxy advisory and ESG rating firm; ISS ESG is a major provider used in institutional due diligence.

dataLineage.source: MSCI, Sustainalytics, ISS, Bloomberg, Refinitiv ESG rating feeds
dataLineage.flow: Normalise all provider scores to common 0â€“100 scale using linear mapping; align on common ISIN universe
dataLineage.output: Provider-normalised score matrix per issuer

dataLineage.flow: Decompose overall divergence into E, S, G pillar contributions using variance decomposition
dataLineage.output: Divergence diagnosis by pillar (scope vs. measurement vs. weight)

dataLineage.source: Coverage universe metadata (provider, date, rating methodology version)
dataLineage.flow: Flag stale ratings >12 months; calculate coverage completeness by region and sector
dataLineage.output: Coverage quality score and staleness flags per issuer

/esg-ratings-comparator.valueSummary: Provides investors with an objective, multi-provider lens on ESG quality that reduces single-provider dependency, improves rating confidence for high-divergence issuers, and satisfies regulatory requirements to disclose ESG data sources and methodological limitations.

/esg-ratings-hub.title: ESG Ratings Hub
/esg-ratings-hub.description: Centralised management platform for ESG rating data across all asset classes, maintaining full rating history, peer comparisons, and provider change logs. Provides workflow tools for internal ESG rating committees, override documentation, and rating committee pack generation. Integrates with portfolio management systems, risk engines, and client reporting platforms as the authoritative ESG data master record.

calculationEngine.formula: ICR = Σ(w_p × Provider_p) + Δ_analyst
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted blend of external provider scores augmented by an analyst overlay (Δ_analyst) captured through the structured override workflow. Override rationale, evidence, and reviewer sign-off are stored in the audit trail. ICR is the master score disseminated to portfolio management, risk, and reporting systems. Score history is maintained at daily granularity with point-in-time reconstruction capability.

dataPoints.name: Internal Composite Rating (0â€“100)
dataPoints.interpretation: Master ESG score after provider blend and approved analyst overlay; sole authoritative source for downstream systems.

dataPoints.name: Rating Drift (12m change)
dataPoints.interpretation: Year-on-year change in ICR; large positive or negative drift triggers rating committee review obligation.

dataPoints.name: Override Frequency (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of ratings with analyst overlay active; excessive override rate may indicate provider data quality issues.

dataPoints.name: Peer Percentile Rank (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Issuer ICR rank within GICS sub-industry peer group; primary relative comparison metric for client reporting.

userInteraction: Onboard new issuer by ISIN/LEI; system automatically loads all available provider ratings and computes initial ICR.
Review rating committee queue for issuers triggering auto-review (>10 point drift, coverage change, controversy event).
Submit analyst overlay with documented rationale and evidence; route for committee approval via built-in workflow.
Publish approved ratings to downstream systems (portfolio management, risk, reporting) via certified API endpoint.
references: ESMA Regulation on ESG Rating Activities 2024
PRI Reporting Framework â€” Strategy & Governance Module 2023
FCA ESG Data and Ratings Code of Conduct 2023
ISO 14097 â€” Finance and Investment Climate Change Framework 2021

acronyms.def: Proprietary ESG rating blending external providers with approved analyst overrides; master record for downstream use.
ISO 17442 code used as primary entity key in the ratings hub to prevent duplicate records across data sources.
Reconstruction of the rating that was live on any historical date; required for backtesting and regulatory audit.
UN-supported investor initiative; PRI reporting requires disclosure of ESG data sources and governance processes.

dataLineage.source: External ESG provider feeds (MSCI, Sustainalytics, ISS, Bloomberg, Refinitiv)
dataLineage.flow: Daily ingest; normalise, timestamp, and store in immutable rating history table with source audit trail
dataLineage.output: Provider score history per issuer with PIT reconstruction capability

dataLineage.source: Analyst override submissions (rationale text, evidence attachments)
dataLineage.flow: Store in structured override log with reviewer, approval date, and expiry; compute ICR post-overlay
dataLineage.output: ICR with overlay audit trail and committee approval record

dataLineage.flow: Push ICR and peer percentile to PM system via certified API; flag covenant breaches (ICR below mandate minimum)
dataLineage.output: Daily ICR feed to downstream systems with change alerts

/esg-ratings-hub.valueSummary: Serves as the single source of ESG rating truth for the entire investment organisation, ensuring consistent data governance, full audit trails for regulatory inspection, and efficient rating committee workflows that scale across large issuer universes.

/esg-report-parser.title: ESG Report Parser
/esg-report-parser.description: AI-powered extraction engine that automatically parses PDF and HTML sustainability reports, integrated annual reports, and CDP questionnaire responses to extract structured ESG KPIs, targets, and commitments. Uses LLM-based document intelligence to handle inconsistent report formats, multiple languages, and non-tabular disclosures. Outputs structured data to the ESG Ratings Hub and entity 360 profiles.

calculationEngine.formula: ECS = P(correct extraction) × (1 − Ambiguity) × SourceQuality
calculationEngine.brief: Bayesian extraction confidence combining model probability of correct parsing, ambiguity penalty from multi-value or contradictory disclosures, and source quality factor (audited > unaudited > estimated). ECS >80% qualifies for auto-ingestion; ECS 50â€“80% routes to analyst review queue; ECS <50% triggers manual extraction flag.

dataPoints.name: KPI Extraction Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of target ESG KPIs successfully extracted from document; benchmark: 70%+ for GRI-indexed reports.

dataPoints.name: Extraction Confidence Score (0â€“100)
dataPoints.interpretation: Weighted average ECS across all extracted values; low scores indicate complex or non-standard disclosure formats.

dataPoints.name: Multi-Value Conflict Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of KPIs with conflicting values across report sections; high rate indicates disclosure inconsistency or restatement.

dataPoints.name: Assurance Level Flag
dataPoints.interpretation: Highest level of independent assurance applied to disclosures (None/Limited/Reasonable); affects downstream data quality score.

userInteraction: Upload sustainability report PDF or provide URL; select target KPI schema (GRI, SASB, TCFD, ESRS, or custom).
Review extraction results with confidence scores; approve auto-ingested items and assign analyst review for low-confidence extractions.
Resolve multi-value conflicts by selecting authoritative disclosure and documenting rationale.
Publish extracted KPIs to ESG Ratings Hub and entity profiles; schedule annual re-parse for the next reporting cycle.
references: GRI Universal Standards 2021 â€” Reporting Principles and Disclosures
TCFD Guidance on Metrics and Targets 2021
CDP Technical Note on Data Quality 2024

acronyms.def: AI neural network (e.g., GPT-4 class) used for reading comprehension and structured data extraction from sustainability reports.
Platform quality metric combining model certainty, ambiguity, and source reliability for each extracted ESG value.
Technology converting scanned PDF images to machine-readable text; pre-processing step before LLM extraction.
EFRAG-developed standards mandating ESG disclosure under the EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive.

dataLineage.source: Corporate sustainability report PDFs and HTML pages
dataLineage.flow: OCR scanned pages; split by section using heading detection; run LLM extraction with GRI/SASB KPI schema
dataLineage.output: Structured KPI table with value, unit, year, page reference, and ECS

dataLineage.flow: Parse standard CDP response format; map module answers to GHG Protocol and TCFD disclosure taxonomy
dataLineage.output: CDP-sourced KPIs with assurance flag and disclosure quality indicator

dataLineage.flow: Extract climate risk disclosures from 10-K Item 1C; identify quantified vs. qualitative disclosures
dataLineage.output: TCFD-mapped disclosures with narrative vs. quantitative classification

/esg-report-parser.valueSummary: Scales ESG data collection from hundreds to thousands of companies without proportional analyst headcount growth, dramatically reducing time-to-insight for ESG research teams while maintaining a documented, reproducible extraction audit trail for regulatory and client audit purposes.

/esg-time-series-forecaster.title: ESG Time Series Forecaster
/esg-time-series-forecaster.description: Applies machine learning and statistical time series models to forecast ESG KPI trajectories at company, sector, and portfolio levels over 1â€“10 year horizons. Combines autoregressive, gradient boosting, and neural network models with macro-climate scenario inputs to generate probabilistic ESG forecast distributions. Supports target-setting, portfolio decarbonisation pathway management, and regulatory forward-looking disclosure requirements.

calculationEngine.formula: KPI_t = f(KPI_{t-1}, ..., KPI_{t-p}, X_macro, ε_t)
calculationEngine.brief: Ensemble model combining an ARIMA baseline with an XGBoost model incorporating macro-climate features (carbon prices, energy prices, regulatory dummies, sector capacity factors). Forecast confidence intervals are generated via quantile regression forests at the 10th, 50th, and 90th percentiles. Model selection uses rolling-origin cross-validation minimising RMSE over the 3-year holdout window.

dataPoints.name: Forecast Accuracy (MAPE %)
dataPoints.interpretation: Mean absolute percentage error on holdout set; below 15% MAPE indicates reliable 3-year forecast horizon.

dataPoints.name: KPI Trend (annual % change)
dataPoints.interpretation: Expected year-on-year KPI change under base scenario; negative trend for carbon intensity indicates decarbonisation progress.

dataPoints.name: Target Attainment Probability (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Probability that forecast trajectory meets committed ESG target by target year; below 50% triggers target review flag.

dataPoints.name: Forecast Uncertainty Band (P10–P90)
dataPoints.interpretation: Interquartile range of KPI forecast at each horizon year; wide bands indicate high uncertainty requiring scenario planning.

userInteraction: Select entity or portfolio, target KPI (e.g., Scope 1 intensity, water withdrawal, LTIR), and forecast horizon.
Choose macro scenario (NGFS NZE, Current Policies, or custom) to condition sector-level macro features.
Review point forecast, confidence bands, and target attainment probability in interactive timeline chart.
Export forecast tables for TCFD forward-looking metrics disclosure, SBTi target monitoring, or investor update.
references: Hyndman & Athanasopoulos â€” Forecasting: Principles and Practice (3rd Ed. 2021)
Taylor & Letham â€” Forecasting at Scale (Prophet Model) 2018
NGFS Scenario Narrative and Data Release 2023
SBTi Monitoring, Reporting and Verification Framework 2024

acronyms.def: Average absolute percentage deviation of forecast from actuals; primary accuracy metric for ESG KPI forecasting models.
Statistical time series model decomposing KPI into trend, seasonality, and autoregressive components.
Square root of mean squared forecast errors; used for model selection in rolling-origin cross-validation.
Corporate target-setting framework requiring 1.5°C-aligned decarbonisation trajectories; primary use case for ESG forecasting.

dataLineage.source: Company ESG KPI history (CDP, Bloomberg, Refinitiv)
dataLineage.flow: Clean and align annual time series; impute missing values using sector median interpolation
dataLineage.output: ESG KPI time series at company level (5â€“10 year history)

dataLineage.source: NGFS macro-climate scenario outputs (carbon price, energy price, GDP)
dataLineage.flow: Align scenario variables to company sector and geography; create forecast feature matrix
dataLineage.output: Macro-conditioned feature set for XGBoost model

dataLineage.source: SBTi target registry and internal target commitments
dataLineage.flow: Map target year and absolute/intensity metric to forecast output; compute attainment probability
dataLineage.output: Target attainment probability and gap-to-target under each scenario

/esg-time-series-forecaster.valueSummary: Enables ESG analysts and portfolio managers to move beyond backward-looking ESG data to forward-looking trajectory management, supporting target-setting credibility assessments, proactive engagement with laggard issuers, and TCFD/IFRS S2 forward-looking metrics requirements.

/esg-value-chain.title: ESG Value Chain
/esg-value-chain.description: Maps and analyses ESG performance across the full corporate value chain including upstream suppliers, direct operations, and downstream customers and end-of-life. Integrates supply chain mapping, Scope 3 emissions by category, supplier ESG assessments, and customer product impact data. Supports GHG Protocol Scope 3 Category 1 and 11 reporting, CSDDD due diligence obligations, and extended producer responsibility analytics.

calculationEngine.formula: VCE = w_up × Upstream_ESG + w_ops × Operations_ESG + w_dn × Downstream_ESG
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted aggregation of ESG performance scores across three value chain stages. Upstream score covers Tier 1 and Tier 2 supplier ESG assessments weighted by spend share. Operations score uses direct company KPIs. Downstream score aggregates use-phase emissions (Cat.11), end-of-life treatment (Cat.12), and downstream franchisee/investment impacts. Weights reflect relative emission and risk materiality by sector.

dataPoints.name: Scope 3 Cat.1 Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of purchased goods and services spend with primary supplier emission data; below 40% requires sector average proxies.

dataPoints.name: Supplier ESG Assessment Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of Tier 1 suppliers with completed ESG assessment; CSDDD mandates risk-based due diligence across supply chain.

dataPoints.name: Value Chain Scope 3 (tCO2e)
dataPoints.interpretation: Total Scope 3 emissions across all 15 categories; typically 70â€“90% of total corporate GHG footprint in consumer sectors.

dataPoints.name: Supplier ESG Score (0â€“100)
dataPoints.interpretation: Aggregated ESG score of rated Tier 1 suppliers weighted by procurement spend; tracks supply chain ESG improvement over time.

userInteraction: Import supply chain map with Tier 1 and key Tier 2 suppliers and annual spend data by category.
Run Scope 3 Category 1 calculation using supplier primary data or spend-based proxies; review coverage gaps.
Distribute supplier ESG assessment questionnaire and track completion rate; escalate non-respondents.
Generate CSDDD adverse impact report, Scope 3 inventory, and value chain ESG scorecard for annual sustainability report.
references: GHG Protocol Corporate Value Chain (Scope 3) Accounting and Reporting Standard 2011
EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) 2024
CDP Supply Chain Programme Technical Note 2024

acronyms.def: EU directive requiring large companies to conduct ESG due diligence across their global value chains.
GHG Protocol Scope 3 category covering purchased goods and services; largest Scope 3 category for most manufacturers.
GHG Protocol Scope 3 category covering use of sold products; dominant category for energy, automotive, and technology sectors.
Regulatory framework requiring producers to fund collection and recycling of products at end-of-life.

dataLineage.flow: Extract Tier 1 and Tier 2 supplier spend by UNSPSC category and country of origin
dataLineage.output: Supplier spend map with category and geography for Scope 3 Cat.1 input

dataLineage.flow: Score against framework (EcoVadis/Sedex/custom); weight by procurement spend
dataLineage.output: Spend-weighted supplier ESG score and assessment coverage rate

dataLineage.source: Product use and end-of-life data (engineering models)
dataLineage.flow: Apply GHG Protocol Cat.11/12 methodology; use product energy ratings and lifetime assumptions
dataLineage.output: Use-phase and end-of-life Scope 3 emissions by product line (tCO2e)

/esg-value-chain.valueSummary: Provides corporate sustainability teams and procurement functions with a systematic framework for mapping, measuring, and improving ESG performance across the entire value chain, enabling credible Scope 3 reporting, CSDDD compliance, and supplier engagement programmes.

/esms.title: ESMS Platform
/esms.description: Environmental and Social Management System platform supporting IFC Performance Standards compliance for project finance lenders, development finance institutions, and project sponsors. Provides a structured workflow for E&S policy development, impact screening, ESAP management, monitoring report intake, and grievance mechanism tracking. Aligns with IFC PS1â€“PS8, World Bank ESF, and ADB SPS requirements.

calculationEngine.formula: ESCS = (PS_Compliant + 0.5 × PS_Minor) / PS_Total × 100
calculationEngine.brief: Scores each applicable IFC Performance Standard sub-requirement as Compliant, Minor Non-Conformance, or Major Non-Conformance. ESCS is the weighted completion ratio where Minor NC carries 50% weight and Major NC carries 0%. ESCS below 80% triggers mandatory corrective action plan before disbursement. Separate sub-scores are reported for each of PS1â€“PS8.

dataPoints.name: ESCS Overall (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Weighted compliance score across all applicable PS sub-requirements; EP Category A projects require 90%+ at first review.

dataPoints.name: Major NC Count
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of unresolved Major Non-Conformances; any Major NC blocks disbursement per EP lender covenant.

dataPoints.name: Grievance Mechanism Response Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of community grievances receiving documented response within SLA (typically 30 days); IFC PS1 mandatory.

dataPoints.name: ESAP Completion Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of Environmental and Social Action Plan items completed vs. schedule; covenant trigger if <80% at milestone.

userInteraction: Create project record and assign applicable IFC Performance Standards based on sector, geography, and impact category.
Complete PS compliance matrix from ESIA findings and assign Compliant/Minor/Major status for each sub-requirement.
Generate ESAP with corrective actions, owners, and milestones; track completion through construction and operations phases.
Intake semi-annual independent monitoring reports; update compliance scores and trigger covenant breach alerts to lending syndicate.
references: IFC Performance Standards on Environmental and Social Sustainability 2012
World Bank Environmental and Social Framework 2018
IFC Environmental, Health, and Safety General Guidelines 2019

acronyms.def: Structured system for identifying, assessing, and managing E&S risks throughout a project's lifecycle.
Eight IFC standards covering E&S risk management, labour, resource efficiency, community health, and indigenous peoples.
World Bank policy framework for environmental and social risk management in Bank-financed projects.
Deviation from IFC PS requirement; Major NC blocks disbursement; Minor NC requires corrective action plan.

dataLineage.flow: Parse PS sub-requirements; classify compliance status from ESIA findings; auto-populate compliance matrix
dataLineage.output: PS compliance matrix with Compliant/Minor/Major ratings and evidence references

dataLineage.flow: Ingest structured monitoring data; update ESAP closure status and PS compliance scores
dataLineage.output: Updated ESCS, ESAP completion rate, and NC trend

dataLineage.source: Grievance register (community complaints and company responses)
dataLineage.flow: Track grievance intake, categorisation, response, and resolution; compute SLA compliance rate
dataLineage.output: Grievance resolution rate, average response time, and open grievance summary by PS category

/esms.valueSummary: Enables project finance lenders and sponsors to systematically manage IFC PS compliance obligations across project lifecycles, reduce disbursement risk from ESAP non-compliance, and demonstrate robust E&S governance to co-lenders, development finance partners, and ESG-mandated investors.

/et-commodity-risk.title: Energy Transition Commodity Risk
/et-commodity-risk.description: Analyses price risk and supply security risk for critical minerals and energy commodities essential to the low-carbon transition, including lithium, cobalt, copper, nickel, rare earths, and green hydrogen. Integrates supply concentration metrics, demand projection models, and commodity price scenario analysis under IEA NZE and Stated Policies pathways. Supports portfolio stress testing, supply chain due diligence, and transition risk financial modelling.

calculationEngine.formula: CTR = w_c × Concentration + w_d × DemandPressure + w_p × PriceVolatility + w_s × SubstitutionDifficulty
standards: World Bank Minerals for Climate Action 2023
calculationEngine.brief: Composite risk score for each critical mineral aggregating four dimensions. Concentration uses HHI of production and refining by country. Demand pressure projects demand-supply gap under NZE vs. STEPS scenario. Price volatility uses 3-year historical realised volatility of commodity spot price. Substitution difficulty reflects material science assessment of viable alternatives.

dataPoints.name: Supply Concentration (HHI)
dataPoints.interpretation: Herfindahl index of mine production by country; cobalt (DRC >70%) and rare earths (China >60%) score near 10,000.

dataPoints.name: Demand-Supply Gap (% by 2030)
dataPoints.interpretation: Projected percentage demand exceeding supply under NZE scenario by 2030; positive gap flags supply security risk.

dataPoints.name: Price Volatility (σ annualised %)
dataPoints.interpretation: Annualised realised price volatility; lithium carbonate 2022 volatility exceeded 80%, creating severe input cost risk.

dataPoints.name: Portfolio Critical Mineral Exposure ($M)
dataPoints.interpretation: Financial exposure to critical mineral price risk in portfolio companies' input cost structures; primary stress test metric.

userInteraction: Select transition scenario (NZE/APS/STEPS) and time horizon (2025/2030/2040) to calibrate demand projections.
Review commodity-level CTR scores and supply-demand gap charts; identify highest-risk minerals for portfolio holdings.
Run price stress scenario (e.g., lithium +200%) and propagate through portfolio company input cost models.
Generate critical mineral supply chain due diligence report and export to investment committee or lender risk pack.
references: World Bank Minerals for Climate Action 2023

acronyms.def: Concentration measure used for supply source diversity; high HHI indicates monopolised production posing supply security risk.
IEA scenario requiring fastest critical mineral demand growth; primary scenario for transition commodity demand stress.
Primary exchange for base metal futures; source of copper, nickel, lithium, and cobalt price data.
Produces >70% of global cobalt; dominant supply concentration risk for battery supply chains.

dataLineage.source: USGS and IEA production data by mine and country
dataLineage.flow: Compute HHI by mineral and production stage (mining vs. refining vs. processing); identify bottleneck stages
dataLineage.output: Supply concentration score by mineral and processing stage

dataLineage.source: IEA NZE and STEPS demand projections by technology
dataLineage.flow: Sum mineral requirements across solar, wind, EV, grid storage, and hydrogen technologies; compare to production forecasts
dataLineage.output: Demand-supply gap by scenario and mineral (2025â€“2040)

dataLineage.flow: Compute realised volatility at 1-year and 3-year windows; calibrate price stress scenarios for input cost modelling
dataLineage.output: Price volatility surface and stress scenarios by commodity

/et-commodity-risk.valueSummary: Equips portfolio managers and risk teams with a systematic framework for quantifying critical mineral supply risk, running transition commodity stress tests, and engaging with investee companies on supply chain resilience strategies essential for the energy transition.

/etl-pipeline.title: ETL Pipeline Manager
/etl-pipeline.description: Manages the configuration, scheduling, monitoring, and quality assurance of ESG data ETL pipelines ingesting data from external providers, regulatory databases, company filings, and internal systems. Provides a visual pipeline builder, run history, data quality monitoring dashboards, and automated alerting for pipeline failures or data anomalies. Supports lineage tracking and audit trails required for GDPR, BCBS 239, and ESMA data governance compliance.

calculationEngine.formula: DQS = w_c × Completeness + w_a × Accuracy + w_t × Timeliness + w_u × Uniqueness
calculationEngine.brief: Composite data quality score computed per pipeline run across four DAMA dimensions. Completeness measures non-null field rates vs. expected schema. Accuracy cross-validates values against reference sources or prior period ranges (outlier detection). Timeliness scores delivery against SLA windows. Uniqueness detects duplicate records. Pipeline DQS below 85% triggers automatic quarantine of the run output pending analyst review.

dataPoints.name: Pipeline Run DQS (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Overall data quality score for a pipeline run; below 85% triggers quarantine and analyst review before downstream propagation.

dataPoints.name: Record Completeness (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of mandatory fields with non-null values; 100% required for financial-grade ESG data ingestion.

dataPoints.name: Timeliness SLA Adherence (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of scheduled pipeline runs completing within the contracted SLA window; critical for market-hours data feeds.

dataPoints.name: Lineage Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of downstream ESG metrics with documented lineage to source system, transformation, and load step.

userInteraction: Configure new pipeline by selecting source connector, transformation rules, target schema, and quality thresholds.
Set schedule (real-time, daily, weekly, or event-triggered) and configure alerting channels for failure and DQS breach.
Monitor pipeline run dashboard for current status, last-run DQS, record counts, and SLA performance.
Review data lineage graph for any ESG metric to trace from target field back to source system field via all transformations.
references: DAMA International â€” DMBOK2 Data Management Body of Knowledge 2017
BCBS 239 â€” Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Reporting 2013
EU GDPR Article 5 â€” Data Quality Principles

acronyms.def: Data integration process extracting data from sources, applying transformations, and loading into target systems.
Platform composite metric assessing ESG data completeness, accuracy, timeliness, and uniqueness per pipeline run.

acronyms.full: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision Principle 239
acronyms.def: Regulatory principles for risk data aggregation; requires full data lineage and quality monitoring for banks.
Professional body publishing the DMBOK data management knowledge framework; basis for the DQS model.

dataLineage.source: External ESG provider APIs (MSCI, Sustainalytics, Bloomberg, CDP)
dataLineage.flow: Scheduled API calls with retry logic; schema validation on ingestion; DQS computation per run
dataLineage.output: Validated ESG data records with DQS metadata and lineage tag

dataLineage.flow: Event-triggered or nightly batch extraction; filing metadata enrichment; deduplication by ISIN/LEI
dataLineage.output: Structured regulatory disclosure records with source URL and filing date

dataLineage.flow: Bidirectional sync via certified API; conflict resolution rules for overlapping data fields
dataLineage.output: Enriched internal records with ESG data fields and lineage documentation

/etl-pipeline.valueSummary: Provides the data engineering and governance foundation for the entire ESG analytics platform, ensuring every metric, rating, and KPI consumed by downstream modules is traceable, quality-controlled, and delivered within SLA â€” a prerequisite for regulatory-grade ESG disclosure and investment decision-making.

/eudr-engine.title: EUDR Compliance Engine
/eudr-engine.description: Automates EU Deforestation Regulation compliance workflows including due diligence statement preparation, geolocation data management, supply chain traceability, and risk classification. Covers all seven EUDR-regulated commodities: cattle, cocoa, coffee, palm oil, soya, wood, and rubber, plus derived products. Supports operators and traders in meeting EUDR obligations from December 2024 under Regulation (EU) 2023/1115.

calculationEngine.formula: DRS = P(deforestation) × (1 − Traceability) × CountryRisk
calculationEngine.brief: Deforestation probability is estimated from satellite-based forest loss data (Hansen/GFW) within the polygon of origin. Traceability factor is the proportion of the supply chain with GPS-level geolocation data verified to plot level. Country risk is the EU Commission benchmark classification (Standard/High). DRS above 0.6 requires enhanced due diligence before a due diligence statement can be submitted.

dataPoints.name: Geolocation Coverage (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of commodity volume with verified GPS polygon data to plot level; 100% required for legal EUDR compliance.

dataPoints.name: Deforestation Risk Score (0â€“1)
dataPoints.interpretation: Probability-based deforestation exposure score; above 0.6 triggers enhanced due diligence obligation.

dataPoints.name: Supply Chain Traceability Depth
dataPoints.interpretation: Deepest supply chain tier with verified origin data; Tier 1 only is insufficient for EUDR high-risk country compliance.

dataPoints.name: DDS Submission Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of regulated commodity batches with submitted and accepted DDS in the EU Information System; 100% required pre-placing on market.

userInteraction: Onboard commodity suppliers with GPS polygon data for all production plots; verify against cadastral records.
Run deforestation risk assessment using GFW satellite forest loss data for each plot polygon after the 2020 cut-off date.
Generate due diligence statement drafts for each commodity batch; review risk flags and enhanced DD requirements.
Submit DDS to the EU EUDR Information System and maintain 5-year record retention in the compliance archive.
references: Global Forest Watch â€” Hansen Tree Cover Loss Data 2024
ZSL SPOTT Supply Chain Transparency Tracker

acronyms.def: EU Regulation 2023/1115 prohibiting placing forest-risk commodities and products on the EU market without DDS.
EUDR-mandated declaration submitted to the EU Information System confirming deforestation-free origin.
Real-time forest monitoring platform using Hansen satellite data; primary deforestation evidence source for EUDR.
EUDR Article 3 requirement that production does not violate land rights of indigenous peoples, including FPIC standards.

dataLineage.flow: Validate geometry against cadastral boundaries; check for overlaps with forest areas and protected zones
dataLineage.output: Verified plot polygons with forest overlay score

dataLineage.flow: Intersect plot polygons with post-2020 forest loss raster; compute deforested area and percentage
dataLineage.output: Plot-level deforestation risk score and forest loss area (ha)

dataLineage.flow: Link batch volumes to originating plots; compute batch-weighted DRS; generate DDS draft
dataLineage.output: DDS package with risk assessment, traceability evidence, and submission status

/eudr-engine.valueSummary: Enables operators and traders placing forest-risk commodities on the EU market to achieve and demonstrate EUDR compliance at scale, avoiding market access suspension and regulatory fines while building a durable, satellite-verified supply chain traceability infrastructure.

/ev-fleet-finance.title: EV Fleet Finance
/ev-fleet-finance.description: Models the financial case for electric vehicle fleet transition, computing total cost of ownership, financing structures, residual value projections, and charging infrastructure capex requirements for corporate and public sector fleet operators. Integrates government incentive programmes, carbon price forecasts, and energy price scenarios to produce investment-grade EV transition business cases. Supports green loan frameworks and sustainability-linked financing for fleet electrification.

calculationEngine.formula: TCO_EV = Capex + PV(Energy) + PV(Maintenance) − PV(Incentives) − PV(CarbonSavings) − RV
calculationEngine.brief: Lifetime total cost of ownership computed as vehicle acquisition capex plus present values of energy and maintenance costs, less government grants, capital allowances, and carbon cost savings (at forward carbon price), less estimated residual value at disposal. Compared against ICE fleet TCO to determine crossover year and NPV of electrification decision.

dataPoints.name: EV vs. ICE TCO Gap (Â£/vehicle/year)
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual TCO difference between EV and ICE equivalent; typically EV TCO parity achieved by 2024â€“2026 for most segments.

dataPoints.name: Fleet Decarbonisation Rate (tCO2e saved/year)
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual GHG savings from electrification vs. ICE baseline; primary metric for sustainability-linked loan target setting.

dataPoints.name: Charging Infrastructure Capex (Â£/vehicle)
dataPoints.interpretation: Capital cost of AC/DC charging infrastructure per fleet vehicle; varies from Â£500 (AC7kW) to Â£15,000+ (DC150kW).

dataPoints.name: Payback Period (years)
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of years for cumulative EV TCO savings to recover incremental capex vs. ICE fleet continuation.

userInteraction: Enter fleet composition (vehicle types, duty cycles, annual mileage) and current ICE total cost data.
Select electrification scenario (full/partial), vehicle OEM options, and charging strategy (depot/destination/public).
Review TCO comparison, NPV sensitivity to energy prices and carbon prices, and fleet decarbonisation trajectory.
Generate EV transition business case and green loan eligibility report with ICMA Green Bond Principles alignment.
references: ICMA Green Bond Principles 2021 â€” Eligible Green Project Categories

acronyms.def: Lifetime cost metric covering acquisition, energy, maintenance, and residual value; key EV vs. ICE comparison basis.
Conventional petrol or diesel vehicle; the baseline against which EV fleet economics are compared.
Projected disposal or trade-in value of vehicles at end of fleet contract; critical but uncertain EV TCO component.
UK government body administering EV grant schemes and charging infrastructure funding programmes.

dataLineage.source: Fleet management system (vehicle specs, mileage, fuel data)
dataLineage.flow: Map each vehicle to EV equivalent; compute current ICE total running cost per vehicle type
dataLineage.output: ICE baseline TCO by vehicle segment (Â£/vehicle/year)

dataLineage.source: OEM EV pricing and charging infrastructure quotes
dataLineage.flow: Input capex, battery warranty, OBC power, and charge time; compute depreciation and financing cost
dataLineage.output: EV capex and depreciation schedule per vehicle type

dataLineage.source: DEFRA emission factors and energy price forecasts
dataLineage.flow: Apply electricity grid carbon factor trajectory; compute annual GHG savings at vehicle level
dataLineage.output: Fleet decarbonisation trajectory (tCO2e) and carbon savings value at forward carbon price

/ev-fleet-finance.valueSummary: Provides fleet managers, CFOs, and sustainability teams with the financial rigour needed to approve EV transition investment decisions, structure green or sustainability-linked financing, and set credible Scope 1 decarbonisation targets aligned with SBTi corporate pathway requirements.

/exchange-intelligence.title: Exchange Intelligence
/exchange-intelligence.description: Analyses and compares ESG listing requirements, sustainability disclosure obligations, and climate reporting rules across major stock exchanges globally. Tracks emerging market sustainability index inclusion criteria, voluntary and mandatory ESG reporting frameworks by exchange, and progressive implementation timelines. Supports investor stewardship, market development analysis, and portfolio ESG regulatory exposure mapping.

calculationEngine.formula: EERS = w_d × Disclosure + w_e × Enforcement + w_i × IndexInclusion + w_c × ClimateAmbition
calculationEngine.brief: Composite readiness score across four exchange governance dimensions. Disclosure component assesses whether ESG reporting is mandatory, apply-or-explain, or voluntary. Enforcement tracks regulatory sanction history for non-compliance. Index inclusion assesses ESG index criteria stringency. Climate ambition scores TCFD mandate status and net zero exchange commitment under UN SSE initiative.

dataPoints.name: Disclosure Mandate Level (0â€“3)
dataPoints.interpretation: Rating 0=voluntary, 1=apply-or-explain, 2=mandatory rule-based, 3=mandatory+assurance; trend toward 3 in developed markets.

dataPoints.name: TCFD Mandate Status
dataPoints.interpretation: Whether exchange or market regulator has mandated TCFD-aligned climate disclosure; binary indicator with effective date.

dataPoints.name: ESG Index AUM ($Bn)
dataPoints.interpretation: Total assets tracking exchange-domiciled ESG indices; proxy for market-level ESG investor demand and capital allocation signal.

dataPoints.name: Emerging Market ESG Inclusion Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of exchange-listed companies included in leading EM ESG indices; low rate signals ESG disclosure infrastructure gap.

userInteraction: Select exchanges for comparison by region, development status, or specific regulatory framework of interest.
Review ESG readiness score components and identify disclosure mandate gaps vs. global best-practice peers.
Map portfolio holdings to exchange domicile to assess regulatory ESG disclosure exposure and improvement trajectory.
Track regulatory update alerts as exchanges publish new ESG listing rules, TCFD mandates, or index inclusion changes.
references: WFE ESG Guidance and Metrics for Exchanges 2021
IFC Sustainable Banking and Finance Network â€” Model Exchange Guidance 2021
UN SSE Initiative ESG Disclosure Guidance 2023
IOSCO ESG Ratings and Data Providers Consultation 2021

acronyms.def: Global trade body for stock and derivatives exchanges; publishes annual ESG metrics and guidance for member exchanges.
UN multi-stakeholder initiative promoting responsible investment and ESG disclosure across 100+ partner exchanges.

acronyms.full: Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures
acronyms.def: FSB-established framework; now basis for mandatory disclosure in UK, EU, Japan, Hong Kong, and emerging markets.
Stock exchanges in developing economies; typically exhibit lower ESG disclosure maturity but improving rapidly.

dataLineage.source: WFE ESG metrics database and exchange annual reports
dataLineage.flow: Extract and standardise disclosure mandate level, effective date, and enforcement mechanism per exchange
dataLineage.output: Exchange ESG governance matrix with mandate level and TCFD status

dataLineage.flow: Compute inclusion rate by exchange; track year-on-year trend in EM index eligibility
dataLineage.output: ESG index inclusion rate and AUM by exchange

dataLineage.source: Exchange listing rule documents and regulatory circulars
dataLineage.flow: Monitor for ESG rule updates; classify as new mandate, enhanced requirement, or guidance only
dataLineage.output: Regulatory change alert with implementation date and compliance implication

/exchange-intelligence.valueSummary: Enables institutional investors, stewardship teams, and market development advisors to navigate the rapidly evolving global landscape of exchange-level ESG requirements, identify markets where regulatory tailwinds will drive ESG disclosure improvement, and calibrate engagement priorities accordingly.

/executive-pay-analytics.title: Executive Pay Analytics
/executive-pay-analytics.description: Analyses the alignment between executive and CEO remuneration structures and ESG/climate performance KPIs, assessing the materiality, measurability, and rigour of sustainability metrics embedded in annual and long-term incentive plans. Supports active ownership voting decisions on remuneration resolutions, engagement with remuneration committees, and regulatory disclosure requirements under UK CA 2006, EU CSRD, and ISSB S2.

calculationEngine.formula: EPAS = w_m × Materiality + w_r × Rigour + w_t × Transparency + w_i × Incentive_Weight
calculationEngine.brief: Composite score assessing quality of ESG integration in executive pay. Materiality checks whether ESG metrics are aligned to company TCFD material risks. Rigour evaluates whether targets have independent verification, science-based calibration, and stretch ambition vs. BAU trajectory. Transparency scores disclosure of metric definitions, targets, and outcomes. Incentive weight measures ESG as % of total variable pay.

dataPoints.name: ESG Incentive Weight (% of variable pay)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of annual bonus and LTIP award tied to ESG/sustainability KPIs; below 10% considered low integration by ISS/Glass Lewis.

dataPoints.name: EPAS (0â€“100)
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite ESG pay alignment score; below 50 triggers vote against remuneration report recommendation for ESG-focused mandates.

dataPoints.name: Science-Based Target Linkage (Y/N)
dataPoints.interpretation: Binary indicator of whether executive decarbonisation KPIs are explicitly tied to SBTi-validated corporate targets.

dataPoints.name: Pay-ESG Performance Correlation (r)
dataPoints.interpretation: Historical correlation between CEO ESG-linked pay outcomes and actual ESG KPI improvements; low correlation signals metric manipulation risk.

userInteraction: Load remuneration report for target company and auto-extract ESG metric definitions and target structures.
Score materiality, rigour, and transparency dimensions using the EPAS rubric; benchmark against sector peers.
Review pay-ESG performance correlation chart to identify historical cases of target manipulation or low ambition.
Generate voting recommendation for AGM remuneration resolution and export engagement letter template for remuneration committee.
references: UK Corporate Governance Code 2024 â€” Provision 40
EU CSRD Article 29c â€” Sustainability Due Diligence in Remuneration
PRI Active Ownership 2.0 â€” Executive Pay Module 2023
UNPRI CEO Pay and Sustainability Report 2023

acronyms.def: Multi-year executive pay scheme typically with 3â€“5 year vesting; primary vehicle for ESG KPI-linked remuneration.
Shareholder meeting where executive remuneration reports and policies are subject to advisory or binding vote.
Proxy advisory firm whose remuneration voting recommendations are followed by many institutional investors.
Platform composite score measuring quality of ESG integration in executive remuneration structures.

dataLineage.flow: NLP extraction of ESG metric names, target definitions, weightings, and actual outcomes; classify by pillar
dataLineage.output: Structured ESG pay metric inventory with weight, target, outcome, and verification flag

dataLineage.source: SBTi target registry and CDP climate data
dataLineage.flow: Match executive climate KPIs to validated SBTi targets; score alignment gap
dataLineage.output: Science-based target linkage status per executive climate metric

dataLineage.source: Historical CEO pay outcomes vs. ESG KPI actuals
dataLineage.flow: Compute rolling 5-year pay-ESG performance correlation; detect target ratcheting
dataLineage.output: Pay-ESG correlation coefficient and target stretch assessment

/executive-pay-analytics.valueSummary: Empowers stewardship teams and active ownership programmes to make evidence-based voting decisions on executive remuneration, engage remuneration committees with quantitative rigour, and drive genuine integration of climate and ESG targets into executive incentive design.

/export-credit-esg.title: Export Credit ESG
/export-credit-esg.description: Manages environmental and social compliance for export credit agency transactions under the OECD Common Approaches on Officially Supported Export Credits and Equator Principles requirements. Covers Category A/B/C project classification, ESIA benchmarking against applicable host country and IFC standards, and public disclosure obligations. Supports ECA lenders, exporters, and project developers navigating Arrangement compliance.

calculationEngine.formula: ECA_ESG = (IFC_PS_Score × w_ps + OECD_CA_Score × w_ca) / (w_ps + w_ca)
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted composite of IFC PS compliance score (PS1â€“PS8, weighted 0.6) and OECD Common Approaches procedural compliance score (weighted 0.4). OECD CA score assesses project categorisation, ESIA benchmarking, monitoring plan, and public information availability requirements. Projects in sensitive sectors (Category A) require stricter benchmarking and independent review before ECA cover is granted.

dataPoints.name: OECD CA Category (A/B/C)
dataPoints.interpretation: Project impact category determining depth of E&S review required; Category A requires full ESIA; >$10M threshold applies.

dataPoints.name: IFC PS Compliance Score (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Weighted IFC PS compliance across applicable standards; minimum 85% required for ECA cover endorsement.

dataPoints.name: Public Information Availability Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Scoring of public disclosure completeness: ESIA summary, monitoring reports, and corrective action disclosure.

dataPoints.name: Host Country Gap Assessment Count
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of IFC PS requirements exceeding host country regulatory standard; gaps drive enhanced ESAP obligations.

userInteraction: Create ECA transaction record and input project details, host country, sector, and estimated ECA-supported value.
Complete OECD Common Approaches categorisation questionnaire; generate category determination with supporting evidence.
Upload ESIA and complete IFC PS compliance matrix; identify host country gaps requiring enhanced mitigation measures.
Generate OECD CA public information package and submit to ECA for endorsement decision; track monitoring cycle.
references: OECD Common Approaches on Officially Supported Export Credits 2016
OECD Recommendation of the Council on Common Approaches 2012
UK UKEF Environmental and Social Statement Policy

acronyms.def: Government-backed institution providing guarantees, loans, or insurance for exports; subject to OECD Common Approaches.
OECD framework for E&S due diligence in officially supported export credit transactions above $10M threshold.
Comprehensive E&S impact review required for Category A projects; must benchmark against IFC PS.
UK government ECA; implements OECD Common Approaches and Equator Principles for UK-supported export transactions.

dataLineage.source: Project information memorandum and ESIA documents
dataLineage.flow: Apply OECD CA categorisation criteria; map ESIA findings to IFC PS sub-requirements
dataLineage.output: OECD CA category determination and IFC PS compliance gap matrix

dataLineage.flow: Compare project mitigation requirements against applicable national law; identify E&S gaps requiring enhanced measures
dataLineage.output: Host country gap count by PS and corresponding ESAP obligations

/export-credit-esg.valueSummary: Enables export credit agencies, banks, and exporters to navigate the complex OECD Common Approaches framework efficiently, ensure IFC PS benchmarking rigour for Category A transactions, and satisfy public information requirements that protect ECA reputational and legal standing.

/financial-flow.title: Financial Flow Analytics
/financial-flow.description: Tracks and analyses global capital flows into sustainable finance instruments including green bonds, sustainability-linked loans, ESG equity funds, blended finance, and climate-aligned infrastructure. Maps Paris-aligned investment volumes against estimated climate finance needs from IPCC and UNFCCC, quantifying the annual green finance gap by sector and region. Supports capital mobilisation strategy, regulatory reporting, and investor stewardship on sustainable finance market development.

calculationEngine.formula: Gap = ClimateNeed − Σ(GreenBond + SLL + BlendedFinance + PublicClimate + ESGEquity)
standards: CPI Global Landscape of Climate Finance 2023
calculationEngine.brief: Estimates the annual green finance gap by subtracting tracked sustainable finance flows from IPCC-estimated climate investment needs by sector (energy system, transport, buildings, agriculture, nature) and region. Sustainable finance flows are aggregated from ICMA-labelled bond data, LMA-labelled loan data, OECD climate ODA, and Morningstar ESG fund flow data. Gap is expressed in absolute terms ($Bn/year) and as % of need.

dataPoints.name: Annual Green Bond Issuance ($Bn)
dataPoints.interpretation: Total labelled green bond volume; 2023 issuance exceeded $575Bn; IPCC estimates $4.5Tn/year needed by 2030.

dataPoints.name: Climate Finance Gap ($Tn/year)
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated shortfall between climate investment needs and tracked flows; CPI estimates $4.1Tn annual gap to 2030.

dataPoints.name: SLL Volume ($Bn)
dataPoints.interpretation: Sustainability-linked loan issuance; key instrument for emerging market corporate climate finance.

dataPoints.name: Blended Finance Mobilisation Ratio (x)
dataPoints.interpretation: Private capital mobilised per dollar of public/concessional capital deployed; target ratio >3x for EM climate finance.

userInteraction: Select geography and sector focus and load the latest sustainable finance flow data from integrated market databases.
Review Paris finance gap waterfall by sector comparing tracked flows against IPCC needs at current and 2030 targets.

Export sustainable finance market development report for government, DFI, or investor strategy alignment review.

/cc-arr-reforestation.title: ARR Reforestation Credits
/cc-arr-reforestation.description: Afforestation, reforestation, and revegetation (ARR) carbon credit methodology engine. Models baseline carbon stocks, additionality tests, permanence buffers, leakage deductions, and net removal credits under Verra VCS VM0047 and Gold Standard LR.

calculationEngine.methodology: VCS VM0047 ARR Net Removal Accounting
calculationEngine.formula: NetCredits = (ProjectCarbon – BaselineCarbon) × (1 – LeakagePct) × (1 – BufferPct)
calculationEngine.brief: Baseline carbon stock estimated via stratified sampling of reference plots. Project carbon accumulation follows species-specific allometric equations. Leakage assessed via activity-shifting and market-leakage pathways. Permanence buffer pool (typically 15–25%) held in VCS buffer account. Net credits = gross removal minus leakage minus buffer.

dataPoints.name: Gross Removal
dataPoints.interpretation: Above-ground, below-ground, and soil organic carbon increments

dataPoints.name: Leakage Deduction
dataPoints.interpretation: Emission displacement outside project boundary

dataPoints.name: Buffer Pool %
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion withheld in buffer to cover reversal risk

dataPoints.name: Net Credit Yield
dataPoints.interpretation: Tradeable verified carbon units per hectare per year

userInteraction: Select project region and species mix

Permanence & Leakage tab quantifies deductions
Net Credit Schedule shows issuance profile to 2050

acronyms.def: IPCC land-use carbon removal category
Biomass carbon stored in trunks, branches, and foliage
Risk rating that determines buffer pool contribution

dataLineage.flow: Allometric equations → carbon stock
dataLineage.output: Gross removal tCO₂/ha

dataLineage.flow: Non-permanence score → buffer %
dataLineage.output: Buffer-adjusted net credits

/cc-arr-reforestation.valueSummary: Net tradeable ARR credits = gross removal × (1–leakage) × (1–buffer). Buffer typically reduces gross credits by 15–25%.

/cc-bicrs-hub.title: Biodiversity & Integrated Carbon-Removal Suite
/cc-bicrs-hub.description: Integrated hub for nature-based solutions combining biodiversity co-benefit scoring, carbon removal stacking, and integrated ecosystem service valuation. Covers TNFD LEAP alignment, SBTN target-setting, and co-benefit premium quantification across ARR, REDD+, blue carbon, and soil projects.

calculationEngine.methodology: Ecosystem service stacking with co-benefit premium
calculationEngine.formula: StackedValue = CarbonValue + Σ(EcoService_i × PremiumFactor_i)
calculationEngine.brief: Carbon value derived from project credit yield times shadow carbon price. Each verified ecosystem service (biodiversity, water, community) carries a premium factor calibrated from voluntary market data (10–40% per service). TNFD LEAP assessment assigns dependency scores. SBTN freshwater and land targets set science-based thresholds.

dataPoints.name: Co-benefit Premium
dataPoints.interpretation: Price premium per verified additional ecosystem service

dataPoints.name: Stacked Credit Value
dataPoints.interpretation: Total per-credit value including biodiversity and water co-benefits

dataPoints.interpretation: Nature dependency and impact composite rating

dataPoints.name: SBTN Land Target
dataPoints.interpretation: Minimum restoration area required for sector science-based target

TNFD four-stage nature risk assessment framework
Nature equivalent of SBTi for biodiversity and ecosystems

/cc-bicrs-hub.valueSummary: Stacked credit value captures carbon plus up to 3 verified co-benefit premiums. Premium layer typically adds 15–45% to base carbon price.

/cc-ccs-biochar-hub.title: CCS & Biochar Carbon Removal Hub
/cc-ccs-biochar-hub.description: Techno-economic and MRV engine for carbon capture & storage (CCS) and biochar carbon removal (BCR) projects. Models capture efficiency, storage permanence, co-firing scenarios, and BCR stability classes under EBC and IBI standards.

calculationEngine.methodology: CCS net removal + BCR stability-weighted permanence
calculationEngine.formula: NetCCS = Captured × (1 – FugitiveRate); NetBCR = Biochar_C × (1 – OxidationFraction)
calculationEngine.brief: CCS net removal accounts for fugitive leakage at compression and injection. Monitoring via seismic and pressure surveys. BCR stability based on H:C​org ratio (EBC stability class): H:C < 0.4 = highly stable (OxidationFraction ≈ 0.03); H:C 0.4–0.6 = moderate (OxidationFraction ≈ 0.12). Permanence horizon: CCS = 1,000 yr; BCR = 100 yr default.

dataPoints.name: CCS Capture Efficiency
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of process emissions captured before atmosphere release

dataPoints.name: Fugitive Leakage Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual CO₂ seepage from geological storage

dataPoints.name: BCR H:C​org Ratio
dataPoints.interpretation: Proxy for biochar recalcitrance; lower = more stable

dataPoints.name: BCR Net Permanence
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of biochar carbon credited as permanent over 100-year horizon

userInteraction: Select technology: Point-source CCS, DAC, or BCR
Techno-Economics tab shows LCOR and cost per net tonne
Permanence tab models storage risk and leakage discount
MRV Protocol shows monitoring requirements and verification frequency
Portfolio Integration exports credits to CarbonCreditContext

acronyms.def: Geological sequestration of captured CO₂
Pyrolysis-derived stable carbon material with long-term sequestration
Quality and carbon standard for biochar production
All-in $/tCO₂ removed including capital, operating, and monitoring

dataLineage.flow: Capture efficiency + fugitive rates → net CCS
dataLineage.output: tCO₂ net stored

dataLineage.flow: H:C ratio + oxidation table → permanence fraction
dataLineage.output: BCR net credits

/cc-ccs-biochar-hub.valueSummary: Net CCS credits = captured tonnes × (1–fugitive rate). Net BCR credits = biochar carbon × permanence fraction (88–97% by stability class).

/cc-certificate-mgmt.title: Carbon Certificate Management
/cc-certificate-mgmt.description: End-to-end lifecycle management for verified carbon certificates: issuance, transfer, retirement, and cancellation workflows across Verra VCS, Gold Standard, and CAR registries. Tracks serial numbers, vintage years, and beneficial ownership chains.

calculationEngine.methodology: Certificate lifecycle state machine with audit ledger
calculationEngine.formula: CertState ∈ {Issued, Reserved, Transferred, Retired, Cancelled}
calculationEngine.brief: Each certificate batch assigned a unique serial range (registry-id-vintage-project-start–end). State transitions logged with timestamp, counterparty, and purpose. Retirement locks serial numbers permanently; cancellation allowed only pre-retirement. Double-counting prevention via cross-registry serial number deduplication check.

dataPoints.interpretation: Unique identifier preventing double-counting across registries

dataPoints.interpretation: Number of ownership transfers from issuance to final retirement

/cc-clean-cooking.title: Clean Cooking Carbon Credits
/cc-clean-cooking.description: Emission reduction quantification for clean cookstove projects under Gold Standard Metered Methodology and UNFCCC CDM AMS-II.G. Models baseline fuel consumption, stove efficiency, fNRB factors, and household monitoring protocols.

calculationEngine.formula: ER_HH = (FC_baseline – FC_project) × NCV × EF_fuelwood × fNRB
calculationEngine.brief: Baseline fuel consumption estimated via kitchen performance tests (KPT) on traditional stoves. Project consumption measured on metered improved stoves. Net calorific value (NCV) and emission factor per fuel type applied. fNRB (fraction of non-renewable biomass) is a country/region-specific factor (0.4–0.95) representing unsustainable wood harvest. ER = household emission reductions per year.

dataPoints.name: fNRB Factor
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of wood fuel harvested unsustainably; drives net credit calculation

dataPoints.interpretation: Daily wood or charcoal consumption on traditional stove

dataPoints.interpretation: Efficiency improvement of improved cookstove vs baseline

dataPoints.name: Annual ER per HH
dataPoints.interpretation: Verified emission reductions per household per year

/cc-dac.title: Direct Air Capture Credits
/cc-dac.description: Techno-economic and MRV engine for Direct Air Capture (DAC) carbon removal projects. Models solid sorbent and liquid solvent pathways, energy source carbon intensity, net removal efficiency, and cost trajectory to 2050 under IRA tax credit and EU Innovation Fund scenarios.

calculationEngine.methodology: DAC net removal = gross capture – energy emissions
calculationEngine.formula: NetDAC = Captured – (EnergyUse × GridCI); LCOR(t) = LCOR₀ × (1–LearningRate)^log₂(CumCapacity(t)/CumCapacity₀)
calculationEngine.brief: Solid sorbent DAC: thermal energy 5–6 GJ/tCO₂, electric 1.5–2 GJ/tCO₂. Liquid solvent: thermal 6–8 GJ/tCO₂, electric 0.5 GJ/tCO₂. Net removal requires low-carbon energy source; grid CI must be below breakeven threshold. Learning rate 15–20% per doubling of cumulative installed capacity. IRA §45Q provides $180/tCO₂ for geologically stored DAC.

dataPoints.name: LCOR 2024
dataPoints.interpretation: Current levelised cost of removal for commercial DAC plants

dataPoints.name: LCOR 2050 (DOE target)
dataPoints.interpretation: Cost target for DAC at scale under aggressive deployment scenario

dataPoints.name: Net Removal Efficiency
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of captured CO₂ that is net of energy-related emissions

dataPoints.name: Breakeven Grid CI
dataPoints.interpretation: Grid carbon intensity above which DAC becomes a net emitter

userInteraction: Select DAC technology: solid sorbent or liquid solvent

Net Removal calculator shows gross vs net credit yield

Policy tab applies IRA §45Q and EU Innovation Fund credit

acronyms.def: Chemical process extracting CO₂ directly from ambient air
All-in cost per net tonne of CO₂ removed
gCO₂/kWh of electricity consumed
US 2022 legislation providing §45Q DAC tax credits

dataLineage.flow: Capture mass flow → gross tonnes
dataLineage.output: Gross DAC tCO₂

dataLineage.flow: kWh × grid CI → energy emissions
dataLineage.output: Net removal tonnes

/cc-dac.valueSummary: Net DAC credits = captured CO₂ minus energy-related emissions. At US average grid CI (386 gCO₂/kWh), solid sorbent DAC achieves ~75% net efficiency on renewable energy.

/cc-energy-efficiency-hub.title: Energy Efficiency Carbon Credits Hub
/cc-energy-efficiency-hub.description: Emission reduction quantification for industrial and building energy efficiency projects under IPMVP Option A/B/C/D measurement and verification protocols. Covers baseline setting, adjustment factors, and credit issuance under ISO 50001 and CDM AMS-II series.

calculationEngine.formula: ER = (BaselineEnergy – ProjectEnergy) × EF_grid; BaselineAdj = Baseline × Σ(Adj_i)
calculationEngine.brief: Baseline energy consumption established over 12-month reference period with regression-based adjustment for production, weather, and occupancy variables. IPMVP Option B: metered savings at system level. Option C: whole-facility utility billing. Adjustment factors normalize for changed conditions. Grid emission factor applies regional annual average or marginal factor per GHG Protocol.

dataPoints.name: Baseline Energy Intensity
dataPoints.interpretation: Energy per unit production in reference period

dataPoints.name: Adjustment Factors
dataPoints.interpretation: Variables used to normalize baseline for changed conditions

dataPoints.name: Grid Emission Factor
dataPoints.interpretation: Carbon intensity of grid electricity displaced by efficiency measures

dataPoints.name: Verified Savings
dataPoints.interpretation: Energy savings verified under selected IPMVP option

/cc-engine-hub.title: Carbon Credit Engine Hub
/cc-engine-hub.description: Central orchestration hub for all 21 carbon credit methodology engines. Provides cross-methodology portfolio analytics, credit quality scoring, methodology comparison, and integration with the CarbonCreditContext data bus for downstream module consumption.

calculationEngine.methodology: Portfolio-level credit aggregation and quality scoring
calculationEngine.formula: PortfolioScore = Σ(w_i × QualityScore_i); QualityScore = f(Additionality, Permanence, MRV_Rigor, CobenefitCount)
calculationEngine.brief: Quality score for each methodology engine based on 4 dimensions: additionality stringency (0–30pts), permanence horizon (0–25pts), MRV rigor (0–25pts), and co-benefit count (0–20pts). Portfolio-level score = weighted average by credit volume. ICVCM CCPs applied as minimum threshold screen (score ≥ 60 to pass CCP bar).

dataPoints.name: Portfolio Quality Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Overall quality rating of carbon credit portfolio

dataPoints.name: ICVCM CCP Pass Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of portfolio credits passing Core Carbon Principles screen

dataPoints.name: Methodology Diversification
dataPoints.interpretation: Concentration metric; lower HHI = more diversified methodology mix

dataPoints.name: Credit Volume by Methodology
dataPoints.interpretation: Breakdown of portfolio by credit type and standard

/cc-grid-renewables.title: Grid Renewable Energy Credits
/cc-grid-renewables.description: Emission reduction quantification for grid-connected renewable energy projects under CDM AMS-I.D and ACM0002. Models combined margin (CM) emission factor, capacity factor projections, additionality via barrier analysis, and RECcertificate issuance.

calculationEngine.formula: EF_CM = (EF_OM × w_OM) + (EF_BM × w_BM); ER = NetGen × EF_CM
calculationEngine.brief: Operating Margin (OM) reflects the average emission factor of existing dispatched power plants weighted by generation. Build Margin (BM) reflects the 5-year average EF of recently commissioned capacity or the 20% most recent additions. Combined margin weights OM at 50% and BM at 50% by default (project-specific adjustment allowed). Net generation = gross generation minus auxiliary consumption.

dataPoints.name: Operating Margin EF
dataPoints.interpretation: Average EF of existing grid generation mix weighted by output

dataPoints.name: Build Margin EF
dataPoints.interpretation: Average EF of recently commissioned capacity

dataPoints.name: Combined Margin EF
dataPoints.interpretation: Default combined margin for ER calculation

dataPoints.name: Net Generation
dataPoints.interpretation: Electricity delivered to grid after station use deduction

dataPoints.name: Annual ER
dataPoints.interpretation: Verified emission reductions from RE displacing grid electricity

acronyms.def: Grid EF of existing dispatched plants
Grid EF of recently constructed capacity
Weighted average of OM and BM used as project emission factor

dataLineage.flow: Net generation → ER

/cc-grid-renewables.valueSummary: Annual ER = net renewable generation × combined margin EF. CM = 0.5×OM + 0.5×BM; project-specific weighting allowed under ACM0002.

/cc-ifm-credits.title: Improved Forest Management Credits
/cc-ifm-credits.description: Carbon credit quantification for Improved Forest Management (IFM) projects including extended rotation, reduced harvest, and conservation easements under Verra VCS VM0010/VM0012 and Climate Action Reserve Forest Protocol.

calculationEngine.methodology: VM0010 IFM baseline-project carbon stock difference
calculationEngine.formula: ER = (C_project – C_baseline) – Leakage – BufferContribution
calculationEngine.brief: Baseline carbon stock modeled using forest growth and yield equations calibrated to local yield tables or FVS model runs. Project carbon stock reflects changed management (extended rotation adds 15–30% standing stock over 20 yr). Leakage via activity-shifting: harvesting displaced to other forests within leakage belt. Buffer pool from CAR/VCS non-permanence risk rating (typically 12–18% for temperate managed forests).

dataPoints.name: Baseline C Stock
dataPoints.interpretation: Carbon stock under business-as-usual harvest schedule

dataPoints.name: Project C Stock
dataPoints.interpretation: Carbon stock under improved management regime

dataPoints.name: Leakage Belt
dataPoints.interpretation: Geographic area within which harvest displacement leakage is assessed

dataPoints.name: Buffer Pool %
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of gross credits withheld for reversal insurance

/cc-industrial-gases.title: Industrial Gas Destruction Credits
/cc-industrial-gases.description: High-GWP industrial gas destruction credit quantification for HFC-23, N₂O, and SF₆ projects under CDM AM0001, AMS-III.L, and Verra VCS VM0024. Models destruction efficiency, baseline emission rates, and additionality for refrigerant and chemical plant applications.

calculationEngine.methodology: High-GWP destruction credit = baseline emissions – project emissions
calculationEngine.formula: ER = (BE_gas – PE_gas) × GWP_gas; BE = ProductionRate × EF_baseline
calculationEngine.brief: Baseline emission rate derived from uncontrolled venting or flaring emission factors per unit of chemical/refrigerant produced. Project emission rate reflects destruction efficiency of thermal oxidation unit (typically 99.9% for HFC-23). GWP100 per IPCC AR6: HFC-23 = 14,600; N₂O = 273; SF₆ = 25,200. Additionality via regulatory surplus test: project must exceed legal minimum destruction requirements.

dataPoints.name: HFC-23 GWP100
dataPoints.interpretation: 100-year global warming potential of HFC-23 trifluoromethane

dataPoints.name: SF₆ GWP100
dataPoints.interpretation: 100-year global warming potential of sulfur hexafluoride

dataPoints.name: Destruction Efficiency
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of high-GWP gas destroyed in thermal oxidation unit

dataPoints.name: Baseline EF
dataPoints.interpretation: Emission factor for uncontrolled industrial gas venting
