/cc-landfill-wastewater.title: Landfill & Wastewater Methane Credits
/cc-landfill-wastewater.description: Methane capture and destruction credit quantification for landfill gas (LFG) and wastewater treatment plants under CDM ACM0001, AMS-III.G, and EPA Landfill Methane Outreach Program. Models first-order decay, gas collection efficiency, and methane oxidation.

calculationEngine.formula: Q_CH4(t) = L₀ × R × exp(-k×t); ER = Q_captured × (1–OxFrac) × GWP_CH4 × (1–Unc)
calculationEngine.brief: Landfill methane generation follows first-order decay model: L₀ = waste-in-place × methane generation potential (DOC×DOCf×MCF×F×16/12). Collection efficiency typically 70–85% for active collection systems. Oxidation fraction at soil surface: 10% default (IPCC). GWP100 CH₄ = 29.8 (IPCC AR6). Uncertainty discount 5–10%. Wastewater: methane generation from COD removal × B₀ factor.

dataPoints.name: L₀ (Methane Potential)
dataPoints.interpretation: Methane generation potential per tonne of waste in place

dataPoints.name: First-Order Decay k
dataPoints.interpretation: Decay constant; higher in wet tropical climates

dataPoints.name: Collection Efficiency
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of generated LFG collected before surface emission

dataPoints.name: GWP100 CH₄
dataPoints.interpretation: 100-year global warming potential of methane

/cc-livestock-methane.title: Livestock Methane Reduction Credits
/cc-livestock-methane.description: Enteric fermentation and manure management methane reduction credit quantification under Verra VCS VM0041 and Gold Standard Animal Husbandry. Models Tier 2 IPCC enteric fermentation baselines, dietary additive interventions (3-NOP, seaweed), and anaerobic digestion co-benefits.

calculationEngine.methodology: IPCC Tier 2 enteric fermentation + manure management
calculationEngine.formula: ER_enteric = (EF_baseline – EF_project) × HeadCount × GWP_CH4; EF = GEI × Ym / 55.65
calculationEngine.brief: Baseline enteric EF = Gross Energy Intake (GEI) × methane conversion factor (Ym) / 55.65 MJ/kg CH₄. Ym ranges 3–6.5% by species and feed quality. 3-NOP additive reduces Ym by 20–30%; seaweed (Asparagopsis) by 50–80% in trials. Manure management: IPCC VS (volatile solids) method for lagoon vs solid storage comparison. Co-benefits: reduced N₂O from manure handling.

dataPoints.name: Ym (Methane Conversion Factor)
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of gross energy intake converted to enteric methane

dataPoints.name: 3-NOP Reduction Efficacy
dataPoints.interpretation: Enteric methane reduction from 3-nitrooxypropanol feed additive

dataPoints.name: VS (Volatile Solids)
dataPoints.interpretation: Organic matter excreted used for manure management calculation

dataPoints.name: Manure System MCF
dataPoints.interpretation: Methane conversion factor for different manure management systems

userInteraction: Select livestock species and production system
Enteric Baseline sets diet quality and Ym factor
Intervention tab applies 3-NOP, seaweed, or diet change
Manure Management tab compares lagoon vs covered digester
Credit Calculator aggregates enteric + manure reductions

acronyms.def: Total dietary energy consumed by livestock per day
Percentage of GEI lost as enteric CH₄
Organic matter in manure used to calculate manure CH₄ and N₂O
Fraction of VS converted to CH₄ in given manure system

dataLineage.flow: Species × Ym × GEI → baseline EF
dataLineage.output: Baseline enteric tCO₂e

dataLineage.flow: Intervention efficacy → project EF
dataLineage.output: ER in tCO₂e/head/yr

/cc-livestock-methane.valueSummary: Enteric ER = (baseline – project Ym) × GEI × headcount × GWP. 3-NOP typically yields 0.3–0.6 tCO₂e/cow/yr; seaweed up to 1.2 tCO₂e/cow/yr in optimal conditions.

/cc-methodology-comparison.title: Carbon Credit Methodology Comparison
/cc-methodology-comparison.description: Side-by-side methodology comparison engine for all 21 carbon credit standard types. Scores each methodology on 8 ICVCM-aligned dimensions and provides filtering, ranking, and cost benchmarking to support project selection and portfolio construction.

calculationEngine.formula: Score_m = Σ(w_d × Rating_md) for d in {Additionality, Permanence, MRV, CobenefitCount, Scalability, Cost, RegAcceptance, CreditIntegrity}
calculationEngine.brief: Each of 8 dimensions rated 0–10 per methodology via structured expert elicitation calibrated to ICVCM CCP assessments and third-party rating agency scores. Weights: Additionality 20%, Permanence 20%, MRV 15%, Co-benefits 15%, Scalability 10%, Cost 10%, Regulatory 5%, Integrity 5%. Composite score normalized to 0–100.

dataPoints.name: Additionality Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Strength of methodology additionality demonstration requirements

dataPoints.name: Permanence Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Credit quality dimension for long-term storage assurance

dataPoints.name: MRV Rigor Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Quality of measurement, reporting, and verification requirements

dataPoints.name: Credit Price Range
dataPoints.interpretation: Observed transaction price range in voluntary carbon market

references: ICVCM Core Carbon Principles & Assessment Framework 2023

acronyms.def: Standard-setting body for voluntary carbon credit quality
ICVCM minimum quality attribute assessed for each methodology

/cc-mineralization.title: Enhanced Weathering & Mineralization Credits
/cc-mineralization.description: Carbon dioxide removal quantification for enhanced rock weathering (ERW) and ocean alkalinity enhancement (OAE) projects. Models dissolution kinetics, alkalinity flux measurement, and MRV uncertainty under Lithos Carbon, Cascading Climate, and emerging ISO standards.

calculationEngine.methodology: ERW carbon removal via alkalinity increase
calculationEngine.formula: CDR = ΔAlkalinity × (MW_CO2 / MW_HCO3) × Volume × (1–LCI)
calculationEngine.brief: ERW spreading of crushed silicate rock (basalt, dunite) on agricultural soils. Dissolution releases alkalinity (Ca²⁺ + Mg²⁺ + OH⁻) which absorbs CO₂ from air via bicarbonate formation. Net CDR = alkalinity increase × CO₂:HCO₃ stoichiometry × watershed area, minus lifecycle emissions of mining, grinding, and transport (LCI). Ocean alkalinity enhancement: similar principle via direct seawater alkalinization.

dataPoints.name: Alkalinity Flux
dataPoints.interpretation: Increase in dissolved inorganic carbon alkalinity relative to control watershed

dataPoints.name: Rock Application Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Tonnes of crushed silicate rock applied per hectare

dataPoints.name: Lifecycle Emission Intensity
dataPoints.interpretation: Carbon cost of producing and applying one tonne of rock amendment

dataPoints.name: Net CDR Efficiency
dataPoints.interpretation: Net CO₂ drawdown per tonne of rock applied at field scale

userInteraction: Select pathway: ERW (agricultural) or OAE (marine)
Rock Properties tab sets mineralogy and dissolution rate
Field Monitoring tab inputs watershed alkalinity measurements
LCI Calculator deducts mining, grinding, transport emissions
Net CDR output with uncertainty range for credit issuance
references: IPCC AR6 Ch.12 CDR Enhanced Weathering

acronyms.def: CDR method spreading crushed silicate rock to accelerate natural CO₂ absorption
Marine CDR method increasing seawater alkalinity to absorb CO₂
Carbon footprint of producing and applying the rock amendment
Carbonate and bicarbonate carbon in water column measured for CDR quantification

dataLineage.output: Gross CDR estimate

dataLineage.flow: Rock supply chain → LCI
dataLineage.output: Net CDR after lifecycle deduction

/cc-mineralization.valueSummary: Net ERW CDR = alkalinity flux × stoichiometry × area – lifecycle emissions. Typical net efficiency: 0.1–0.3 tCO₂ per tonne basalt applied at field scale.

/cc-portfolio-analytics.title: Carbon Credit Portfolio Analytics
/cc-portfolio-analytics.description: Portfolio-level analytics for carbon credit holdings across 21 methodology types. Provides vintage diversification, methodology concentration, credit rating distribution, cost basis analysis, and retirement planning tools integrated with the CarbonCreditContext data bus.

calculationEngine.methodology: Portfolio analytics: vintage-weighted quality and cost basis
calculationEngine.formula: WtdQuality = Σ(vol_i × Q_i) / Σvol_i; CostBasis = Σ(vol_i × price_i) / Σvol_i
calculationEngine.brief: Volume-weighted average quality score aggregates ICVCM dimension ratings across all positions. Vintage diversification measured by Shannon entropy across vintage years. Methodology HHI captures concentration risk. Retirement planning uses FIFO, LIFO, or quality-optimised retirement sequencing. Mark-to-market revaluation applies current market prices by methodology and vintage.

dataPoints.name: Portfolio Volume
dataPoints.interpretation: Total carbon credit holdings across all methodologies and vintages

dataPoints.name: Weighted Quality Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Volume-weighted average ICVCM quality score across portfolio

dataPoints.name: Vintage Entropy
dataPoints.interpretation: Diversification across vintage years; higher = more diversified

dataPoints.name: Methodology HHI
dataPoints.interpretation: Methodology concentration; below 0.15 = well-diversified

userInteraction: Portfolio Overview tab shows volume, quality, cost basis summary
Vintage Analysis tab charts holdings by issuance year
Methodology Breakdown tab shows concentration and HHI

Retirement Planner sequences retirement by strategy (FIFO/LIFO/quality)

/cc-redd-wetlands-hub.title: REDD+ & Wetlands Carbon Hub
/cc-redd-wetlands-hub.description: Integrated hub for REDD+ avoided deforestation and wetland carbon (blue carbon) projects. Covers Verra VCS JNR jurisdiction-level REDD+, ART TREES crediting, and VM0033 tidal wetland restoration with methane co-assessment.

calculationEngine.methodology: JNR jurisdictional REDD+ reference level and ART TREES accounting
calculationEngine.formula: ER_REDD = (RefLevel – ProjectEmissions) × (1–Uncertainty) – Leakage
calculationEngine.brief: Reference level (RL) derived from 10-year historical deforestation rate adjusted for national forest cover trend. Project emissions = remote sensing–verified residual deforestation within project boundary. Uncertainty deduction 10–15% based on activity data and emission factor uncertainty. Leakage: within-country leakage belt monitoring via PRODES/Hansen GFC. Wetland: VM0033 soil carbon accumulation minus CH₄ penalty for non-tidal areas.

dataPoints.name: Reference Level
dataPoints.interpretation: Baseline deforestation rate against which project is measured

dataPoints.name: Activity Data Uncertainty
dataPoints.interpretation: Uncertainty in deforestation area detection used for discount

dataPoints.name: Wetland Soil C Accumulation
dataPoints.interpretation: Soil carbon sequestration rate in restored tidal wetland

dataPoints.name: Methane Penalty (VM0033)
dataPoints.interpretation: Methane emission penalty applied in non-tidal or impermanently flooded zones

references: Verra VCS JNR Jurisdictional REDD+ v3.0

acronyms.def: UN Framework for forest-based emission reductions

/cc-registry-hub.title: Carbon Registry Hub
/cc-registry-hub.description: Unified interface for carbon credit registry operations across Verra VCS, Gold Standard, Climate Action Reserve, ACR, and CDM. Provides account management, serial number tracking, cross-registry deduplication, and CORSIA eligibility screening.

calculationEngine.methodology: Cross-registry serial deduplication and CORSIA eligibility matrix
calculationEngine.formula: DuplicateFlag = serial_i ∈ RegistrySet_j (j ≠ registry_i); CORSIAEligible = vintage ≥ 2016 AND standard ∈ CORSIAApprovedList
calculationEngine.brief: Serial number deduplication checks each credit serial against a unified cross-registry ledger to detect double-issuance or double-retirement. CORSIA eligibility requires vintage year ≥ 2016, approved programme, and meeting CORSIA Sustainability Criteria. Registry API integrations pull live account balances; status updates propagated to CarbonCreditContext.

/cc-retirement-workflow.title: Carbon Credit Retirement Workflow
/cc-retirement-workflow.description: Structured retirement workflow engine for carbon credits including retirement purpose selection, beneficial owner declaration, registry instruction generation, and retirement certificate creation. Supports compliance, voluntary, and CORSIA retirement categories.

calculationEngine.methodology: Retirement state machine with certificate generation
calculationEngine.formula: RetirementComplete = RegistryConfirmation AND CertificateIssued AND AuditLog.appended
calculationEngine.brief: Retirement workflow: (1) Select serial range and retirement purpose; (2) Declare beneficial owner entity and jurisdiction; (3) Submit registry instruction with counterparty authentication; (4) Registry confirms serial status change to Retired; (5) Generate retirement certificate with registry confirmation ID; (6) Append immutable audit log entry. Cancel path available before registry confirmation only.

dataPoints.interpretation: Required declaration of why credits are being retired

dataPoints.interpretation: Final entity in whose name emission reductions are claimed

dataPoints.interpretation: Unique identifier confirming successful retirement in registry ledger

dataPoints.interpretation: Date and time of retirement certificate generation

/cc-rice-cultivation.title: Rice Cultivation Methane Credits
/cc-rice-cultivation.description: Methane emission reduction quantification for sustainable rice intensification (SRI) and alternate wetting and drying (AWD) projects under CDM AMS-III.AU and Verra VCS VM0042. Models IPCC Tier 2 paddy methane baselines and water management intervention effects.

calculationEngine.methodology: IPCC Tier 2 rice paddy CH₄ with water regime scaling factors
calculationEngine.formula: ER = (EF_baseline – EF_project) × Area × GWP_CH4; EF = EF₀ × SF_w × SF_s × SF_o × t_season
calculationEngine.brief: Reference emission factor EF₀ = 1.30 kg CH₄/ha/day (IPCC default for continuously flooded, no organic amendment). Scaling factors: SF_w for water regime (AWD reduces to 0.52 of continuous flooding), SF_s for soil type, SF_o for organic amendments. Season length t_season = days of flooding. Project EF reflects AWD protocol measured via pore-water tube readings. GWP100 CH₄ = 29.8 (IPCC AR6).

dataPoints.name: EF₀ (Base Emission Factor)
dataPoints.interpretation: Reference methane EF for continuously flooded paddy with no organic amendments

dataPoints.name: SF_w (Water Regime Factor)
dataPoints.interpretation: Scaling factor for alternate wetting and drying vs continuous flooding

dataPoints.name: AWD Threshold
dataPoints.interpretation: Soil water level at which re-flooding is triggered in AWD protocol

dataPoints.name: Annual ER
dataPoints.interpretation: Emission reductions per hectare per growing season

userInteraction: Select water management practice: AWD, SRI, or Aerobic Rice
Baseline tab sets water regime and organic amendment inputs

acronyms.def: Intermittent irrigation technique that reduces paddy methane by ~50%
Agronomic practice reducing water use and methane in rice cultivation

dataLineage.flow: EF₀ × scaling factors → paddy EF
dataLineage.output: Baseline and project EF

dataLineage.flow: Soil water level readings → AWD compliance
dataLineage.output: Project emission factor

/cc-rice-cultivation.valueSummary: AWD reduces paddy methane EF by ~48% (SF_w = 0.52). Annual ER = season length × (EF_base – EF_AWD) × area × GWP100. Typical yield: 0.8–1.8 tCO₂e/ha/yr.

/cc-soil-carbon.title: Soil Carbon Sequestration Credits
/cc-soil-carbon.description: Soil organic carbon (SOC) credit quantification for agricultural soil carbon projects under Verra VCS VM0042, Indigo Ag Protocol, and Soil Carbon Initiative. Models SOC change via IPCC Tier 2 stock-difference method, sampling design, permanence, and additionality.

calculationEngine.formula: DSOC = (SOC_t2 – SOC_t1) / T × Area × (44/12); Net = DSOC – Leakage – Buffer
calculationEngine.brief: SOC stock measured at t1 (baseline) and t2 (verification) via stratified random soil sampling to 30cm depth. Stock-difference method: ΔSOC/yr = (SOC_t2–SOC_t1)/T. Carbon fraction: multiply by 44/12 to convert C to CO₂. Sampling uncertainty drives buffer deduction (typically 20–30%). Permanence horizon: 100 years; practice reversal risk assessed via farming system stability score. Additionality: practice not common in region (common practice test).

dataPoints.name: SOC Stock (t1)
dataPoints.interpretation: Baseline soil organic carbon stock measured at project start

dataPoints.name: SOC Stock (t2)
dataPoints.interpretation: Project soil organic carbon stock at first verification event

dataPoints.name: Sampling Uncertainty
dataPoints.interpretation: Statistical uncertainty used to set buffer pool contribution

dataPoints.name: Permanence Buffer
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of gross credits withheld for reversal insurance

Indigo Ag Soil Carbon Protocol v2.1

acronyms.def: Carbon stored in soil organic matter measured in tC/ha
More stable, longer-lived SOC fraction associated with mineral particles
Less stable, particulate SOC fraction with shorter residence time
Relative standard deviation used to quantify sampling uncertainty

dataLineage.flow: Bulk density × C% → tC/ha
dataLineage.output: SOC stock per stratum

/cc-soil-carbon.valueSummary: Net soil C credits = ΔSOC × area × 44/12 – leakage – buffer. Buffer typically 20–30% of gross. Typical yields: no-till 0.2–0.5 tCO₂e/ha/yr; compost + cover crops 0.5–1.5 tCO₂e/ha/yr.

/change-management.title: Sustainability Change Management
/change-management.description: Organisational change management framework for sustainability transformation programmes. Assesses stakeholder readiness, models adoption curves using Everett Rogers diffusion theory, and tracks KPI progress across 5 ADKAR stages (Awareness, Desire, Knowledge, Ability, Reinforcement).

calculationEngine.methodology: ADKAR change readiness composite + Rogers S-curve adoption
calculationEngine.formula: ADKAR_score = avg(A,D,K,A₂,R) × 20; Adoption(t) = L / (1 + exp(-k(t–t_mid)))
calculationEngine.brief: ADKAR scored 0–5 per dimension via stakeholder survey; composite × 20 normalises to 0–100. Rogers adoption curve fit to rollout data: L = maximum adoption rate, k = steepness parameter, t_mid = inflection point (when 50% adoption reached). Critical mass threshold at 16% (innovators + early adopters per Rogers). Kotter stages mapped to ADKAR progression milestones.

dataPoints.name: ADKAR Composite Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Overall change readiness; below 60 = high change failure risk

dataPoints.name: Adoption Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of target population currently using new sustainability practice

dataPoints.name: Critical Mass Threshold
dataPoints.interpretation: Adoption level required for self-sustaining momentum

dataPoints.name: Change Resistance Index
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite measure of stakeholder resistance severity

acronyms.def: Prosci individual change management framework
Logistic function modelling technology or practice diffusion

dataLineage.output: Change readiness by group

dataLineage.flow: Adoption counts over time → S-curve fit
dataLineage.output: Adoption rate and critical mass timing

/change-management.valueSummary: ADKAR composite below 60 indicates high change failure risk. Critical mass (16%) is key inflection point; adoption typically accelerates 3× once threshold is crossed.

/circular-economy-tracker.title: Circular Economy Tracker
/circular-economy-tracker.description: Circularity performance measurement and reporting platform aligned with Ellen MacArthur Foundation Material Circularity Indicator (MCI). Tracks material flows, recycled content, waste diversion, and product lifetime extension across 5 supply chain tiers.

calculationEngine.methodology: Ellen MacArthur Foundation Material Circularity Indicator
calculationEngine.formula: MCI = 1 – LFI; LFI = (V + W) / (2F + 0.09); F = M – V – W
calculationEngine.brief: Linear Flow Index (LFI) measures fraction of material following a linear path (extraction→disposal). V = virgin material input; W = unrecoverable waste; F = total material flow minus virgin input minus waste. MCI = 1 – LFI on a 0–1 scale (1 = fully circular, 0 = fully linear). Utility adjustment factor accounts for product use phase extension (lifetime multiplier).

dataPoints.name: Material Circularity Indicator
dataPoints.interpretation: Circularity score; 1.0 = fully circular, 0.0 = fully linear

dataPoints.name: Virgin Material Input %
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of material inputs sourced as virgin (non-recycled)

dataPoints.name: Recycled Content %
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of recycled or recovered material in product inputs

dataPoints.name: Waste Diversion Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of production waste diverted from landfill via reuse, recycling, or composting

acronyms.def: EMF metric measuring circularity of product material flows

Organisation that developed the circular economy MCI framework

dataLineage.output: Material input composition

dataLineage.flow: Disposal routes → W calculation
dataLineage.output: Unrecoverable waste fraction

/circular-economy-tracker.valueSummary: MCI = 1 – LFI. LFI = (V + W) / (2F + 0.09). Fully circular products achieve MCI close to 1.0; industry average typically 0.2–0.4. EU Taxonomy encourages MCI targets above 0.6.

/client-portal.title: Client Sustainability Portal
/client-portal.description: Secure self-service portal for institutional clients to access portfolio sustainability analytics, ESG reports, and regulatory disclosure packages. Provides real-time portfolio carbon footprint, temperature score, and regulatory filing status dashboards.

calculationEngine.methodology: Client-facing portfolio aggregation with entitlement filtering
calculationEngine.formula: ClientView = PortfolioData ∩ ClientEntitlements; CarbonFootprint = Σ(w_i × EVIC_i_scope12) × (1M / AUM)
calculationEngine.brief: Client entitlement matrix controls which portfolios and modules are visible per client account. Carbon footprint per GHG Protocol Scope 2 MB method for financed emissions; WACI = portfolio-weighted average carbon intensity (tCO₂e/$M revenue). Temperature score follows TCFD-aligned weighted ITR methodology. Regulatory filing status tracks submission deadlines for SFDR PAI, CSRD ESG, and TCFD reports.

/client-report.title: Client Sustainability Report Generator
/client-report.description: Automated sustainability report generation engine producing TCFD-aligned, SFDR-compliant, and CSRD-ready client reports. Combines portfolio analytics, narrative generation, chart exports, and regulatory appendices into formatted PDF-ready output packages.

calculationEngine.methodology: Templated report assembly with data binding
calculationEngine.formula: Report = Template(Section_i → DataBinding_i) ∀ i; Completeness = Σ(FilledSections) / TotalSections
calculationEngine.brief: Report sections bound to live portfolio analytics via data binding layer. Narrative paragraphs generated from structured data using regulatory-aligned templates. Chart exports use portfolio visualisation API. Completeness score tracks data coverage; sections with <80% data completeness flagged for manual review. Regulatory appendix auto-populates SFDR PAI table and TCFD disclosure matrix.

/client-transition-command-center.title: Client Transition Command Center
/client-transition-command-center.description: Integrated transition plan monitoring and execution dashboard for institutional clients. Tracks decarbonisation milestones, interim SBTi targets, engagement actions, and portfolio reallocation progress against Paris-aligned transition pathways.

calculationEngine.methodology: Transition milestone tracking with SBTi pathway gap
calculationEngine.formula: PathwayGap(t) = ActualCI(t) – SBTiTarget(t); MilestoneCompletion = CompletedMilestones / TotalMilestones
calculationEngine.brief: Carbon intensity trajectory benchmarked against SBTi sector-specific 1.5°C pathway. Pathway gap = actual portfolio-level CI minus required SBTi CI target at each year. Engagement actions tracked via CA100+ benchmark indicators. Portfolio reallocation monitors shift in green vs brown asset allocation. Milestone completion ratio drives overall transition score.

dataPoints.name: Pathway Gap
dataPoints.interpretation: Deviation from science-based emission intensity pathway

dataPoints.name: Milestone Completion Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Progress on defined transition plan milestones

dataPoints.name: Engagement Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Issuer-level engagement progress on net-zero commitment and action

dataPoints.name: Green Asset Allocation
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of portfolio in Paris-aligned or EU Taxonomy-aligned assets

Engagement Tracker tab manages active company engagement workflows

Milestone Planner sets and tracks transition action items
Alert Centre flags pathway gap breaches and overdue milestones
references: TCFD Guidance on Climate Transition Plans 2023
Climate Action 100+ Net Zero Benchmark v2.0

acronyms.def: Framework for corporate emission reduction targets aligned with Paris Agreement
Investor engagement initiative targeting largest corporate emitters
GHG emissions per unit of revenue used for SBTi pathway comparison

/client-transition-command-center.valueSummary: Pathway gap = actual CI minus SBTi target CI. Negative gap = ahead of pathway. Command center integrates engagement actions, portfolio reallocation, and milestone tracking in single dashboard.

/climate-benchmark-constructor.title: Climate Benchmark Constructor
/climate-benchmark-constructor.description: Custom climate index and benchmark construction engine aligned with EU Climate Benchmarks Regulation (EU 2019/2089). Builds Paris-Aligned Benchmarks (PAB) and Climate Transition Benchmarks (CTB) with WACI reduction, fossil fuel exclusions, and green revenue tilts.

calculationEngine.methodology: EU PAB/CTB construction with WACI constraint
calculationEngine.formula: w_i = w_parent_i × TiltFactor_i; WACI_bench = Σ(w_i × CI_i) ≤ 0.50 × WACI_parent (PAB)
calculationEngine.brief: PAB minimum decarbonisation: WACI at least 50% below parent index at inception; minimum 7%/yr ongoing reduction. CTB: at least 30% below parent + 7%/yr. Tilt factors increase weight for low-CI companies and decrease for high-CI. Exclusions: companies with >1% revenue from coal, >10% from oil/gas extraction (PAB). Green revenue tilt applies EU Taxonomy-aligned revenue fraction as upweight signal.

dataPoints.name: PAB WACI Reduction
dataPoints.interpretation: Minimum weighted average carbon intensity reduction vs parent benchmark

dataPoints.name: Annual Decarbonisation Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Required annual reduction in benchmark carbon intensity

dataPoints.name: Fossil Fuel Exclusion Threshold
dataPoints.interpretation: Revenue thresholds triggering exclusion from PAB universe

dataPoints.name: Green Revenue Tilt
dataPoints.interpretation: Upweight factor based on EU Taxonomy-aligned revenue fraction

userInteraction: Select benchmark type: PAB or CTB
Universe tab applies fossil fuel exclusions and controversy screens
Tilt Optimiser sets CI reduction weights to meet WACI constraint
Green Revenue tab applies EU Taxonomy tilt factors
Backtest tab shows historical WACI reduction vs parent index

acronyms.def: EU climate benchmark with ≥50% WACI reduction and 7%/yr decarbonisation
EU climate benchmark with ≥30% WACI reduction and 7%/yr decarbonisation
Portfolio-level carbon intensity metric used for PAB/CTB compliance

dataLineage.flow: Holdings → CI data → WACI
dataLineage.output: Parent benchmark WACI

dataLineage.flow: Company-level aligned revenue → tilt factors
dataLineage.output: Green revenue upweights

/climate-benchmark-constructor.valueSummary: PAB requires ≥50% WACI reduction vs parent at inception, then 7%/yr minimum. CTB requires ≥30% then 7%/yr. Fossil fuel revenue thresholds: >1% coal or >10% oil/gas triggers PAB exclusion.

/climate-capital-adequacy.title: Climate Capital Adequacy
/climate-capital-adequacy.description: Climate-adjusted capital adequacy engine for banks and insurers. Computes climate-risk-adjusted Risk-Weighted Assets (RWA), supplementary Pillar 2 climate capital buffers, and transition/physical scenario stress overlays per ECB and BCBS climate risk supervisory guidance.

calculationEngine.formula: ClimateRWA_i = RWA_i × (1 + α×PhysicalRisk_i + β×TransitionRisk_i); CapitalBuffer = max(0, ClimateRWA–RWA)
calculationEngine.brief: Physical risk multiplier α calibrated to ECB climate stress test haircuts by sector and geography. Transition risk multiplier β calibrated to NGFS scenario transition cost curves. Climate capital buffer = excess of climate-adjusted RWA over standard RWA, held as Pillar 2 add-on. Concentration adjustment for carbon-intensive sector lending concentration above ECB threshold.

dataPoints.name: Climate RWA Uplift
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage increase in RWA after climate risk adjustment

dataPoints.name: Pillar 2 Climate Buffer
dataPoints.interpretation: Additional capital requirement for climate risk under Pillar 2 SREP

dataPoints.name: Carbon-Intensive Concentration
dataPoints.interpretation: Lending concentration in carbon-intensive sectors triggering supervisory attention

dataPoints.name: Physical Risk Haircut
dataPoints.interpretation: Asset value reduction under severe physical scenario applied to RWA

userInteraction: Input loan book sector and geography breakdown
Physical Risk tab applies ECB haircut factors by sector
Transition Risk tab applies NGFS scenario multipliers
Capital Buffer Calculator shows Pillar 2 add-on requirement
Concentration tab flags carbon-intensive sector limits
references: ECB Climate Risk Supervisory Review and Stress Test 2022
BCBS d532 Climate-Related Financial Risks Principles

acronyms.def: Bank assets adjusted by credit risk weights under Basel III
ECB annual review setting Pillar 2 capital requirements
International standard-setter for bank prudential regulation
EU implementation of Basel III with climate risk provisions

dataLineage.flow: Sector × geography → RWA breakdown
dataLineage.output: Standard RWA by exposure

dataLineage.flow: Haircuts → climate multipliers
dataLineage.output: Climate-adjusted RWA and Pillar 2 buffer

/climate-capital-adequacy.valueSummary: Climate RWA uplift = standard RWA × (1 + α×physical + β×transition). ECB typically calibrates combined multiplier at 5–25% for carbon-intensive sector exposures under 2°C disorderly transition.

/climate-claims-forecasting.title: Climate Claims Forecasting
/climate-claims-forecasting.description: Actuarial engine for climate-adjusted insurance claims forecasting. Projects loss ratios, claims frequency, and severity trends under physical climate scenarios for property, agricultural, and liability lines using CAT model integration and climate scenario overlays.

calculationEngine.formula: E[Loss](t) = Σ_{perils} FreqBase(t) × SevBase(t) × ClimateMultiplier(t,RCP); ClimateMultiplier(t,RCP) = 1 + γ(RCP) × (t–t₀)
standards: Lloyd's Market Association Climate Change Scenarios
calculationEngine.brief: Baseline claims frequency and severity derived from 20-year historical NatCat loss data by peril and region. Climate multiplier applied linearly with rate parameter γ calibrated to IPCC AR6 projected hazard intensity changes by RCP. Attritional loss trends separated from CAT loss trends. Demand surge modelled as post-event severity uplift (15–35% for major events).

dataPoints.name: Climate Frequency Multiplier
dataPoints.interpretation: Multiplication factor applied to baseline claims frequency under climate scenario

dataPoints.name: Severity Trend
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual severity increase rate combining economic growth and climate intensification

dataPoints.name: Combined Loss Ratio
dataPoints.interpretation: Insurance loss ratio; above 100% indicates underwriting loss

dataPoints.name: CAT Loss Load
dataPoints.interpretation: Catastrophe loss component of total projected claims

userInteraction: Select insurance line: property, agriculture, or liability
Peril Selection tab activates hazard-specific climate multipliers
Frequency Model projects event counts under selected RCP
Severity Model projects cost per event with demand surge
Loss Ratio Dashboard shows combined ratio trend to 2050
references: IPCC AR6 Interactive Atlas Hazard Projections
Institute and Faculty of Actuaries Climate Risk Guidance

acronyms.def: Large-scale natural or man-made event generating above-attritional insurance losses
IPCC emission scenario; RCP2.6, 4.5, 6.0, 8.5
Natural disaster category tracked by reinsurance databases

dataLineage.flow: 20-yr loss data → baseline frequency/severity
dataLineage.output: Attritional and CAT base rates

dataLineage.flow: Hazard intensity changes → climate multiplier
dataLineage.output: Climate-adjusted projected losses

/climate-claims-forecasting.valueSummary: Climate-adjusted expected loss = baseline E[Loss] × ClimateMultiplier(t, RCP). Multiplier compounds at 1–3%/yr depending on peril and scenario. Flood and wildfire show highest multiplier acceleration under RCP8.5.

/climate-credit-integration.title: Climate Credit Integration Engine
/climate-credit-integration.description: Integration layer connecting carbon credit positions from the CarbonCreditContext data bus with portfolio-level climate analytics. Maps credit retirements to Scope 1/2/3 offset claims, validates additionality, and adjusts portfolio temperature score and financed emissions.

calculationEngine.methodology: Credit retirement offset adjustment to portfolio emissions
calculationEngine.formula: AdjustedFE = GrossFE – Σ(RetiredCredits_i × QualityFactor_i); AdjustedITR = f(AdjustedFE)
calculationEngine.brief: Gross financed emissions (FE) from PCAF methodology. Retired carbon credits offset FE only if they meet quality criteria: ICVCM CCP score ≥60 and additionality verified. Quality factor (0–1) discounts low-integrity credits. Adjusted ITR recalculated from adjusted FE using portfolio temperature model. VCMI Claims Code governs permissible claims (Gold/Silver/Bronze tier based on credit quality and scope coverage).

dataPoints.name: Gross Financed Emissions
dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio emissions before carbon credit offset adjustment

dataPoints.name: Offset Quality Factor
dataPoints.interpretation: Credit quality discount; 1.0 for CCP-eligible, lower for non-eligible

dataPoints.name: Net Financed Emissions
dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio emissions after quality-adjusted credit retirements

dataPoints.name: VCMI Claims Tier
dataPoints.interpretation: Permissible voluntary claim tier based on credit quality and abatement coverage

userInteraction: Link to CarbonCreditContext to import retired credit positions
Quality Screening tab applies ICVCM CCP filter
Offset Mapping tab allocates retired credits to emission scopes
Adjusted Metrics tab shows net FE and adjusted ITR
VCMI Claims tab determines permissible claim tier
references: GHG Protocol Corporate Standard Ch.9 Offsets

acronyms.def: Portfolio-attributed GHG emissions calculated under PCAF methodology
Body governing permissible voluntary carbon market claims
Credit quality discount multiplier applied to offset claim

dataLineage.flow: Retired credit registry data → offset pool
dataLineage.output: Available offsets by quality tier

dataLineage.flow: Gross FE – QF-weighted retirements → net FE
dataLineage.output: Adjusted financed emissions

/climate-credit-integration.valueSummary: Net FE = gross FE minus quality-adjusted credit retirements. VCMI Gold tier requires 100% of residual emissions covered by CCP-eligible credits and near-term SBTi target in place.

/climate-cvar-suite.title: Climate Conditional Value-at-Risk Suite
/climate-cvar-suite.description: Climate-adjusted Conditional Value-at-Risk (CVaR) engine for portfolio risk management. Integrates physical and transition scenario losses into tail risk metrics using NGFS scenario set, Expected Shortfall methodology, and climate factor augmented Monte Carlo simulation.

calculationEngine.methodology: Climate-augmented CVaR via scenario-weighted Expected Shortfall
calculationEngine.formula: CVaR_climate(q) = E[Loss | Loss > VaR_q] weighted by π_s for s ∈ {NGFS scenarios}; π_s from scenario probability weights
calculationEngine.brief: Standard CVaR at 95th percentile augmented with climate scenario losses. Monte Carlo draws from multi-factor return distribution augmented by physical (heat, flood, wildfire) and transition (carbon price, stranded asset) risk factors. Scenario probability weights (π_s) applied per NGFS likelihood assessment. Climate CVaR = probability-weighted average loss in tail conditional on climate scenario realisation.

dataPoints.name: Climate CVaR (95%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Expected portfolio loss in worst 5% of outcomes under climate scenarios

dataPoints.name: Physical Risk Contribution
dataPoints.formula: Physical factor share of tail loss
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of climate CVaR attributable to physical hazard factors

dataPoints.name: Transition Risk Contribution
dataPoints.formula: Transition factor share of tail loss
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of climate CVaR attributable to transition risk factors

dataPoints.name: Scenario Probability Weights
dataPoints.interpretation: Probability assigned to each of 5 NGFS climate scenarios

userInteraction: Select portfolio and confidence level (95% or 99%)

CVaR Decomposition shows physical vs transition contribution
Sensitivity tab tests CVaR to carbon price and warming assumptions
references: BCBS d532 Climate-Related Financial Risk Principles

acronyms.def: Expected loss in worst quantile of outcome distribution (Expected Shortfall)
Loss threshold exceeded with probability (1–q)

acronyms.full: Network for Greening the Financial System
acronyms.def: Central bank network producing climate scenarios for financial sector

dataLineage.flow: Carbon price + warming trajectories → risk factors
dataLineage.output: Climate scenario loss factors

dataLineage.flow: Historical covariance + climate factor loading → Monte Carlo
dataLineage.output: Climate-adjusted tail loss distribution

/climate-cvar-suite.valueSummary: Climate CVaR augments standard VaR/ES with NGFS scenario losses. Probability-weighted across 5 scenarios. Physical risk dominates tail in RCP8.5 late-century; transition risk dominates under disorderly 1.5°C.

/climate-data-marketplace.title: Climate Data Marketplace
/climate-data-marketplace.description: Curated catalogue and procurement hub for third-party climate and ESG data providers. Covers 60+ datasets across physical hazard, transition risk, company disclosures, carbon markets, and nature data. Includes data quality scoring, coverage assessment, and trial data access.

calculationEngine.formula: DataScore_p = 0.30×Coverage + 0.25×Accuracy + 0.20×Timeliness + 0.15×Methodology + 0.10×Price
calculationEngine.brief: Coverage score = fraction of portfolio companies with non-blank data from provider. Accuracy assessed via back-test against Scope 1 disclosures where available (correlation with CDP reported data). Timeliness = lag between reporting year-end and data availability. Methodology score based on transparency of estimation model documentation. Price score inversely scales with per-record cost.

dataPoints.name: Data Coverage Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Percentage of portfolio universe with non-missing data from provider

dataPoints.name: Accuracy vs CDP
dataPoints.interpretation: Correlation between estimated and self-reported Scope 1 for CDP disclosers

dataPoints.name: Data Vintage Lag
dataPoints.formula: Calendar months from year-end to release
dataPoints.interpretation: Time lag between reporting year-end and dataset availability

dataPoints.name: Composite Quality Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Overall data quality ranking for provider comparison

/climate-finance-hub.title: Climate Finance Hub
/climate-finance-hub.description: Integrated climate finance analytics hub tracking green bond issuance, sustainability-linked loan pricing, blended finance structures, and climate ODA flows. Covers use-of-proceeds verification, KPI step-down mechanics, and OECD Rio Marker accounting.

calculationEngine.methodology: Climate finance flow accounting and KPI step-down pricing
calculationEngine.formula: SLL_Spread(t) = BaseSpread × (1 – StepDown × KPI_achieved(t)); GreenFinanceTotal = Σ(GreenBonds + SLL + BlendedFinance + ODA)
calculationEngine.brief: Green bond use-of-proceeds tracked via project register against ICMA eligible categories. SLL pricing: KPI step-down (typically 5–15bps) triggered if SPT achieved at measurement date. Blended finance: first-loss tranche sizing uses expected loss coverage model. ODA climate finance: OECD Rio Marker II (principal objective) and I (significant objective) coding drives additionality assessment.

dataPoints.interpretation: Total proceeds allocated to eligible green projects

dataPoints.interpretation: Interest rate reduction triggered by hitting sustainability performance target

dataPoints.interpretation: Ratio of private finance mobilised per unit of concessional capital

dataPoints.interpretation: Climate-focused ODA calculated using Rio Marker weighting

SLL Pricing tab models KPI step-down schedules
Blended Finance tab structures first-loss tranche sizing

KPI threshold triggering SLL interest rate adjustment
Government aid meeting OECD DAC concessionality criteria

/climate-finance-tracker.title: Climate Finance Tracker
/climate-finance-tracker.description: Real-time tracking of climate finance commitments versus disbursements across public and private sources. Monitors progress toward UNFCCC $100Bn goal, NDC financing gaps, and Article 6.4 credit flow accounting with country-level finance need assessment.

calculationEngine.methodology: Climate finance gap = need minus committed minus disbursed
calculationEngine.formula: FinGap(c) = NDCNeed(c) – Committed(c) – Disbursed(c); GlobalGap = Σ_c FinGap(c)
standards: CPI Global Landscape of Climate Finance
calculationEngine.brief: Country-level NDC financing need estimated from nationally determined contribution implementation costs (UNFCCC SCF compilation). Committed finance from OECD DAC CRS tracked by Rio Marker. Disbursed finance from bilateral and multilateral development bank project data. Article 6.4 credit flows tracked via UNFCCC Supervisory Body. Finance gap = need minus (committed + disbursed).

dataPoints.name: Global $100Bn Commitment Progress
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual climate finance mobilised toward developed-country $100Bn commitment

dataPoints.name: NDC Financing Gap
dataPoints.interpretation: Aggregate developing country climate financing shortfall

dataPoints.name: Adaptation Finance Share
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of climate finance allocated to adaptation vs mitigation

dataPoints.name: Article 6.4 Credit Flow
dataPoints.interpretation: International carbon credit flow under Paris Agreement Article 6.4 mechanism

CPI Global Landscape of Climate Finance 2023

acronyms.def: Country-level climate pledge submitted under Paris Agreement
Carbon unit transferred between countries under Paris Agreement Art. 6

OECD database tracking aid and development finance flows

dataLineage.output: Annual climate finance flow

dataLineage.output: Country-level NDC finance gap

/climate-finance-tracker.valueSummary: Global climate finance mobilised reached ~$89Bn/yr in 2021 vs $100Bn commitment. Adaptation finance remains <10% of total. NDC implementation gap estimated at $3–5Tn/yr for developing countries through 2030.

/climate-financial-statements.title: Climate-Adjusted Financial Statements
/climate-financial-statements.description: Climate-adjusted income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow statement engine. Applies NGFS scenario overlays to financial projections, quantifies stranded asset impairments, carbon cost line items, and physical damage provisions under IFRS and ISSB S2 disclosure requirements.

calculationEngine.formula: EBIT_adj = EBIT_base – CarbonCost(t) – PhysicalDamage(t); AssetValue_adj = AssetValue × (1–ImpairmentPct)
calculationEngine.brief: Carbon cost line = Scope 1 emissions × internal carbon price (NGFS scenario-specific trajectory). Physical damage provision = asset replacement cost × annual loss probability from hazard model. Stranded asset impairment per IAS 36: recoverable amount test using climate-adjusted DCF. IFRS 9 ECL adjustment for climate transition risk in loan books. ISSB S2 requires quantitative financial impact disclosure for material climate risks.

dataPoints.name: Carbon Cost (P&L)
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual P&L impact of internal or regulatory carbon pricing on operations

dataPoints.name: Physical Damage Provision
dataPoints.interpretation: Provision for climate-related physical asset damage in financial period

dataPoints.name: Stranded Asset Impairment
dataPoints.interpretation: Write-down of assets where climate-adjusted NPV < carrying value

dataPoints.name: IFRS 9 ECL Uplift
dataPoints.interpretation: Expected credit loss increase from climate transition risk on loan book

userInteraction: Select NGFS scenario and projection horizon
Income Statement tab shows carbon cost and physical damage overlays
Balance Sheet tab applies stranded asset impairments
Cash Flow tab shows capex shifts for decarbonisation
ISSB S2 Disclosure tab formats financial impact narrative
references: TCFD Guidance on Financial Impact Disclosure 2021

acronyms.def: IFRS 9 probability-weighted estimate of credit losses over asset life
IFRS Foundation body issuing IFRS S1/S2 sustainability disclosure standards
IFRS standard governing impairment testing of non-financial assets

dataLineage.output: Climate-adjusted financials

dataLineage.flow: Scenario-specific price trajectory → carbon cost line

/climate-fintech-hub.title: Climate Fintech Hub
/climate-fintech-hub.description: Maps the climate fintech landscape including carbon accounting software, ESG data platforms, green lending tools, and sustainable investment technology providers.

calculationEngine.brief: Counts verified climate fintech deployments against total addressable market segment.

/climate-insurance.title: Climate Insurance Analytics
/climate-insurance.description: Analyses climate insurance product design, actuarial pricing under physical risk scenarios, and quantifies the protection gap between economic and insured losses.

calculationEngine.formula: PGR = 1 – (Insured Loss / Economic Loss)
calculationEngine.brief: Divides total insured catastrophe losses by total economic losses to derive uninsured fraction.

dataPoints.interpretation: Projected increase in insured losses by 2050 relative to 2020 baseline under RCP 4.5–8.5.

IPCC greenhouse gas concentration trajectory used for climate scenario modelling.
Long-run expected annual loss from a peril, integrating across the full loss exceedance probability curve.

/climate-legal-intelligence-dashboard.title: Climate Legal Intelligence Dashboard
/climate-legal-intelligence-dashboard.description: Aggregates and analyses climate litigation filings, court outcomes, and regulatory enforcement actions to surface legal risk trends across jurisdictions and counterparties.
