/climate-litigation-risk-scorer.title: Climate Litigation Risk Scorer
/climate-litigation-risk-scorer.description: Scores individual entities on their exposure to climate litigation using disclosure quality, emissions trajectory, greenwashing signals, and jurisdictional enforcement intensity.

calculationEngine.formula: ELRS = w₁×Disclosure + w₂×EmissionsTrajectory + w₃×GreenwashSignal + w₄×JurisdictionIntensity
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted composite of four risk dimensions scored 0–100; weights sum to 1.0 and are calibrated to historical litigation outcomes.

dataPoints.name: High-Risk Entity Threshold
dataPoints.interpretation: Entities scoring 70 or above are classified as high litigation risk based on historical case filing rates.

dataPoints.name: Greenwashing Case Growth
dataPoints.interpretation: Year-on-year increase in greenwashing-specific climate litigation filings globally.

userInteraction: Collect entity-level emissions data, climate targets, and public disclosure quality scores
Assess greenwashing signals: target specificity, interim milestones, verification status
Weight jurisdictional enforcement intensity using historical prosecution rates and regulatory framework scores
Aggregate into ELRS; flag P0 risk entities for immediate legal due diligence
references: UNEP Global Trends in Climate Change Litigation 2023
FSB Report on Climate-Related Financial Risks 2022

acronyms.def: Composite score (0–100) representing an entity's probability of being named in climate litigation.
A corporate GHG reduction target aligned with climate science to limit warming to 1.5°C or 2°C.

dataLineage.source: Corporate disclosures, CDP submissions, court filing databases, regulatory registers
dataLineage.flow: Multi-factor scoring, greenwashing NLP flags, jurisdictional intensity mapping
dataLineage.output: Entity risk scores, peer benchmarking, red-flag summary for investment committees

/climate-litigation-risk-scorer.valueSummary: Provides investment and legal teams with a systematic, evidence-based entity scoring tool to integrate climate litigation risk into credit, equity, and ESG ratings workflows.

/climate-mortality-longevity.title: Climate Mortality & Longevity Analytics
/climate-mortality-longevity.description: Models the impact of climate-driven heat stress, air quality deterioration, and extreme weather on mortality rates and longevity assumptions for life insurance and pension liabilities.

calculationEngine.formula: CAMR = BaselineMR × (1 + Σ HazardImpactᵢ)
calculationEngine.brief: Applies additive hazard multipliers (heat, air quality, vector-borne disease) to baseline age-sex mortality tables.

dataPoints.name: Heat-Attributable Deaths (2023)
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated global annual deaths attributable to heat exposure above 1990 baseline temperatures.

dataPoints.name: Longevity Liability Shift (2°C)
dataPoints.interpretation: Reduction in expected lifespan under 2°C scenario relative to no-climate-change baseline, affecting annuity pricing.

userInteraction: Load age-sex mortality tables by country and apply climate hazard adjustment factors
Map heat index trends by region using SSP scenarios (SSP2-4.5, SSP5-8.5)
Adjust life expectancy and curtate future lifetime for portfolio geographies
Reprice annuity reserves and term life premiums under adjusted mortality assumptions
references: Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change 2023
Institute and Faculty of Actuaries Climate Mortality Working Paper 2022

acronyms.def: Mortality rate modified by climate hazard impact factors applied to baseline actuarial tables.
IPCC scenario framework combining socioeconomic development assumptions with greenhouse gas trajectories.
UK professional body for actuaries, issuing climate risk guidance for life and general insurance.

dataLineage.source: National life tables, climate hazard indices, epidemiological literature
dataLineage.flow: Hazard factor derivation, mortality table adjustment, liability repricing
dataLineage.output: Adjusted mortality curves, reserve sensitivity outputs, longevity risk dashboards

/climate-mortality-longevity.valueSummary: Enables life insurers, reinsurers, and pension funds to incorporate climate-driven mortality shifts into actuarial assumptions, reserve adequacy testing, and product pricing.

/climate-nature-repo.title: Climate-Nature Repository
/climate-nature-repo.description: Serves as a unified data repository integrating climate risk metrics and nature/biodiversity data to support dual climate-nature risk assessment and TNFD-aligned reporting.

calculationEngine.formula: IRI = α×ClimateRisk + β×NatureRisk
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted combination of physical climate risk and nature dependency/impact scores at asset or portfolio level; weights α and β calibrated to sector materiality.

dataPoints.name: Datasets Integrated
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of distinct climate and nature datasets harmonised in the repository spanning biodiversity, water, land use, and physical climate hazards.

dataPoints.name: TNFD Coverage
dataPoints.interpretation: Repository spans TNFD's four natural realms and six biome categories enabling LEAP-aligned disclosure.

userInteraction: Ingest climate hazard layers (flood, heat, drought) and nature risk layers (biodiversity intactness, ecosystem services)
Harmonise spatial resolution to common grid (0.1° × 0.1°)
Link assets and portfolios to repository via lat/long or facility identifiers
Compute integrated risk scores and expose via API for downstream modules

acronyms.def: Global framework for nature-related risk and opportunity assessment and reporting.
TNFD's four-phase nature risk assessment process.
Combined climate and nature risk score for an asset or portfolio.

dataLineage.source: IPBES, WRI, GBIF, satellite land-cover, NGFS climate hazard grids
dataLineage.flow: Spatial harmonisation, taxonomy linkage, dual risk scoring
dataLineage.output: Integrated risk layers, TNFD-ready disclosures, API endpoints for portfolio modules

/climate-nature-repo.valueSummary: Provides a single source of truth for combined climate and nature risk data, reducing duplication across modules and enabling consistent dual-risk disclosure under TCFD and TNFD.

/climate-news-sentiment-feed.title: Climate News Sentiment Feed
/climate-news-sentiment-feed.description: Applies real-time NLP analysis to climate-related news, regulatory announcements, and social media to generate sentiment scores and event-driven risk signals for portfolio monitoring.

calculationEngine.formula: SS = (Positive – Negative) / Total Articles × 100
calculationEngine.brief: Net sentiment index ranging from –100 (fully negative) to +100 (fully positive) computed over a rolling 24-hour article window.

dataPoints.name: Articles Processed Daily
dataPoints.interpretation: Volume of climate-relevant news articles ingested and scored per day across 200+ media sources.

dataPoints.name: Sentiment Signal Lag
dataPoints.interpretation: Median latency from article publication to sentiment score availability in the feed.

userInteraction: Ingest news via RSS, API, and web scraping across climate, regulatory, and financial news sources
Apply FinBERT and climate-domain NLP classifier to extract entity mentions and sentiment polarity
Aggregate scores by entity, sector, topic (transition, physical, litigation, policy)
Publish event-driven alerts when sentiment breaches configured thresholds

acronyms.def: AI techniques for extracting structured information and meaning from unstructured text.
A BERT language model fine-tuned on financial text corpora for sentiment and information extraction tasks.

dataLineage.source: News APIs, regulatory gazettes, social media streams, corporate press releases
dataLineage.flow: NLP classification, entity resolution, rolling sentiment aggregation
dataLineage.output: Sentiment time series, entity signal dashboards, threshold-triggered alerts

/climate-news-sentiment-feed.valueSummary: Delivers real-time climate sentiment intelligence to portfolio managers, risk officers, and analysts enabling early identification of reputational, regulatory, and market-moving climate events.

/climate-policy-intelligence.title: Climate Policy Intelligence
/climate-policy-intelligence.description: Tracks NDC ambition, carbon pricing mechanisms, climate legislation pipelines, and policy implementation progress across 190+ countries to support transition risk assessment.

calculationEngine.formula: PAS = Σ wᵢ × Policyᵢ / MaxScore
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted composite of NDC unconditional/conditional targets, carbon price level, sectoral coverage, and enforcement track record.

dataPoints.name: Carbon Pricing Jurisdictions
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of jurisdictions with operational carbon taxes or emissions trading systems as of 2024.

dataPoints.name: Global Weighted Carbon Price
dataPoints.interpretation: GDP-weighted average carbon price across all operational pricing instruments globally.

userInteraction: Aggregate NDC submissions and climate law databases by country and sector
Score NDC ambition: 1.5°C compatibility, conditionality, economy-wide coverage
Map carbon pricing instruments: ETS cap trajectory, tax rate, revenue use
Compute policy risk delta: gap between current policy trajectory and Paris-compatible pathway
references: Climate Change Laws of the World (Grantham Institute)

acronyms.def: A country's climate plan submitted under the Paris Agreement specifying emissions reduction targets and policies.
A cap-and-trade market mechanism that sets a limit on aggregate emissions and allows trading of allowances.
Composite metric rating a country's climate policy strength relative to science-based benchmarks.

dataLineage.source: UNFCCC NDC registry, World Bank CPD, Grantham climate laws database
dataLineage.flow: Ambition scoring, carbon price normalisation, trajectory gap analysis
dataLineage.output: Country policy dashboards, sector transition risk flags, carbon price forecasts

/climate-policy-intelligence.valueSummary: Provides actionable policy intelligence for transition risk modelling, sovereign bond analysis, and regulatory horizon scanning in climate-exposed investment portfolios.

/climate-portfolio-optimizer.title: Climate Portfolio Optimizer
/climate-portfolio-optimizer.description: Implements multi-objective portfolio optimisation balancing financial return, risk, and climate objectives including temperature alignment, carbon intensity, and green revenue exposure.

calculationEngine.formula: CASR = (Rₚ – Rₑ) / (σₚ × (1 + λ×CIₚ))
calculationEngine.brief: Penalises portfolio Sharpe ratio by a climate intensity loading factor λ and carbon intensity CIₚ to embed transition risk in optimisation objective.

dataPoints.name: Efficient Frontier Solutions
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of Pareto-optimal portfolios generated per optimisation run across return, risk, and climate dimensions.

dataPoints.name: Carbon Intensity Reduction vs Benchmark
dataPoints.interpretation: Typical carbon intensity reduction achievable with <0.5% tracking error versus a standard equity benchmark.

userInteraction: Define optimisation universe, constraints (sector limits, ESG floor, liquidity), and climate objectives
Estimate expected returns using factor model; compute covariance matrix
Solve mean-variance-climate optimisation using quadratic programming with climate penalty term
Generate efficient frontier; report carbon intensity, temperature score, and green revenue at each solution
references: Pedersen, Fitzgibbons & Pomorski (2021) Responsible Investing: The ESG-Efficient Frontier
FTSE Russell Smart Sustainability Index Series

acronyms.def: Modified Sharpe ratio that penalises portfolio performance for elevated carbon intensity.
Portfolio weighted-average tCO₂e per $M revenue or EVIC.
Portfolio-level temperature alignment metric expressed in degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels.

dataLineage.source: Return and risk factor data, carbon intensity and ITR scores, green revenue datasets
dataLineage.flow: Quadratic optimisation solver, climate penalty calibration, efficient frontier generation
dataLineage.output: Optimal portfolio weights, carbon/temperature metrics, green revenue breakdown

/climate-portfolio-optimizer.valueSummary: Enables portfolio managers to construct climate-aligned portfolios that meet financial objectives while systematically reducing transition risk and increasing climate solution exposure.

/climate-reserve-adequacy.title: Climate Reserve Adequacy
/climate-reserve-adequacy.description: Assesses the adequacy of insurer technical reserves under physical and transition climate scenarios, quantifying reserve shortfalls and capital implications across product lines.

calculationEngine.formula: CRAR = ClimateAdjustedReserves / UnadjustedReserves
calculationEngine.brief: Ratio above 1.0 indicates climate loading increases reserve requirements; below 1.0 signals potential over-reserving for transition-benefiting lines.

dataPoints.name: Reserve Uplift (RCP 8.5, 2050)
dataPoints.interpretation: Range of reserve increases for property and liability lines under high-warming scenario by 2050.

dataPoints.name: CBES Reserve Shortfall Signal
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated aggregate reserve gap identified in Bank of England Climate Biennial Exploratory Scenario for the UK insurance market.

userInteraction: Stratify in-force portfolio by product line, geography, and climate hazard exposure
Apply NGFS/RCP scenario severity multipliers to loss development factors
Rerun chain-ladder and Bornhuetter-Ferguson reserving under climate-adjusted development patterns
Calculate CRAR by line; flag lines where shortfall exceeds 10% threshold for capital action
references: EIOPA Climate Sensitivity Analysis of the Insurance Sector 2022
Bank of England Climate Biennial Exploratory Scenario 2021

acronyms.def: Ratio comparing climate-scenario reserves to unadjusted reserves to assess adequacy.
Bank of England stress test assessing long-term climate risk to UK financial system participants.

acronyms.full: European Insurance and Occupational Pensions Authority
acronyms.def: EU supervisory authority for insurance and pension sectors.

dataLineage.source: Policy data, historical loss triangles, climate hazard severity projections
dataLineage.flow: Scenario-adjusted loss development, chain-ladder/BF repricing, reserve gap quantification
dataLineage.output: Reserve adequacy ratios by line, capital shortfall estimates, regulatory stress outputs

/climate-reserve-adequacy.valueSummary: Supports actuarial teams and prudential supervisors in identifying climate-driven reserve gaps before they crystallise into solvency issues, enabling proactive capital and product management.

/climate-resilient-design.title: Climate Resilient Design
/climate-resilient-design.description: Quantifies physical climate adaptation options for infrastructure and real estate assets, scoring resilience measures by cost-benefit, hazard reduction, and asset life extension.

calculationEngine.formula: ABCR = NPV(Risk Reduction) / AdaptationCapEx
calculationEngine.brief: Net present value of avoided losses from a climate adaptation measure divided by the upfront capital expenditure; ABCR > 1 indicates cost-effective adaptation.

dataPoints.name: Average ABCR for Flood Adaptation
dataPoints.interpretation: Benefit-cost range for flood resilience measures in urban infrastructure documented in global literature.

dataPoints.name: Asset Life Extension from Adaptation
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated additional asset service life from implementing appropriate physical resilience upgrades.

userInteraction: Map asset to primary physical hazards using lat/long and hazard return-period layers
Generate adaptation option menu (elevation, flood barriers, green infrastructure, materials upgrade)
Model residual risk post-adaptation for each measure using engineering reduction factors
Calculate ABCR, payback period, and carbon co-benefits; rank options by combined score

acronyms.def: Ratio of expected risk reduction benefits to adaptation capital cost over a defined time horizon.
Present value of future cash flows discounted at a chosen rate; positive NPV indicates value creation.
Funds invested in physical assets or adaptations that extend asset life or reduce risk exposure.

dataLineage.source: Asset coordinates, engineering specifications, hazard return-period maps, adaptation cost databases
dataLineage.flow: Hazard linkage, residual risk modelling, NPV and ABCR calculation
dataLineage.output: Adaptation option rankings, ABCR heat maps, resilience certification inputs

/climate-resilient-design.valueSummary: Helps infrastructure owners, real estate investors, and lenders optimise adaptation investment decisions using rigorous cost-benefit analysis grounded in climate hazard science.

/climate-risk-budget-allocator.title: Climate Risk Budget Allocator
/climate-risk-budget-allocator.description: Allocates a portfolio's total climate risk budget across asset classes, sectors, and geographies using marginal contribution to climate VaR as the allocation driver.

calculationEngine.formula: MC-CVaRᵢ = ∂CVaRₚ / ∂wᵢ
calculationEngine.brief: Partial derivative of total portfolio Climate VaR with respect to asset weight; used to identify which positions consume disproportionate climate risk budget.

dataPoints.name: Typical Portfolio Climate VaR (RCP 4.5)
dataPoints.interpretation: Range of Climate Value-at-Risk for a diversified equity portfolio under RCP 4.5 scenario over a 15-year horizon.

dataPoints.name: Budget Breach Trigger
dataPoints.interpretation: Risk budget policy threshold; breaches trigger rebalancing review or sector exposure cap.

userInteraction: Compute asset-level Climate VaR using physical and transition risk scenarios
Aggregate to portfolio Climate VaR using weighted covariance structure
Decompose into marginal contributions by asset, sector, and geography
Compare allocations to risk budget limits; generate rebalancing recommendations

acronyms.def: The incremental change in portfolio Climate VaR for a unit increase in the weight of a given position.
Forward-looking estimate of portfolio value loss attributable to physical and transition climate risk under defined scenarios.

dataLineage.source: Position-level weights, Climate VaR scores, scenario probability distributions
dataLineage.flow: Marginal contribution decomposition, budget allocation optimisation
dataLineage.output: Budget utilisation dashboards, breach alerts, rebalancing trade lists

/climate-risk-budget-allocator.valueSummary: Provides portfolio risk managers with a systematic framework to enforce climate risk limits and optimise budget allocation without sacrificing return objectives.

/climate-risk-migration.title: Climate Risk Migration Analytics
/climate-risk-migration.description: Models population displacement driven by physical climate hazards (sea level rise, extreme heat, drought, flooding) and quantifies downstream economic and credit risks from migration flows.

calculationEngine.formula: CMPI = Σᵢ (Hazardᵢ × Exposureᵢ × Vulnerabilityᵢ) / AdaptationCapacity
calculationEngine.brief: Multi-hazard index combining hazard intensity, population exposure, socioeconomic vulnerability, and adaptive capacity; higher scores indicate elevated displacement risk.

dataPoints.name: Climate Migrants by 2050 (Base)
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated internal climate migrants across 6 regions under reference scenario by 2050.

dataPoints.name: GDP Loss per 1% Population Outflow
dataPoints.interpretation: Range of GDP reduction in origin countries from climate-driven population outflow of 1% of workforce.

userInteraction: Map population density against multi-hazard climate risk layers under SSP1/2/5 scenarios
Estimate displacement probability using CMPI thresholds calibrated to historical displacement data
Model receiving region economic and social infrastructure strain from net in-migration
Quantify credit risk implications for sovereign bonds and real estate portfolios in high-outflow regions
references: IMF Working Paper WP/21/200 Climate and Migration

acronyms.def: Composite index measuring the combined hazard, exposure, vulnerability, and adaptive capacity driving displacement risk.
IPCC scenario framework integrating socioeconomic development trajectories with climate projections.
UN agency tracking and analysing global migration patterns including climate-induced displacement.

dataLineage.source: Population grids, physical hazard maps, socioeconomic vulnerability indices, historical displacement records
dataLineage.flow: CMPI computation, displacement scenario modelling, GDP and credit impact estimation
dataLineage.output: Migration flow maps, sovereign credit risk flags, real estate demand shift projections

/climate-risk-migration.valueSummary: Equips sovereign debt analysts, real estate investors, and development finance institutions with forward-looking migration risk assessments to incorporate demographic climate risk into portfolio decisions.

/climate-solution-taxonomy.title: Climate Solution Taxonomy
/climate-solution-taxonomy.description: Provides a structured IEA Net Zero-aligned taxonomy of climate technology and nature-based solutions, classifying investments by mitigation potential, technology readiness, and financial characteristics.

calculationEngine.formula: MPS = AnnualAbatement(GtCO₂e) × TRL Weight × CostCurve Position
calculationEngine.brief: Combines a solution's annual abatement potential with technology readiness level weight and marginal abatement cost position on the global cost curve.

dataPoints.name: Technologies Mapped
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of distinct climate solutions categorised in the taxonomy spanning energy, transport, buildings, industry, AFOLU, and CDR.

dataPoints.name: Required Clean Energy Investment (2030)
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual clean energy investment needed globally by 2030 to remain on a 1.5°C trajectory.

userInteraction: Structure taxonomy hierarchy: sector → sub-sector → technology → solution
Assign IEA NZE abatement potential, technology readiness level (TRL 1–9), and marginal abatement cost
Map EU Taxonomy alignment and Paris Agreement Article 2.1(c) finance alignment criteria
Enable filtering by investor type, geography, and ticket size for deal sourcing
references: IEA Net Zero by 2050 2021 and 2023 Update
IPCC AR6 WG3 Chapter 12 Cross-sector Perspectives
EU Taxonomy Regulation Technical Screening Criteria

acronyms.def: Nine-point scale (1–9) measuring the maturity of a technology from basic research to full commercial deployment.

acronyms.full: Agriculture, Forestry and Other Land Use
acronyms.def: IPCC sector covering land-based emissions and removals including deforestation and soil carbon.
Technologies and practices that remove CO₂ from the atmosphere, including BECCS, DAC, and enhanced weathering.
Composite metric ranking climate solutions by abatement scale, readiness, and cost competitiveness.

dataLineage.source: IEA technology briefs, IPCC abatement cost data, EU Taxonomy technical annexes
dataLineage.flow: Taxonomy structuring, TRL assignment, abatement potential normalisation
dataLineage.output: Searchable taxonomy browser, deal sourcing filters, mitigation potential rankings

/climate-solution-taxonomy.valueSummary: Enables climate investors, green bond issuers, and taxonomy compliance teams to systematically identify, classify, and evaluate climate solutions against internationally recognised frameworks.

/climate-sovereign-bonds.title: Climate Sovereign Bonds
/climate-sovereign-bonds.description: Analyses sovereign green, sustainability, and sustainability-linked bond issuances for use of proceeds alignment, NDC consistency, and credit-adjusted green premium (greenium) quantification.

calculationEngine.formula: Greenium = YTM(Conventional) – YTM(Green)
calculationEngine.brief: Yield spread between a sovereign's conventional bond and its green bond of equivalent duration, reflecting market pricing of green credentials; negative greenium means green bond yields less.

dataPoints.name: Sovereign Green Bond Issuance (2023)
dataPoints.interpretation: Total sovereign green, social, and sustainability bond issuance in 2023 across 40+ issuing governments.

dataPoints.name: Average Sovereign Greenium
dataPoints.interpretation: Range of yield concession on sovereign green bonds versus conventional comparators across primary markets.

userInteraction: Collect sovereign GSS bond prospectuses and use of proceeds frameworks
Assess alignment with ICMA Green Bond Principles and NDC sectoral targets
Compute greenium using interpolated yield curve comparison for matched-maturity conventional bonds
Score issuer climate credibility: framework robustness, reporting quality, impact metrics
references: BIS Working Paper No. 923 (2021) Green Bonds and Carbon Emissions
OECD Green and Sustainable Finance Monitor 2023
Climate Bonds Initiative State of the Market 2023

acronyms.def: Collective term for labelled bonds whose proceeds are directed to environmental or social projects.
Trade body publishing the Green Bond Principles and other sustainable finance market standards.
Internal rate of return of a bond if held to maturity, accounting for all coupon payments and capital gain/loss.

dataLineage.source: Bond prospectuses, yield curve data, NDC frameworks, post-issuance impact reports
dataLineage.flow: Use of proceeds alignment scoring, greenium calculation, issuer credibility assessment
dataLineage.output: Bond league tables, greenium time series, NDC alignment heat maps

/climate-sovereign-bonds.valueSummary: Provides fixed income investors and sovereign debt analysts with rigorous tools to evaluate climate sovereign bonds on both financial and impact dimensions, supporting green portfolio allocation decisions.

/climate-stress-test.title: Climate Stress Test Suite
/climate-stress-test.description: Implements NGFS and ECB climate stress testing frameworks to quantify transition and physical risk losses across credit, equity, and insurance portfolios under orderly, disorderly, and hot-house-world scenarios.

calculationEngine.formula: CSL = Σᵢ EADᵢ × PDᵢ(Δscenario) × LGDᵢ
calculationEngine.brief: Aggregates exposure-at-default weighted by scenario-conditional probability of default and loss given default across all obligors.

dataPoints.name: NGFS Scenarios Implemented
dataPoints.interpretation: Full set of NGFS Phase IV scenarios: Orderly (Below 2°C, Net Zero 2050), Disorderly (Divergent Net Zero, Delayed Transition), Hot House World (Nationally Determined, Current Policies).

dataPoints.name: ECB Aggregate Credit Loss (adverse)
dataPoints.interpretation: Range of climate stress credit losses as percentage of risk-weighted assets in ECB's economy-wide climate stress test.

userInteraction: Map portfolio exposures to NACE/NAICS sector codes for NGFS transition risk factor assignment
Apply physical hazard overlays using NGFS climate scenarios and asset geolocation
Compute scenario-conditional PD uplifts using regression on carbon price, energy cost, and physical damage paths
Aggregate CSL by scenario, sector, and time horizon; calculate capital adequacy implications
references: NGFS Climate Scenarios for Central Banks and Supervisors Phase IV 2023
ECB Economy-Wide Climate Stress Test Methodology 2021
EBA Discussion Paper on Climate Stress Testing 2021

acronyms.full: Network for Greening the Financial System
acronyms.def: Coalition of central banks and supervisors developing climate scenario standards for financial risk assessment.
Aggregate credit or market loss attributable to climate scenario conditions above baseline expectations.
Expected loan or exposure value at the time a borrower defaults.
Fraction of exposure not recovered after a default event, expressed as a percentage of EAD.

dataLineage.source: Portfolio credit data, NGFS scenario variable paths, physical hazard maps, sector carbon intensity
dataLineage.flow: PD uplift modelling, physical risk overlay, loss aggregation by scenario and horizon
dataLineage.output: Stress loss tables, capital impact analysis, sector vulnerability heat maps

/climate-stress-test.valueSummary: Provides banks, insurers, and asset managers with a regulatory-grade climate stress testing suite aligned with NGFS, ECB, and Bank of England frameworks to support supervisory submissions and internal capital planning.

/climate-tech.title: Climate Tech Intelligence
/climate-tech.description: Analyses the climate technology investment landscape including venture capital flows, patent activity, startup ecosystem mapping, and technology readiness progression across mitigation and adaptation sectors.

calculationEngine.formula: CTIGR = (Investmentₜ – Investmentₜ₋₁) / Investmentₜ₋₁ × 100
calculationEngine.brief: Year-on-year percentage growth in climate tech venture and growth capital deployment by sector and geography.

dataPoints.name: Global Climate Tech VC Investment (2023)
dataPoints.interpretation: Total venture and growth equity invested in climate technology companies globally in 2023.

dataPoints.name: Patent Filings Growth (Clean Energy)
dataPoints.interpretation: Compound annual growth rate in clean energy patent applications filed with major patent offices.

userInteraction: Map climate tech sectors: solar, wind, storage, hydrogen, EV, carbon removal, sustainable food, adaptation
Track VC and PE investment flows by stage (seed, Series A–D, growth), geography, and technology category
Monitor patent activity as leading indicator of technology maturity using EPO and USPTO databases
Score startups on technology readiness, commercial traction, and climate impact potential
references: European Patent Office Insights on Climate Technologies 2024

acronyms.def: Nine-point scale measuring technology maturity from basic research (TRL 1) to full commercial deployment (TRL 9).
Geometric mean annual growth rate over a specified period.
High-risk equity financing for early-stage technology companies.

dataLineage.source: Crunchbase/Dealroom VC data, EPO/USPTO patent databases, startup self-reporting, BNEF market intelligence
dataLineage.flow: Investment flow aggregation, patent trend analysis, TRL scoring, ecosystem mapping
dataLineage.output: Investment dashboards, patent heat maps, startup scorecards, sector maturity curves

/climate-tech.valueSummary: Provides climate investors, corporate strategists, and policy makers with data-driven intelligence on the climate technology ecosystem to guide investment allocation, partnership decisions, and innovation policy.

/co-investment.title: Co-Investment Analytics
/co-investment.description: Supports direct co-investment decision-making in climate and sustainability projects by providing deal screening, return modelling, ESG scoring, and LP syndication analytics.

calculationEngine.methodology: Co-Investment Return Adjusted for Climate Risk
calculationEngine.formula: CIRA = IRR – λ × ClimateVaR
calculationEngine.brief: Adjusts project IRR for climate-specific downside risk using a risk aversion parameter λ and project-level Climate VaR to enable apples-to-apples comparison across co-investment opportunities.

dataPoints.name: Avg Climate Infrastructure IRR
dataPoints.interpretation: Internal rate of return range for core climate infrastructure co-investments (renewables, green transport, water).

dataPoints.name: Co-Investment Share of PE Deals
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of private equity deals involving LP co-investment rights across infrastructure and growth equity strategies.

userInteraction: Screen deal pipeline against ESG and climate taxonomy alignment criteria
Build discounted cash flow model with climate scenario sensitivities (NGFS orderly/disorderly)
Score counterparty ESG quality, governance standards, and climate management maturity
Synthesise CIRA ranking, syndication fit, and concentration limits for investment committee
references: IFC Performance Standards on Environmental and Social Sustainability
PRI Guidance on Private Equity ESG Integration 2023

acronyms.def: Discount rate at which the net present value of a project's cash flows equals zero; primary private market return metric.
Investor in a private equity or infrastructure fund who co-invests alongside the general partner.

acronyms.full: Co-Investment Return Adjusted for Climate Risk
acronyms.def: IRR modified downward by a climate risk penalty to enable risk-adjusted co-investment comparison.

dataLineage.source: Deal documentation, project financial models, ESG due diligence reports, NGFS scenario paths
dataLineage.flow: DCF modelling, climate scenario sensitivity, CIRA calculation, ESG scoring
dataLineage.output: Deal scorecards, syndication recommendations, portfolio concentration reports

/co-investment.valueSummary: Enables institutional investors to systematically evaluate and compare direct co-investment opportunities in climate and sustainability projects on both financial and impact dimensions.

/commodity-deforestation.title: Commodity Deforestation Risk
/commodity-deforestation.description: Assesses supply chain deforestation risk embedded in commodity exposure across soy, palm oil, beef, timber, cocoa, and coffee using satellite-verified land-use change data and EUDR compliance frameworks.

calculationEngine.formula: DRS = Σ (Commodityᵢ × SourceCountryRiskᵢ × SupplyChainOpacityᵢ)
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted product of commodity volume, source country deforestation intensity, and supply chain transparency deficit.

dataPoints.name: Forest Loss Attributable to Commodity Production (2023)
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual tropical forest loss directly linked to commercial commodity agriculture and logging globally.

dataPoints.name: EUDR Affected Trade Value
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated value of EU commodity imports subject to EU Deforestation Regulation due diligence requirements.

userInteraction: Map commodity purchasing to origin geographies using supply chain disclosure and Trase trade flow data
Overlay Global Forest Watch deforestation alerts and Hansen annual tree cover loss data
Score each supply chain segment on deforestation risk: country risk × opacity × volume weight
Assess EUDR compliance readiness: geolocation traceability, due diligence statements, risk classification
references: Global Forest Watch Hansen Tree Cover Loss 2023
TNFD LEAP Nature Risk Assessment Framework

acronyms.def: Composite metric quantifying supply chain exposure to commodity-driven deforestation.
EU law requiring companies to ensure commodities placed on the EU market have not contributed to deforestation after 2020.
WRI platform providing near-real-time satellite monitoring of global forest cover change.

dataLineage.source: Trase trade flows, GFW deforestation alerts, CDP forest questionnaire, supplier disclosures
dataLineage.flow: Origin mapping, hazard overlay, DRS computation, EUDR readiness scoring
dataLineage.output: Commodity risk heat maps, supplier-level flags, EUDR compliance dashboards

/commodity-deforestation.valueSummary: Helps financial institutions, corporates, and supply chain managers identify, quantify, and mitigate deforestation risk in commodity supply chains while preparing for EUDR and TNFD disclosure requirements.

/nbs-finance.title: NbS Finance
/nbs-finance.description: Analyses the financing landscape for Nature-based Solutions including blue carbon, green carbon, biodiversity credits, ecosystem service payments, and blended finance structures.

calculationEngine.formula: NbS-ROC = (EcosystemServiceValue + CarbonRevenue + BiodiversityCredits) / ConservationCost
calculationEngine.brief: Total economic return on NbS investment combining carbon credit revenue, biodiversity credit income, and ecosystem service payments relative to conservation and restoration expenditure.

dataPoints.name: NbS Investment Gap (2030)
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual NbS investment shortfall relative to what is required to achieve biodiversity and climate targets by 2030.

dataPoints.name: Blue Carbon Credit Value
dataPoints.interpretation: Market price range for high-quality blue carbon (mangrove, seagrass, salt marsh) credits in voluntary markets.

userInteraction: Identify NbS project categories: afforestation, mangrove restoration, peatland conservation, grassland management
Model carbon sequestration potential using IPCC Tier 2/3 land-use emission factors
Quantify co-benefits: biodiversity, water regulation, coastal protection, livelihood
Structure blended finance: grant de-risking, first-loss tranche, carbon credit pre-purchase
references: IUCN Global Standard for Nature-based Solutions 2020
UNEP State of Finance for Nature 2023
Ecosystem Marketplace Voluntary Carbon Market Insights 2024

acronyms.def: Actions to protect, restore, or sustainably manage ecosystems that address societal challenges including climate change.
Market or policy mechanism compensating landowners for maintaining or enhancing ecosystem services.
Total value return metric for nature-based solution investments across carbon, biodiversity, and ecosystem service revenue streams.

dataLineage.source: Project documentation, IPCC land-use emission factors, carbon registry data, biodiversity credit prices
dataLineage.flow: Sequestration modelling, co-benefit quantification, blended finance structuring
dataLineage.output: Project finance models, NbS credit analytics, blended finance structure templates

/nbs-finance.valueSummary: Enables development finance institutions, conservation funds, and impact investors to evaluate, structure, and monitor NbS investments that deliver combined climate, biodiversity, and social co-benefits.

/ndc-alignment-tracker.title: NDC Alignment Tracker
/ndc-alignment-tracker.description: Tracks the ambition, conditionality, and implementation progress of National Determined Contributions across 190+ countries against Paris Agreement 1.5°C and 2°C compatible benchmarks.

calculationEngine.formula: NAG = EmissionsProjected(CurrentNDC) – EmissionsRequired(1.5°C Pathway)
calculationEngine.brief: Difference between emissions trajectory implied by a country's current NDC and the emissions level consistent with a 1.5°C global pathway in the same year.

dataPoints.name: Global NDC Ambition Gap (2030)
dataPoints.interpretation: Aggregate gap between current NDC trajectories and 1.5°C-compatible emissions by 2030.

dataPoints.name: Countries with 1.5°C Compatible NDCs
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of countries whose NDC commitments are rated as compatible with limiting warming to 1.5°C.

userInteraction: Ingest NDC submissions from UNFCCC registry; parse targets, base years, and conditionality
Benchmark against CAT-compatible 1.5°C and 2°C national emission budgets
Score implementation: policy coverage fraction, MRV quality, historical target adherence
Track NDC revision cycles and score ambition improvement across successive submissions
references: World Resources Institute Climate Watch NDC Database

acronyms.def: A country's self-determined climate action plan under the Paris Agreement updated every five years.
System for tracking and verifying greenhouse gas emissions data and policy implementation progress.
Quantitative shortfall between a country's NDC emission trajectory and a science-based compatible pathway.

dataLineage.source: UNFCCC NDC registry, CAT assessments, Climate Watch emissions data, IEA country energy data
dataLineage.flow: Target parsing, ambition gap calculation, implementation scoring
dataLineage.output: Country NDC scorecards, ambition gap rankings, transition risk flags for sovereign analysis

/ndc-alignment-tracker.valueSummary: Provides investors, policy analysts, and climate negotiators with a rigorous, data-driven assessment of global NDC ambition and implementation to support sovereign risk and transition risk analysis.

/net-zero-commitment-tracker.title: Net Zero Commitment Tracker
/net-zero-commitment-tracker.description: Evaluates the credibility of corporate and country net-zero pledges by assessing target coverage, interim milestones, Scope 3 inclusion, and independent verification quality.

calculationEngine.formula: NZCS = Σ wᵢ × Dimensionᵢ
standards: NewClimate Institute Corporate Climate Responsibility Monitor
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted composite of coverage (scope 1/2/3), interim target specificity, offset reliance, governance, and third-party verification; each dimension scored 0–100.

dataPoints.name: Corporate Net Zero Pledges (Fortune 500)
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of Fortune 500 companies with a publicly stated net-zero or carbon neutrality commitment.

dataPoints.name: High-Credibility Pledges
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of corporate net-zero commitments rated as high-integrity by the NewClimate Institute Corporate Climate Responsibility Monitor.

userInteraction: Collect net-zero pledges from SBTi registry, Net Zero Tracker, and company disclosures
Score coverage: year of net-zero, included gases, Scope 3 inclusion, offset cap
Assess interim milestones: presence, linearity, sectoral alignment with SBTi pathways
Flag greenwashing signals: over-reliance on offsets, absent governance, no verification
references: Net Zero Tracker (Energy & Climate Intelligence Unit) 2024
NewClimate Institute Corporate Climate Responsibility Monitor 2023
UN High Level Expert Group on Net Zero Pledges Report 2022

acronyms.def: Composite metric rating the robustness of a net-zero pledge across coverage, milestones, offsets, and governance.
Organisation that defines and validates corporate GHG reduction targets aligned with climate science.
UN panel established to define global standards for credible net-zero pledges.

dataLineage.source: SBTi registry, Net Zero Tracker database, company sustainability reports, third-party assurance statements
dataLineage.flow: Pledge parsing, credibility dimension scoring, greenwashing flag generation
dataLineage.output: Entity NZCS scores, peer benchmarking, engagement priority lists for active stewardship

/net-zero-commitment-tracker.valueSummary: Enables investors, rating agencies, and civil society to systematically distinguish genuine net-zero commitments from empty pledges, supporting stewardship, ESG ratings, and portfolio alignment decisions.

/net-zero-portfolio-alignment.title: Net Zero Portfolio Alignment
/net-zero-portfolio-alignment.description: Measures the alignment of investment portfolios with science-based net-zero trajectories using implied temperature rise, sector decarbonisation benchmarks, and portfolio temperature score methodologies.

calculationEngine.formula: PTS = Σ wᵢ × ITRᵢ
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted average of company-level Implied Temperature Rise scores across portfolio holdings; target PTS ≤ 1.5°C for Paris alignment.

dataPoints.name: Average Listed Equity Portfolio Temperature
dataPoints.interpretation: Average portfolio temperature score for a global equity index portfolio before climate-aligned rebalancing.

dataPoints.name: Net Zero Asset Owner Alliance Target
dataPoints.interpretation: Interim and long-term temperature alignment targets committed to by NZAOA member asset owners.

userInteraction: Collect company-level emissions data and SBT or long-term target commitments
Compute ITR using sector-specific carbon budget allocation and company decarbonisation trajectory
Weight ITRs by portfolio exposure (market value or EVIC-based)
Compare PTS to 1.5°C / 2°C benchmarks; identify top contributors to temperature overshoot
references: TCFD Portfolio Alignment Measurement: Technical Supplement 2021
MSCI Net Zero Target Tracker Methodology 2022
Net Zero Asset Owner Alliance Target Setting Protocol v3 2022

acronyms.def: Exposure-weighted average of holding-level implied temperature rise, representing the portfolio's warming trajectory.
Company-level temperature alignment metric derived from comparing actual and science-based emission trajectories.
Coalition of institutional investors committing to net-zero portfolios by 2050.

dataLineage.source: Company emissions data, SBT registry, sector carbon budgets, portfolio weights
dataLineage.flow: ITR computation, portfolio aggregation, benchmark gap analysis
dataLineage.output: Portfolio temperature dashboards, sector decarbonisation heat maps, rebalancing recommendations

/net-zero-portfolio-alignment.valueSummary: Provides asset owners and managers with a standardised, science-based framework to measure, report, and improve portfolio temperature alignment in line with Paris Agreement commitments.

/net-zero-portfolio-builder.title: Net Zero Portfolio Builder
/net-zero-portfolio-builder.description: Constructs investment portfolios explicitly targeting net-zero alignment by 2050 through constrained optimisation across emissions pathways, financial objectives, and climate solution tilts.

calculationEngine.formula: NZCS = α×FinancialScore + β×ClimateAlignmentScore + γ×GreenRevenueScore
calculationEngine.brief: Composite portfolio construction score balancing financial efficiency, Paris pathway alignment, and clean revenue exposure; weights α+β+γ=1.

dataPoints.name: Emission Reduction vs Benchmark (5yr)
dataPoints.interpretation: Minimum absolute emissions reduction target set for net-zero portfolio relative to benchmark over a 5-year rolling period.

dataPoints.name: Green Revenue Minimum Tilt
dataPoints.interpretation: Recommended minimum green revenue exposure to ensure portfolio alignment with climate solution provision.

userInteraction: Screen investable universe: exclude highest-emitting assets without credible transition plans
Apply PAII net-zero portfolio alignment criteria: WACI reduction, temperature score floor, green revenue tilt
Solve constrained optimisation: minimise tracking error subject to net-zero and financial constraints
Generate annual glide path: emission reduction schedule to net zero by 2050 with interim waypoints
references: IIGCC Paris Aligned Asset Owner Framework 2020

acronyms.def: Portfolio-level composite score measuring alignment with net-zero construction objectives.
Portfolio carbon intensity weighted by holding exposure; primary TCFD carbon metric.
Investor coalition developing practical frameworks for Paris-aligned portfolio construction.

dataLineage.source: Universe emissions data, SBT registry, green revenue taxonomies, return/risk factor data
dataLineage.flow: Screen and constrained optimisation, glide path modelling, PAII criteria scoring
dataLineage.output: Net-zero portfolio weights, emission trajectory, annual reporting metrics

/net-zero-portfolio-builder.valueSummary: Enables institutional investors to systematically construct Paris-aligned portfolios that deliver competitive financial returns while following a credible science-based pathway to net zero by 2050.

/ngfs-iea-scenario.title: NGFS/IEA Scenario Analytics
/ngfs-iea-scenario.description: Integrates NGFS climate scenarios with IEA Net Zero energy pathway data to provide a comprehensive macroeconomic and sectoral transition risk framework for financial analysis.

calculationEngine.formula: ITRS = w₁×NGFSMacroImpact + w₂×IEAEnergyShift + w₃×PolicyCarbonPrice
calculationEngine.brief: Combines macroeconomic GDP shock from NGFS, sectoral energy demand shift from IEA WEO, and carbon price path to produce a sector-level transition risk score.

dataPoints.name: NGFS Scenarios Available
dataPoints.interpretation: Six canonical scenarios from Net Zero 2050 (orderly) to Current Policies (hot house world), each with GDP, inflation, and carbon price paths.

dataPoints.name: IEA NZE Carbon Price 2030
dataPoints.interpretation: Carbon price required in advanced economies by 2030 on the IEA Net Zero Emissions by 2050 pathway.

userInteraction: Load NGFS Phase IV macro variable paths: GDP, unemployment, carbon price by scenario and region
Layer IEA WEO sector energy demand and technology mix projections
Compute sector transition risk by mapping energy intensity and carbon exposure to NGFS variable shocks
Reconcile differences in scenario definitions; produce unified 5-variable dashboard per sector

acronyms.full: Network for Greening the Financial System
acronyms.def: Coalition of central banks providing climate scenario standards for financial risk assessment.
IEA's flagship annual report projecting global energy supply and demand across multiple scenarios.
Combined metric drawing on NGFS macro impacts and IEA energy system shifts to score sector transition risk.

dataLineage.source: NGFS scenario data portal, IEA WEO data download, IPCC AR6 scenario explorer
dataLineage.flow: Scenario reconciliation, variable path interpolation, sector risk computation
dataLineage.output: Integrated scenario dashboards, sector transition trajectories, portfolio stress outputs

/ngfs-iea-scenario.valueSummary: Provides banks, asset managers, and supervisors with a harmonised view of NGFS and IEA scenario data to support rigorous, internally consistent transition risk assessments across investment and lending portfolios.

/ngfs-scenarios.title: NGFS Scenarios
/ngfs-scenarios.description: Provides an interactive exploration and application tool for the full Network for Greening the Financial System scenario suite, covering macro-financial variables, carbon price paths, and physical risk indicators across all six scenarios.

calculationEngine.formula: SDI = Σ |Variableₜ(ScenarioA) – Variableₜ(ScenarioB)| / Baselineₜ
calculationEngine.brief: Measures cumulative deviation between scenario pairs across key macro variables (GDP, carbon price, energy demand), quantifying scenario uncertainty span.

dataPoints.name: NGFS Scenarios
dataPoints.interpretation: Current canonical set: Net Zero 2050, Below 2°C, Delayed Transition, Divergent Net Zero, Nationally Determined Contributions, Current Policies.

dataPoints.name: Carbon Price Range (2030, across scenarios)
dataPoints.interpretation: Span of modelled carbon prices across NGFS scenarios by 2030, illustrating the width of policy ambition uncertainty.

userInteraction: Load NGFS Phase IV scenario variable sets: GDP loss, carbon price, energy prices, physical risk indicators
Enable scenario comparison across up to 3 simultaneous scenarios for selected macro variables
Apply scenario paths to portfolio sector exposures to produce transition and physical loss estimates
Support regulatory disclosure: compute scenario-conditional VaR and expected shortfall
references: NGFS Climate Scenarios Phase IV Technical Documentation 2023
FSB Supervisory Practices Report on Climate Risk 2022

acronyms.full: Network for Greening the Financial System
acronyms.def: Coalition of 130+ central banks and supervisors developing climate scenario standards.
Metric quantifying the cumulative difference between two NGFS scenario paths across macro variables.

acronyms.full: International Institute for Applied Systems Analysis
acronyms.def: Research institution hosting the NGFS scenario database and IAM modelling.

dataLineage.source: NGFS scenario data portal via IIASA, REMIND and MESSAGEix IAM outputs
dataLineage.flow: Variable path ingestion, scenario comparison computation, portfolio loss estimation
dataLineage.output: Interactive scenario explorer, sector loss tables, regulatory stress test outputs

/ngfs-scenarios.valueSummary: Delivers a comprehensive NGFS scenario workbench enabling financial institutions to explore, compare, and apply all six canonical climate scenarios to portfolio risk and regulatory stress testing workflows.

/nlp-disclosure-parser.title: NLP Disclosure Parser
/nlp-disclosure-parser.description: Uses natural language processing to extract structured ESG KPIs, metrics, and qualitative disclosures from regulatory filings (CSRD, TCFD, CDP, SFDR), reducing manual data collection burden.

calculationEngine.formula: F1 = 2 × (Precision × Recall) / (Precision + Recall)
calculationEngine.brief: Harmonic mean of precision (correct extractions / total extractions) and recall (correct extractions / total relevant instances) for each KPI category.

dataPoints.name: KPI Categories Extracted
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of distinct ESG and climate KPI categories the parser can extract across GHG, water, social, governance, and regulatory dimensions.

dataPoints.name: Extraction F1 Score (Scope 1/2 GHG)
dataPoints.interpretation: Measured F1 accuracy for Scope 1 and 2 GHG extraction from CSRD and TCFD-aligned reports in validation corpus.

userInteraction: Ingest disclosure documents (PDF, HTML, XBRL) and pre-process for NLP pipeline
Apply domain-specific entity recognition to identify GHG values, target years, and KPI labels
Validate extracted values using unit consistency checks and cross-document reconciliation
Output structured JSON datasets tagged by standard (CSRD, TCFD, SFDR, GRI, CDP) for downstream use
references: Devlin et al. BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers 2018

acronyms.def: AI techniques that enable machines to understand, extract, and generate human language.
XML-based standard for tagging financial and sustainability data in machine-readable format.
Harmonic mean of precision and recall; standard metric for NLP extraction task quality.

dataLineage.source: Regulatory filing PDFs, XBRL iXBRL documents, CDP questionnaire responses, TCFD reports
dataLineage.flow: NLP pipeline: tokenisation, NER, relation extraction, unit normalisation
dataLineage.output: Structured ESG KPI datasets, extraction confidence scores, audit trails

/nlp-disclosure-parser.valueSummary: Automates the labour-intensive extraction of ESG KPIs from regulatory filings, enabling faster, more consistent data collection for ESG scoring, portfolio monitoring, and regulatory reporting workflows.

/ocean-marine-risk.title: Ocean & Marine Risk
/ocean-marine-risk.description: Quantifies climate-driven ocean and marine risks including acidification, sea level rise, storm surge, and marine heatwaves for coastal assets, fisheries, and blue economy finance.

calculationEngine.formula: MCRI = Σ (Hazardᵢ × Exposureᵢ × Sensitivityᵢ) / AdaptiveCapacity
standards: UNEP-WCMC Ocean Risk and Resilience Action Alliance
calculationEngine.brief: Multi-hazard composite combining ocean acidification intensity, sea level rise trajectory, storm surge probability, and marine heatwave frequency weighted by asset exposure and sensitivity.

dataPoints.name: Sea Level Rise by 2100 (SSP5-8.5)
dataPoints.interpretation: Likely range of global mean sea level rise by 2100 under high-emission SSP5-8.5 scenario.

dataPoints.name: Ocean Acidification Change (pH, since 1850)
dataPoints.interpretation: Observed reduction in global average ocean pH since pre-industrial period, representing ~30% increase in hydrogen ion concentration.

userInteraction: Map coastal and marine assets to sea level rise, storm surge, and acidification hazard layers
Apply IPCC AR6 sea level projections at 0.5° resolution under SSP2-4.5 and SSP5-8.5
Score fisheries and aquaculture exposure to ocean warming and acidification using species sensitivity curves
Compute MCRI by asset; estimate damage costs and adaptation investment requirements
references: IPCC Special Report on the Ocean and Cryosphere in a Changing Climate (SROCC) 2019
IPCC AR6 WG1 Chapter 9 Ocean, Cryosphere and Sea Level
UNEP-WCMC Ocean Risk and Resilience Action Alliance

acronyms.def: Composite hazard-exposure-sensitivity metric for climate risks affecting ocean and marine assets.

acronyms.full: Special Report on the Ocean and Cryosphere in a Changing Climate
acronyms.def: IPCC 2019 report assessing climate impacts on ocean systems and sea level change.
IPCC scenario framework combining emissions trajectories with socioeconomic development assumptions.

dataLineage.source: IPCC AR6 sea level projections, GEBCO bathymetry, CoSMO storm surge models, NOAA acidification data
dataLineage.flow: Hazard layer mapping, MCRI computation, damage cost estimation
dataLineage.output: Coastal risk maps, asset MCRI scores, fisheries impact assessments

/ocean-marine-risk.valueSummary: Enables coastal asset owners, blue economy investors, and marine insurers to quantify and manage climate-driven ocean and marine risks using IPCC-aligned hazard science and financial impact modelling.

/owid-evic-analytics.title: EVIC Analytics
/owid-evic-analytics.description: Calculates and analyses Enterprise Value Including Cash for portfolio companies to support PCAF financed emissions attribution, SFDR principal adverse impact metrics, and TCFD carbon intensity reporting.

calculationEngine.formula: AFᵢ = Investmentᵢ / EVICᵢ
calculationEngine.brief: Fraction of a company's total enterprise value held by the investor; used to apportion financed emissions from the company to the investor's portfolio.

dataPoints.name: EVIC Components
dataPoints.value: Market Cap + Debt + Minority Interest – Cash
dataPoints.interpretation: Standard EVIC definition: market capitalisation of equity plus book value of total debt plus minority interest less cash and cash equivalents.

dataPoints.name: Typical Attribution Factor Range
dataPoints.interpretation: Range of PCAF attribution factors for a diversified equity portfolio holding individual positions, reflecting typical institutional ownership levels.

userInteraction: Retrieve market capitalisation, debt, minority interest, and cash from financial databases
Compute EVIC per PCAF v2 definition; handle missing data via proxy estimation hierarchy
Calculate attribution factor AFᵢ for each portfolio holding
Link AFᵢ to company GHG emissions data to compute financed emissions and WACI
references: PCAF Global GHG Accounting and Reporting Standard for the Financial Industry v2 2022
TCFD Guidance on Metrics, Targets and Transition Plans 2021

acronyms.def: Total market value of a company including equity, debt, minority interest, and cash; used as PCAF emissions attribution denominator.
Global initiative developing GHG accounting standards for financial institutions' portfolios and loans.
Investor's proportional share of a company's EVIC, used to allocate financed emissions.

dataLineage.source: Bloomberg/Refinitiv financial data, company balance sheets, equity market prices
dataLineage.flow: EVIC computation, missing data proxy estimation, attribution factor calculation
dataLineage.output: EVIC datasets, financed emissions attribution tables, PCAF and SFDR disclosure outputs

/owid-evic-analytics.valueSummary: Provides a rigorous, PCAF-aligned EVIC computation engine that underpins accurate financed emissions reporting, SFDR PAI metric calculation, and TCFD carbon intensity disclosures across investment portfolios.

/pc-climate-pricing.title: PC Climate Pricing
/pc-climate-pricing.description: Analyses preferred creditor climate financing terms across multilateral development banks and development finance institutions, benchmarking concessional spreads, tenor extensions, and climate co-benefits.

calculationEngine.formula: CI = 1 – (NPV(ProjectTerms) / NPV(MarketTerms))
standards: World Bank MDB Climate Finance Tracking 2023
calculationEngine.brief: Measures the discount provided relative to market terms; higher CI indicates greater concessionality and effective subsidy for climate projects.

dataPoints.name: MDB Climate Finance (2022)
dataPoints.interpretation: Total climate finance mobilised by multilateral development banks globally in 2022 across mitigation and adaptation.

dataPoints.name: Typical PC Spread vs Market
dataPoints.interpretation: Typical spread benefit (concessionality) for preferred creditor climate loans relative to market-rate sovereign financing.

userInteraction: Map MDB and DFI loan terms: tenor, grace period, spread, co-financing requirements
Compute NPV of project cash flows under preferred creditor terms vs market benchmark rate
Calculate CI and grant equivalent for each financing instrument
Benchmark across IBRD, IDA, ADB, AfDB, IADB, and bilateral DFIs by sector and country income group
references: Joint MDB Climate Finance Tracking Report 2022
World Bank Blended Finance Guidance Note 2019

acronyms.def: Measure of the subsidy element in a development finance instrument relative to market-rate financing.
Internationally owned financial institution providing development finance, including IBRD, ADB, and AfDB.
Government-backed institution providing financing for private sector development in emerging markets.
UNFCCC financial mechanism providing climate finance to developing countries for mitigation and adaptation.

dataLineage.source: MDB annual reports, loan term sheets, OECD DAC aid statistics, Bloomberg fixed income data
dataLineage.flow: Term mapping, NPV calculation, CI computation, cross-institution benchmarking
dataLineage.output: Concessionality dashboards, MDB term benchmarks, blended finance structure analysis

/pc-climate-pricing.valueSummary: Enables development finance practitioners and climate investors to compare preferred creditor climate financing terms, optimise blended finance structures, and maximise concessionality for climate projects.

/pcaf-india-brsr.title: PCAF India BRSR
/pcaf-india-brsr.description: Applies PCAF GHG accounting methodology to India Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting requirements, enabling Indian financial institutions to compute and disclose financed emissions in BRSR format.

calculationEngine.formula: FEᵢ = AFᵢ × CompanyEmissionsᵢ
calculationEngine.brief: PCAF attribution factor multiplied by portfolio company Scope 1+2 emissions; aggregated across all holdings to produce BRSR-compliant financed emissions disclosure.

dataPoints.name: BRSR Core KPIs
dataPoints.interpretation: SEBI mandated nine ESG attributes in BRSR Core including greenhouse gas intensity, water intensity, and supply chain responsible sourcing.

dataPoints.name: BRSR Mandatory Scope
dataPoints.interpretation: Indian companies required to submit BRSR under SEBI mandate covering BSE/NSE top 1,000 by market capitalisation.

userInteraction: Map portfolio holdings to BRSR-covered Indian companies using ISIN/CIN identifiers
Retrieve Scope 1/2 GHG emissions from BRSR filings or estimate using PCAF Tier 4/5 proxies
Compute EVIC and attribution factor per PCAF v2 guidance adapted for Indian capital market structures
Aggregate financed emissions and format output per SEBI BRSR Core disclosure template
references: PCAF Global GHG Accounting and Reporting Standard v2 2022
MoEFCC India GHG Programme Reporting Guidelines

acronyms.def: SEBI-mandated ESG disclosure framework for listed Indian companies replacing the Business Responsibility Report.

acronyms.full: Securities and Exchange Board of India
acronyms.def: Indian capital markets regulator mandating BRSR disclosure for top listed companies.
Global standard for GHG accounting in financial portfolios adopted by Indian financial institutions.
PCAF standard denominator for emissions attribution in listed equity and corporate bond portfolios.

dataLineage.source: SEBI BRSR filings, NSE/BSE company master data, PCAF emissions proxies, MCA financial data
dataLineage.flow: Entity matching, EVIC computation, financed emissions aggregation, BRSR format mapping
dataLineage.output: BRSR Core disclosure tables, financed emissions by sector, PCAF data quality scores

/pcaf-india-brsr.valueSummary: Enables Indian banks, asset managers, and insurance companies to compute and report financed emissions in full compliance with SEBI BRSR Core requirements using PCAF-aligned GHG accounting methodology.

/pe-deal-pipeline.title: PE Deal Pipeline
/pe-deal-pipeline.description: Manages and analyses the private equity deal pipeline with integrated ESG and climate screening, enabling systematic pre-screening of opportunities before full due diligence investment.

calculationEngine.formula: CADS = α×FinancialScore + β×ESGScore + γ×ClimateRiskScore
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted composite of financial attractiveness, ESG quality, and climate risk assessment; α+β+γ=1 with weights calibrated to fund mandate and LP requirements.

dataPoints.name: ESG Screen Pass Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Fraction of sourced deals passing initial ESG and climate screen before proceeding to full diligence, consistent with PE industry norms.

dataPoints.name: Climate Taxonomy Alignment Check
dataPoints.interpretation: Minimum taxonomy alignment assessment performed at pipeline stage to assess SFDR product classification implications.

userInteraction: Capture deal pipeline: company name, sector, geography, deal stage, investment thesis
Apply ESG screen: sector exclusions, UNGC violations, severe ESG controversies
Overlay climate risk pre-screen: transition exposure, physical hazard, carbon intensity vs peers
Compute CADS; triage deals into green-light, amber-watch, and red-exclude categories
references: PRI Guidance on Private Equity ESG Integration 2023

acronyms.def: Composite pre-screening metric combining financial, ESG, and climate risk signals for PE deal triage.
LP industry body publishing PE ESG and governance standards.
EU regulation requiring fund managers to disclose ESG integration and sustainability risk policies.

dataLineage.source: Deal sourcing CRM, company financial data, ESG controversy databases, climate hazard maps
dataLineage.flow: Sector exclusion screening, ESG controversy check, climate pre-score, CADS computation
dataLineage.output: Pipeline ESG/climate scorecard, deal triage dashboard, LP reporting inputs

/pe-deal-pipeline.valueSummary: Provides PE investment teams with a disciplined, data-driven pipeline screening framework that integrates ESG and climate risk signals early in the deal cycle to improve capital allocation quality.

/pe-esg-diligence.title: PE ESG Diligence
/pe-esg-diligence.description: Provides a structured pre-investment ESG due diligence framework for PE transactions, covering environmental, social, and governance risk identification, materiality assessment, and value creation planning.

calculationEngine.formula: EMS = Σ wᵢ × TopicScoreᵢ × SeverityMultiplierᵢ
calculationEngine.brief: SASB sector-specific material topics weighted by financial impact potential and scored on management quality; produces a sector-calibrated ESG diligence rating.

dataPoints.name: PE ESG DD Topics
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of material ESG topics assessed in a typical sector-specific PE diligence using SASB standards.

dataPoints.name: ESG Value Creation Upside
dataPoints.interpretation: Documented multiple-on-invested-capital improvement attributable to ESG value creation plans in case studies of PE-backed companies.

userInteraction: Identify target sector and map to SASB material ESG topics
Conduct management interview and document review across environmental, social, and governance dimensions
Score each material topic on current performance and management quality
Develop 100-day ESG value creation plan: key initiatives, KPI targets, resource requirements
references: PRI Due Diligence Questionnaire for PE 2023
UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights 2011

acronyms.def: Weighted composite score assessing PE target ESG performance against sector-specific material topics.
Standard-setter providing industry-specific sustainability accounting standards identifying material ESG topics.
Private equity return metric: total value received divided by total equity invested.
Structured questionnaire used to gather ESG information from PE targets or fund managers.

dataLineage.source: Management interviews, company documents, ESG controversy databases, regulatory filings, site assessments
dataLineage.flow: SASB materiality mapping, topic scoring, severity weighting, EMS computation
dataLineage.output: ESG diligence report, value creation plan, investment committee risk and opportunity summary

/pe-esg-diligence.valueSummary: Equips PE deal teams with a rigorous, sector-calibrated ESG diligence process that identifies material risks, surfaces value creation opportunities, and supports responsible investment decision-making.

/pe-vc-esg.title: PE/VC ESG Analytics
/pe-vc-esg.description: Integrates ESG performance monitoring and impact measurement across PE and VC portfolios, supporting LP reporting, fund-level ESG scoring, and portfolio company improvement tracking.

calculationEngine.formula: FES = Σ wᵢ × CompanyESGᵢ
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted average of portfolio company ESG scores using invested capital weights; supports ILPA and LP quarterly reporting.

dataPoints.name: ESG KPIs Tracked
dataPoints.interpretation: Core ESG metrics tracked across PE/VC portfolios under the ESG Data Convergence Initiative (EDCI) protocol.

dataPoints.name: EDCI Signatory AUM
dataPoints.interpretation: Total AUM represented by EDCI signatories who have committed to standardised ESG data collection and reporting.

userInteraction: Collect company-level ESG KPIs per EDCI protocol: GHG intensity, board diversity, work-related injuries
Aggregate to fund level using invested capital weights; compute year-on-year improvement rates
Benchmark fund ESG score against GRESB PE assessment and peer universe
Produce LP ESG report: fund score, portfolio heat map, progress against ESG action plans
references: GRESB Private Equity and Infrastructure Assessment 2023

acronyms.def: Capital-weighted average of portfolio company ESG ratings at fund level.
PE industry initiative standardising ESG KPI collection across portfolio companies for consistent LP reporting.
ESG benchmark for real estate and infrastructure funds; also assesses PE portfolios for ESG integration.
Investor in a PE or VC fund receiving ESG performance reporting from the general partner.

dataLineage.source: Portfolio company ESG questionnaires, EDCI data submissions, financial ownership data
dataLineage.flow: EDCI KPI aggregation, capital-weighted scoring, GRESB benchmark comparison
dataLineage.output: Fund ESG scorecards, LP reporting packs, portfolio improvement heat maps

/pe-vc-esg.valueSummary: Provides PE and VC fund managers with a comprehensive ESG performance monitoring system aligned with EDCI, GRESB, and LP reporting standards, supporting responsible investment and fund-raising commitments.

/physical-hazard-map.title: Physical Hazard Map
/physical-hazard-map.description: Multi-peril physical climate hazard mapping across asset locations using gridded climate datasets and SSP scenarios.

calculationEngine.formula: H = Σ(wᵢ × perilᵢ) / Σwᵢ
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted aggregation of normalised peril intensities across flood, heat, drought, wind, wildfire, and sea-level rise.

dataPoints.name: Peak Flood Depth (m)
dataPoints.interpretation: Modelled 1-in-100-year inundation depth at asset centroid.

dataPoints.name: Wildfire Hazard Index
dataPoints.interpretation: Normalised 0–1 fire weather index under SSP5-8.5 by 2050.

dataPoints.name: Heat Stress Days/yr
dataPoints.interpretation: Annual days exceeding 35°C wet-bulb threshold at location.

userInteraction: Select SSP scenario and time horizon.

Deduplicate and fuzzy-match entity names across lists using LEI and ISIN cross-reference.
Score each entity on combined sanctions severity and ESG controversy dimensions.
Deliver portfolio-specific alerts and generate periodic watchlist summary reports.

acronyms.def: Individual with prominent public function posing higher risk of bribery or corruption.
Global 20-character alphanumeric code uniquely identifying legal entities in financial transactions.
Process of verifying client identity and assessing suitability and risk before onboarding.

/satellite-climate-monitor.title: Satellite Climate Monitor
/satellite-climate-monitor.description: Satellite-derived climate hazard monitoring providing near-real-time physical risk signals including fire, flood, drought, and sea-level anomaly indicators for portfolio assets.

calculationEngine.formula: (Current Reading – Baseline Mean) ÷ Baseline σ
calculationEngine.brief: Z-score of current satellite-derived hazard reading relative to historical baseline, flagging statistically significant anomalies.

dataPoints.name: Assets Monitored
dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio assets with satellite hazard monitoring coverage as of latest mosaic date.

dataPoints.name: Active Alerts
dataPoints.interpretation: Assets currently within a declared hazard anomaly zone above 2σ threshold.

dataPoints.name: Mosaic Refresh
dataPoints.interpretation: Average time between satellite image acquisitions for monitored asset locations.

userInteraction: Geocode portfolio assets and define monitoring perimeters.
Ingest satellite feeds: Sentinel-1/2 (flood/fire), MODIS/VIIRS (fire), Landsat (drought index).
Compute hazard anomaly indices against 10-year baselines and flag threshold breaches.
Publish asset-level alerts and aggregate portfolio exposure summaries.
references: NASA FIRMS Fire Information for Resource Management

acronyms.full: Fire Information for Resource Management System
acronyms.def: NASA system providing near-real-time global active fire detection from satellite data.
Satellite-derived index measuring vegetation health and drought stress.
Satellite imaging technique penetrating cloud cover, used for flood mapping.

dataLineage.source: Satellite image feeds, asset geocodes, historical baselines
dataLineage.flow: Raster processing, anomaly detection, perimeter intersection
dataLineage.output: Asset-level hazard alerts, portfolio exposure maps, time-series charts

/satellite-climate-monitor.valueSummary: Provides near-real-time satellite-derived physical risk signals linked directly to portfolio asset locations.

/sbti-climate-trace.title: SBTi Climate Trace
/sbti-climate-trace.description: Integration of Climate TRACE independent emissions inventory data with SBTi target-setting and validation workflows for corporate emissions accountability.

calculationEngine.formula: |Climate TRACE Estimate – Company Reported| ÷ Company Reported × 100
calculationEngine.brief: Percentage divergence between independently estimated and self-reported emissions, used to flag potential underreporting.

dataPoints.name: Companies Matched
dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio companies with Climate TRACE asset-level emissions estimates available.

dataPoints.name: Avg Verification Gap
dataPoints.interpretation: Mean absolute divergence between Climate TRACE estimates and company Scope 1+2 disclosures.

dataPoints.name: SBTi Targets Validated
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of matched companies with SBTi-approved near-term or net-zero targets.

userInteraction: Match portfolio companies to Climate TRACE asset-level emissions database using LEI and ISIN.
Compare Climate TRACE sector estimates with company-disclosed Scope 1 and 2 emissions.
Calculate verification gap and flag companies above 15% divergence threshold.
Overlay SBTi target status and assess alignment credibility given verification gap.

acronyms.def: Initiative validating corporate emissions reduction targets consistent with Paris Agreement pathways.
Climate TRACE independent global GHG emissions tracking coalition using AI and satellite data.
Atmospheric gases trapping heat; CO₂, CH₄ and N₂O are primary sources tracked in corporate accounting.

calculationEngine.brief: Percentage of scheduled report runs delivered within the configured tolerance window.

Set recurrence schedule (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly) and delivery window.
Add subscriber list with role-based access and delivery channel preferences.
Monitor delivery status, retry failed runs, and archive completed reports.

acronyms.def: Committed performance standard specifying acceptable delivery time and reliability thresholds.
Time-based job scheduler syntax used to define recurring report execution triggers.
Protocol enabling programmatic report delivery to external systems and data lakes.

/scope3-upstream-tracker.title: Scope 3 Upstream Tracker
/scope3-upstream-tracker.description: Scope 3 upstream emission tracking across GHG Protocol Categories 1–8, combining supplier data, spend-based proxies and IO-LCA models for comprehensive value-chain accounting.

calculationEngine.formula: Procurement Spend (£) × Emission Intensity (kgCO₂e/£)
calculationEngine.brief: Applies sector-specific spend-based emission factors to procurement expenditure when primary supplier data is unavailable.

dataPoints.interpretation: Number of upstream Scope 3 categories (Cat 1–8) with at least partial data coverage.

dataPoints.name: Total Upstream S3
dataPoints.interpretation: Aggregated upstream Scope 3 emissions across categories 1–8 in reporting year.

userInteraction: Map procurement spend to GHG Protocol Scope 3 categories 1–8.
Collect primary emission data via supplier portal; apply spend-based factors for gaps.
Apply IO-LCA emission intensities from Exiobase for categories lacking supplier data.
Aggregate by category, supplier tier and geography; report against prior year baseline.

acronyms.def: Indirect GHG emissions in a company's value chain, both upstream (Cat 1–8) and downstream (Cat 9–15).
Macroeconomic modelling approach estimating emissions across supply chains using national accounts data.
IO model augmented with environmental satellite accounts to derive emission intensities by sector.

/scope4-avoided-emissions.title: Scope 4 Avoided Emissions
/scope4-avoided-emissions.description: Scope 4 and enabled emissions quantification measuring emission reductions achieved by products and services compared to a defined baseline scenario.

calculationEngine.formula: Baseline Scenario Emissions – Product/Service Lifecycle Emissions
calculationEngine.brief: Net emission benefit of a product or service versus the counterfactual baseline it replaces, expressed in tCO₂e.

dataPoints.name: Products Assessed
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of products or services with completed avoided emission assessments.

dataPoints.interpretation: Aggregate avoided emissions across all assessed products in the reporting year.

dataPoints.interpretation: Ratio of total avoided emissions to company operational (Scope 1+2) footprint.

userInteraction: Define functional unit and system boundary for each product or service.
Establish baseline scenario representing the most likely alternative technology or service.
Calculate full lifecycle emissions of the assessed product.
Report avoided emissions as baseline minus product emissions with sensitivity analysis.

GHG Protocol Product Life Cycle Standard

acronyms.def: Unofficial category for avoided or enabled emission reductions occurring outside a company's value chain.
Systematic analysis of environmental impacts across a product's entire life from cradle to grave.

calculationEngine.brief: Proportion of required rule elements not yet implemented, providing a top-line gap measure for board reporting.

/sentiment-alpha-engine.title: Sentiment Alpha Engine
/sentiment-alpha-engine.description: ESG sentiment as alpha factor in equity portfolios, extracting return-predictive signals from news sentiment, controversy data and stakeholder engagement analytics.

calculationEngine.formula: (Σ Sentiment Score_i × w_i) – Benchmark Sentiment Score
calculationEngine.brief: Portfolio-weighted average ESG sentiment score minus benchmark equivalent, producing the active sentiment factor exposure.

dataPoints.name: Signal Universe
dataPoints.interpretation: Companies with active daily ESG sentiment signals from news and social media.

dataPoints.name: Factor IC
dataPoints.interpretation: Information coefficient of sentiment alpha factor versus 1-month forward equity returns.

dataPoints.interpretation: Annualised excess return attributable to sentiment alpha factor tilt in live portfolio.
