Aggregate entity-level daily sentiment scores and compute 30-day exponential moving average.
Construct long-short sentiment alpha factor and estimate IC via rolling backtest.
Integrate sentiment factor into portfolio optimiser as tilt constraint or return forecast.

acronyms.def: Correlation between a factor's predicted and realised returns; measures factor predictive power.
Weighted average giving greater weight to recent observations, used to smooth noisy daily sentiment scores.
AI field enabling computers to understand and extract structured signals from unstructured text.

dataLineage.source: News and social media text feeds, equity return data, portfolio weights
dataLineage.flow: NLP classification, factor construction, IC backtesting
dataLineage.output: Sentiment factor exposures, alpha attribution, portfolio overlays

calculationEngine.formula: (E_sentiment × 0.4) + (S_sentiment × 0.35) + (G_sentiment × 0.25)

acronyms.def: AI technique for extracting structured sentiment signals from unstructured text data.
Three-pillar sustainability framework used to classify and weight sentiment topics.

/solvency-capital-climate.title: Solvency Capital Climate
/solvency-capital-climate.description: Solvency II climate stress capital requirement analytics quantifying climate-driven changes to Solvency Capital Requirement for insurers under EIOPA climate stress scenarios.

calculationEngine.formula: SCR_stressed – SCR_base
calculationEngine.brief: Increase in Solvency Capital Requirement under EIOPA climate stress scenario relative to base SCR, measuring climate capital adequacy impact.

dataPoints.name: Base SCR Coverage
dataPoints.interpretation: Solvency Capital Requirement coverage ratio under standard EIOPA formula pre-climate stress.

dataPoints.name: Climate SCR Delta
dataPoints.interpretation: Estimated increase in SCR under EIOPA 2022 climate stress scenario (orderly transition + physical).

dataPoints.name: Post-Stress Coverage
dataPoints.interpretation: SCR coverage ratio after applying EIOPA climate stress scenario shocks to investment and underwriting portfolios.

userInteraction: Apply EIOPA 2022 climate stress scenario shocks to investment portfolio (transition risk).
Model physical risk claims inflation on non-life underwriting portfolio under RCP 8.5.

Report climate SCR delta and assess capital adequacy buffer versus solvency floor.

dataLineage.flow: SCR recalculation, climate shock application, solvency ratio analysis
dataLineage.output: Climate SCR delta reports, solvency waterfall charts, ORSA inputs

/sovereign-climate-intelligence.title: Sovereign Climate Intelligence
/sovereign-climate-intelligence.description: Integrated sovereign climate risk intelligence platform combining physical hazard, transition risk, fiscal climate vulnerability and adaptation capacity into a unified sovereign scoring model.

calculationEngine.formula: (Physical Risk × 0.35) + (Transition Risk × 0.30) + (Fiscal Vulnerability × 0.20) + (100 – Adaptation Capacity) × 0.15
calculationEngine.brief: Composite sovereign climate score weighting physical exposure, transition sensitivity, fiscal space and adaptive capacity.

dataPoints.name: Countries Scored
dataPoints.interpretation: Sovereign universe with full intelligence scoring across all four dimensions.

dataPoints.name: Highest Risk Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Country with the highest composite sovereign climate intelligence score indicating greatest overall vulnerability.

dataPoints.name: Portfolio Avg Score
dataPoints.interpretation: AUM-weighted average climate intelligence score across sovereign bond holdings.

userInteraction: Score physical risk using ND-GAIN vulnerability, exposure and readiness indices.
Assess transition risk via fossil fuel revenue dependence and carbon intensity of economy.
Model fiscal vulnerability: climate-related expenditure needs versus available fiscal space.
Integrate scores with adaptive capacity to produce sovereign climate intelligence composite.
references: Notre Dame Global Adaptation Initiative (ND-GAIN) Index
IMF World Economic Outlook Climate Module

acronyms.def: Annual index ranking 192 countries on climate vulnerability and readiness to adapt.

acronyms.full: Network for Greening the Financial System
acronyms.def: Central bank network providing climate scenario guidance for financial risk assessment.

calculationEngine.brief: Country climate risk composite weighting physical hazard exposure, economic transition sensitivity and climate policy ambition gap.

/sovereign-debt-sustainability.title: Sovereign Debt Sustainability
/sovereign-debt-sustainability.description: Climate-adjusted debt sustainability analysis incorporating physical damage costs, transition expenditure, and stranded asset losses into IMF DSA framework projections for sovereign issuers.

calculationEngine.formula: (Baseline Debt + Climate Shock) ÷ (GDP × (1 – GDP Impact))
calculationEngine.brief: Debt-to-GDP ratio adjusted for climate-related fiscal shocks including disaster costs, stranded assets and transition expenditures.

dataPoints.name: Countries in DSA
dataPoints.interpretation: Sovereign issuers with active climate-adjusted debt sustainability analysis.

dataPoints.interpretation: Average addition to debt/GDP ratio from cumulative climate fiscal shocks under RCP 4.5 by 2035.

dataPoints.name: DSA Breach Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of sovereign issuers breaching IMF high-risk debt thresholds after applying climate adjustment.

userInteraction: Apply NGFS physical risk GDP shocks to IMF baseline debt sustainability projections.
Add climate adaptation and mitigation expenditure to fiscal financing requirements.
Estimate stranded asset revenue losses for fossil-fuel-dependent economies under transition scenarios.
Produce climate-adjusted debt trajectory and assess breach of IMF sustainability thresholds.
references: NGFS Climate Scenarios for Central Banks 2023
World Bank Sovereign Climate Debt Guidance

acronyms.def: IMF framework assessing whether a sovereign's debt trajectory remains sustainable under baseline and stressed projections.

acronyms.full: Network for Greening the Financial System
acronyms.def: Central bank network providing macro-financial climate scenarios used in sovereign DSA stress testing.

/sovereign-esg-scorer.title: Sovereign ESG Scorer
/sovereign-esg-scorer.description: Sovereign ESG scoring model producing country-level pillar scores with underlying indicator weights, transparency into data sources, and portfolio sovereign ESG exposure reporting.

calculationEngine.formula: Σ (Indicator_i × Weight_i) ÷ Σ Weight_i
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted mean of normalised indicator values within each ESG pillar, producing a 0–100 pillar score per country.

dataPoints.name: Indicators per Pillar
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of underlying data indicators used to construct each ESG pillar score.

dataPoints.name: Data Vintage
dataPoints.interpretation: Range of data vintage years across underlying indicators; more recent data preferred.

dataPoints.name: Avg Score Spread
dataPoints.interpretation: Score range between highest and lowest country for each pillar, indicating discriminating power.

Compute weighted pillar scores and combine into composite sovereign ESG score.
Update scores on data refresh cycle and flag countries with material score changes.

World Bank primary dataset of 1,600+ indicators covering development, environment and governance.

calculationEngine.brief: Country physical climate vulnerability combining multi-hazard exposure, economic sensitivity, and adaptive capacity deficit.

/spending-carbon.title: Spending Carbon
/spending-carbon.description: Personal and corporate spend-based carbon footprint calculator that converts expenditure categories into GHG emissions using environmentally extended input-output (EEIO) coefficients.

calculationEngine.formula: Emissions = Spend (×) EF (kgCO2e/£)
calculationEngine.brief: Multiply spend in each category by the corresponding EEIO emission factor to derive tCO2e.

dataPoints.name: Total Footprint
dataPoints.interpretation: Sum of all category emissions; benchmark against national per-capita average of 10–12 tCO2e.

dataPoints.name: Highest Category
dataPoints.interpretation: Category contributing largest share of footprint; primary reduction lever.

dataPoints.name: Emission Intensity
dataPoints.interpretation: Weighted average emission factor across all spend; lower is greener.

Benchmark against sector peers and national averages

acronyms.def: Economic model linking financial spend to environmental impact via supply-chain multipliers.
kgCO2e emitted per unit of economic activity or physical output.

calculationEngine.brief: For each ESG score improvement band the financing spread is reduced; deterioration triggers spread widening.

/transition-credibility.description: Corporate climate transition plan credibility assessment platform evaluating plans against GFANZ, IPCC and regulatory criteria for ambition, specificity, governance, financing and accountability.

calculationEngine.formula: TCS = Σ (Criterion Score × Weight) / Σ Weight
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted average of credibility criteria scores covering ambition, interim targets, policy coherence, governance and capital allocation alignment.

dataPoints.name: Avg Credibility Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Mean transition plan credibility score across assessed companies; 70+ considered credible by GFANZ standards.

dataPoints.name: Net Zero Commitment Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of assessed companies with net zero commitments backed by interim science-based targets.

dataPoints.name: Capital Alignment Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Companies whose stated net zero commitments are reflected in capex and R&D allocation patterns.

userInteraction: Collect transition plan disclosures and supporting data
Score plan on GFANZ credibility criteria: ambition, targets, governance, financing, implementation
Cross-check stated targets against actual capital allocation
Compare plans to IPCC 1.5°C mitigation pathways
Generate credibility scorecard and red-flag report
references: ISSB IFRS S2 Transition Plan Requirements

acronyms.full: Glasgow Financial Alliance for Net Zero
acronyms.def: Coalition of financial institutions committed to net zero with transition plan credibility standards.
Composite metric evaluating whether corporate transition plans are ambitious, specific, financed and accountable.

dataLineage.source: Corporate Transition Plans, ESG Disclosures, Capex Data, SBTi Registry
dataLineage.flow: Credibility scoring engine + capital alignment analysis
dataLineage.output: Credibility scorecards, red-flag reports, ISSB S2 transition plan disclosures

/transition-credibility.valueSummary: A 2023 GFANZ analysis found only 10% of Fortune 500 transition plans meet minimum credibility criteria; most lack interim targets, explicit financing plans or governance accountability mechanisms.

/transition-finance.title: Transition Finance
/transition-finance.description: Financing platform for credible climate transitions in hard-to-abate sectors (steel, cement, shipping, aviation, chemicals) using sustainability-linked instruments, blended finance and Just Transition provisions.

calculationEngine.formula: TFES = Ambition Score × Credibility Score × Additionality Factor
calculationEngine.brief: Three-dimensional screening for transition finance eligibility: ambition of decarbonisation pathway, credibility of plan, and financial additionality of instrument.

dataPoints.name: Transition Finance Deployed
dataPoints.interpretation: Total capital deployed in transition finance instruments in current year across hard-to-abate sectors.

dataPoints.name: Avg TFES
dataPoints.interpretation: Mean transition finance eligibility score across funded companies; 60+ required for instrument approval.

dataPoints.name: Hard-to-Abate Coverage
dataPoints.interpretation: Sectors covered: steel, cement, shipping, aviation, chemicals, heavy road transport.

userInteraction: Assess company transition plan credibility and sector pathway alignment
Score TFES across ambition, credibility and additionality dimensions
Structure appropriate financing instrument (SLB, green loan, blended)
Set KPI-linked financial incentives to milestones
Monitor and report transition progress against financing covenants
references: EU Platform on Sustainable Finance Transition Finance Report 2023

acronyms.def: Composite eligibility metric covering ambition, credibility and financial additionality for transition finance instruments.
Bond with coupon step-up/step-down tied to achievement of pre-defined ESG performance targets.
Industrial sectors where direct electrification is technically or economically challenging and where transition requires significant capital investment.

dataLineage.source: Company Transition Plans, Financial Terms, Sector Pathway Data
dataLineage.flow: TFES engine + instrument structuring + milestone monitoring
dataLineage.output: Transition finance deal registry, KPI dashboards, impact reports

/transition-finance.valueSummary: Hard-to-abate sectors account for 30% of global GHG emissions; GFANZ estimates they require ≈1 trillion annually in transition finance to achieve net zero, 10× current deployment levels.

/transition-plan-builder.title: Transition Plan Builder
/transition-plan-builder.description: ISSB IFRS S2 and TCFD-aligned climate transition plan builder enabling companies to construct, document and stress-test comprehensive transition plans with science-based milestones and governance commitments.

calculationEngine.formula: TPCI = Populated Sections / Required Sections × 100
calculationEngine.brief: Percentage of mandatory transition plan sections complete per IFRS S2 and TCFD requirements; below 75% triggers gap alert.

dataPoints.name: Plan Completeness
dataPoints.interpretation: Current transition plan completeness score against IFRS S2 mandatory disclosure requirements.

dataPoints.name: Science-Based Milestones
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of interim science-based milestones defined between current year and 2050 net zero target.

dataPoints.name: Capital Aligned to Plan
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of 5-year capex budget allocated to activities consistent with transition plan objectives.

userInteraction: Define net zero commitment and target year
Set interim science-based milestones by decade
Document governance structure and board accountability
Map capital allocation to transition activities
Stress-test plan against NGFS scenarios and disclose under IFRS S2

acronyms.def: Percentage of mandatory IFRS S2/TCFD transition plan sections populated; primary quality metric.
ISSB standard requiring disclosure of climate-related risks, opportunities and transition plans.

acronyms.full: Glasgow Financial Alliance for Net Zero
acronyms.def: Alliance defining minimum elements of credible financial sector transition plans.

dataLineage.source: Corporate Strategy Documents, Capex Budgets, SBTi Commitments, NGFS Pathways
dataLineage.flow: Plan completeness engine + scenario stress-test + IFRS S2 mapping
dataLineage.output: IFRS S2 transition plan disclosures, TCFD reports, investor-grade plan summaries

/transition-plan-builder.valueSummary: IFRS S2 mandatory from FY2024 for ISSB-adopting jurisdictions; transition plan quality is the most scrutinised element by institutional investors and proxy advisors in 2024–2025 reporting cycle.

/transition-planning-hub.title: Transition Planning Hub
/transition-planning-hub.description: Integrated climate transition planning platform consolidating scenario analysis, target setting, capital planning, supply chain decarbonisation and stakeholder communication into a single workflow.

calculationEngine.formula: TRI = Σ (Module Score × Weight) / 5
calculationEngine.brief: Equal-weighted composite of five transition readiness dimensions: ambition, capital, operations, supply chain, and stakeholder engagement.

dataPoints.name: Transition Readiness Index
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite transition readiness score across all five dimensions; 75+ is considered transition-ready by institutional investors.

dataPoints.name: Decarbonisation Pathways Modelled
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of transition pathways modelled (NZE 2050, SDS, STEPS, Delayed Action).

dataPoints.name: Stakeholder Sign-off
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of transition plan elements reviewed and approved by board or board sub-committee.

userInteraction: Establish baseline emissions and transition gap
Model decarbonisation pathways under multiple scenarios
Set science-based targets and interim milestones
Align capital planning and supply chain programmes
Communicate plan to investors, regulators and employees

acronyms.def: Composite metric assessing organisational preparedness across ambition, capital, operations, supply chain and engagement dimensions.
SBTi standard requiring 90%+ absolute emissions reduction by 2050 with neutralisation of residual emissions.

dataLineage.source: Emissions Data, Capex Plans, Scenario Pathways, Stakeholder Engagement Records
dataLineage.flow: Multi-module integration + TRI computation + scenario modelling
dataLineage.output: Transition planning dashboard, investor-grade plan documents, regulatory disclosures

/transition-planning-hub.valueSummary: Integrated transition planning hubs reduce plan development time by 50% and improve inter-module consistency; key requirement for ISSB IFRS S2 and CSRD ESRS E1 compliance from 2024–2025.

/transition-scenario-modeller.title: Transition Scenario Modeller
/transition-scenario-modeller.description: Corporate scenario analysis platform for transition risk modelling; applies NGFS, IEA and proprietary scenarios to company financial projections to quantify revenue, cost and asset value impacts.

calculationEngine.formula: ΔEBITDA = ΔRevenue(carbon price, demand) + ΔCost(energy, compliance) – ΔCapex(transition)
calculationEngine.brief: Net EBITDA impact from scenario-driven revenue changes, operating cost changes and transition capex requirements under each pathway.

dataPoints.name: EBITDA Impact (Orderly 1.5°C)
dataPoints.interpretation: Projected EBITDA reduction under NGFS Orderly transition by 2030 vs baseline.

dataPoints.name: EBITDA Impact (Disorderly)
dataPoints.interpretation: Projected EBITDA reduction under NGFS Disorderly transition; higher from abrupt carbon price shock.

dataPoints.name: Scenarios Available
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of scenarios available including NGFS Phase IV, IEA NZE/SDS/STEPS and proprietary variants.

userInteraction: Select relevant transition scenarios from library
Map carbon price, energy price and demand pathways to company financials
Project revenue impact from demand shifts and product mix changes
Model cost impact from energy transition and carbon compliance
Stress-test financial covenants and communicate results to board

acronyms.full: Network for Greening the Financial System
acronyms.def: Central bank network producing climate scenarios widely adopted for supervisory and corporate stress testing.
SBTi methodology aligning company targets to sector-specific 1.5°C decarbonisation pathways.

dataLineage.source: Company P&L, Carbon Price Curves, Energy Price Projections, NGFS Pathways
dataLineage.flow: Scenario mapping engine + financial impact modelling
dataLineage.output: Scenario impact reports, TCFD Strategy section disclosures, board presentations

/transition-scenario-modeller.valueSummary: TCFD recommends at least three scenario pathways including a <2°C scenario; CSRD ESRS E1 requires quantitative scenario analysis for all large companies from 2024; modellers reduce analyst time by 70%.

/transport-decarbonisation.title: Transport Decarbonisation
/transport-decarbonisation.description: Road, rail, aviation and shipping GHG reduction analytics platform modelling decarbonisation pathways, technology transition costs and policy compliance for transport operators and investors.

calculationEngine.formula: MAC = ΔCost / ΔEmissions (tCO2e)
calculationEngine.brief: Marginal abatement cost in £/tCO2e for each transport mode and technology; primary input for decarbonisation pathway optimisation.

dataPoints.name: Fleet Average Emissions Intensity
dataPoints.interpretation: Fleet-weighted average carbon intensity across all transport modes; benchmark vs regulatory targets.

dataPoints.name: EV Fleet Share
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of road fleet electrified; target 100% for new sales by 2030–2035 under UK ZEV mandate.

dataPoints.name: SAF Blend Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Sustainable aviation fuel proportion in aviation fuel uplift; EU ReFuelEU target 2% by 2025.

userInteraction: Inventory transport assets by mode and fuel type
Apply sector-specific decarbonisation pathways (electrification, hydrogen, SAF)
Compute marginal abatement costs by technology option
Model fleet transition capex and operating cost trajectories
Report against IMO GHG Strategy, CORSIA and national zero-emission vehicle mandates
references: IMO Initial GHG Strategy 2018 / Enhanced Strategy 2023

acronyms.def: Incremental cost per tonne of CO2e abated from a specific measure or technology; key input for decarbonisation planning.
Vehicle producing zero tailpipe emissions; UK mandate requires 80% ZEV new car sales by 2030.
UN agency regulating international shipping; 2023 strategy targets net zero GHG by 2050.

dataLineage.source: Fleet Registry, Fuel Consumption Data, Technology Cost Curves
dataLineage.flow: MAC engine + pathway optimiser + regulatory compliance tracker
dataLineage.output: Decarbonisation roadmaps, capex plans, CORSIA/IMO compliance reports

/transport-decarbonisation.valueSummary: Transport accounts for 21% of global CO2 emissions; road transport electrification is cost-competitive in most markets; maritime and aviation represent the hardest decarbonisation challenges requiring green fuels.

/uk-sdr.title: UK SDR
/uk-sdr.description: UK Sustainability Disclosure Requirements compliance analytics platform covering investment product labelling, naming and marketing rules under FCA SDR PS23/16, with anti-greenwashing integration.

calculationEngine.formula: LES = Min(Sustainability Focus, Paris Alignment, Robustness) ≥ 70
calculationEngine.brief: Composite eligibility check across sustainability focus proportion, climate alignment and governance robustness; all three dimensions must exceed threshold for label award.

dataPoints.name: Products with SDR Label
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of investment products awarded UK SDR sustainability labels (Focus, Improvers, Impact, Mixed Goals).

dataPoints.name: Sustainability Focus %
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of AUM in assets meeting the sustainability focus test across SDR-labelled products.

dataPoints.name: Anti-Greenwashing Alerts
dataPoints.interpretation: Marketing materials flagged for potential breach of FCA anti-greenwashing rule requiring substantiated claims.

userInteraction: Assess product eligibility for SDR label category
Verify sustainability focus proportion meets 70%+ threshold
Review fund naming and marketing materials against anti-greenwashing rule
Prepare sustainability product disclosure document (PDD)
Submit to FCA register and conduct ongoing label maintenance

acronyms.def: FCA policy statement PS23/16 introducing four investment labels, naming rules and anti-greenwashing requirements.
Consumer-facing disclosure document required for SDR-labelled products summarising sustainability objectives and methodology.
UK financial regulator implementing SDR and anti-greenwashing rules.

dataLineage.source: Portfolio Holdings, ESG Scores, Marketing Materials, FCA Register
dataLineage.flow: Label eligibility engine + anti-greenwashing scanner + PDD generator
dataLineage.output: Label eligibility reports, PDDs, anti-greenwashing compliance alerts, FCA submission packages

/uk-sdr.valueSummary: UK SDR labels are operational from July 2024; the anti-greenwashing rule applies to all FCA-authorised firms; non-compliance risks FCA enforcement action and reputational damage in a scrutinised market.

/underwriting-esg.title: Underwriting ESG
/underwriting-esg.description: ESG integration platform for insurance underwriting; scores policy risks against environmental, social and governance criteria to support risk selection, pricing and exclusion policy implementation.

calculationEngine.brief: Weighted ESG score applied at policy level during underwriting; below-threshold policies trigger review, exclusion or loading.

dataPoints.name: Policies with ESG Flag
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of policies flagged for ESG review during underwriting; most common flags: coal, deforestation, governance.

dataPoints.name: Coal Exclusion Compliance
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of new policies compliant with coal underwriting exclusion threshold (>30% revenue from thermal coal).

dataPoints.name: ESG Premium Loading (Avg)
dataPoints.interpretation: Average premium loading applied to policies with ESG risk factors that do not trigger outright exclusion.

userInteraction: Collect ESG data at point of underwriting submission
Score policy against ESG underwriting criteria and exclusion list
Apply premium loading or trigger exclusion review for flagged risks
Aggregate portfolio ESG metrics for management reporting
Report against ClimateWise Principles and UNEP PSI commitments
references: ClimateWise Principles on Climate Action in Insurance
Lloyd’s of London ESG Underwriting Requirements 2022
ABI UK Net Zero Underwriting Commitment

acronyms.def: Policy-level composite ESG metric used in risk selection, pricing and exclusion decisions.
UNEP FI framework for integrating ESG into insurance operations adopted by 170+ insurers.
Industry-led group of insurers committed to managing climate risk exposure in underwriting portfolios.

dataLineage.source: Policy Submission Data, ESG Databases, Exclusion Lists, Premium Data
dataLineage.flow: ESG scoring engine + exclusion checker + loading calculator
dataLineage.output: Underwriting ESG reports, exclusion compliance tracker, PSI disclosures

/underwriting-esg.valueSummary: Insurers globally have withdrawn £1.3 trillion in coal underwriting capacity since 2017; ESG underwriting integration is accelerating as physical climate losses threaten insurability of high-risk sectors.

/user-role-management.title: User Role Management
/user-role-management.description: Platform role-based access control (RBAC), permissions management and user administration; configures who can view, edit and approve ESG data, reports and module configurations.

calculationEngine.formula: ACS = Configured Users / Total Users × Role Assignment Completeness
calculationEngine.brief: Proportion of platform users with fully configured role assignments and permission sets; gaps create compliance and data security risk.

dataPoints.name: Configured Users
dataPoints.interpretation: Total platform users with active role assignments across all modules and data domains.

dataPoints.name: Roles Defined
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of distinct role definitions spanning Viewer, Analyst, Manager, Admin, Auditor and custom roles.

dataPoints.name: Permission Gaps
dataPoints.interpretation: Users with incomplete or conflicting role assignments flagged for immediate resolution.

userInteraction: Define role hierarchy and permission matrix
Assign users to roles based on function and data access requirements
Configure module-level and data-domain permissions
Conduct periodic access reviews and remove stale accounts
Generate access reports for internal audit and GDPR/SOC 2 compliance
references: GDPR Article 25 Data Protection by Design
SOC 2 Type II CC6 Logical Access

acronyms.def: Security model granting permissions based on organisational roles rather than individual user identity.
AICPA audit framework covering security, availability, confidentiality and privacy of cloud services.
Authentication requiring two or more verification factors; required for SOC 2 and ISO 27001 compliance.

dataLineage.source: Corporate Directory / IdP, Module Access Logs, Role Definitions
dataLineage.flow: RBAC engine + permission matrix + access audit tools
dataLineage.output: Role assignment reports, access gap alerts, audit logs, GDPR/SOC 2 evidence

/user-role-management.valueSummary: Least-privilege access control is foundational to ESG platform security; GDPR Article 25 requires data protection by design including role-based data minimisation for personal and sensitive ESG data.

/value-chain-dashboard.title: Value Chain Dashboard
/value-chain-dashboard.description: Upstream and downstream ESG performance mapping dashboard providing a unified view of value chain sustainability KPIs from raw material extraction through end-of-life product management.

calculationEngine.formula: VCEI = Σ (Tier ESG Score × Emissions Weightᴵᵉʳ) / Σ Weight
calculationEngine.brief: Scope 3 emissions-weighted aggregation of ESG scores across all upstream and downstream value chain tiers; aligns with ESRS E1-6 Scope 3 categories.

dataPoints.name: Value Chain ESG Index
dataPoints.interpretation: Composite ESG score across all 15 Scope 3 categories weighted by emissions magnitude.

dataPoints.name: Scope 3 Coverage
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of estimated Scope 3 emissions with primary or secondary data; CSRD requires primary for material categories.

dataPoints.name: Highest-Impact Category
dataPoints.interpretation: Scope 3 category with largest absolute emissions; primary focus for downstream product design interventions.

userInteraction: Map value chain tiers and identify material Scope 3 categories
Collect primary and secondary ESG data across upstream suppliers and downstream customers
Score each value chain node on E, S and G dimensions
Weight scores by emissions magnitude to compute VCEI
Report value chain performance in CSRD ESRS E1 and S1–S4 disclosures
references: GHG Protocol Corporate Value Chain (Scope 3) Standard
ESRS S2 Workers in the Value Chain

acronyms.def: Scope 3 emissions-weighted composite ESG score across all upstream and downstream value chain tiers.
Indirect GHG emissions in a company’s value chain; typically 70–90% of total corporate footprint for manufacturers.

dataLineage.source: Supplier ESG Data, Customer Product Data, Scope 3 Inventory, Tier Mapping
dataLineage.flow: VCEI engine + Scope 3 category weighting + ESG aggregation
dataLineage.output: Value chain dashboard, CSRD ESRS E1/S2 disclosures, Scope 3 hotspot map

/value-chain-dashboard.valueSummary: Scope 3 emissions average 11.4× Scope 1+2 for S&P 500 companies (CDP 2023); value chain ESG management is the largest lever for corporate climate impact and supply chain due diligence compliance.

/vc-impact.title: VC Impact
/vc-impact.description: Venture capital impact investment measurement and reporting platform; tracks impact KPIs across portfolio companies against IMP, IRIS+ and SDG frameworks with financial return attribution.

calculationEngine.formula: IMM = Impact Value Created / Capital Invested
calculationEngine.brief: Ratio of monetised social/environmental impact to capital invested; complements financial IRR with impact return measure.

dataPoints.name: Portfolio IMM
dataPoints.interpretation: Average impact multiple of money across active VC impact portfolio; 2×+ considered impact-additive.

dataPoints.name: Companies with IRIS+ KPIs
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of portfolio companies with standardised IRIS+ impact metrics tracked quarterly.

dataPoints.name: SDG Contribution Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Average SDG contribution score across portfolio; mapped to primary and secondary SDGs by sector.

userInteraction: Define impact thesis and target SDGs at fund level
Onboard portfolio companies with IRIS+ KPI framework
Collect quarterly impact data and validate against IRIS+ standards
Compute IMM using sector-appropriate monetisation coefficients
Report to LPs against IMP five dimensions and UN SDG targets
references: UN SDG Impact Standards for Private Equity 2021

acronyms.def: Ratio of monetised impact created to capital invested; impact analogue to financial return multiple.
GIIN’s system of generally accepted impact metrics enabling standardised impact reporting across asset classes.
Global initiative establishing five dimensions of impact (What, Who, How Much, Contribution, Risk) for impact measurement.

dataLineage.source: Portfolio Company Impact Data, IRIS+ Metrics, SDG Mapping, Financial Returns
dataLineage.flow: IMM computation + IRIS+ aggregation + SDG contribution scoring
dataLineage.output: LP impact reports, IRIS+ dashboards, SDG contribution statements, IMP disclosures

/vc-impact.valueSummary: Impact VC has grown from $2B to ≄18B AUM 2015–2023; GIIN reports median VC impact funds achieve 3.0× IMM while delivering competitive financial returns to conventional venture benchmarks.

/vcm-registry-analytics.title: VCM Registry Analytics
/vcm-registry-analytics.description: Voluntary carbon market registry analytics platform aggregating issuance, retirement and pricing data across Verra VCS, Gold Standard, ACR and CAR registries with integrity scoring.

calculationEngine.formula: RIS = Additionality Score × Permanence Score × Verification Score
calculationEngine.brief: Multiplicative integrity score across additionality, permanence and third-party verification; below 0.6 flags credits as low-integrity.

dataPoints.name: Credits Tracked (MtCO2)
dataPoints.interpretation: Total carbon credits tracked across all major voluntary registries including issuance and retirements.

dataPoints.name: Avg Credit Price
dataPoints.interpretation: Volume-weighted average voluntary carbon credit price across all project types and vintages.

dataPoints.name: Low-Integrity Flags
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of queried credits flagged as low-integrity based on ICVCM CCP assessment.

userInteraction: Connect to registry APIs (Verra, GS, ACR, CAR)
Ingest real-time issuance and retirement data
Score each credit type against ICVCM Core Carbon Principles
Track price discovery across project categories and vintages
Generate integrity reports for procurement and claims verification
references: Gold Standard for the Global Goals

acronyms.def: Market for carbon credits generated by emissions reduction or removal projects, purchased voluntarily by corporates.

acronyms.full: Integrity Council for the Voluntary Carbon Market
acronyms.def: Governance body establishing Core Carbon Principles (CCPs) as the global standard for high-integrity carbon credits.
Initiative establishing Claims Code of Practice defining legitimate corporate use of voluntary carbon credits.

dataLineage.flow: Integrity scoring + registry aggregation + price analytics
dataLineage.output: Credit integrity dashboard, registry analytics, procurement recommendations

/vcm-registry-analytics.valueSummary: The VCM grew to 200 MtCO2e retired in 2023; integrity concerns following investigative journalism (Guardian 2023) have driven ICVCM CCP adoption; only CCP-approved credits should support corporate claims.

/water-agriculture-risk.title: Water Agriculture Risk
/water-agriculture-risk.description: Water stress impact on agricultural supply chains; quantifies crop yield risk, food price volatility and supply disruption from water scarcity across sourcing geographies using WRI Aqueduct Agricultural data.

calculationEngine.formula: AWRS = Water Stress × Crop Sensitivity × Sourcing Concentration
calculationEngine.brief: Composite risk score combining basin-level water stress, crop-specific sensitivity to water deficit and supply chain sourcing concentration in stressed basins.

dataPoints.name: High Water Stress Sourcing
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of agricultural sourcing from basins with WRI Aqueduct score >3 (High or Extremely High stress).

dataPoints.name: Yield Loss Exposure
dataPoints.interpretation: Expected average crop yield reduction in sourcing basins under 2°C warming scenario by 2030.

dataPoints.name: Supply Disruption VaR
dataPoints.interpretation: Value at risk from agricultural supply disruption due to water stress at 95th percentile.

userInteraction: Map agricultural sourcing to originating river basins
Apply WRI Aqueduct water stress scores by basin
Weight by crop type water sensitivity and yield impact coefficients
Compute AWRS and supply disruption VaR
Report to TNFD and CSRD biodiversity and water disclosures
references: WRI Aqueduct Agricultural Water Risk Framework 2022
IPCC AR6 WG2 Chapter 5: Food Systems

acronyms.def: Composite metric combining water stress, crop sensitivity and sourcing concentration for agricultural supply chain risk assessment.
FAO’s global water information system providing national water use and availability statistics.
Framework for nature-related risk and opportunity disclosure including freshwater ecosystem impacts.

dataLineage.source: Sourcing Geographies, WRI Aqueduct Data, Crop Yield Models, FAO AQUASTAT
dataLineage.flow: AWRS engine + yield impact modelling + supply disruption VaR
dataLineage.output: Agricultural water risk dashboard, TNFD water disclosures, supply chain resilience report

/water-agriculture-risk.valueSummary: Water scarcity already reduces global crop yields by 7–14% annually; climate change will increase water-stressed agricultural area by 40% by 2050, with implications for food security and commodity price volatility.

/water-risk.title: Water Risk
/water-risk.description: Corporate water risk assessment using WRI Aqueduct methodology; scores facility and supply chain exposure to physical, regulatory and reputational water risk across five sub-indicators.

calculationEngine.formula: AWRI = Σ (Sub-indicator Score × Weight)
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted composite of five sub-indicators: baseline water stress, interannual variability, seasonal variability, groundwater depletion and return flow ratio.

dataPoints.name: Facilities in High Stress
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of facilities in high or extremely high water stress catchments (Aqueduct score >3).

dataPoints.name: Avg Aqueduct Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Portfolio-weighted mean Aqueduct risk score; 3–4 = High, 4–5 = Extremely High stress.

dataPoints.name: Water Withdrawal Intensity
dataPoints.interpretation: Revenue-normalised water withdrawal; benchmark against sector median.

userInteraction: Geocode facility locations to WRI Aqueduct catchment polygons
Extract Aqueduct sub-indicator scores for each facility
Compute weighted overall risk score
Identify high-priority facilities for water stewardship interventions
Report to CDP Water Security questionnaire and ESRS E3
references: ESRS E3 Water and Marine Resources

acronyms.def: Composite water risk score computed from WRI Aqueduct sub-indicators at catchment level.
International standard for responsible corporate water use in water-stressed catchments.

dataLineage.source: Facility Geocodes, WRI Aqueduct 4.0 Geodata, Water Withdrawal Data
dataLineage.flow: Catchment join + Aqueduct scoring + stewardship prioritisation
dataLineage.output: Water risk heatmap, CDP Water disclosures, ESRS E3 data package, stewardship action plans

/water-risk.valueSummary: Water is identified as a top 3 systemic risk in the World Economic Forum Global Risks Report 2024; 40% of global industrial facilities are in high water stress catchments, rising to 60% by 2050.

/water-risk-analytics.title: Water Risk Analytics
/water-risk-analytics.description: Detailed water stress, scarcity, quality and flood analytics platform providing granular basin-level assessment integrating WRI Aqueduct, GEMS/Water quality data and IPCC hydrology projections.

calculationEngine.formula: CWRI = 0.4 × Stress + 0.25 × Quality + 0.2 × FloodRisk + 0.15 × Regulatory
calculationEngine.brief: Weighted composite spanning physical water stress, quality degradation, flood exposure and regulatory risk; enables multi-dimensional water risk prioritisation.

dataPoints.name: Basins Assessed
dataPoints.interpretation: Number of HydroBASINS level-6 catchments with full CWRI assessment.

dataPoints.name: Critical Quality Hotspots
dataPoints.interpretation: Catchments with combined high stress and poor water quality requiring operational water treatment investment.

dataPoints.name: 2050 Stress Increase
dataPoints.interpretation: Projected increase in population-weighted water stress by 2050 under SSP2-4.5 scenario.

userInteraction: Integrate WRI Aqueduct, GEMS/Water and flood hazard layers by catchment
Compute sub-indicator scores for stress, quality, flood and regulatory risk
Aggregate to CWRI using sector-adjusted weights
Project forward to 2030/2050 under IPCC scenarios
Report to ESRS E3, TNFD and CDP Water with basin-level evidence
references: IPCC AR6 WG1 Chapter 8: Water Cycle Changes

acronyms.def: Multi-dimensional water risk score covering physical stress, quality, flood and regulatory dimensions.
UN Environment Programme’s global freshwater quality monitoring database covering 4,000+ stations worldwide.
Global catchment delineation dataset at multiple Pfafstetter levels; basis for WRI Aqueduct spatial analysis.

dataLineage.source: WRI Aqueduct Geodata, GEMS/Water Quality, Flood Hazard Maps, IPCC Projections
dataLineage.flow: Multi-source integration + CWRI computation + scenario projection
dataLineage.output: Detailed water risk analytics, basin-level reports, ESRS E3/TNFD disclosures, CDP Water package

/water-risk-analytics.valueSummary: Freshwater quality degradation affects 40% of global river length (UN 2023); combined stress and quality risk is most acute in South Asia, North Africa and Northern China where industrial and agricultural demand converges.

/worker-heat-stress.title: Worker Heat Stress
/worker-heat-stress.description: Outdoor worker heat exposure risk and labour productivity loss analytics platform using WBGT and UTCI indices, ILO productivity loss curves and IPCC warming projections by geography and occupation.

calculationEngine.formula: WBGT = 0.7 × Tₘᵣ + 0.2 × Tᵍ + 0.1 × Tₐᵒᵐ
standards: ILO Working on a Warmer Planet 2019
calculationEngine.brief: Composite thermal stress index combining natural wet bulb (humidity), globe (radiation) and dry bulb (air) temperatures; threshold for heavy work: WBGT ≥25°C.

dataPoints.name: Workers at Risk (>2°C)
dataPoints.interpretation: Proportion of outdoor workforce exposed to unsafe WBGT levels (>28°C for heavy work) under 2°C warming scenario.

dataPoints.name: Productivity Loss (2030)
dataPoints.interpretation: Projected reduction in productive work capacity due to heat stress across outdoor operations by 2030.

dataPoints.name: High-Risk Geographies
dataPoints.interpretation: Countries where WBGT will exceed ISO 7933 safety thresholds for heavy outdoor work for >60 days/year by 2050.

userInteraction: Map workforce locations and outdoor exposure hours by role
Apply WBGT calculations using local temperature, humidity and radiation data
Project WBGT under IPCC warming scenarios to 2030/2050
Quantify productivity loss using ILO sector-specific curves
Report to ESRS S1 working conditions and GRI 403 health and safety
references: ILO Working on a Warmer Planet 2019
ISO 7933 Ergonomics of the Thermal Environment
IPCC AR6 WG1 Chapter 11: Weather and Climate Extremes
Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change 2023

acronyms.def: Composite heat stress index accounting for temperature, humidity, radiation and wind; primary metric for occupational heat risk.
Physiologically equivalent temperature index applicable across global climatic zones; alternative to WBGT for outdoor settings.
UN agency estimating 2.4% of global working hours lost annually to heat stress, equivalent to 80M full-time jobs.

/workplace-health-safety.title: Workplace Health & Safety
/workplace-health-safety.description: WHS incident analytics platform tracking LTIFR, TRIR, fatality rates and near-miss incidents with benchmarking against sector peers and GRI 403 regulatory reporting.

calculationEngine.formula: LTIFR = (Lost Time Injuries × 1,000,000) / Hours Worked
calculationEngine.brief: Industry-standard injury rate normalised per million hours worked; primary lagging indicator for workforce safety performance.

dataPoints.name: LTIFR
dataPoints.interpretation: Lost time injury frequency rate per million hours worked; sector median 2.4 (manufacturing).

dataPoints.name: TRIR
dataPoints.interpretation: Total recordable incident rate per million hours; includes injuries requiring medical treatment beyond first aid.

dataPoints.name: Fatalities (YTD)
dataPoints.interpretation: Year-to-date workplace fatalities; zero is the universal target; any fatality triggers mandatory investigation.

userInteraction: Collect incident data from operational safety management systems
Compute LTIFR, TRIR and fatality rate against hours worked
Benchmark against sector peers using ILO and IOGP data
Identify high-risk sites and causal factors
Report against GRI 403, ESRS S1 and national regulatory requirements
references: ILO R194 List of Occupational Diseases 2019

acronyms.def: LTIs per million hours worked; primary lagging safety indicator for international benchmarking.
All work-related injuries requiring more than first aid per million hours; broader than LTIFR.

acronyms.full: International Association of Oil & Gas Producers
acronyms.def: Industry body publishing annual safety performance benchmarks covering 1+ billion exposure hours.

/climate-risk-premium.title: Climate Risk Premium
/climate-risk-premium.description: Quantifies the additional return premium demanded by investors for bearing climate-related financial risks across asset classes. Decomposes climate risk premium into physical risk, transition risk, and climate policy uncertainty components using factor model regression across equity, credit, and real asset markets.

calculationEngine.formula: CRP = α + β_phys × PhysRisk + β_trans × TransRisk + β_pol × PolicyUnc + ε
calculationEngine.brief: Climate risk premium estimated via Fama-MacBeth cross-sectional regression of excess returns on physical and transition risk factor loadings; policy uncertainty factor from Baker-Bloom-Davis EPU index climate component.

dataPoints.name: Equity Climate Risk Premium
dataPoints.interpretation: Annualised return premium for high climate risk stocks vs low climate risk peers

dataPoints.name: Credit Climate Spread Premium
dataPoints.interpretation: Additional credit spread for high-carbon issuers controlling for credit rating and sector

userInteraction: Select asset class and climate risk factor decomposition
View factor loading β estimates for physical, transition, and policy uncertainty
Compare climate risk premium across sectors and geographies
Use premium estimates for climate-adjusted discount rate in DCF valuations
references: Giglio et al. (2021) Climate Change and Long-Run Discount Rates

acronyms.def: Required excess return demanded by investors for exposure to climate-related financial risks
Baker-Bloom-Davis index measuring uncertainty around climate policy; driver of climate policy risk factor

dataLineage.source: Asset return data and climate risk scores
dataLineage.flow: Fama-MacBeth cross-sectional regression
dataLineage.output: Climate beta estimates and risk premium decomposition

dataLineage.flow: Scenario-conditional factor loading
dataLineage.output: Forward-looking CRP under each scenario

/climate-risk-premium.valueSummary: The climate risk premium is foundational for climate-adjusted asset valuation and portfolio construction. Understanding whether climate risk is priced by markets determines whether climate-aware portfolios earn alpha or simply reflect rational risk compensation, with direct implications for TCFD risk and opportunity disclosure.

/undp-blended-finance.title: UNDP Blended Finance Framework
/undp-blended-finance.description: Comprehensive UNDP Blended Finance Framework implementation covering all 6 strategic pillars for mobilizing private capital toward SDGs and climate goals in developing countries. Includes leverage calculators, instrument design tools, market intelligence across 8 regions, vehicle structuring (SPV/fund/facility), IRIS+ impact measurement, deal pipeline management, risk matrices, and DFI benchmark analytics. Covers the full blended finance lifecycle from national strategy alignment through deal structuring to impact reporting.

calculationEngine.methodology: UNDP 6-Pillar Blended Finance + OECD-DAC Leverage Ratio
calculationEngine.formula: LeverageRatio = TotalMobilized / ConcessionalDeployed; CatalyticRatio = PrivateCapital / PublicCapital; IRR_Blended = Σ(CFt / (1+r)^t) where CFt includes concessional terms
standards: IFC Operating Principles for Impact Management
calculationEngine.brief: The module implements UNDP's 6-pillar approach: (1) Public Sector Coordination — maps NDC/SDG financing gaps and inter-agency roles; (2) Policy & Regulatory Environment — legal frameworks for PPPs, SPVs, green taxonomy, carbon pricing; (3) Supply & Deployment of Concessional Capital — DFI commitments, minimum concessionality principle, transparent pipeline mapping; (4) Market Development — domestic institutional capital mobilization, blended finance ecosystem building; (5) Innovation & Impact Instruments — guarantees, first-loss tranches, results-based finance, outcome bonds; (6) Knowledge & Capacity Building — south-south learning, practitioner training, evidence base. The calculator engine computes leverage ratios (typically 3-8x for climate, 1-3x for adaptation), blended IRR under different tranche structures, first-loss absorption capacity, and IRIS+ impact metrics (lives improved, tCO2e avoided, MW installed).

dataPoints.name: 6 Strategic Pillars
dataPoints.interpretation: Public coordination, policy/regulatory, concessional supply, market development, innovation instruments, knowledge & capacity — each with subsections, indicators, and maturity levels

dataPoints.name: Maturity Assessment
dataPoints.interpretation: Each pillar scored from No Strategy/Ad Hoc → Awareness → Planning → Implementation → Scaling/Market-Standard

dataPoints.name: Leverage Ratio
dataPoints.interpretation: Climate infrastructure averages 5-8x; adaptation 1-3x; social sectors 2-4x. Higher ratios indicate more efficient use of scarce concessional resources

dataPoints.name: Catalytic Ratio
dataPoints.interpretation: Measures how much private capital each dollar of public/concessional capital mobilizes. DFI target: minimum $3 private per $1 concessional

dataPoints.name: Market Intelligence
dataPoints.interpretation: Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, East Asia & Pacific, Latin America & Caribbean, MENA, Eastern Europe & Central Asia, Pacific SIDS, Global — each with transaction count, financing volume, top sectors, avg deal size

dataPoints.name: Vehicle Types
dataPoints.interpretation: SPV (Special Purpose Vehicle), Pooled Fund, DFI Facility, Syndication Platform — each with governance structure, investor waterfall, fee model

dataPoints.name: IRIS+ Impact Metrics
dataPoints.interpretation: Lives improved, tCO2e avoided, MW installed, hectares protected, jobs created, SMEs financed — aligned with SDG indicator framework

dataPoints.name: Deal Pipeline
dataPoints.interpretation: Origination → Screening → Due Diligence → Structuring → IC Approval → Closing → Disbursement → Monitoring → Exit — with conversion rates and avg timeline

dataPoints.name: Risk Matrix
dataPoints.interpretation: Market Risk, Credit/Counterparty Risk, Political/Regulatory Risk, Currency/FX Risk, Climate/Environmental Risk — each scored L/M/H with mitigation instruments

dataPoints.interpretation: IFC, AfDB, ADB, EBRD, EIB, KfW, FMO, CDC, Proparco, JICA — leverage ratios, portfolio size, climate allocation %, blended finance share

dataPoints.name: Concessionality Calculator
dataPoints.interpretation: Calculates the grant element (concessionality level) of a loan facility based on interest rate, grace period, maturity, and discount rate — ensuring minimum concessionality compliance

/undp-blended-finance.valueSummary: This module is the definitive tool for structuring, analyzing, and monitoring blended finance transactions aligned with UNDP's framework for mobilizing private capital toward SDGs. It enables practitioners to (1) assess national readiness across 6 strategic pillars, (2) design deal structures with appropriate concessionality and leverage, (3) benchmark against DFI performance, (4) measure impact using IRIS+ standards, and (5) manage deal pipeline from origination to exit. Critical for development finance professionals, DFI investment officers, impact investors, and government officials designing blended finance strategies to close the $4.2T annual SDG financing gap.

/india-ccts.title: India Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS)
/india-ccts.description: Ultra-detailed India CCTS compliance, trading, and financial impact module covering all 9 BEE-approved offset methodologies (BM-EN01 through BM-FR05), 18 approved calculation tools (BM-T-001 through BM-T-AR-0006), 740 designated entities across 9 sectors, carbon pricing scenarios, CBAM linkage analysis, PAT scheme transition, and full calculation engine with assurance-ready audit trails per ISO 14064/14065.

calculationEngine.methodology: BEE CCTS Methodology Engine (9 methodologies × 18 tools)
calculationEngine.formula: ER_y = Baseline_y - Project_y - Leakage_y; CCC = floor(ER_y); Value = CCC × Price_INR
calculationEngine.brief: The engine implements all 9 BEE-approved methodologies end-to-end: (1) BM-EN01.001/002 for grid-connected and captive renewable energy using CEA grid emission factors; (2) BM-IN02.001/002 for industrial fuel switching and process emissions reduction with sector-specific benchmarks (cement clinker ratio, steel scrap ratio); (3) BM-WA03.001/002 for landfill methane recovery and waste-to-energy using IPCC Tier 2 FOD model; (4) BM-AG04.001 for agricultural residue methane avoidance; (5) BM-FR05.001/002 for mangrove and terrestrial afforestation/reforestation with 30-year crediting and buffer pools. Each methodology pipeline: Data Capture → Validation → Baseline Calc → Project Calc → Leakage Calc → Net Reduction → CCC Issuance → Audit Trail → Assurance Checklist.

dataPoints.name: Designated Entities
dataPoints.interpretation: 9 sectors: Iron & Steel (253), Textiles (90), Cement (50+), Petrochemicals (39), Pulp & Paper (30+), Chlor-Alkali (30+), Fertiliser (29), Petroleum Refining (21), Aluminium (13)

/rbi-climate-risk.title: RBI Climate Risk Directions 2025
/rbi-climate-risk.description: India-specific climate risk compliance module implementing RBI's Climate Risk and Sustainable Finance Directions 2025 for all Scheduled Commercial Banks, Small Finance Banks, AIFIs, and Top/Upper Layer NBFCs. Covers CRAR capital adequacy (9% CET1 vs Basel 8%), D-SIB buffers, NGFS scenario analysis with India-specific GDP/carbon price paths, financed emissions attribution using CEA grid factors, physical risk exposure across 15 Indian states, and BRSR Core alignment with ESRS crosswalk.

calculationEngine.methodology: RBI CRAR Capital Framework + NGFS India Scenario Engine
calculationEngine.formula: CRAR = (CET1 + AT1 + Tier2) / RWA_climate_adjusted; Climate_Addon = Σ(sector_exposure × climate_multiplier)
standards: RBI Master Circular on Basel III Capital (CRAR)
calculationEngine.brief: The module implements RBI's four-pillar climate risk framework (Governance, Strategy, Risk Management, Metrics & Targets) with India-specific capital calculations. CRAR uses RBI's 9% CET1 minimum (vs Basel 8%) with D-SIB surcharges for SBI (0.6%), HDFC Bank (0.4%), ICICI Bank (0.4%). Climate risk add-on computed from sector-level exposure weighted by emission intensity and transition risk multiplier. Physical risk modeled across 15 Indian states for flood, cyclone, heatwave, and drought using state-level vulnerability indices.

It provides: (1) A compliance checklist mapped to RBI's 4-pillar framework with 25 sub-requirements; (2) India-specific capital adequacy modeling using CRAR 9% CET1 with D-SIB surcharges; (3) NGFS scenario analysis calibrated for India's GDP, carbon price, and energy transition trajectory; (4) Lending book emissions attribution using official CEA grid emission factors; (5) Physical risk exposure mapping across Indian states; (6) BRSR Core alignment tracking for the 15 mandatory assurance KPIs.

/green-building-valuation.title: Green Building Valuation

calculationEngine.formula: GreenPremium = (RentGreen - RentBrown) / RentBrown × 100; StrandedRisk = max(0, CarbonIntensity - SectorDecarbPath)
calculationEngine.brief: Computes energy use intensity (EUI kWh/m²/yr), embodied carbon (kgCO2e/m²), and rental/capital value premium versus brown-discount peers

/real-estate-climate-risk.title: Real Estate Climate Risk
/real-estate-climate-risk.description: Assesses physical climate hazards (flood, wildfire, heat, subsidence) at property level using geocoded exposure data and IPCC SSP scenarios. Calculates expected annual loss (EAL), insurance affordability cliff, and mortgage default probability uplift.

calculationEngine.formula: EAL = Σ [P(hazard_i) × Damage_i × AssetValue]; InsuranceAffordabilityRatio = Premium / AnnualRent
calculationEngine.brief: Integrates multi-hazard exceedance probability curves with vulnerability functions to produce asset-level EAL, then aggregates to portfolio level with spatial correlation

dataPoints.name: EAL (Flood, 1.5°C)

dataPoints.interpretation: Expected annual flood loss as % of property value — doubles under RCP8.5 by 2050

dataPoints.name: Insurance Coverage Gap
dataPoints.interpretation: Share of climate-exposed residential properties uninsured or underinsured globally

dataPoints.name: Mortgage Default Uplift
dataPoints.interpretation: Climate-risk exposure adds 18–34 bps to mortgage default probability in high-hazard zones

Review hazard exposure scores by peril

ECB Working Paper 2022 — Climate Change and the Valuation of Real Estate

Synonymous with EAL — used in (re)insurance pricing and catastrophe modelling

/climate-mortgage-analytics.title: Climate Mortgage Analytics
/climate-mortgage-analytics.description: Integrates physical and transition climate risks into mortgage portfolio analysis. Models climate-adjusted LTV, probability of default (PD) uplift, loss given default (LGD) impact, and regulatory capital implications under Basel IV climate risk add-ons.

calculationEngine.formula: PD_climate = PD_base × (1 + β_physical × HazardScore + β_transition × EPC_gap); LGD_climate = max(LGD_base, 1 - ClimateAdjustedLTV)
standards: ECB Guide on Climate and Environmental Risks 2020

/infrastructure-climate-resilience.title: Infrastructure Climate Resilience
/infrastructure-climate-resilience.description: Quantifies climate physical risk to infrastructure assets (transport, energy, water, telecoms) using engineering vulnerability curves and IPCC SSP scenarios. Calculates resilience investment NPV, adaptation cost-benefit ratios, and regulatory compliance gaps.

calculationEngine.formula: ResilienceScore = Σ [w_i × (1 - VulnScore_i × HazardFreq_i)]; AdaptNPV = Σ [ΔEALt / (1+r)^t] - CapexAdapt
standards: EU Taxonomy Annex II — Climate Change Adaptation

calculationEngine.formula: FE = Σ [Attribution_i × (OpCarbon_i + EmbodiedCarbon_i)]; Attribution_i = OutstandingLoan_i / PropertyValue_i
standards: PCAF Standard Part C — Real Estate 2022

/green-hydrogen-economics-df.title: Green Hydrogen Economics
/green-hydrogen-economics-df.description: Models the full green hydrogen value chain economics from electrolyser CapEx and renewable electricity costs through compression, storage, transport, and end-use applications. Calculates levelised cost of hydrogen (LCOH), emissions intensity, and investment payback under capacity factor and electricity price scenarios.

calculationEngine.formula: LCOH = [CapEx × CRF + OpEx_fixed] / AnnualH2Production + (ElecCost × ElecConsumption/kg) + StorageTransport; CRF = r(1+r)^n / [(1+r)^n - 1]
calculationEngine.brief: Capital recovery factor (CRF) amortises electrolyser CapEx; electricity cost dominates (60–75% of LCOH); capacity factor critically affects cost through denominator

dataPoints.interpretation: Current green hydrogen cost range — $1/kg target by 2030 requires electrolyser cost <$200/kW + <$20/MWh renewable

/carbon-capture-finance.title: Carbon Capture Finance
/carbon-capture-finance.description: Evaluates the economics of carbon capture, utilisation, and storage (CCUS) projects including point-source CCS, direct air capture (DAC), and bioenergy with CCS (BECCS). Models capture costs, storage capacity, 45Q tax credits, carbon market revenues, and permanence risk for CDR credits.

calculationEngine.formula: NPV_CCS = Σ [(CarbonRevenue_t + 45Q_t - CaptureCost_t - StorageCost_t) / (1+r)^t]; CaptureEfficiency = CO2Captured / CO2TotalEmitted
standards: IEA CCUS in Clean Energy Transitions 2020

/energy-storage-analytics.title: Energy Storage Analytics
/energy-storage-analytics.description: Models battery energy storage system (BESS) and long-duration storage (LDES) project economics including revenue stacking from frequency response, capacity markets, energy arbitrage, and renewable firming. Calculates levelised cost of storage (LCOS) and storage investment NPV.

calculationEngine.formula: LCOS = [CapEx + Σ(OpEx_t + EnergyCost_t) / (1+r)^t] / Σ[EnergyDispatched_t / (1+r)^t]; RoundTripEfficiency = EnergyOut / EnergyIn

/water-food-energy-nexus.title: Water-Food-Energy Nexus Analytics

/loss-and-damage-finance.title: Loss & Damage Finance Analytics
/loss-and-damage-finance.description: Quantifies climate loss and damage (L&D) — economic costs from climate change that cannot be adapted to — across developing countries. Analyses the Santiago Network, UNFCCC L&D Fund modalities, parametric insurance solutions, and national L&D financing strategies.

calculationEngine.formula: L&D_Economic = DirectLoss + IndirectLoss + NonEconomicLoss; DirectLoss = AssetDamage + BusinessInterruption; ParametricPayout = max(0, (TriggerIndex - Threshold) × PayoutRate × SumInsured)
standards: IPCC AR6 WGII Chapter 16 — Key Risks Across Sectors
ARC African Risk Capacity Parametric Framework
calculationEngine.brief: L&D distinct from adaptation costs — represents residual climate harm; parametric insurance uses index triggers to accelerate payouts and reduce basis risk versus indemnity products

/climate-displacement-risk.title: Climate Displacement Risk Analytics

/blue-carbon-finance.title: Blue Carbon Finance
/blue-carbon-finance.description: Evaluates blue carbon ecosystem investment — mangroves, seagrasses, tidal marshes — for carbon sequestration, coastal protection co-benefits, and biodiversity credits. calculationEngine.formula: SupplierESG = w_E × EnvironmentalScore + w_S × SocialScore + w_G × GovernanceScore; EnvironmentalScore = ClimateScore × 0.5 + WaterScore × 0.25 + BiodiversityScore × 0.25

/scope3-category-analytics.title: Scope 3 Category Deep Analytics
/scope3-category-analytics.description: Provides deep-dive analytics for the highest-impact Scope 3 categories — Cat 1 (purchased goods), Cat 4 (upstream transport), Cat 11 (use of sold products), and Cat 15 (investments). calculationEngine.formula: S3reduction_cat_i = BaselineEmissions_i × (1 - ReductionLever_j × AdoptionRate_j); SupplierEngagementROI = EmissionsReduced × CarbonPrice / EngagementCost

/climate-trade-flow-analytics.title: Climate Trade Flow Analytics

/green-procurement-intelligence.title: Green Procurement Intelligence
/green-procurement-intelligence.description: Provides analytics for public and corporate green procurement — lifecycle cost analysis, green product market intelligence, environmental label verification, and EU Green Public Procurement criteria compliance. /wind-energy-finance.title: Wind Energy Finance Analytics
/wind-energy-finance.description: Models onshore and offshore wind project economics — turbine CapEx, capacity factor analysis, wake losses, cable/grid connection costs, O&M curves, and levelised cost of energy. Calculates project IRR, DSCR, and optimises turbine configuration for site-specific conditions.

calculationEngine.formula: LCOE_wind = (CapEx × CRF + OpEx_annual) / (8760 × CapacityFactor × InstalledCapacity); WakeEffect = 1 - (1 - CT/(4A)) for Betz limit considerations

calculationEngine.brief: LCOE strongly driven by capacity factor (wind class) and CapEx; wake losses from array layout modelled using Jensen/Gaussian wake models; offshore adds submarine cable and foundation costs

/ppa-analytics.title: Power Purchase Agreement Analytics
/ppa-analytics.description: Analyses corporate and utility Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) — price risk, volume risk, basis risk, and contract structure optimisation. Models PPA vs merchant price comparison, renewable energy certificate (REC) value, and additionality for corporate net zero claims.

calculationEngine.formula: PPANetValue = Σ [(PPAprice - MerchantPrice_t) × Volume_t / (1+r)^t]; VaR_PPA = PPANetValue - PPANetValue(P05); BasisRisk = CapturePrice - HubPrice
standards: GHG Protocol Scope 2 Guidance — Market-Based Method
calculationEngine.brief: PPA net value is the NPV of price hedge benefit vs merchant; VaR measures downside if electricity prices fall below PPA strike; basis risk captures location differential — key for CFD vs physical PPAs

/cdm-methodology-calculator.title: CDM Methodology Calculator
/cdm-methodology-calculator.description: Implements the UN Clean Development Mechanism (CDM) methodology suite for calculating emission reductions. Covers ACM0002 (grid electricity), ACM0014 (industrial energy efficiency), AMS-I.D (grid-connected renewables), TOOL07 project emissions tool, and real-time carbon registry pricing for CER, ERU, and voluntary offsets.

calculationEngine.formula: ER_y = BE_y - PE_y - LE_y; BE_y = EG_y × EF_grid,y; EF_grid,y = (OM_y × w_OM + BM_y × w_BM) / (w_OM + w_BM)
standards: CDM EB47 Annex II — Combined Margin Methodology
TOOL07 v4 — Tool to Calculate the Emission Factor for an Electricity System
calculationEngine.brief: Baseline emissions (BE) computed using TOOL07 v4 combined margin; project emissions (PE) from TOOL07; leakage (LE) from displaced generation; OM/BM weighted by grid dispatch data

/baseline-additionality-analyzer.title: Baseline & Additionality Analyzer
/baseline-additionality-analyzer.description: Implements the UNFCCC additionality and baseline methodology suite — TOOL01 (project additionality), TOOL02 (financial analysis), TOOL21 (suppressed demand), and TOOL07 baseline emission factors. Provides barrier analysis, investment analysis (IRR/NPV with bisection), and regulatory surplus demonstration.

calculationEngine.formula: Additionality: Project not viable without CDM/VCM revenue; IRR_withoutCarbon < WACC; NPV_withoutCarbon < 0; BarrierIdentified; NotCommonPractice
standards: TOOL01 v8 — Tool for the Demonstration of Additionality 2012
TOOL02 v6 — Tool for the Assessment of the Investment Barrier 2012
calculationEngine.brief: TOOL01 uses 3 steps: Step 1 regulatory surplus, Step 2 investment analysis (IRR/NPV with bisection convergence to 0.01%), Step 3 barrier analysis — any single barrier suffices for additionality

dataPoints.name: Financial Test IRR Threshold
dataPoints.interpretation: Investment barrier established if project IRR without carbon finance < (WACC - 2%); sensitivity tested at ±20%

dataPoints.interpretation: 70%+ of registered CDM projects rely on barrier analysis — most common: first-of-its-kind, technology risk

Demonstrate regulatory surplus (Step 1)

references: UNFCCC TOOL01 v8 — Tool for the Demonstration and Assessment of Additionality (2012)
UNFCCC TOOL02 v6 — Tool for the Assessment of the Investment Barrier (2012)
ICVCM Core Carbon Principles — Additionality Criterion 2023

/monte-carlo-uncertainty-engine.title: Monte Carlo Uncertainty Engine
/monte-carlo-uncertainty-engine.description: Implements GUM JCGM 100:2008 and Monte Carlo JCGM 101:2008 uncertainty quantification for carbon credit projects. Calculates combined standard uncertainty, expanded uncertainty (k=2, 95%), and probabilistic emission reduction distributions for CDM EB65 Annex 29 compliance.

calculationEngine.formula: u_c(y) = √[Σ(∂f/∂xᵢ)² × u²(xᵢ) + 2Σᵢ<ⱼ(∂f/∂xᵢ)(∂f/∂xⱼ)u(xᵢ,xⱼ)]; U = k × u_c (k=2 for 95% CI)
standards: GUM JCGM 100:2008 — Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement
CDM EB65 Annex 29 — Uncertainty Assessment Procedure
calculationEngine.brief: Sensitivity coefficients (∂f/∂xᵢ) derived analytically for CDM emission reduction formula; MC supplement uses Box-Muller normal sampling; combined uncertainty applies coverage factor k=2 for 95% confidence; deduction table from EB65

dataPoints.name: CDM Uncertainty Deduction Table
dataPoints.interpretation: EB65 uncertainty deduction: U<10%→0%, U<20%→3%, U<30%→5%, U<50%→10%, U≥50%→project ineligible

dataPoints.name: Typical CDM Project Uncertainty
dataPoints.interpretation: Most CDM projects achieve ±8–18% expanded uncertainty (k=2) — measurement and activity data dominant sources

dataPoints.name: MC Simulation Convergence
dataPoints.interpretation: JCGM 101 recommends ≥10,000 Monte Carlo trials for 95% CI convergence

userInteraction: Input emission reduction formula and uncertainty sources
Calculate sensitivity coefficients analytically
Apply GUM propagation formula for combined uncertainty
Run Monte Carlo simulation with Box-Muller normal sampling
Apply CDM EB65 deduction table to final ER quantity
references: JCGM 100:2008 — Evaluation of Measurement Data — Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement (GUM)
JCGM 101:2008 — Supplement 1 to GUM — Propagation of distributions using a Monte Carlo method
CDM EB65 Annex 29 — Procedure for Uncertainty Assessment of Emission Reductions (2011)
ISO 14064-3:2019 Section 6.7 — Uncertainty in GHG Statements

acronyms.full: Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement
acronyms.def: BIPM/ISO international standard for uncertainty quantification — JCGM 100:2008 is definitive reference
JCGM 101:2008 uncertainty propagation via random sampling — preferred for non-linear emission formulas
Statistical distribution of input parameter — Normal, Log-normal, or Rectangular per ISO 14064
PCAF DQ score 1–5 for input data — lower DQ increases uncertainty and EB65 deduction

dataLineage.flow: Uncertainty type A/B classification
dataLineage.output: Standard uncertainty by source with sensitivity coefficients

dataLineage.source: Activity data time series (metered or estimated)
dataLineage.flow: Uncertainty propagation
dataLineage.output: Combined standard uncertainty u_c for total ER

dataLineage.flow: ER adjustment
dataLineage.output: Verified emission reduction after uncertainty deduction

/carbon-credit-audit-trail.title: Carbon Credit Audit Trail
/carbon-credit-audit-trail.description: Provides immutable, hash-linked audit trail for carbon credit transactions and project lifecycle events. Implements ISO 14064-3:2019 §6.7 uncertainty records, VVB Corrective Action Request (CAR) and Clarification (CL) tracking, Forward Action Request (FAR) management, and registry retirement verification.

calculationEngine.formula: Hash_n = SHA256(EventData_n + Hash_{n-1} + Timestamp_n); AuditIntegrity = all Hash_n match recomputed values; ISO14064_3_record = {Event, Evidence, Uncertainty, VVBopinion, Timestamp, Hash}
standards: Verra VCS Standard v4.0 — Validation/Verification Requirements
CDM Validation and Verification Standard v2.0
ICVCM CCP Principle 3 — Transparent and Robust Independent Third-Party Validation and Verification
calculationEngine.brief: SHA-256 hash chain ensures tamper-evidence — any modification breaks the chain; VVB audit findings classified as CAR (must resolve), CL (must clarify), or FAR (future monitoring); ISO 14064-3 requires complete chain of evidence

dataPoints.name: Audit Trail Completeness
dataPoints.interpretation: ISO 14064-3 requires complete audit trail from data collection to verified statement — no gaps permitted

dataPoints.name: VVB CAR Resolution Rate
dataPoints.interpretation: All CARs must be resolved before VVB can issue positive verification statement — non-resolution = rejection

dataPoints.name: Registry Retirement Verification
dataPoints.source: Verra, Gold Standard, ACR Registry Rules
dataPoints.interpretation: Retired credits have unique serial numbers on public registries — prevents double counting

references: ISO 14064-3:2019 Section 6.7 — Record Keeping and Retention of Evidence
Verra VCS Validation and Verification Requirements v4.0
CDM Validation and Verification Manual v2.0
ICVCM Core Carbon Principles — Principle 3 (Transparent Validation/Verification)

VVB finding requiring explanation — does not require corrective action but must be closed

/solar-project-finance.title: Solar Project Finance Engine
/solar-project-finance.description: End-to-end project finance model for utility-scale solar IPP transactions. Computes LCOE, IRR (equity/project), NPV, DSCR, LLCR, and PLCR from user-defined capital structure, ITC/PTC IRA 2022 incentives, MACRS depreciation, and LP/GP 4-tier waterfall. Supports Monte Carlo P10/P50/P90 energy generation and scenario stress-testing across 12 analytical tabs.

calculationEngine.formula: IRR: Σ CF_t/(1+IRR)^t = 0 (Newton-Raphson 200 iter); LCOE = (CAPEX + Σ O&M_t/(1+r)^t) / Σ E_t/(1+r)^t; DSCR = CFADS / DebtService; LLCR = NPV(CFADS) / Outstanding Debt
calculationEngine.brief: Equity IRR computed via Newton-Raphson iteration on post-tax equity cash flows including ITC, MACRS depreciation shield, and LP/GP waterfall distributions. LCOE uses real levelized cost approach over project life. DSCR covers all 25 annual periods; LLCR uses NPV(CFADS) / loan balance. IRA 2022 ITC stack: base 30% + domestic content 10% + energy community 10% + low-income 10%; half-basis MACRS reduction applied. MACRS 5-year GDS: [20%, 32%, 19.2%, 11.52%, 11.52%, 5.76%].

dataPoints.name: LCOE
dataPoints.interpretation: Levelized Cost of Energy — all-in breakeven price per MWh over project life; competitive solar is $28–42/MWh for utility-scale

dataPoints.name: Equity IRR
dataPoints.interpretation: After-tax equity internal rate of return; institutional RE funds target 10–14%; merchant projects may reach 16–18%

dataPoints.name: DSCR (min)
dataPoints.interpretation: Minimum DSCR over loan tenor; lenders typically require ≥1.25× on PPA projects, ≥1.35× on merchant; covenant breach triggers cash trap

dataPoints.name: LLCR
dataPoints.interpretation: Loan Life Coverage Ratio — forward-looking; if LLCR falls below 1.0 the project cannot service remaining debt from projected cash flows

dataPoints.name: ITC Basis
dataPoints.formula: IRA 2022 §48E stack: Base + DC + EC + LIC
dataPoints.interpretation: Investment Tax Credit percentage; domestic content (+10%) requires ≥40% US-manufactured steel/iron/components; energy community (+10%) requires location in fossil fuel employment zone

dataPoints.name: P90 Energy Yield
dataPoints.formula: Monte Carlo Box-Muller, combined σ across 6 uncertainty sources
dataPoints.interpretation: P90 (1-in-10 exceedance) used by lenders for debt sizing; P50 used for equity returns; gap reflects GHI variability, wake losses, and availability uncertainty

dataPoints.name: MOIC (equity)
dataPoints.interpretation: Multiple on invested capital; typical RE equity fund target 2.0–2.5× over 7-year hold; includes carried interest promote at 80/20 split above preferred return

Review "Returns Engine" for equity IRR (Newton-Raphson), project IRR, and MOIC; "Financial Model" shows year-by-year cash flows including construction draw, ITC receipt, and debt amortization
Use "Scenario Engine" to define 4 stress scenarios (Base/Bull/Bear/Tail) and toggle inputs — IRR sensitivity waterfall and DSCR breach probability update instantly

SEIA/Wood Mackenzie U.S. Solar Market Insight — Utility-Scale Benchmarks 2024
NREL System Advisor Model (SAM) — Financial Metrics Technical Reference
S&P Global — Renewable Energy Project Finance Default and Recovery Study 2023

acronyms.def: All-in lifetime cost per MWh including CAPEX, O&M, financing — the breakeven price at which a project neither gains nor loses NPV
CFADS / (principal + interest) — lender covenant; min DSCR <1.0 triggers default; min DSCR covenant typically 1.20–1.35×
NPV(CFADS over remaining loan life) / outstanding balance — forward-looking solvency metric used at financial close
IRA 2022 §48E credit against federal tax liability; base 30% + stackable bonuses up to 70%; requires prevailing wage for full credit
5-year GDS depreciation for solar: [20%, 32%, 19.2%, 11.52%, 11.52%, 5.76%]; half-basis reduction when ITC elected
IRA 2022 §45Y; per-MWh credit ($27.50/MWh base 2024) over 10 years; alternative to ITC — better for high CF projects

acronyms.full: Cash Flow Available for Debt Service
acronyms.def: EBITDA − taxes − capex reserve − DSRA funding; primary metric lenders use to assess debt repayment capacity

dataLineage.source: User inputs: CAPEX $/Wdc, O&M, PPA price, debt terms, ITC toggles
dataLineage.flow: Financial model engine (Newton-Raphson IRR, MACRS, ITC stack)

dataLineage.source: Monte Carlo engine: combined σ across 6 uncertainty sources

dataLineage.source: IRA 2022 adder rules: domestic content, energy community, low-income
