/nature-based-solutions-finance.title: Nature-Based Solutions Project Finance
/nature-based-solutions-finance.description: NbS project finance covering REDD+, reforestation, blue carbon, and peatland restoration. Models carbon credit revenue, biodiversity and watershed co-benefit valuation, ICROA and Verra VCS standard compliance, and NbS blended finance structures.

calculationEngine.formula: NbS IRR = f(Carbon Revenue + Ecosystem Service Revenue - CAPEX - OPEX - Buffer Contribution); Carbon Revenue = Credits × Price × (1 - Buffer%)
calculationEngine.brief: Integrated financial model combining carbon credit revenue, biodiversity credit income, and ecosystem service payments against project costs and buffer requirements

dataPoints.name: Carbon Credit Revenue
dataPoints.formula: Net credits issued × carbon price × (1 - buffer contribution)
dataPoints.interpretation: Dependent on verified emissions reductions; REDD+ typically 0.5-5 tCO2/ha/yr net of leakage and buffer

dataPoints.name: Biodiversity Co-benefit Premium
dataPoints.formula: Price premium for CCB-certified vs standard VCS credits
dataPoints.interpretation: CCB (Climate, Community & Biodiversity) certification adds $2-8/credit; biodiversity credits (BioCarbon) separate market

dataPoints.name: Ecosystem Service Value
dataPoints.formula: Watershed protection + pollination + flood regulation services valued at shadow prices
dataPoints.interpretation: Co-benefits critical for blended finance from impact investors; watershed services often fundable via water utility payments

userInteraction: Define NbS project type and establish baseline carbon stock and ecosystem service inventory
Model carbon credit issuance schedule accounting for leakage (10-30%), permanence buffer (10-20%), and vintage timing
Value co-benefits using TEEB shadow prices, biodiversity credit prices, and ecosystem service payment data
Structure blended finance (grant + concessional + impact investment) and compute project IRR sensitivity
references: Verra (2022) VCS REDD+ Methodology VM0007 v1.6
ICROA (2023) Code of Best Practice — NbS Quality Criteria
SBTN (2023) Step-by-Step Guide for Business — Science Based Targets for Nature
TNFD (2023) Recommendations v1.0 — Nature-related Financial Disclosures

acronyms.def: Actions protecting, restoring, or sustainably managing ecosystems to address societal challenges including climate change

acronyms.full: Reducing Emissions from Deforestation and Forest Degradation
acronyms.def: UN Framework Convention mechanism incentivising developing countries to preserve forest carbon stocks
Verra standard certifying carbon projects for co-benefits in biodiversity and community development

acronyms.full: The Economics of Ecosystems and Biodiversity
acronyms.def: Global initiative quantifying economic value of ecosystem services for decision-making

dataLineage.flow: Issued credits, buffer contributions, methodology details → carbon revenue model
dataLineage.output: Project-level carbon revenue schedule

dataLineage.flow: Historical NbS credit prices by certification type → revenue assumptions
dataLineage.output: Price deck and premium analysis

dataLineage.flow: Ecosystem service shadow prices by habitat type → co-benefit monetisation
dataLineage.output: Total economic value and blended finance attractiveness

/nature-based-solutions-finance.valueSummary: Provides integrated NbS project finance modelling combining carbon, biodiversity, and ecosystem service revenues with rigorous VCS/ICROA compliance, enabling impact investor decision-making.

/forestry-timber-finance.title: Sustainable Forestry & Timber Project Finance
/forestry-timber-finance.description: Sustainable forestry and timber project finance analytics covering timber REIT structures, FSC/PEFC certification premium, carbon-timber dual revenue streams, biological asset valuation under IAS 41, and climate risk to timber yield.

calculationEngine.formula: Timberland Value = PV(Timber Revenue) + PV(Carbon Credits) - PV(Costs); IAS 41 Fair Value = Σ(Volume_i × Price_i × (1 - Harvesting Cost%))
standards: IPCC Good Practice Guidance for LULUCF
calculationEngine.brief: Integrated valuation combining standing timber biological asset fair value with carbon credit revenue streams, adjusted for climate risk to yield

dataPoints.name: Timberland IRR
dataPoints.formula: PV(timber + carbon revenue) / Timberland acquisition cost, solving for discount rate
dataPoints.interpretation: Historical timberland IRR 6-9%; outperforms listed equities over 20yr with low correlation; climate risk may reduce 1-2%

dataPoints.name: FSC Certification Premium
dataPoints.formula: (FSC timber price - non-certified price) / non-certified price
dataPoints.interpretation: Premium ranges 5-25% depending on end market (UK/EU higher); certification cost $0.5-2/ha/yr

dataPoints.name: Carbon-Timber Dual Revenue
dataPoints.formula: Incremental carbon stock growth per ha × carbon price
dataPoints.source: Verra VCS improved forest management methodology
dataPoints.interpretation: IFM additionality critical; cannot double-count with timber sale GHG accounting

userInteraction: Value standing timber inventory using IAS 41 fair value approach (volume × price × net realisable value)
Model FSC/PEFC certification premium impact on timber revenue assumptions
Layer carbon credit revenue from improved forest management (IFM) VCS methodology
Stress-test yield assumptions against climate risk scenarios (drought, fire, pest) and compute IRR sensitivity
references: NCREIF (2023) Timberland Property Index Annual Report
IAS 41 Agriculture — IASB Standard on Biological Assets Fair Value
FSC (2022) International Forest Management Standard FSC-STD-01-001
Verra (2022) Improved Forest Management Methodology VM0010

acronyms.def: Tax-advantaged vehicle; Timberland REITs (Weyerhaeuser, PotlatchDeltic) are major listed forestry investors
Verra VCS carbon methodology for forestry operations maintaining higher than baseline carbon stocks
International non-profit certifying forests managed to environmental, social, and economic standards

acronyms.full: Programme for the Endorsement of Forest Certification
acronyms.def: International umbrella body for national forest certification schemes; largest by area certified

dataLineage.flow: Historical returns, valuations by species and region → IRR benchmarks
dataLineage.output: Return expectations and risk calibration

dataLineage.flow: Certified vs uncertified timber price differentials by product → premium model
dataLineage.output: Revenue uplift from certification

dataLineage.flow: Carbon credit issuance rates by forest type and management practice → carbon revenue model
dataLineage.output: Dual revenue stream analysis

/forestry-timber-finance.valueSummary: Enables integrated timberland investment analysis combining IAS 41 biological asset valuation, FSC certification premium, and carbon-timber dual revenue with climate risk stress-testing.

/marine-blue-carbon-finance.title: Marine Blue Carbon Project Finance
/marine-blue-carbon-finance.description: Marine blue carbon project finance for mangrove, seagrass, and saltmarsh restoration. Models carbon sequestration rates (mangrove 6-8 tCO2/ha/yr), ICROA methodology compliance, coastal protection co-benefit valuation, and blue carbon credit market dynamics.

calculationEngine.formula: Blue Carbon NPV = Σ[(Carbon Credits × Price + Coastal Protection Value) - Restoration Cost - Monitoring Cost] / (1+r)^t; Sequestration = Area × Rate × (1 - Buffer%)
standards: Verra VCS VM0033 Tidal Wetland and Seagrass Restoration
ICROA Blue Carbon Code of Best Practice
calculationEngine.brief: NPV model integrating blue carbon sequestration revenue with avoided coastal damage co-benefit value against restoration and monitoring costs

dataPoints.name: Mangrove Sequestration Rate
dataPoints.formula: Above-ground + below-ground + soil organic carbon accumulation
dataPoints.interpretation: Mangrove soil carbon (8-10× soil depth) drives high rates; seagrass 0.5-2 t/ha/yr; saltmarsh 1.5-3 t/ha/yr

dataPoints.name: Coastal Protection Co-benefit
dataPoints.formula: Avoided storm damage + avoided erosion based on replacement cost
dataPoints.interpretation: Often exceeds carbon revenue; enables project financing via coastal insurance premium reduction or municipal payment

dataPoints.name: Blue Carbon Credit Price
dataPoints.formula: Market price for Verra VM0033 or Gold Standard mangrove credits
dataPoints.interpretation: Blue carbon premium over standard forestry credits; limited supply; tightening MRV standards may compress issuance

userInteraction: Map restoration site extent and classify by habitat type (mangrove, seagrass, saltmarsh)
Apply IPCC 2013 Wetlands Supplement sequestration rates with site-specific adjustments
Value coastal protection co-benefits using replacement cost or avoided damage approaches
Model credit issuance schedule under Verra VM0033, apply buffer contribution, and compute project NPV/IRR
references: Verra (2021) VCS Methodology VM0033 Tidal Wetland and Seagrass Restoration
IPCC (2013) 2013 Supplement to the 2006 IPCC Guidelines — Wetlands
IUCN (2020) Blue Carbon Initiative — Mangrove and Seagrass Restoration Guide
Ecosystem Marketplace (2023) Voluntary Carbon Markets Report

acronyms.def: Verra-approved methodology for carbon credits from tidal wetland and seagrass restoration
Carbon stored in soil organic matter; dominant carbon pool in blue carbon ecosystems and key credit driver

acronyms.full: Integrated Valuation of Ecosystem Services and Trade-offs
acronyms.def: Natural Capital Project software for mapping and valuing ecosystem services including coastal protection

acronyms.full: International Carbon Reduction and Offset Alliance
acronyms.def: Alliance setting quality standards for voluntary carbon offsets including blue carbon projects

dataLineage.flow: Habitat-specific sequestration rates by region and soil type → carbon stock change calculations
dataLineage.output: Annual carbon credit issuance estimate

dataLineage.source: Natural Capital Project InVEST coastal model
dataLineage.flow: Wave attenuation, storm surge reduction by habitat type → coastal protection valuation
dataLineage.output: Co-benefit revenue / avoided loss

dataLineage.source: Ecosystem Marketplace blue carbon price data
dataLineage.flow: Historical transaction prices for blue carbon credits → price deck assumptions
dataLineage.output: Project revenue sensitivity analysis

/marine-blue-carbon-finance.valueSummary: Delivers rigorous blue carbon project finance modelling integrating IPCC-standard sequestration accounting, coastal protection co-benefit valuation, and VM0033 credit issuance scheduling.

/municipal-green-bond-analytics.title: Municipal Green Bond Analytics
/municipal-green-bond-analytics.description: Municipal green bond analytics covering use-of-proceeds allocation (transport 35%, energy 25%, water 20%), ICMA GBP compliance, impact reporting KPIs, yield spread versus vanilla munis, and credit enhancement structures.

calculationEngine.formula: Greenium = Yield(Vanilla Muni) - Yield(Green Muni) in bps; Impact KPI Score = Σ(Category Weight × KPI Achievement Rate)
standards: MSRB Rule G-17 and Muni Disclosure Standards
Climate Bonds Initiative Municipal Bonds Guidance
calculationEngine.brief: Measures green bond quality through ICMA GBP four-component compliance and greenium analysis relative to issuer vanilla curve

dataPoints.name: Greenium vs Vanilla Muni
dataPoints.formula: YTM(green) - YTM(vanilla) by matched maturity/rating issuer
dataPoints.interpretation: Negative greenium (green trades tighter) observed for high-quality issuers; averages -3 to -8 bps in established markets

dataPoints.name: Use of Proceeds Alignment
dataPoints.interpretation: ICMA GBP requires all proceeds allocated to eligible categories; 92%+ typical; residual in liquidity management

dataPoints.name: Impact KPI Coverage
dataPoints.formula: KPIs reported / KPIs committed in framework × 100
dataPoints.interpretation: High KPI coverage strengthens investor confidence and secondary market liquidity; below 70% risks reputational downgrade

userInteraction: Classify use-of-proceeds across ICMA GBP eligible categories and quantify per-category allocation
Calculate greenium by constructing issuer vanilla yield curve and spread to green bond yield
Score ICMA GBP four-component compliance (proceeds, project evaluation, management, reporting)
Model credit enhancement structures (bond insurance, reserve fund, intercept mechanism) and rating uplift
references: ICMA (2021) Green Bond Principles — Updated June 2021
Climate Bonds Initiative (2023) Municipal Bonds Briefing and Market Intelligence
MSRB (2023) Muni Green Bond Market Report
Moody's (2023) Municipal Green Bond Ratings Methodology

acronyms.def: ICMA voluntary process guidelines for issuing green bonds, covering four core components
US self-regulatory organisation overseeing the municipal securities market
Independent review of green bond framework confirming alignment with GBP or CBI standards
Municipal bond pricing metric accounting for all interest and discount/premium costs over bond life

dataLineage.flow: Real-time and historical muni yields by issuer, maturity, rating → greenium calculation
dataLineage.output: Green vs vanilla pricing differential

dataLineage.flow: Use-of-proceeds allocation and KPI achievement data → compliance scoring
dataLineage.output: ICMA GBP compliance score

dataLineage.source: Climate Bonds Initiative certified issuance data
dataLineage.flow: CBI-certified muni bonds with verified impact metrics → benchmark comparison
dataLineage.output: Peer issuance and impact benchmarking

/municipal-green-bond-analytics.valueSummary: Provides comprehensive municipal green bond analytics integrating ICMA GBP compliance scoring, greenium measurement, and impact KPI tracking to support issuance and investor decisions.

/climate-revenue-bond-modeler.title: Climate Revenue Bond Modeller
/climate-revenue-bond-modeler.description: Climate revenue bond modelling with dedicated revenue stream analysis (toll, utility, tax increment), debt service coverage calculation, climate risk to revenue base assessment, and green certification premium. Covers MSRB and CDFA frameworks.

calculationEngine.methodology: Debt Service Coverage & Climate Risk Adjustment
calculationEngine.formula: DSCR = Net Revenue / Annual Debt Service; Climate-Adjusted Revenue = Base Revenue × (1 - Physical Risk Discount) × (1 + Green Premium)
calculationEngine.brief: Forward-looking revenue bond model incorporating climate physical risk discounts to revenue streams and green certification premium

dataPoints.name: Debt Service Coverage Ratio
dataPoints.formula: Net pledged revenue / annual debt service (principal + interest)
dataPoints.interpretation: Investment grade threshold typically 1.25x; strong revenue bonds 1.5x+; climate risk stress requires 1.35x+ floor

dataPoints.name: Climate Risk Revenue Discount
dataPoints.formula: Physical risk scenario reduction to pledged revenue base over bond term
dataPoints.interpretation: Flood, drought, or extreme heat can reduce toll, utility, or TIF revenue; quantified using RCP 4.5/8.5 scenarios

dataPoints.name: Green Certification Premium
dataPoints.formula: Yield differential for CBI-certified vs uncertified revenue bond
dataPoints.interpretation: Smaller than GO greenium due to structure complexity; emerging as infrastructure green finance matures

userInteraction: Model dedicated revenue stream (toll, utility rate, tax increment, special assessment) under base and stress scenarios
Calculate DSCR under current, climate-stressed, and rate-adjustment scenarios
Apply physical climate risk discounts to revenue projections using scenario-specific impact factors
Assess green/climate certification eligibility and compute pricing premium benefit vs SPO cost
references: MSRB (2023) Revenue Bond Disclosure and Market Practice
S&P (2023) Environmental Credit Factors in US Municipal Finance

acronyms.def: Revenue bond credit metric: net pledged revenue divided by annual debt service
Municipal finance mechanism using future tax revenue growth in a district to fund current infrastructure
National association supporting public finance practitioners in climate and sustainable infrastructure finance
Municipal bond backed by full faith and credit taxing power, as distinct from revenue bonds backed by specific revenue streams

dataLineage.source: Municipal financial statements and rate studies
dataLineage.flow: Historical pledged revenue, DSCR, rate covenant compliance → base case model
dataLineage.output: Revenue bond credit metrics

dataLineage.source: Physical climate risk models (RMS, AIR, Moody's)
dataLineage.flow: Asset-level physical risk exposure by peril and scenario → revenue impact factors
dataLineage.output: Climate-adjusted DSCR

dataLineage.source: Bloomberg BVAL and EMMA disclosure data
dataLineage.flow: Market pricing and disclosure for comparable revenue bonds → greenium benchmarking
dataLineage.output: Pricing premium analysis

/climate-revenue-bond-modeler.valueSummary: Enables rigorous climate revenue bond modelling integrating DSCR analysis, physical risk revenue discounting, and green certification premium to support issuance structuring and investor credit assessment.

/mdb-sub-sovereign-lending.title: MDB/DFI Sub-Sovereign Climate Lending Analytics
/mdb-sub-sovereign-lending.description: MDB/DFI sub-sovereign climate lending analytics covering sovereign guarantee structures, on-lending terms, fiscal space analysis, and climate co-financing ratios for World Bank, ADB, and AfDB operations.

calculationEngine.formula: Fiscal Space = Revenue × Coverage Ratio - Existing Debt Service; Co-Financing Ratio = Private Finance Mobilised / MDB Climate Finance Committed
calculationEngine.brief: Assesses sub-sovereign borrowing capacity, sovereign guarantee terms, and private capital mobilisation ratio for MDB climate lending programmes

dataPoints.name: Climate Co-Financing Ratio
dataPoints.formula: Private and other public climate finance mobilised / MDB committed climate finance
dataPoints.interpretation: World Bank Group target 3x mobilisation; higher ratios in middle-income countries (infrastructure) vs LDCs (grants needed)

dataPoints.name: Sub-Sovereign Fiscal Space
dataPoints.formula: Net present value of revenue capacity above existing debt service obligations
dataPoints.interpretation: Determines maximum additional borrowing capacity for climate investment without crowding out essential services

dataPoints.name: On-Lending Spread
dataPoints.formula: Interest rate on-lent to sub-sovereign minus MDB sovereign window funding cost
dataPoints.interpretation: Covers credit enhancement, administrative cost, and currency risk; concessional windows offer 50-100 bps

userInteraction: Map sub-sovereign entity (city, state, utility, SOE) against MDB eligibility criteria and sovereign guarantee requirements
Analyse fiscal space using IMF framework — revenue base, existing debt service, and debt sustainability thresholds
Model on-lending structure: sovereign window rate, guarantee fee, sub-sovereign on-lending spread
Calculate climate co-financing ratio and OECD DAC attribution across contributing financiers
references: World Bank (2023) Sub-Sovereign and Structured Finance Primer
ADB (2023) Climate Finance Tracking and Reporting Methodology
AfDB (2022) Climate Change Action Plan and Co-Financing Guidelines
OECD DAC (2023) Climate-Related Official Development Finance

acronyms.def: Government-controlled entity; common sub-sovereign borrower for infrastructure climate finance
World Bank concessional window for least-developed countries; lower lending rates than IBRD

acronyms.full: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
acronyms.def: World Bank standard lending window for middle-income countries
UN designation for 46 poorest countries; eligible for maximum concessionality in MDB climate finance

dataLineage.source: IMF World Economic Outlook and Article IV reports
dataLineage.flow: GDP, revenue, debt stock by country → fiscal space calculation
dataLineage.output: Sub-sovereign borrowing capacity

dataLineage.source: MDB project databases (World Bank ARAP, ADB PPIS)
dataLineage.flow: Historical on-lending terms, co-financing ratios by sector → benchmark analysis
dataLineage.output: Structuring comparables

dataLineage.flow: Bilateral and multilateral climate finance flows by country → co-financing ratio validation
dataLineage.output: Climate finance attribution

/mdb-sub-sovereign-lending.valueSummary: Provides analytical framework for MDB sub-sovereign climate lending decisions, integrating fiscal space analysis, co-financing ratio benchmarking, and sovereign guarantee structuring.

/municipal-climate-resilience-hub.title: Municipal Climate Resilience Investment Hub
/municipal-climate-resilience-hub.description: Municipal climate resilience investment planning analytics. Assesses physical risk to municipal assets (roads, buildings, utilities), prioritises adaptation capex, models resilience ROI, and values green infrastructure benefit-cost ratios.

calculationEngine.formula: Resilience ROI = (Avoided Damage PV + Co-benefits PV - Adaptation CAPEX PV) / Adaptation CAPEX PV; BCR = Total Benefits / Total Costs
standards: World Bank CURB Tool for Urban Resilience
IPCC AR6 WG2 Chapter 17 — Adaptation Options
calculationEngine.brief: Multi-hazard benefit-cost analysis for municipal adaptation investments, combining avoided physical damage, insurance savings, and ecosystem co-benefits

dataPoints.name: Average Asset Physical Risk Score
dataPoints.formula: Composite score across flood, heat, drought, and sea-level rise hazards
dataPoints.interpretation: Scores above 70 require near-term adaptation investment; 50-70 monitor and plan; roadways and utilities typically highest risk

dataPoints.name: Adaptation Investment BCR
dataPoints.formula: Total adaptation benefits PV / adaptation capex PV
dataPoints.interpretation: FEMA minimum BCR threshold for grant eligibility is 1.0; resilient infrastructure typically 3-6x; nature-based solutions often higher

dataPoints.name: Green Infrastructure Cost Savings
dataPoints.formula: Green infrastructure cost vs grey infrastructure equivalent at same performance standard
dataPoints.interpretation: Green stormwater infrastructure 20-40% cheaper than grey; additional co-benefits in heat reduction, air quality, habitat

userInteraction: Inventory municipal assets by category and map to multi-hazard physical risk exposure using RCP 4.5 and 8.5 scenarios
Prioritise adaptation investments using risk score, asset criticality, and benefit-cost ratio
Model resilience ROI for top-priority interventions using FEMA BCA methodology
Identify green infrastructure opportunities and co-benefit valuation for grant applications (FEMA BRIC, CDBG-DR)
references: FEMA (2023) Benefit-Cost Analysis Reference Guide v6.0
World Bank (2023) CURB Tool for Urban Climate Resilience
IPCC AR6 WG2 (2022) Chapter 17 — Decision-Making Options for Managing Risk
C40 Cities (2023) Climate Action Planning — Adaptation Investment Framework

acronyms.def: Total discounted benefits divided by total discounted costs; primary metric for public infrastructure investment decisions
FEMA grant programme funding pre-disaster mitigation; minimum BCR 1.0 required for eligibility
Nature-based approaches to stormwater, flood, and heat management using vegetation and natural systems
World Bank model quantifying economic benefits of climate adaptation investments in cities

dataLineage.flow: Asset locations, conditions, replacement values → physical risk exposure mapping
dataLineage.output: Asset-level risk scores

dataLineage.source: RMS / AIR municipal physical risk models
dataLineage.flow: Hazard intensity by location and RCP scenario → damage functions and loss estimates
dataLineage.output: Avoided damage benefit calculation

dataLineage.source: FEMA BCA Toolkit / natural hazard loss data
dataLineage.flow: Damage functions, unit costs, co-benefit valuation → BCR calculation
dataLineage.output: Grant application support and investment prioritisation

/municipal-climate-resilience-hub.valueSummary: Provides rigorous municipal climate resilience investment analytics integrating multi-hazard risk scoring, FEMA-methodology BCR calculation, and green infrastructure co-benefit valuation.

/cpace-climate-finance.title: C-PACE Climate Finance Analytics
/cpace-climate-finance.description: C-PACE (Commercial Property Assessed Clean Energy) financing analytics covering assessment lien structure, LTV impact, IPMVP energy savings verification, market volume by state, and lender consent requirements.

calculationEngine.methodology: C-PACE Debt Service and Energy Savings Modelling
calculationEngine.formula: Net Cash Flow = Energy Savings - PACE Assessment Payment; Savings-to-Investment Ratio = NPV(Energy Savings) / PACE Loan Amount; LTV Impact = (First Mortgage + PACE) / Property Value
standards: ASTM E2797 Energy Assessment Standard for PACE
calculationEngine.brief: Models C-PACE financing net cash flow by comparing IPMVP-verified energy savings against assessment payment, with LTV and lender consent analysis

dataPoints.name: Savings-to-Investment Ratio
dataPoints.formula: NPV of projected energy savings over PACE term / PACE assessment amount
dataPoints.source: IPMVP Option C measurement and verification
dataPoints.interpretation: Ratio above 1.0 indicates positive net cash flow; lenders typically require 1.2x+ coverage for project approval

dataPoints.name: C-PACE Market Volume (US)
dataPoints.interpretation: Growing 20-30% annually; California, Florida, New York largest markets; 39 states with enabling legislation

dataPoints.name: LTV Combined (with PACE)
dataPoints.formula: (First mortgage + PACE assessment) / appraised property value
dataPoints.interpretation: Most mortgage lenders require combined LTV below 80%; PACE lien seniority creates lender consent requirement in most states

userInteraction: Calculate PACE assessment amount, term, and rate based on eligible improvements (solar, HVAC, EV charging, resilience)
Model IPMVP-verified energy savings using baseline consumption and improvement performance data
Assess lender consent requirement by state and calculate combined LTV impact
Generate savings-to-investment ratio, simple payback, and net present value summary
references: PACENation (2023) C-PACE Market Intelligence Annual Report
IPMVP (2023) Core Concepts for Determining Energy and Water Savings
ASTM (2023) E2797 Standard Practice for Building Energy Performance Assessment
Fannie Mae / Freddie Mac PACE Lender Consent Guidelines 2022

acronyms.def: Long-term financing for commercial building energy efficiency and resilience improvements repaid through property tax assessment

acronyms.full: International Performance Measurement and Verification Protocol
acronyms.def: Global standard for quantifying energy and water savings from efficiency improvements
C-PACE underwriting metric: NPV of energy savings / PACE assessment amount; threshold typically 1.2x
Residential PACE programme; C-PACE is the commercial equivalent with larger deal sizes

dataLineage.flow: Pre-retrofit energy consumption and costs → IPMVP baseline establishment
dataLineage.output: Energy savings projection

dataLineage.flow: State-specific PACE programme rates, terms, eligible improvements → product structuring
dataLineage.output: Assessment payment schedule

dataLineage.flow: As-is and as-improved property values → LTV calculation and lender consent analysis
dataLineage.output: Combined LTV and consent requirement

/cpace-climate-finance.valueSummary: Delivers end-to-end C-PACE financing analytics integrating IPMVP savings verification, LTV impact modelling, and state-specific programme comparison to support origination and underwriting decisions.

/cdfi-climate-finance.title: CDFI Climate Finance Analytics
/cdfi-climate-finance.description: CDFI climate finance analytics covering equity-focused climate finance targeting LMI communities, green home improvement lending, clean energy access metrics, and Treasury CDFI Fund certification requirements.

calculationEngine.formula: LMI Targeting Rate = LMI-targeted climate loans / total climate portfolio × 100; Climate Equity Score = 0.4×LMI Rate + 0.3×Environmental Justice + 0.3×Energy Burden Reduction
calculationEngine.brief: Composite scoring of climate finance equity impact combining LMI community targeting, environmental justice co-benefits, and energy burden reduction for low-income households

dataPoints.name: LMI Climate Finance Targeting Rate
dataPoints.formula: Climate loans to LMI census tracts / total climate portfolio × 100
dataPoints.source: CDFI annual report to Treasury CDFI Fund
dataPoints.interpretation: Treasury CDFI certification requires 60%+ activity in LMI; leading climate CDFIs achieve 80-95%

dataPoints.name: Energy Burden Reduction
dataPoints.formula: Pre-investment energy cost as % income minus post-investment energy cost % income
dataPoints.interpretation: Average LMI energy burden 8-10% vs 3% national avg; clean energy investment reduces by 2-5 percentage points

dataPoints.name: Treasury CDFI Award per $
dataPoints.formula: Treasury CDFI Fund award / total financing catalysed
dataPoints.interpretation: CDFIs leverage $8-12 of private capital per $1 CDFI Fund award; climate focus may achieve higher leverage via IRA

userInteraction: Map CDFI lending portfolio to LMI census tracts using FFIEC and CDFI Fund geographies
Calculate LMI targeting rate and environmental justice screening using EPA EJSCREEN scores
Model energy burden reduction impact from green home improvement and clean energy loan products
Assess Treasury CDFI Fund certification compliance and IRA new market tax credit eligibility
references: Treasury CDFI Fund (2024) CDFI Certification Application and Standards
EPA (2023) EJSCREEN Environmental Justice Screening and Mapping Tool
DOE (2022) Justice40 Initiative — Clean Energy Equity Framework
ACEEE (2023) The High Cost of Energy in Rural America — Low-Income Energy Burden Report

acronyms.def: Treasury-certified lender serving LMI communities; eligible for CDFI Fund awards and CRA credit
Census tract or individual income below 80% of area median income; CDFI primary targeting requirement
Federal tax credit for investments in low-income communities; allocated by Treasury CDFI Fund to CDFIs
Biden Administration commitment to direct 40% of clean energy benefits to disadvantaged communities

dataLineage.source: FFIEC census tract data and CDFI Fund mapping
dataLineage.flow: LMI census tract designations → portfolio targeting rate calculation
dataLineage.output: LMI compliance and CDFI certification support

dataLineage.source: EPA EJSCREEN scores by census tract
dataLineage.flow: Environmental justice indicators (pollution burden, demographics) → EJ screening for loan targeting
dataLineage.output: Environmental justice co-benefit measurement

dataLineage.flow: Pre-investment energy burden by geography and housing type → impact measurement baseline
dataLineage.output: Energy burden reduction outcome tracking

/coastal-resilience-finance.title: Coastal Resilience Project Finance
/coastal-resilience-finance.description: Coastal resilience project finance analytics covering seawall, living shoreline, and mangrove buffer investments. Models benefit-cost ratios from avoided storm damage, insurance premium reductions, ecosystem service valuation, and FEMA BRIC grant eligibility.

calculationEngine.formula: BCR = (Avoided Storm Damage + Insurance Reduction + Ecosystem Services) / (CAPEX + OPEX); Annual Expected Loss Reduction = AAL(without) - AAL(with)
standards: Nature-based Solutions for Coastal Resilience — Swiss Re Institute 2023
calculationEngine.brief: Multi-benefit coastal resilience BCR calculation combining engineering-based flood damage reduction with ecosystem service co-benefits and insurance market impacts

dataPoints.name: Coastal Resilience BCR
dataPoints.formula: (Avoided damage PV + insurance reduction + ecosystem services) / CAPEX
dataPoints.source: FEMA BCA and Swiss Re model
dataPoints.interpretation: Coastal resilience BCR typically 3-8x; living shorelines often higher than grey infrastructure due to co-benefits; FEMA threshold 1.0x

dataPoints.name: Annual Expected Loss Reduction
dataPoints.formula: AAL without resilience project - AAL with project (expected annual loss from storm events)
dataPoints.interpretation: Primary benefit driver; depends on coastal exposure value, hazard intensity, and project attenuation performance

dataPoints.name: Mangrove Storm Attenuation
dataPoints.formula: Wave height reduction per 100m mangrove belt width
dataPoints.source: IUCN / Nature Conservancy coastal protection research
dataPoints.interpretation: Mangroves reduce wave height 50-70% over 500m; direct analogue to 30-50cm seawall at fraction of cost

userInteraction: Map coastal assets at risk and establish without-project annual expected loss baseline using storm surge models
Model wave attenuation and flood reduction performance for each intervention type (seawall, living shoreline, mangrove)
Calculate multi-benefit BCR including avoided damage, insurance premium reduction, and ecosystem service values
Assess FEMA BRIC grant eligibility and identify co-financing from insurance sector resilience programmes
references: FEMA (2023) Benefit-Cost Analysis Reference Guide v6.0 — Coastal Flood Section
Swiss Re Institute (2023) Nature-based Solutions for Coastal Resilience
Nature Conservancy (2020) Valuing the Coastal Protection Services of Mangroves

acronyms.def: Probabilistic measure of expected annual damage from natural hazards across all return periods
FEMA pre-disaster mitigation grant programme; 6% of total FEMA disaster mitigation budget
Using natural and semi-natural systems to address coastal flooding and erosion challenges
Systematic comparison of quantified project costs and benefits to support investment decisions

dataLineage.source: RMS / AIR North Atlantic storm surge models
dataLineage.flow: Probabilistic hazard intensity and damage functions by location → AAL baseline and reduction
dataLineage.output: Avoided damage benefit calculation

dataLineage.source: NOAA coastal bathymetry and asset exposure data
dataLineage.flow: Coastal topography and asset values → flood inundation modelling
dataLineage.output: Physical risk quantification

dataLineage.source: Swiss Re / Munich Re coastal resilience insurance data

/ocean-carbon-credit-market.title: Ocean Carbon Credit Market Analytics
/ocean-carbon-credit-market.description: Ocean-based carbon credit market analytics covering kelp/macroalgae, ocean alkalinity enhancement, seagrass, and shellfish aquaculture credits. Compares MRV methodologies, permanence challenges, and emerging price benchmarks.

calculationEngine.methodology: Ocean CDR Credit Pricing & MRV Maturity
calculationEngine.formula: Ocean CDR Price = f(Permanence Tier, MRV Maturity, Co-benefit Score); Permanence-Adjusted Value = Spot Price × Permanence Factor × (1 - Reversal Risk)
standards: High Level Panel for a Sustainable Ocean Economy — Ocean Carbon Guide
Verra VCS emerging ocean methodology pipeline
calculationEngine.brief: Market analytics integrating MRV maturity scoring, permanence risk assessment, and emerging price benchmarks across ocean CDR pathways

dataPoints.name: MRV Maturity Score

dataPoints.interpretation: Most ocean CDR pathways at pilot stage; geological marine CCS highest maturity; macroalgae/OAE pre-commercial

dataPoints.name: Ocean CDR Market Volume

dataPoints.interpretation: Nascent but rapidly growing; Stripe/Shopify advance purchases driving methodology development

Score MRV maturity for each pathway: methodology approval status, monitoring approach, verification body availability
Assess permanence risk and assign permanence tier (geological, century-scale, decadal)
Benchmark prices against advance purchase data and model portfolio risk-return under different pathway mixes

NOAA (2023) Ocean Acidification and Carbon Cycle Research — CDR Implications
Carbon180 (2023) Ocean CDR State of the Field
Frontier Climate (2023) Ocean CDR Procurement and MRV Standards

Carbon pathway in ocean CDR via marine photosynthesis; uncertain permanence as DOC can be remineralised
NASEM category including marine biomass CDR pathways with geological or long-lived storage
Shellfish calcification pathway; potential CDR credit but DNSH concerns regarding CO2 release on dissolution

dataLineage.output: Ocean CDR price discovery

dataLineage.flow: Methodology development status for ocean pathways → MRV maturity scoring

/blue-bond-analytics.title: Blue Bond Market Analytics
/blue-bond-analytics.description: Blue bond market analytics covering sovereign and corporate issuance, sustainable ocean economy use-of-proceeds (fisheries, maritime transport, coastal resilience), ICMA Blue Bond Principles, and World Bank/ADB blue bond structures.

calculationEngine.formula: Blue Bond Alignment Score = Σ(Category Weight × Category Compliance); Blue Premium = Yield(conventional) - Yield(blue) in bps
standards: Sustainable Ocean Economy Finance Principles (UNEP FI)
calculationEngine.brief: Measures blue bond quality via use-of-proceeds alignment scoring and pricing premium analysis relative to vanilla issuer comparables

dataPoints.name: Global Blue Bond Issuance
dataPoints.source: Climate Bonds Initiative Blue Bond Database
dataPoints.interpretation: Fastest growing sustainable bond segment; Seychelles (2018) sovereign first; ADB, World Bank leading MDB issuers

dataPoints.name: Blue Bond Alignment Score

dataPoints.interpretation: Scores above 80 qualify for CBI Climate Bonds certification; 70-80 standard green-equivalent; below 70 risks greenwashing

dataPoints.name: Blue Greenium vs Vanilla
dataPoints.formula: Yield differential for blue vs conventional bond by matched issuer/maturity
dataPoints.interpretation: Emerging premium as investor demand grows; sovereign blue bonds show larger premium than corporate

userInteraction: Map use-of-proceeds across ICMA Blue Bond Principles categories (fisheries, aquaculture, shipping, coastal, ocean energy, waste)

Calculate pricing premium by constructing issuer vanilla yield curve and comparing to blue bond yield

World Bank (2018) Blue Bond Framework and Impact Report
UNEP FI (2022) Sustainable Ocean Economy Finance Principles
Climate Bonds Initiative (2023) Marine and Fisheries Criteria

Economic activities deriving value from oceans while preserving ocean health and ecosystem services
Fisheries sustainability certification; MSC-certified fisheries typically blue bond eligible
UN agency regulating international shipping; shipping decarbonisation is key blue bond use-of-proceeds category

dataLineage.flow: Stock status, fishing pressure by species → sustainable fisheries eligibility assessment

/aquaculture-climate-finance.title: Aquaculture Climate Finance Analytics
/aquaculture-climate-finance.description: Aquaculture climate finance analytics covering sustainable aquaculture investment, ASC/BAP certification premiums, climate risk to production from sea temperature and ocean acidification, blue bond eligible capex, and insurance products.

calculationEngine.formula: Climate-Adjusted IRR = Base IRR - Physical Risk Discount + Certification Premium; Production Risk = f(SST Anomaly, pH Change, Extreme Event Frequency)
standards: Swiss Re Climate Risk in Aquaculture 2023
calculationEngine.brief: IRR model adjusting for climate physical risks to aquaculture production combined with certification premium uplift and insurance product availability

dataPoints.name: ASC Certification Premium
dataPoints.formula: Price premium for ASC-certified product vs standard at point of sale
dataPoints.interpretation: Premium varies by species and market; salmon 10-18%; shrimp 8-15%; critical for accessing EU and US premium retail

dataPoints.name: Sea Surface Temperature Risk
dataPoints.formula: Production volume reduction under RCP 4.5 SST projections for salmon farming sites
dataPoints.interpretation: Norwegian salmon sites face reduced optimal temperature window; tropical shrimp farms face increased disease pressure

dataPoints.name: Blue Bond Eligible CapEx Share
dataPoints.formula: Capex on sustainable feed systems, recirculating aquaculture, habitat restoration / total capex
dataPoints.interpretation: RAS (recirculating aquaculture systems) and offshore cage technology qualify; feed mill upgrade eligible if using non-FMFO feeds

userInteraction: Map aquaculture production sites against climate risk variables: SST projections, ocean acidification pH change, extreme storm frequency
Calculate production risk discount by species and site using climate-impact dose-response functions
Assess ASC/BAP certification status and premium uplift on revenue projections
Classify capex as blue bond eligible and model financing structure combining blue bonds, insurance, and concessional finance
references: ASC (2023) Aquaculture Stewardship Council Standard v3.0
FAO (2022) The State of World Fisheries and Aquaculture
IPCC SROCC (2019) Special Report on Ocean and Cryosphere — Chapter 5 Changing Ocean
Swiss Re (2023) Aquaculture Climate Risk Insurance Solutions

acronyms.def: Global certification body for responsibly farmed seafood; premium access to EU/US retail markets
Global Aquaculture Alliance certification; four-star BAP covers farm, hatchery, feed mill, and processing
Land-based indoor aquaculture using water recycling; higher CAPEX but lower climate risk and site constraints
Feed ingredient from wild-caught forage fish; reduction critical for sustainable aquaculture certification

dataLineage.source: CMIP6 sea surface temperature projections (IPCC AR6)
dataLineage.flow: SST anomalies by site and RCP scenario → production risk model inputs
dataLineage.output: Climate-adjusted production volume projections

/shipping-decarbonization-finance.title: Shipping Decarbonisation Finance Analytics
/shipping-decarbonization-finance.description: Shipping decarbonisation finance analytics covering Poseidon Principles alignment, CII rating, IMO GHG Strategy carbon levy, alternative fuel investment (methanol, ammonia, LNG), and OPEX/CAPEX trade-off modelling.

calculationEngine.formula: CII Score = AER or cgDIST / Reference Line; Decarbonisation CAPEX NPV = ΔFuel Cost × OPEX Savings - Retrofit CAPEX + Carbon Levy Avoidance
standards: Poseidon Principles v3.0 — Ship Finance Alignment
SEA-LNG / Ammonia Energy Association fuel cost benchmarks
calculationEngine.brief: Investment model for shipping decarbonisation comparing fuel switching CAPEX against fuel cost and carbon levy savings, benchmarked to Poseidon Principles CII alignment

dataPoints.name: Poseidon Principles Alignment Score
dataPoints.formula: Vessel AER vs Poseidon Principles climate trajectory for vessel type (% above/below)
dataPoints.interpretation: Negative = above trajectory (worse); aligned banks require fleets to converge to 0% by 2050; ESRS disclosure required

dataPoints.name: CII Rating
dataPoints.formula: Carbon Intensity Indicator annual rating (A-E) vs IMO reference line
dataPoints.interpretation: D or E rating for 3 consecutive years triggers corrective action plan; lenders increasingly requiring B or better

dataPoints.name: Alternative Fuel CapEx Premium
dataPoints.formula: Incremental cost of methanol-ready dual-fuel newbuild vs conventional
dataPoints.source: Clarkson Research / DNV fuel-ready ship cost data
dataPoints.interpretation: Ammonia $25-35M premium; LNG $8-15M; methanol $15-25M; fuel availability and price spread determine payback period

userInteraction: Calculate fleet-wide CII ratings and Poseidon Principles alignment using AER and cgDIST methodology
Model carbon levy exposure under IMO GHG Strategy 2023 flat levy and basket of measures scenarios
Evaluate alternative fuel investment NPV: CAPEX premium vs fuel cost savings and levy avoidance over 20yr ship life
Generate lender-ready Poseidon Principles disclosure and fleet transition roadmap to Paris-aligned trajectory
references: IMO (2023) Revised GHG Strategy MEPC 80 — 2030/2050 Targets and Carbon Levy
Poseidon Principles (2023) Framework v3.0 — Financial Sector Climate Alignment
DNV (2023) Maritime Forecast to 2050 — Fuel and Technology Pathways
UMAS / UCL (2023) IMO Carbon Levy Revenue and Shipping Transition Finance

acronyms.def: IMO annual operational efficiency rating for ships in service; grades A (best) to E (worst) vs reference line
CII metric: CO2 emitted per tonne-nautical mile; applicable to most vessel types
IMO technical efficiency standard for existing ships; one-time compliance requirement effective January 2023
IMO GHG Strategy targets 20-30% emissions reduction by 2030 and net-zero GHG by 2050

dataLineage.source: IMO GISIS ship registry and CII data
dataLineage.flow: Vessel DWT, type, fuel consumption, voyage data → CII calculation
dataLineage.output: Fleet alignment and CII ratings

dataLineage.flow: Trajectory benchmarks by vessel type and year → alignment gap calculation
dataLineage.output: Lender disclosure and portfolio alignment score

dataLineage.flow: Alternative fuel cost projections (methanol, ammonia, green hydrogen) → NPV sensitivity
dataLineage.output: Fuel switching investment case

/climate-wacc-engine.title: Climate-Adjusted WACC Engine
/climate-wacc-engine.description: Climate-adjusted WACC engine for corporate valuation. Estimates carbon beta, decomposes climate risk premium into physical and transition components, applies sector-specific WACC uplift, and enables IFRS S2 cost of capital disclosure.

calculationEngine.formula: Climate WACC = WACC_base + Carbon Beta × (Rm - Rf) + Physical Risk Premium + Transition Risk Premium; Carbon Beta = Cov(Return, Carbon Price) / Var(Return)
standards: ECB Guide on Climate and Environmental Risks 2020
Carney (2015) Breaking the Tragedy of the Horizon — Carbon Beta Framework
calculationEngine.brief: WACC adjustment framework decomposing climate risk into systematic carbon beta and idiosyncratic physical/transition premiums by sector

dataPoints.name: Carbon Beta
dataPoints.formula: Regression of stock return vs EUA carbon price return over 3yr rolling window
dataPoints.source: Bloomberg EUA price data + company equity returns
dataPoints.interpretation: Beta>0.5 indicates high carbon price sensitivity (utilities, cement); <0.2 low sensitivity (tech, healthcare); negative possible for clean energy

dataPoints.name: Climate WACC Uplift
dataPoints.formula: Total climate risk premium added to conventional WACC
dataPoints.interpretation: Uplift ranges 30-200 bps by sector; oil & gas 120-180 bps; utilities 60-90 bps; real estate coastal 80-150 bps

dataPoints.name: Transition Risk Premium
dataPoints.formula: Additional cost of capital from stranded asset risk and regulatory transition costs
dataPoints.source: NGFS transition scenario implied equity risk premia
dataPoints.interpretation: Dominant component for high-emitters; decomposed from scenario analysis of stranded asset NPV impact on enterprise value

userInteraction: Estimate carbon beta using 3-year rolling regression of equity returns against EUA carbon price changes
Decompose climate WACC uplift into physical risk premium (asset impairment) and transition risk premium (stranded assets, carbon cost)
Apply sector-specific calibration using ECB/NGFS scenario analysis
Generate IFRS S2-compliant cost of capital disclosure with sensitivity analysis
references: IFRS S2 (2023) Climate-related Disclosures — Cost of Capital Disclosure Requirements
ECB (2020) Guide on Climate and Environmental Risks — WACC Section
NGFS (2023) Climate Scenarios for Central Banks and Supervisors — Macro-Financial Implications
Dietz et al. (2023) Carbon Beta and the Cost of Capital — Journal of Finance

acronyms.def: Blended cost of equity and debt financing; key discount rate in DCF valuation
EU ETS carbon credit; EUA price used as carbon price proxy for carbon beta estimation
Standard equity cost of capital model extended to include climate risk premium in climate-adjusted WACC

acronyms.full: Network for Greening the Financial System
acronyms.def: Central bank network providing climate scenarios used for WACC calibration and stress testing

dataLineage.source: Bloomberg equity returns and EUA price time series
dataLineage.flow: Daily returns for carbon beta regression → systematic climate risk component
dataLineage.output: Carbon beta by company

dataLineage.source: NGFS climate scenarios (orderly, disorderly, hot house)
dataLineage.flow: GDP impact, carbon price, stranded asset trajectories → transition risk premium calibration
dataLineage.output: Climate WACC uplift by scenario

dataLineage.source: IPCC physical risk data and asset exposure
dataLineage.flow: Physical hazard by location and RCP scenario → physical risk premium by asset geography
dataLineage.output: Total climate WACC decomposition

/climate-wacc-engine.valueSummary: Provides sector-specific climate-adjusted WACC calculation decomposing carbon beta, physical risk, and transition risk premiums, enabling IFRS S2-compliant cost of capital disclosures.

/green-debt-structuring.title: Green Debt Structuring Analytics
/green-debt-structuring.description: Green debt structuring analytics for green bonds, green loans, and sustainability-linked loans. Models coupon step-up/down mechanics, second-party opinion costs, post-issuance reporting burden, and greenium analysis. Covers ICMA GBP and GLP frameworks.

calculationEngine.formula: Greenium = Yield(conventional) - Yield(green) in bps; Net Benefit = Greenium Savings - SPO Cost - Reporting Cost over bond life
standards: Climate Bonds Initiative Certification Standard v4.0
calculationEngine.brief: Pricing model comparing greenium benefit against incremental green debt costs (SPO, ongoing reporting) to determine net financial attractiveness

dataPoints.name: Greenium vs Conventional
dataPoints.formula: Yield differential for green vs conventional bond by matched issuer/maturity/rating
dataPoints.interpretation: Average greenium -5 to -8 bps in EUR investment grade; USD market smaller (-2 to -4 bps); high-yield minimal

dataPoints.name: Net Green Bond Benefit
dataPoints.formula: Coupon saving from greenium - SPO cost - annual reporting cost × 5
dataPoints.source: ICMA and CBI issuance cost survey
dataPoints.interpretation: SPO cost $50-150k; annual reporting $30-80k; greenium saving typically covers costs for bonds >€200M

dataPoints.name: SLL Margin Ratchet
dataPoints.formula: Coupon adjustment range for meeting/missing sustainability performance targets
dataPoints.interpretation: Market convention ±5-10 bps; symmetric ratchet required by ICMA to avoid tokenism; tied to externally verified KPIs

userInteraction: Model green bond structure: use-of-proceeds allocation, ICMA GBP four-component compliance, SPO provider selection
Calculate greenium benefit using comparable conventional bond yield and current demand technicals
Assess net financial benefit: greenium savings minus SPO, verification, and annual reporting costs
Structure SLL margin ratchet: KPI selection, SPT calibration, verification agent, step-up/step-down symmetry
references: ICMA (2021) Green Bond Principles — Voluntary Process Guidelines
Climate Bonds Initiative (2023) CBI Certification Standard v4.0
ICMA (2023) Green, Social, Sustainability Bond Market Survey

acronyms.def: ICMA four-component framework (proceeds, evaluation, management, reporting) for green bond issuance
LMA/APLMA/LSTA equivalent to GBP for private green loan markets
Loan with margin ratchet tied to borrower achieving pre-agreed sustainability performance targets
Independent external review confirming green bond/loan framework alignment with market standards

dataLineage.source: Bloomberg BVAL green bond pricing data
dataLineage.flow: Real-time and historical green vs conventional yield spreads → greenium calculation
dataLineage.output: Net financial benefit analysis

dataLineage.source: ICMA/CBI SPO and reporting cost surveys
dataLineage.flow: SPO provider fees and annual reporting cost benchmarks → total cost of green issuance
dataLineage.output: Break-even analysis by deal size

dataLineage.flow: Margin ratchet size, KPI types, verification agent market practice → SLL structure benchmarking
dataLineage.output: SLL term sheet optimisation

/green-debt-structuring.valueSummary: Provides rigorous green debt structuring analytics quantifying greenium benefit against SPO and reporting costs, enabling corporate treasurers to optimise green financing instrument selection.

/climate-ma-due-diligence.title: Climate M&A Due Diligence Engine
/climate-ma-due-diligence.description: Climate M&A due diligence engine assessing target company physical and transition risk, stranded asset exposure, carbon liability quantification, climate-adjusted enterprise value, and TCFD-aligned due diligence checklist.

calculationEngine.formula: Climate EV = Reported EV - Carbon Liability NPV - Stranded Asset Writedown - Physical Risk Impairment; Carbon Liability = Scope 1+2+3 × Shadow Carbon Price
standards: ACE (Accounting for Carbon Emissions) Framework — WBCSD 2023
calculationEngine.brief: EV adjustment framework quantifying climate liabilities (carbon cost, stranded assets, physical impairment) to derive climate-adjusted acquisition price

dataPoints.name: Carbon Liability NPV
dataPoints.formula: Σ(Scope 1+2+3 emissions × shadow price trajectory) discounted to PV
dataPoints.interpretation: Social cost of carbon $185/t (IWG 2021 update); internal carbon price trajectory fed through DCF model; dominant for energy assets

dataPoints.name: Stranded Asset Exposure
dataPoints.formula: Assets at risk of early retirement under 1.5°C / 2°C scenario / total PP&E
dataPoints.source: IEA NZE scenario asset stranding analysis
dataPoints.interpretation: Unburnable reserves plus fossil fuel infrastructure; quantified using IEA NZE remaining carbon budget by asset type

dataPoints.name: Physical Risk Impairment
dataPoints.formula: Expected value of physical asset writedowns from climate hazards over holding period
dataPoints.interpretation: Coastal, flood-prone, and water-stressed assets most vulnerable; key for real estate and infrastructure M&A

userInteraction: Complete TCFD-aligned climate due diligence checklist across governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics
Quantify carbon liability using Scope 1+2+3 emissions and shadow carbon price trajectory (NGFS orderly/disorderly)
Identify stranded assets using IEA NZE scenario asset retirement timeline
Calculate climate-adjusted EV and implied climate risk discount to headline enterprise value
references: TCFD (2021) Implementing the Recommendations of the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures
WBCSD (2023) ACE Framework — Accounting for Carbon Emissions in M&A
IEA (2023) World Energy Outlook — Net Zero Emissions Scenario Asset Stranding

acronyms.def: Total firm value (market cap + net debt); climate adjustments create climate-adjusted EV as acquisition price floor
Monetary value of damages from one additional tonne of CO2; US IWG estimate $185/t (2021 update)
Tangible long-lived assets; stranded asset analysis focuses on fossil fuel PP&E at risk of early retirement
WBCSD framework integrating carbon pricing into financial statements and M&A valuation

dataLineage.source: Target company CDP disclosures and ESG reports
dataLineage.flow: Scope 1+2+3 emissions, climate governance, physical risk exposure → due diligence inputs
dataLineage.output: Carbon liability and TCFD checklist

dataLineage.flow: Asset-type retirement timelines by scenario → stranded asset quantification
dataLineage.output: Stranded asset EV adjustment

dataLineage.flow: Asset-level physical hazard exposure → impairment risk quantification
dataLineage.output: Physical risk EV adjustment

/climate-ma-due-diligence.valueSummary: Delivers systematic climate M&A due diligence integrating TCFD checklist, carbon liability quantification, stranded asset identification, and physical risk impairment into a climate-adjusted enterprise value.

/carbon-adjusted-valuation.title: Carbon-Adjusted Corporate Valuation
/carbon-adjusted-valuation.description: Carbon-adjusted corporate valuation covering Scope 1+2+3 emissions monetisation, shadow carbon price sensitivity analysis, carbon liability EV adjustment, and sector peer comparison on carbon-adjusted EV/EBITDA multiples.

calculationEngine.formula: Carbon-Adj EV/EBITDA = (EV + Carbon Liability NPV) / EBITDA; Carbon Liability = Scope 1+2+3 × Shadow Price × (1-PassThrough) × Duration Factor
standards: IIGCC (2023) Net Zero Investment Framework — Carbon-Adjusted Valuation Guidance
calculationEngine.brief: Adjusts reported EV/EBITDA multiples for unpriced carbon liability, enabling like-for-like peer comparison that reflects climate transition exposure

dataPoints.name: Carbon-Adjusted EV/EBITDA
dataPoints.source: Company GHG data + shadow carbon price deck
dataPoints.interpretation: Premium vs unadjusted multiple quantifies hidden carbon value destruction; high-emitter premium often 2-4x turns

dataPoints.name: Scope 3 Carbon Liability Share
dataPoints.formula: Scope 3 carbon liability NPV / total carbon liability NPV
dataPoints.interpretation: Scope 3 dominates for consumer goods, financials, and transportation; often excluded → understatement of true liability

dataPoints.name: Shadow Carbon Price (2030)
dataPoints.formula: Internal carbon price assumption aligned with IEA NZE orderly transition trajectory
dataPoints.interpretation: EU ETS spot ~€60-70; IEA NZE implies $130/t by 2030; high-ambition internal price $150-200/t used by Stern/Wagner

userInteraction: Collect Scope 1, 2, and 3 GHG inventory with quality flags and boundary alignment check
Apply shadow carbon price trajectory (base, high, low) over explicit forecast period and terminal year
Calculate net carbon liability after pass-through to customers and government (carbon tax rebates)
Compute carbon-adjusted EV/EBITDA and benchmark against sector peers on same carbon-adjusted basis
references: GHG Protocol (2023) Corporate Accounting and Reporting Standard — Revised Edition
WBCSD (2023) ACE Framework — Carbon-Adjusted Valuation Methodology
IIGCC (2023) Net Zero Investment Framework 2.0 — Portfolio Analytics
Dietz et al. (2023) Carbon Risk and Corporate Valuation — Nature Climate Change

acronyms.def: Standard valuation multiple; carbon adjustment adds unpriced carbon liability to EV for climate-adjusted comparison
Targets aligned with Paris Agreement; companies with SBTi targets show lower carbon liability uncertainty
CO2, CH4, N2O, F-gases per GHG Protocol; monetised at shadow carbon price for valuation adjustment
GHG Protocol denominator for WACI; distinct from EV excluding cash used in standard multiples

dataLineage.source: Company CDP and GRI GHG inventories
dataLineage.flow: Scope 1+2+3 emissions by category → carbon liability calculation
dataLineage.output: Gross carbon liability

dataLineage.source: IEA NZE / NGFS carbon price trajectories
dataLineage.flow: Forward carbon price path by scenario → shadow price deck
dataLineage.output: Carbon liability NPV sensitivity

dataLineage.flow: Reported multiples → baseline for carbon-adjusted peer comparison
dataLineage.output: Sector carbon-adjusted multiple ranking

/carbon-adjusted-valuation.valueSummary: Enables systematic carbon-adjusted corporate valuation incorporating Scope 1+2+3 monetisation and shadow carbon price sensitivity, producing carbon-adjusted EV/EBITDA peer comparisons.

/treasury-climate-risk.title: Corporate Treasury Climate Risk Analytics
/treasury-climate-risk.description: Corporate treasury climate risk analytics covering FX exposure to climate-stressed economies, commodity price climate sensitivity, liquidity risk from physical events, climate VaR for treasury portfolio, and TCFD treasury disclosures.

calculationEngine.formula: Climate VaR = VaR_conventional × (1 + Climate Multiplier); Climate Multiplier = f(Physical Risk Score, Transition Risk Score, Concentration); Liquidity Risk = P(Physical Event) × Working Capital Disruption
standards: BIS Working Paper 627 (2017) — FX and Climate Risk
calculationEngine.brief: Augments conventional treasury VaR with climate multipliers derived from physical and transition risk scores, and models liquidity stress from physical climate events

dataPoints.name: Climate-Adjusted Treasury VaR (95%)
dataPoints.formula: Conventional VaR × (1 + physical risk multiplier + transition risk multiplier)
dataPoints.source: Internal treasury risk model + NGFS calibration
dataPoints.interpretation: Climate multiplier adds 15-40% to conventional VaR for high-exposure corporate treasuries; discloses per TCFD recommendations

dataPoints.name: Commodity Climate Sensitivity
dataPoints.formula: Increase in commodity price volatility under RCP 4.5 physical risk scenario
dataPoints.source: NGFS physical risk × commodity supply model
dataPoints.interpretation: Agricultural and energy commodities most sensitive; metals relatively stable; hedging cost increases proportionally

dataPoints.name: Physical Event Liquidity Risk
dataPoints.formula: Estimated working capital disruption from 1-in-50yr physical climate event affecting key facilities
dataPoints.interpretation: Drives contingency liquidity requirement; increasingly relevant for climate-exposed supply chains and coastal assets

userInteraction: Map treasury portfolio exposures (FX, commodity, interest rate, counterparty) to climate risk drivers
Apply physical risk multipliers to FX positions in climate-exposed currencies (commodity-dependent, coastal economies)
Model commodity price climate sensitivity under RCP 4.5 and 8.5 scenarios
Calculate climate VaR uplift and liquidity buffer requirement; generate TCFD treasury risk disclosure
references: TCFD (2021) Implementing TCFD Recommendations — Treasury and Risk Management Section
BIS (2020) Working Paper 899 — Climate Change and Financial Risk
Moody's (2023) Climate Risk in Corporate Treasury — Methodological Guidance
NGFS (2023) Climate Scenarios for Central Banks — Macro-Financial Models

acronyms.def: Statistical measure of potential portfolio loss at given confidence level; climate-adjusted VaR adds climate multiplier
Liquidity metric; physical climate events reducing operating cashflow can breach DSCR covenants

acronyms.full: Network for Greening the Financial System
acronyms.def: Central bank network providing climate scenarios used to calibrate treasury climate risk models
Expected loss beyond VaR threshold; climate tail events make CVaR increasingly important for treasury risk

dataLineage.flow: Historical and forward price data → conventional VaR baseline
dataLineage.output: Standard treasury VaR

dataLineage.source: NGFS climate scenarios and physical risk data
dataLineage.flow: Climate multipliers by exposure type and scenario → climate VaR adjustment
dataLineage.output: Climate-adjusted VaR

dataLineage.flow: Physical event probability × business interruption loss → liquidity gap analysis
dataLineage.output: Contingency liquidity requirement

/treasury-climate-risk.valueSummary: Provides integrated corporate treasury climate risk analytics augmenting conventional VaR with physical and transition risk multipliers, enabling TCFD-compliant climate treasury risk disclosure.

/climate-capital-markets.title: Climate Capital Markets Intelligence
/climate-capital-markets.description: Climate capital markets intelligence covering green, sustainability, and SLB issuance volumes by sector and region, investor demand signals, pricing dynamics, regulatory pipeline (EU Green Bond Standard, SEC climate disclosure), and market growth projections.

calculationEngine.formula: Market Penetration = GSS+ Issuance / Total Bond Issuance × 100; Demand Pressure Index = Order Book Coverage Ratio × Green Investor Share × -Greenium
standards: Climate Bonds Initiative Market Intelligence Q4 2023
calculationEngine.brief: Market flow analytics combining issuance volume tracking, investor demand signals, and pricing dynamics to forecast GSS+ market development

dataPoints.name: Annual GSS+ Issuance
dataPoints.formula: Global green, social, sustainability, and sustainability-linked bond issuance (2023)
dataPoints.interpretation: Green bonds largest segment ($580Bn); SLBs growing fastest (+34% YoY); EU leading with 40%+ of global green issuance

dataPoints.name: Average Order Book Coverage
dataPoints.formula: Order book size / deal size for green bond new issues
dataPoints.interpretation: Higher than vanilla comparables (typically 2.5-3x); signals strong green investor demand; enables pricing at tight end

dataPoints.name: EU GBS Premium Estimate
dataPoints.formula: Expected additional greenium for EU Green Bond Standard-labelled bonds vs standard green bonds
dataPoints.interpretation: EU GBS entered into force December 2024; first issues expected 2025; strict eligibility likely narrows supply but deepens premium
