/tcfd-physical-risk-assessment.title: TCFD Physical Risk Assessment
/tcfd-physical-risk-assessment.description: Comprehensive TCFD physical risk assessment covering both chronic hazards (sea level rise, heat stress, precipitation change, drought) and acute hazards (riverine flood, coastal flood, wildfire, storm surge, cyclone/typhoon). Produces Overall Risk Ratings (ORR), asset-level exposure mapping, and financial materiality quantification per TCFD recommendations.

calculationEngine.formula: ORR = max(chronic_risk_score, acute_risk_score) × exposure_weight × financial_sensitivity
standards: IPCC AR6 WGI Physical Climate Basis
calculationEngine.brief: Chronic risk scores aggregate multi-decade climate projections for temperature, precipitation, and sea level change at asset location coordinates, normalised to IPCC AR6 RCP/SSP scenario pathways. Acute risk scores use return-period loss modelling (100yr, 200yr, 1000yr events) from catastrophe model databases (JBA flood, RMS wildfire, AIR tropical cyclone). Financial materiality is estimated by multiplying hazard intensity by asset replacement value, adaptation cost discount, and insurance coverage haircut.

dataPoints.name: Physical Risk ORR
dataPoints.interpretation: ORR 4-5 (High/Critical) triggers TCFD mandatory disclosure under UK TCFD rules and CSRD ESRS E1-4; requires quantitative financial impact estimate.

dataPoints.name: 100-Year Flood Loss (% asset value)
dataPoints.source: JBA/Fathom flood model + AIR damage functions
dataPoints.interpretation: Loss >5% of asset value at 100-year return period is considered material for property and infrastructure assets under TCFD guidance.

dataPoints.name: Climate Financial Exposure (USD)
dataPoints.interpretation: Uninsured climate financial exposure is the key metric for TCFD Strategy section; aggregated to portfolio level for sector/geography heat maps.

userInteraction: Upload asset portfolio with geolocation data
Select hazard types, time horizons (2030/2050/2100), and SSP scenarios
Review ORR heat map and individual asset risk profiles
Generate TCFD physical risk disclosure narrative and financial impact summary
references: TCFD Technical Supplement: Physical Risks and Opportunities (2017)
IPCC AR6 WGI Summary for Policymakers
NGFS Physical Climate Risk Assessment Methodology (2024)

acronyms.def: TCFD-aligned composite physical risk score combining chronic and acute hazard exposure
IPCC AR6 climate scenario framework replacing RCPs; SSP1-1.9 to SSP5-8.5 covering 1.5°C to 4°C+ warming
IPCC AR5 climate scenario framework; RCP 2.6/4.5/6.0/8.5; being replaced by SSP framework in AR6

dataLineage.source: Asset geolocation data + JBA/Fathom/RMS hazard databases + IPCC AR6 climate projections
dataLineage.flow: Hazard overlay → vulnerability assessment → financial exposure modelling → ORR scoring
dataLineage.output: TCFD-compliant physical risk assessment with asset-level ORRs and financial materiality quantification

/tcfd-physical-risk-assessment.valueSummary: Used by real estate investors, infrastructure funds, corporate treasury, and insurance underwriters to systematically quantify and disclose physical climate risk exposure per TCFD recommendations.

/tnfd-biodiversity-baseline.title: TNFD Biodiversity Baseline Assessment
/tnfd-biodiversity-baseline.description: TNFD LEAP (Locate, Evaluate, Assess, Prepare) process implementation for biodiversity baseline assessment. Overlays business locations with biodiversity hotspot and KBA databases, evaluates ecosystem service dependencies, assesses impact drivers (land use, pollution, climate, invasive species, overexploitation), and computes TNFD CORE disclosure metrics.

calculationEngine.formula: nature_risk_score = exposure × dependency × impact_driver_intensity
standards: KBA Partnership – Key Biodiversity Areas Criteria
IPBES Global Biodiversity Framework – Kunming-Montreal Targets
calculationEngine.brief: The LEAP process begins with location mapping of all operated and upstream sites against IUCN WDPA (protected areas), KBA (Key Biodiversity Areas), and IBAT (biodiversity threat database) spatial layers. Ecosystem service dependency is assessed using ENCORE (Exploring Natural Capital Opportunities, Risks, and Exposure) for each NACE sector. Impact drivers are scored using the IPBES direct driver taxonomy (land/sea use change, direct exploitation, climate change, pollution, invasive species).

dataPoints.name: KBA Proximity Score
dataPoints.interpretation: Higher scores indicate greater biodiversity exposure risk; triggers enhanced disclosure obligations under TNFD and EU CSRD ESRS E4.

dataPoints.name: Ecosystem Service Dependency Score
dataPoints.formula: weighted avg(ENCORE dependency rating across services)
dataPoints.interpretation: Sectors with high dependency (agriculture, food, construction) face material revenue-at-risk if ecosystem services degrade; ENCORE rates 21 ecosystem services.

dataPoints.name: Biodiversity Impact Driver Count
dataPoints.interpretation: Sites with 3+ high-intensity drivers require dedicated biodiversity management plans and TNFD CORE metric disclosure.

userInteraction: Upload operational and supply chain site locations
Run LEAP spatial analysis against KBA/WDPA/IBAT databases
Assess ecosystem service dependencies using ENCORE sector mapping
Generate TNFD CORE metrics and biodiversity risk disclosure
references: KBA Partnership Key Biodiversity Areas Criteria and Category Definitions

acronyms.def: TNFD four-stage assessment process for nature-related risk and opportunity identification
Sites contributing significantly to global persistence of biodiversity; identified using standardised IUCN criteria

acronyms.full: Exploring Natural Capital Opportunities, Risks, and Exposure
acronyms.def: NCFA/UNEP-WCMC tool mapping sector dependencies and impacts on ecosystem services

dataLineage.source: Operational site data + KBA/WDPA/IBAT spatial databases + ENCORE sector maps
dataLineage.flow: LEAP spatial overlay → dependency scoring → impact driver assessment → CORE metrics
dataLineage.output: TNFD-compliant biodiversity baseline assessment with CORE disclosure metrics

/tnfd-biodiversity-baseline.valueSummary: Used by corporates, financial institutions, and asset managers to implement TNFD recommendations, comply with CSRD ESRS E4, and contribute to Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework Target 15.

/biodiversity-natural-capital.title: Biodiversity & Natural Capital Accounting
/biodiversity-natural-capital.description: Advanced biodiversity and natural capital accounting analytics covering SEEA EA natural capital stock valuation, ecosystem service flow accounting (provisioning, regulating, cultural), TNFD dependency mapping, MSA footprint calculation, and biodiversity net gain (BNG) metric computation including UK 2024 mandatory BNG units.

calculationEngine.methodology: Natural Capital Stock & Ecosystem Service Flow Accounting
calculationEngine.formula: NCA_value = Σ(ecosystem_service_flow_i × unit_value_i × asset_condition_discount)
standards: TEEB (The Economics of Ecosystems and Biodiversity)
calculationEngine.brief: Natural capital stock is valued using SEEA EA extent and condition accounts: extent (ha of each ecosystem type) × condition score × unit economic value from benefit transfer studies. Ecosystem service flows are annual benefits derived from the stock (e.g., carbon sequestration, water purification, pollination services). MSA (Mean Species Abundance) footprint is calculated per GLOBIO model: MSA_loss = Σ(pressure_type_i × area_i × MSA_loss_factor_i). UK BNG units = post-development biodiversity units − pre-development biodiversity units, using the Natural England statutory metric.

dataPoints.name: Natural Capital Asset Value (USD/ha)
dataPoints.source: TEEB / SEEA EA benefit transfer studies
dataPoints.interpretation: Global average natural capital value ~$1,000-5,000/ha; tropical forests ~$20,000-100,000/ha when carbon, water, and biodiversity services are included.

dataPoints.name: MSA Footprint (MSA.ha)
dataPoints.interpretation: MSA.ha measures biodiversity footprint in terms of habitat area equivalents at pristine condition; lower is better; used for corporate biodiversity target setting.

dataPoints.name: BNG Units (UK Statutory Metric)
dataPoints.interpretation: Mandatory ≥10% net gain required for UK developments under Environment Act 2021 from January 2024; BNG credits can be purchased if on-site gain is insufficient.

userInteraction: Input land use / ecosystem extent and condition data
Calculate natural capital asset value and ecosystem service flows
Compute MSA footprint for corporate biodiversity accounting
Generate BNG calculation for UK development projects and credit purchase plan
references: UN SEEA Ecosystem Accounting Technical Recommendations (2021)

acronyms.full: System of Environmental-Economic Accounting – Ecosystem Accounting
acronyms.def: UN statistical standard for measuring natural capital stocks and ecosystem service flows alongside GDP accounts
Biodiversity integrity indicator measuring average population size of original species relative to pristine conditions; used in GLOBIO model
UK mandatory requirement under Environment Act 2021 for developments to achieve at least 10% net gain in biodiversity value

dataLineage.source: Land use mapping + GLOBIO pressure data + SEEA EA extent/condition accounts
dataLineage.flow: Natural capital stock valuation → ecosystem service flow → MSA footprint → BNG metric
dataLineage.output: Corporate biodiversity accounting metrics for TNFD reporting, UK BNG compliance, and natural capital investment decisions

/biodiversity-natural-capital.valueSummary: Used by property developers, infrastructure companies, corporates, and natural capital investors to quantify biodiversity dependencies and impacts, comply with UK BNG, and report against TNFD and CSRD ESRS E4 nature-related standards.

/bifacial-agrivoltaic-finance.title: Bifacial & Agrivoltaic Finance Analytics
/bifacial-agrivoltaic-finance.description: Bifacial PV module and agrivoltaic co-location project finance analytics. Quantifies bifacial energy gain from rear-side irradiance, agrivoltaic crop yield retention by type, dual land revenue economics, and financing structures for solar-agriculture co-deployment.

calculationEngine.formula: AEP_uplift = base_AEP × bifaciality_factor × GHI_rear/GHI_front; Land_revenue = solar_$/ha + crop_$/ha
calculationEngine.brief: Bifacial gain modelled using bifacial factor (0.65-0.85) × rear/front irradiance ratio from albedo and mounting height. Agrivoltaic yield retention is crop-type dependent: shade-tolerant crops (herbs, lettuce) retain 80-95% yield; field crops (wheat, soy) 60-75%. Dual land revenue: solar lease plus retained agricultural income.

dataPoints.name: Bifacial Energy Gain (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Depends on ground albedo, module elevation, row spacing; higher albedo and elevated mounts maximise rear-side irradiance.

dataPoints.name: Agri Yield Retention (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Shade-tolerant crops retain 80-95%; cereal crops 60-75%; optimal panel height and E-W orientation minimise yield loss.

dataPoints.name: LCOE ($/MWh)
dataPoints.interpretation: IRA dual-use land credit reduces effective LCOE by $5-15/MWh in the US.

userInteraction: Select project or country filter to explore bifacial gain distribution
Review agrivoltaic co-benefits tab for crop yield analysis
Analyse land economics tab for dual revenue stream comparison
Use financing structure tab to model equity/debt ratios and DSCR
references: IEA PVPS Task 13 (2021) – Bifacial Solar Technologies

acronyms.def: Total electricity generated by a solar project in a year, MWh/year
Total solar radiation received per unit area on a horizontal surface

acronyms.full: Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan
acronyms.def: India government scheme providing solar power to farmers
All-in cost of generating one MWh over project lifetime

dataLineage.source: Irradiance + albedo + crop yield trials + land cost data
dataLineage.flow: Bifacial gain model + agri yield retention + dual revenue stack
dataLineage.output: Project LCOE, IRR, NPV for bifacial agrivoltaic project finance

/floating-solar-finance.title: Floating Solar (FPV) Project Finance
/floating-solar-finance.description: Floating photovoltaic (FPV) project finance analytics. Covers water body suitability, panel cooling benefit quantification, evaporation water savings, structural premium economics, and project finance for reservoir, lake, and irrigation canal installations.

calculationEngine.formula: LCOE_FPV = LCOE_land × (1 + structural_premium) - cooling_benefit; Evap_savings = A_covered × ET_rate × shade_factor
standards: World Bank ESMAP FPV Technical Guidance
calculationEngine.brief: FPV panels benefit from water surface cooling: module temperature 5-10°C lower boosts output 2-5%. Evaporation savings significant in water-stressed regions: 1 ha reservoir → 5,000-10,000 m³/year. Structural premium 15-25% above land-mounted due to pontoon/mooring.

dataPoints.name: Cooling AEP Boost (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Temperature coefficient γ ≈ -0.35%/°C; FPV panels run 5-10°C cooler, yielding 1.7-3.5% output boost.

dataPoints.name: Evaporation Savings (m³/yr/ha)
dataPoints.interpretation: In arid regions, evaporation savings valued at $0.5-2/m³, providing significant dual benefit.

dataPoints.name: Structural CAPEX Premium (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Partly offset by no land lease and reduced O&M (cooler panels, less dust).

userInteraction: Filter by water body type to compare reservoir vs lake economics
Review cooling benefit analysis for module temperature reduction
Analyse evaporation savings for water-stressed project regions
Examine project finance tab for DSCR modelling
references: IEA PVPS Task 13 (2022) – Floating PV Roadmap
World Bank ESMAP FPV Technical Guidance Note 2022

acronyms.def: Solar PV installed on floating platforms on water bodies
Net operating income / total debt service; lenders require >1.25x

dataLineage.source: Water body GIS + irradiance + evaporation rate + structural costs
dataLineage.flow: FPV yield model (cooling + evap savings) + project finance DSCR
dataLineage.output: FPV project LCOE, IRR, NPV and bankability assessment

/floating-solar-finance.valueSummary: Used by solar IPPs, infrastructure funds, water utilities, and irrigation authorities evaluating FPV deployment on reservoirs and canals where land is scarce and water conservation is valued.

/solar-plus-storage-finance.title: Solar + Storage Co-located Finance
/solar-plus-storage-finance.description: Co-located solar PV and BESS project finance analytics. Covers IRA Investment Tax Credit stacking (base 30% plus domestic content, energy community, and low-income adders), revenue stack modelling across energy/capacity/ancillary markets, and merchant risk quantification.

calculationEngine.formula: ITC_total = ITC_base + DC_adder + EC_adder + LI_adder; Revenue = energy_arb + capacity_payment + AS_revenue
calculationEngine.brief: IRA ITC base 30%; domestic content adder +10%; energy community adder +10%; low-income adder +20%. Revenue stacking: energy arbitrage, capacity market payments (PJM/MISO/CAISO), ancillary services (frequency regulation highest $/MW in CAISO).

dataPoints.name: IRA ITC Total (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Maximum 70% ITC combining all four adders; domestic content requires ≥40% US steel/iron and manufactured products.

dataPoints.name: BESS Duration (hours)
dataPoints.interpretation: 4-hour BESS qualifies for capacity market; 2-hour for energy arbitrage and frequency regulation.

dataPoints.name: Revenue Stack ($/MWh solar)
dataPoints.interpretation: Ancillary services $20-60/MW-hr in CAISO; capacity $50-200/MW-yr in PJM; energy arbitrage $15-50/MWh in ERCOT.

userInteraction: Use IRA incentive model to calculate total ITC by project location

Examine storage sizing for optimal battery duration

/utility-solar-epc-intelligence.title: Utility-Scale EPC Intelligence
/utility-solar-epc-intelligence.description: Utility-scale solar EPC contract intelligence. Benchmarks CAPEX by contractor and technology, quantifies single-axis tracker vs fixed-tilt AEP uplift, analyses warranty and performance guarantee terms, and assesses contractor bankability for project finance lenders.

calculationEngine.formula: CAPEX_total = modules + BOS + labor + interconnection; SAT_uplift = 4-6% AEP vs fixed_tilt
calculationEngine.brief: EPC CAPEX: modules ~40%, BOS ~25%, labor ~20%, interconnection ~15%. SAT cost 8-12% more than fixed-tilt but delivers 4-6% AEP improvement. Contractor bankability: lenders require net worth ≥ 20% of contract value.

dataPoints.name: SAT AEP Uplift (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Optimal in mid-latitude, high-DNI sites; E-W trackers gaining share for grid stability.

dataPoints.name: Contractor Bankability Score
dataPoints.formula: Financial strength + track record + warranty depth
dataPoints.interpretation: Lenders require EPC net worth ≥ 20% of contract; performance guarantees backed by parent company.

userInteraction: Filter EPC landscape by country or mounting type to benchmark CAPEX
Review tracker analysis for SAT vs fixed-tilt AEP comparison
Use warranty & performance tab to compare guarantee terms
Run risk assessment for EPC contractor default scenarios
references: BNEF H2 2024 Solar PV Market Outlook

Solar mounting rotating panels east-west, increasing AEP vs fixed-tilt
All solar components except modules: racking, wiring, inverters, monitoring

/distributed-community-solar.title: Distributed & Community Solar Analytics
/distributed-community-solar.description: Distributed generation and community solar program analytics. Covers SREC market pricing, net metering policy economics, community solar subscription models, LMI access requirements, and distributed generation economics across US state programs.

calculationEngine.formula: SREC_revenue = AEP_MWh × SREC_price; NEM_savings = exported_kWh × rate (retail NEM1 or avoided_cost NEM3)
calculationEngine.brief: SREC value driven by state RPS compliance: DC $400-500/MWh, MA $250-300, NJ $150-220, IL $75-100. Community solar: subscribers receive 10-15% bill discount. LMI set-asides: IL CEJA 50%, NY VDER adder, MA DG 10%.

dataPoints.formula: Set by RPS compliance demand and SACP
dataPoints.interpretation: DC SRECs highest ($400-500/MWh) due to 100% RPS; lower-demand states $15-50.

dataPoints.name: Community Solar Bill Credit (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Typical 10-15% discount; LMI subscribers 20-30% under state carve-outs.

dataPoints.name: LMI Set-Aside (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: LMI = households <80% area median income.

Billing mechanism crediting solar customers for exported electricity

State policy requiring % of electricity from renewables

/solar-repowering-analytics.title: Solar Asset Repowering & Life Extension
/solar-repowering-analytics.description: Solar PV asset repowering and life extension analytics. Models degradation trajectories for aging fleets, quantifies AEP uplift from technology upgrades, evaluates repowering economics, restructures PPA agreements, and provides incremental IRR analysis for capital allocation.

calculationEngine.formula: P_current = P_initial × (1-d)^age; Repowering_NPV = ΔAEP × PPA_price × life_remaining - repowering_CAPEX
calculationEngine.brief: Annual degradation 0.5-0.8%/yr for c-Si (Jordan et al. 2016). After 20-25 years, typical c-Si retains 70-82% initial power. Repowering CAPEX $0.25-0.65/Wdc. AEP uplift 15-35% from bifacial TOPCon + tracker replacing polycrystalline fixed-tilt.

dataPoints.name: Annual Degradation Rate (%/yr)
dataPoints.interpretation: Year 1 LID 1-3%; subsequent 0.3-0.7%/yr mono-Si; PERC LeTID can increase mid-life degradation.

dataPoints.name: Repowering AEP Uplift (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Bifacial TOPCon SAT replacing 2008 polycrystalline fixed-tilt can deliver 35% AEP uplift.

dataPoints.name: Incremental IRR (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Sites with existing grid connection and long remaining land leases have highest incremental returns.

/solar-cell-technology-analyzer.title: Solar Cell Technology Analyzer
/solar-cell-technology-analyzer.description: Solar cell technology comparison and market analysis. Benchmarks efficiency, cost, temperature coefficient, and degradation rate across BSF, PERC, TOPCon, HJT, IBC, Perovskite-Si tandem, CdTe, and CIGS. Tracks market share transition and projects technology roadmaps to 2030.

calculationEngine.formula: Wright_Law: C(Q) = C_0 × (Q/Q_0)^(-b); b = log2(1-LR), LR = 24% for c-Si
calculationEngine.brief: NREL 2024 records: PERC 24%, TOPCon 26%, HJT 26.7%, IBC 26.7%, Perovskite-Si tandem 33.9%. PERC ~83% production 2022; TOPCon ~35% by 2024. Wright's Law: each capacity doubling → 24% cost reduction.

dataPoints.name: Cell Efficiency Record (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Commercial module efficiency lags cell by 2-4pp; Perovskite-Si tandem target 30%+ commercialization by 2027.

dataPoints.name: Temperature Coefficient (%/°C)
dataPoints.interpretation: HJT best: -0.26%/°C; PERC -0.35%; BSF worst: -0.45%; critical for hot climates.

dataPoints.name: Learning Rate (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Module cost $76/W (1977) → $0.16/W (2023); 99.8% cost reduction.

calculationEngine.formula: Cost_module = Σ(BOM_i × price_i) + (labor + overhead + depreciation) / output; LR = 24%
standards: IRA §48C Advanced Energy Manufacturing Tax Credit
calculationEngine.brief: BOM for 400W module: cells ~55%, glass ~10%, frame ~8%, backsheet ~5%, EVA ~5%, junction box ~3%, ribbon ~2%, other ~12%. Chinese integrated producer $0.10-0.16/W; US/EU $0.25-0.40/W. IRA §48C: 30% ITC on US factory investment.

/solar-manufacturing-carbon-lca.title: Solar Manufacturing Carbon LCA
/solar-manufacturing-carbon-lca.description: Lifecycle carbon assessment of solar PV module manufacturing. Quantifies cradle-to-gate carbon intensity (gCO2e/kWh), scope 1/2/3 emissions, energy payback period, and carbon payback across manufacturing locations and technology types per IEC 63274 and ISO 14040/44.

calculationEngine.formula: CI = mfg_emissions_gCO2e / (AEP_kWh × lifetime_yrs); EPBT = E_mfg / P_annual
calculationEngine.brief: Mono-Si module China grid: 35-50 gCO2e/kWh; Europe renewables: 15-25 gCO2e/kWh; CdTe: 15-22 gCO2e/kWh. Energy payback: 0.5-2.5 years. Carbon payback: 0.5-2.0 years. Coal power: 820 gCO2e/kWh.

dataPoints.name: Carbon Intensity (gCO2e/kWh)
dataPoints.source: NREL LCA Harmonization + IEA PVPS Task 12
dataPoints.interpretation: Xinjiang polysilicon (coal) can double CI vs European or US-manufactured polysilicon.

dataPoints.name: Energy Payback Period (years)
dataPoints.interpretation: MENA/India 2000+ kWh/m²/yr → 0.5-0.8 yr; central Europe → 1.5-2.5 yr.

dataPoints.name: Scope 3 Share (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: Cell manufacturing 60-70% of module carbon; upstream polysilicon 15-25%.

calculationEngine.formula: Effective_cost = module_price × (1 + AD_CVD_rate); IRA_benefit = CAPEX × ITC_rate + output × PTC_rate
calculationEngine.brief: US AD/CVD on Chinese modules: 30-250% effective rate. IRA §48E: $0.04/W modules, $0.12/kWh cells. EU NZIA: 40% EU demand met by EU manufacturers by 2030. CBAM fertilizers from 2026; potential solar phase-in 2028-2030.

/solar-module-quality-bankability.title: Solar Module Quality & Bankability
/solar-module-quality-bankability.description: Solar module quality assurance and bankability analytics for project finance. Models P90/P50 25-year yield scenarios, quantifies degradation by technology, assesses PVEL/DNV bankability rankings, verifies IEC testing certifications, and provides warranty adequacy analysis for debt sizing.

calculationEngine.formula: P90_yield = P_initial × (1-LID) × (1-ann_deg)^24 × P90_factor; P50_yield = P90_yield / 0.96
calculationEngine.brief: P90 yield: energy exceeded with 90% probability over 25 years. P90/P50 ratio 0.95-0.97 for characterized modules. PVEL Top Performers score in top quartile across all test categories.

dataPoints.name: 25-yr P90 Yield Ratio
dataPoints.interpretation: Lower-ratio modules face lender yield haircuts; bifacial have higher P90/P50 spread due to albedo variability.

dataPoints.name: PVEL Bankability Score
dataPoints.formula: Outdoor reliability + accelerated tests + power measurement
dataPoints.interpretation: Non-Top Performers have 50-150% higher failure rates in accelerated lifetime testing.

dataPoints.interpretation: Premium manufacturers guarantee 0.4-0.5%/yr; standard 0.55-0.7%/yr.

/green-ammonia-production-economics.title: Green Ammonia Production Economics
/green-ammonia-production-economics.description: Green ammonia LCOA analysis. Models electrolyser+Haber-Bosch project economics, decomposes LCOA into capital, operating, and electricity cost components, tracks green premium vs grey ammonia, and projects pathway to cost-competitive $300/t target by 2030.

calculationEngine.formula: LCOA = (CAPEX × CRF + OPEX + E_price × 10 MWh/t) / NH3_output_t; CRF = r(1+r)^n/((1+r)^n-1)
calculationEngine.brief: Green NH3: 178 kg H2/tonne, 10 MWh electricity/tonne, 0.8 t N2/tonne. LCOA 2024: $400-1200/t green vs $200-350/t grey. Cost breakdown: electrolysis 60-70%, Haber-Bosch 15-20%, CAPEX financing 15-25%.

dataPoints.name: LCOA ($/tonne NH3)
dataPoints.interpretation: At $20/MWh RE, electricity = $200/t; at $50/MWh = $500/t; key lever is cheap renewable electricity.

dataPoints.name: Green Premium ($/tonne)
dataPoints.interpretation: Grey NH3 FOB $200-300/t; green premium needs to close to <$100/t for commercial viability without subsidies.

dataPoints.name: Electrolyser Capacity Factor (%)
dataPoints.formula: Operating hours / total hours per year
dataPoints.interpretation: Higher CF reduces CAPEX per tonne; 50% CF (intermittent wind) doubles CAPEX vs 90% CF (24/7 hydro).

userInteraction: Review project portfolio for LCOA distribution by country and RE source
Use LCOA decomposition for electricity/CAPEX/OPEX sensitivity

/green-ammonia-shipping-storage.title: Green Ammonia Shipping & Terminal Infrastructure

calculationEngine.formula: Total_cost = LCOA + freight + terminal_handling + cracking_penalty
standards: IMO Ammonia Marine Fuel Safety Guidelines
calculationEngine.brief: NH3 shipping in refrigerated liquid form (-33°C) using VLGCs (90,000 DWT). Freight: $50-120/t. NH3 cracking (reconversion to H2): 15-20% energy penalty plus $50-100/t capital cost. Safety: LC50 = 2,000 ppm.

dataPoints.name: VLGC Freight Rate ($/tonne NH3)
dataPoints.interpretation: Australia→Japan $80-90/t; Chile→Rotterdam $90-110/t; Middle East→Japan $50-70/t; fleet needs to grow 200+ vessels by 2030.

calculationEngine.formula: Total_cost = production_$/GJ + carrier_conversion + shipping_$/GJ/1000km + reconversion_$/GJ
standards: IRENA Green H2 Cost Reduction Report

calculationEngine.brief: At 10,000 km: GH2 prohibitive (>$20/GJ); LH2 $2-4/GJ transport + $1-2/GJ liquefaction; LOHC $1.5-2.5/GJ transport + $1-2/GJ dehydrogenation; NH3 $0.8-1.5/GJ transport + $0.5-1.0/GJ cracking; NH3 cheapest long-distance after pipeline.

dataPoints.interpretation: NH3 LHV = 18.6 GJ/t; at $80/t for 10,000 km = $0.43/GJ per 1000km; cheapest long-haul carrier.

dataPoints.interpretation: Liquefaction energy = 6-12 kWh/kg; Kawasaki world's first commercial LH2 supply chain operational 2023.

/saf-lcof-engine.title: SAF Levelised Cost of Fuel Engine
/saf-lcof-engine.description: Comprehensive LCOF analysis for all six ASTM D7566 sustainable aviation fuel pathways. Models HEFA-UCO, HEFA-Tallow, AtJ-Cellulosic, FT-MSW, FT-Agricultural, and PtL-DAC including feedstock cost, CAPEX learning curves (Wright's Law), IRA §40B tax credit ($1.25–$1.75/gal), and 24 seeded project benchmarks.

calculationEngine.formula: LCOF = (CAPEX×CRF + OPEX) / Annual_Production + Feedstock_Cost − IRA_§40B_Credit
standards: ICAO CORSIA Default Life Cycle Values
calculationEngine.brief: HEFA: $2.5–4.0/gal (2024); PtL: $8–15/gal (2024) declining to $3–5/gal by 2035. Wright's Law learning rate: 15–20% per doubling.

dataPoints.name: HEFA LCOF ($/gal)
dataPoints.interpretation: UCO feedstock $0.60–1.20/gal; CAPEX $2–3/gal amortised; IRA §40B reduces by $1.25–1.75/gal.

dataPoints.name: PtL LCOF ($/gal)
dataPoints.interpretation: Electricity 60–70% of cost; requires <$20/MWh green power for competitiveness by 2035.

dataPoints.name: IRA §40B Credit ($/gal)
dataPoints.interpretation: Minimum 50% CI reduction vs petroleum jet; expires end 2027; replaced by §45Z.

/saf-project-finance.title: SAF Project Finance Modeler
/saf-project-finance.description: Full project finance model for SAF facilities: DSCR analysis, Newton-Raphson IRR engine, NPV, and blended finance structuring across 20 seeded SAF deals. Interactive sliders for CAPEX, capacity, offtake price, and debt rate. Risk register with probability-impact scatter.

calculationEngine.formula: IRR: NPV(r) = Σ CF_t/(1+r)^t = 0; DSCR = EBITDA / Debt_Service
calculationEngine.brief: Typical unlevered IRR: 8–14% for HEFA; 6–10% for FT/PtL with IRA §40B; DSCR minimum 1.25× for lenders.

dataPoints.name: DSCR (min for bankability)
dataPoints.interpretation: Aviation sector lenders require 1.20–1.25× minimum; DFI blended finance may accept 1.15×.

dataPoints.name: Equity IRR target
dataPoints.interpretation: Higher risk pathways (PtL) require 16–18%; mature HEFA accepts 12–14%.

dataPoints.name: CAPEX range ($/gal/yr)
dataPoints.interpretation: HEFA $3–4/gal/yr; FT $5–7/gal/yr; PtL $6–9/gal/yr; learning expected to halve PtL by 2035.

calculationEngine.formula: Feedstock_cost = Collection + Processing + Transport; CI_reduction = (Petroleum_CI − SAF_CI) / Petroleum_CI
calculationEngine.brief: UCO global supply ~5–7 Mt/yr; required SAF feedstock 2030: 30–50 Mt/yr; significant feedstock gap by 2030.

/saf-policy-mandate.title: SAF Policy & Mandate Intelligence
/saf-policy-mandate.description: Tracks all major global SAF mandates and policy frameworks: EU ReFuelEU (2–70%), UK SAF Mandate (2–75%), IRA §40B production tax credit, US SAF Grand Challenge, Japan GIF, Singapore CAAS, CORSIA, and Australia. Includes interactive IRA §40B calculator.

calculationEngine.formula: Credit = Gallons × $1.25 × max(1, (50 − CI_reduction_pct) / 50 + 0.5)
standards: IRS Notice 2023-06 and FAA CORSIA guidance
calculationEngine.brief: IRA §40B: $1.25/gal base; scaling up to $1.75/gal for CI >50% reduction. Replaces §45Z from 2025.

/saf-carbon-credits.description: SAF carbon credit intelligence covering CORSIA CEF, ISCC+, RSB, Verra VCU, Gold Standard, and EU ETS integration. LCA waterfall by pathway, revenue stacking model ($SAF + IRA §40B + ISCC+ + CORSIA + EU ETS), registry landscape, and credit market volume forecast.

calculationEngine.formula: Total_Revenue = SAF_price + IRA_§40B + CORSIA_CEF + ISCC+_premium + EU_ETS_offset
calculationEngine.brief: Revenue stacking materially improves SAF economics: HEFA base $2.8/gal + §40B $1.25/gal + CORSIA CEF $0.20/gal + ISCC+ $0.10/gal = $4.35/gal total.

dataPoints.interpretation: SAF displacing conventional jet in EU ETS scope reduces allowance surrender; valued at EU ETS spot.

/green-steel-lcop-engine.title: Green Steel LCOP Engine
/green-steel-lcop-engine.description: Levelised Cost of Production analysis for 6 steel production routes: BF-BOF, DRI-EAF-NG, DRI-EAF-H₂, EAF-Scrap, Molten Oxide Electrolysis, and HIsarna+CCS. Models CBAM certificate exposure, H₂ break-even price calculator, and carbon price sensitivity across 22 projects.

calculationEngine.formula: LCOP = (CAPEX×CRF + OPEX + Feedstock + Energy) / Annual_Output + CBAM_exposure − Green_premium
standards: IEA Iron and Steel Technology Roadmap 2020
calculationEngine.brief: BF-BOF baseline ~$500/t; DRI-EAF-H₂ ~$650–950/t (2024) declining to ~$500–700/t by 2030 with H₂ cost reduction; EAF-Scrap ~$400/t where scrap available.

dataPoints.name: DRI-EAF H₂ break-even H₂ price ($/kg)
dataPoints.interpretation: At carbon price $100/tCO2: green H2 <$2.0/kg makes DRI-EAF-H2 competitive with BF-BOF. IEA projects $1–2/kg H2 by 2030.

dataPoints.name: CBAM certificate price (€/tCO₂)
dataPoints.formula: EU ETS spot price applicable to embedded carbon
dataPoints.source: EU ETS current price + CBAM Regulation
dataPoints.interpretation: CBAM fully phased in 2034; steel at 1.85 tCO2/t BF-BOF faces €92–148/t certificate cost per ton at €50–80 ETS.

dataPoints.name: BF-BOF carbon intensity (tCO₂/t steel)
dataPoints.interpretation: DRI-EAF-H2 achieves 0.05–0.08 tCO2/t with green H2; EAF-Scrap 0.06–0.3 tCO2/t (grid-dependent).

/industrial-hydrogen-integration.title: Industrial Hydrogen Integration Finance

calculationEngine.formula: Abatement_cost = (H2_system_LCOH − Fossil_baseline_opex) / CO2_displaced
calculationEngine.brief: Steel DRI-H2: $50–80/tCO2 abatement (2024), declining to $10–20/tCO2 by 2035. Chemicals (ammonia): currently negative abatement cost where H2 <$3/kg.

/green-cement-concrete-finance.title: Green Cement & Concrete Finance
/green-cement-concrete-finance.description: Financial and technology analysis for low-carbon cement and concrete: Conventional Portland, Oxyfuel+CCS, LC³, Post-Combustion CCS, Geopolymer, Electric Kiln, and LEILAC. LCA waterfall separating calcination and thermal CO₂, break-even carbon price, and abatement progress by technology.

calculationEngine.formula: LCOP = Energy_cost + Raw_material + CAPEX_CRF + CO2_cost − Green_premium
standards: EU ETS embedded carbon data for cement
calculationEngine.brief: Calcination CO₂ (0.55 tCO2/t) is process-inherent; only CCS or alternative binders can address it. Thermal CO₂ (0.10 tCO2/t) addressable via fuel switching or electrification.

dataPoints.name: Cement calcination CO₂ (tCO₂/t clinker)
dataPoints.formula: Chemical decomposition of CaCO3 → CaO + CO2
dataPoints.interpretation: Irreducible without CCS or supplementary cementitious materials; LEILAC captures this stream directly.

dataPoints.name: LEILAC CAPEX premium (% vs conventional)
dataPoints.interpretation: LEILAC separates process CO2 for low-cost capture; CAPEX 25–40% higher than conventional kiln; demonstrated at HeidelbergCement Lixhe.

dataPoints.name: LC³ cost saving vs OPC (%)
dataPoints.interpretation: LC³ (Limestone Calcined Clay Cement) reduces clinker factor to 50% vs 95% OPC; cuts CO2 by 40% and cost by 5–15%.

/industrial-electrification-finance.title: Industrial Electrification Finance
/industrial-electrification-finance.description: Financial analysis of industrial electrification technologies: Industrial Heat Pumps, Electric Boilers, EAF, Microwave/RF Heating, Induction Heating, and Thermal Storage. Covers 18 projects, simple payback calculator, ROI vs electricity price sensitivity, temperature profile, and grid flexibility benefits.

calculationEngine.formula: Simple_payback = CAPEX / Annual_savings; Annual_savings = (Fossil_opex − Elec_opex) + Carbon_cost_avoided
standards: EU Commission Electrification of Industry Study
calculationEngine.brief: Industrial heat pumps: 2–5yr payback at gas vs electricity ratio <3:1; EAF already economical where scrap available; microwave heating 30–40% energy saving over conventional.

dataPoints.name: Industrial heat pump COP
dataPoints.formula: Heat delivered / Electricity input at process temperature
dataPoints.interpretation: COP decreases with higher temperature; <100°C: COP 3–4; 100–200°C: COP 2–3; >200°C: COP <2; above 300°C: not viable.

dataPoints.name: Electric boiler efficiency
dataPoints.interpretation: Fuel switch from gas boiler (90% efficiency) to electric (98%); economics depend entirely on electricity/gas price ratio.

dataPoints.name: EAF energy consumption (MWh/t steel)
dataPoints.formula: Scrap-based EAF; varies with scrap quality
dataPoints.interpretation: EAF uses 0.5–0.65 MWh/t vs BF-BOF indirect electricity of 0.1–0.15 MWh/t; direct emissions near-zero with renewable power.

/direct-air-capture-finance.title: Direct Air Capture Finance Platform
/direct-air-capture-finance.description: Full-cycle finance analysis for 5 DAC technologies: Solid Sorbent (Climeworks), Liquid Solvent (Carbon Engineering/Oxy), Electroswing (Verdox), Moisture-Swing, and DACCS-Wind. IRA §45Q credit ($180/tCO₂ geological), LCOC electricity sensitivity, learning curves, and advance offtake buyers including Stripe Frontier.

calculationEngine.formula: LCOC = (CAPEX×CRF + OPEX + Electricity_cost) / Annual_CDR − IRA_§45Q
calculationEngine.brief: Current: $400–1,000/tCO2; IEA target $100–300/tCO2 by 2030 via scale + learning; IRA §45Q reduces effective cost by $180/tCO2 for geological storage.

dataPoints.name: IRA §45Q DAC geological ($/tCO₂)
dataPoints.formula: Direct payment credit for geological storage
dataPoints.interpretation: Requires CO2 injection into Class VI well; verification via EPA MRV; Inflation Reduction Act 2022 increased from $50 to $180.

dataPoints.name: DAC electricity intensity (MWh/tCO₂)
dataPoints.interpretation: Electricity is 50–70% of DAC LCOC; requires <$30/MWh dedicated renewable power for $200/tCO2 target at 2030 scale.

dataPoints.name: Climeworks Mammoth capacity (ktCO₂/yr)
dataPoints.interpretation: Mammoth: 36 ktCO2/yr; 10× Orca (3.6 ktCO2/yr); demonstrates modular scale-up; geothermal provides low-cost process heat.

calculationEngine.formula: CDR = Feedstock_mass × Carbon_yield × (1 − labile_fraction) × stable_C_fraction
calculationEngine.brief: Agricultural residues: 0.25–0.35 tCO2/t feedstock net CDR; forest residues: 0.30–0.40 tCO2/t; stable C fraction >80% for Puro.earth registration.

dataPoints.name: Biochar stable carbon fraction
dataPoints.interpretation: H:Corg is key permanence proxy; Puro requires H:Corg <0.7; EBC Premium requires <0.4; measured via elemental analysis.

dataPoints.formula: Saline aquifer or depleted reservoir injection cost
dataPoints.interpretation: Transport + injection: $10–25/tCO2; monitoring $3–8/tCO2/yr; reduces net LCOC of BECCS by $15–30/tCO2 vs DAC.

dataPoints.name: IRA §45Q BECCS power ($/tCO₂)
dataPoints.formula: Bioenergy power plant with CCS geological storage
dataPoints.interpretation: Eligible for $85/t if >80% capture efficiency; DAC-equivalent ($180/t) not applicable to BECCS unless integrated with DAC.

calculationEngine.formula: ΔDIC = (TA_added × Revelle_factor) / seawater_volume; net_CDR = ΔDIC × verification_factor

dataPoints.interpretation: Kelp burial at >200m: decades to centuries; at >2km: centuries to millennia; highly location-dependent.

/re-climate-stress-test.title: RE Climate Stress Test
/re-climate-stress-test.description: NGFS-aligned climate stress testing for real estate portfolios: 4 scenario stress parameters (LTV/NOI/cap rate/vacancy), 6 sector risk profiles, 20-asset portfolio stress table, Radar chart for physical vs transition risk, and stressed NAV vs base NAV calculation.

calculationEngine.formula: StressedNAV = BaseNAV × (1 + ΔLTV_impact) × (1 + ΔNOI_impact / CapRate); Stressed_CapRate = BaseCapRate + Δcap_rate; Stressed_NOI = BaseNOI × (1 + ΔNOI_pct); StressedValue = StressedNOI / Stressed_CapRate
calculationEngine.brief: ECB 2022 stress test found climate risk adds 10–15% additional loss given default for commercial RE under severe physical risk scenarios.

dataPoints.formula: Transition risk managed; physical risk limited
dataPoints.interpretation: Orderly transition: early action limits physical damage; managed transition costs are priced in gradually.

/climate-smart-infrastructure.title: Climate-Smart Infrastructure Finance
/climate-smart-infrastructure.description: Infrastructure resilience analytics: 6 sectors with climate risk and adaptation BCR, 22 projects including coastal barriers/urban cooling/NbS drainage, IFC Performance Standards PS 1–8, 6 climate finance funds (GCF/Adapt/IFC/AIIB/EU GG/WB), and long-horizon IRR comparison (5–50yr).

calculationEngine.formula: BCR = Σ(PV_Benefits) / Σ(PV_Costs); NbS_BCR = (FloodLossAvoided + EcosystemServices + CarbonValue) / (CapEx + OpEx + LandCost); Long_Horizon_IRR solves NPV=0 over 25–50yr period
standards: World Bank Green Bonds Adaptation Framework 2021
calculationEngine.brief: NbS infrastructure commands BCR 4–8x vs grey infrastructure 3–6x over 50-year asset lives; increasingly preferred by MDBs for adaptation finance.

dataPoints.name: Adaptation infrastructure BCR (avg)
dataPoints.interpretation: Average $1 invested in adaptation returns $6.10 in avoided losses; early warning systems show highest BCR (12–32x) at lowest cost.

dataPoints.name: GCF adaptation window
dataPoints.formula: Of GCF portfolio allocated to adaptation
dataPoints.interpretation: GCF 50% allocation target for adaptation; SIDS/LDCs prioritised; grant element up to 100% for most vulnerable countries.

dataPoints.name: IFC PS 4 involuntary resettlement
dataPoints.formula: At replacement value for affected persons
dataPoints.interpretation: PS 4 requires livelihood restoration to pre-project level; non-compliance triggers E&S action plan and can delay financing.

/climate-adaptation-portfolio.title: Climate Adaptation Portfolio
/climate-adaptation-portfolio.description: 28-asset multi-hazard adaptation portfolio (coastal/flood/heat/NbS/water/EWS) with BCR, IRR, adaptation score, risk-return scatter, benefit attribution (physical risk reduction/productivity/co-benefits/carbon credits), regional radar (EU/APAC/LATAM/Africa/MENA), and 30-year cash flow.

calculationEngine.formula: PortfolioBCR = Σ(weight_i × BCR_i); Adaptation_Score = 0.4 × PhysicalRisk_reduction + 0.3 × CommunityResilience + 0.3 × Cobenefits; Risk_Adjusted_IRR = IRR / sqrt(ClimateRiskScore); CumulativeNCF grows to positive payback year
calculationEngine.brief: World Bank GFDRR portfolio average BCR 6.1x; EWS shows highest BCR at lowest cost; NbS increasingly preferred over grey infrastructure for blended finance eligibility.

dataPoints.interpretation: Ecosystem service co-benefits rarely monetised in project finance; if captured, NbS BCR rises from 7.6x to 12–18x.

/just-transition-adaptation.title: Just Transition & Adaptation
/just-transition-adaptation.description: Regional vulnerability assessment for 22 fossil fuel communities (Appalachia/Ruhr/Mpumalanga/Jharkhand), JETP finance mechanisms ($147Bn committed), net job creation/displacement analytics, social justice outcomes radar (procedural/distributive/restorative), and investor ILO/PRI framework.

calculationEngine.formula: JT_Score = 0.25 × IncomeEquity + 0.20 × EmploymentAccess + 0.20 × CommunityVoice + 0.20 × HealthSafety + 0.15 × GenderInclusion; JETP_Leverage = (TotalCommitment − GrantElement) / GrantElement; Worker_Retraining_Cost = DisplacedWorkers × AvgRetraining_Cost
calculationEngine.brief: South Africa JETP $8.5Bn: workers in Mpumalanga coal region without just transition support face 22% income loss and 34% employment rate decline within 5 years of plant closure.

dataPoints.interpretation: JETP leverage ratio 3:1 public:private; grant element typically 10–25%; most disbursement slow — SA JETP 18 months post-announcement, <$200M disbursed.

dataPoints.interpretation: Silesia (PL), Ruhr (DE), Lignite regions (DE/CZ) top recipients; conditions: coal phase-out timeline commitment; 85% grants for deprived regions.

/power-grid-transmission-finance.title: Power Grid & Transmission Finance
/power-grid-transmission-finance.description: Regulatory Asset Base (RAB) model for 12 European TSOs, revenue requirement waterfall (RIIO-T2/ACER), capex programme screener with project IRR, 36-month congestion & redispatch cost analytics, credit metric evolution (FFO/Debt, DSCR, gearing), cross-border interconnector portfolio (IFA/NEMO/NordLink/NSL), and RAB-based valuation model with WACC sensitivity.

calculationEngine.formula: Allowed_Return = RAB × WACC_real_pre_tax; Revenue_Req = Allowed_Return + Depreciation + Opex + Taxes + Pass-Throughs; Equity_Value = RAB × (1 − Gearing) × Premium_to_RAB; Congestion_Rent = Σ(Price_A − Price_B) × Flow_MW × Hours; DSCR = EBITDA / (Interest + Scheduled_Principal)
calculationEngine.brief: NordLink interconnector (1.4GW Norway-Germany): avg utilisation 85%, annual congestion rent €210M/yr, project IRR 7.8% — benchmark for future HVDC investment economics.

dataPoints.name: European TSO aggregate RAB
dataPoints.interpretation: UK RAB growth driven by North Sea offshore HVDC; TenneT NL/DE HVDC Corridor largest single capex programme €25Bn+.

dataPoints.name: Regulatory lag (transmission)
dataPoints.formula: Formula rate mechanisms eliminate lag for FERC-regulated TSOs
dataPoints.interpretation: RIIO-T2 uncertainty mechanisms (re-openers, indexation) also reduce effective lag; key differentiator vs gas/water networks.

dataPoints.name: Interconnector utilisation
dataPoints.interpretation: Congestion rents €851M/yr across 6 operational interconnectors surveyed; merchant revenue can comprise 30–50% of project economics.

/water-wastewater-utility-finance.title: Water & Wastewater Utility Finance
/water-wastewater-utility-finance.description: Ofwat PR24/AMP8 framework for 12 global water utilities, totex efficiency benchmarking (allowed vs actual), asset serviceability grades (A–E) across 5 asset classes, Outcome Delivery Incentives (ODIs) performance vs target, RAB evolution by AMP period with WACC trends, 20-year water quality & leakage trend, and RAB-based regulatory valuation with dividend yield analysis.

calculationEngine.formula: WACC_real = Equity_Ratio × ROE + (1 − Equity_Ratio) × Cost_of_Debt × (1 − Tax); Allowed_Return = RAB × WACC_real; Totex_Efficiency = Actual_Totex / Allowed_Totex; Leakage_Saving_NPV = Reduction_Mld × 365 × £0.42/m³ × PV_Factor; ODI_Incentive = (Performance_Actual − PC_Target) × P_A_Rate
calculationEngine.brief: Thames Water at 82% gearing (vs Ofwat guidance 60-65%) with £17.2Bn RAB — illustrates how financial stress at high leverage can threaten the regulatory compact and trigger special administration.

dataPoints.name: UK water industry RAB (2024)
dataPoints.interpretation: AMP8 allowed totex £96Bn 2025-30; inflation indexation means RAB grows ~12% in nominal terms per 5yr period on top of net new investment.

dataPoints.name: Average water gearing (UK)
dataPoints.interpretation: Ofwat considers 55-65% gearing "efficient"; Thames Water at 82% outside comfort zone; CMA precedent: Anglian 2021 appeal set WACC at 2.96% real.

dataPoints.name: Leakage target (UK average)
dataPoints.formula: AMP8 target per water company average
dataPoints.interpretation: Industry-wide leakage must halve by 2050 (Net Zero Water); PR24 leakage ODIs worth £280M/yr incentive income to outperformers.

/regulated-utility-rate-case.title: Regulated Utility Rate Case Analytics
/regulated-utility-rate-case.description: Cost-of-service framework for 12 US investor-owned utilities (IOUs) across FERC and state PUC jurisdictions, detailed rate base build-up (original cost, ADIT, CWIP), revenue requirement decomposition, 16-year allowed vs earned ROE history with Fed Funds overlay, case timeline visualisation (41-week standard process), 10-year capex forecast by programme, and WACC calculator with equity ratio sensitivity.

calculationEngine.methodology: Rate Case Cost of Service & WACC
calculationEngine.formula: Revenue_Req = (Rate_Base × WACC_after_tax) + Depreciation + O&M + Taxes + Revenue_Grossup; WACC_AT = Eq_Ratio × ROE + (1 − Eq_Ratio) × Cost_LTD × (1 − Tax_Rate); Reg_Lag_Cost = Rate_Base × (ROE_allowed − ROE_earned) × Lag_Days/365; AFUDC_Equity = CWIP × Equity_AFUDC_Rate
standards: FERC Uniform System of Accounts 18 CFR Part 101
Edison Electric Institute Rate Case Summary 2023
calculationEngine.brief: Pacific Gas & Electric: $52Bn rate base, 510-day regulatory lag, earned 9.4% vs allowed 10.0% ROE — $328M annual regulatory lag cost; longest lag in peer group reflects CPUC complexity and wildfire liability proceedings.

dataPoints.name: Median allowed ROE (US electric)
dataPoints.interpretation: Range 9.3-10.2% across states; historically 11.5% pre-2012 rate cycle; decline tracking 10yr UST compression + lower market risk premium.

dataPoints.name: Median regulatory lag (US)
dataPoints.formula: Filing to new rates effective — contested cases
dataPoints.interpretation: Settlement cases average 240 days; litigated full cases 420+ days; FERC formula rates have zero lag — key advantage for large interstate utilities.

dataPoints.name: Capex recovery mechanisms
dataPoints.value: DSIC/riders cover ~40% of new utility capex
dataPoints.formula: Estimated share of spend recovered outside base rates
dataPoints.interpretation: Reduces effective regulatory lag for capex; FERC formula rates cover all transmission capex; state riders vary by jurisdiction and programme type.

/gas-network-decarbonisation.title: Gas Network Decarbonisation Finance
/gas-network-decarbonisation.description: Decarbonisation pathway analysis for 8 European gas DSOs/TSOs (UK/France/Belgium/Netherlands/Germany), H2 blending economics (0–20% cost/emission/HHV curves), biomethane injection pipeline (6 projects), stranded asset NPV modelling at 2030/2035/2040 horizons, gas demand forecasting by scenario (baseline/H2 blend/biomethane/electrification), and network H2-readiness scorecard.

calculationEngine.methodology: H2 Blending Economics & Stranded Asset NPV
calculationEngine.formula: Blend_Cost_Uplift = H2_Pct × (H2_Price − CH4_Price) / Gas_LHV_Mix; HHV_Reduction = H2_Pct × 0.37; Biomethane_IRR = Σ(Tariff × GWh_t) / (1+r)^t − Capex; Stranded_NPV = Σ(RAB_decline_t × WACC) / (1+r)^t; Network_Readiness = 0.40×H2_Material + 0.20×Blend_Approved + 0.20×Biomethane + 0.20×Demand_Retention
calculationEngine.brief: Gasunie Netherlands: 55% H2-ready network, 0% blend (converting entire backbone to pure H2), biomethane 3.6TWh — leading European gas-to-hydrogen transition; HyWay27 project converts 1,200km of gas pipeline to H2.

dataPoints.formula: Injected into the gas distribution network
dataPoints.interpretation: Target: 35 TWh/yr by 2030 per UK Biomass Strategy; requires 600+ new AD/landfill/WWTP connections; grid injection tariff under GGSS.

/utility-physical-climate-resilience.title: Utility Physical Climate Risk & Asset Resilience
/utility-physical-climate-resilience.description: Multi-peril physical risk scoring for 15 utility assets (T&D substations, gas compressors, hydro dams, water treatment, offshore wind, solar, nuclear, control centres) across flood/heat/wind/wildfire/ice perils, hardening measure cost-effectiveness matrix (10 interventions), RCP 2.6/4.5/8.5 annual loss trajectories, insurance gap analysis, SAIDI/SAIFI improvement by intervention, and adaptation finance structures.

calculationEngine.methodology: Physical Risk Loss Modelling & Hardening ROI
calculationEngine.formula: AAEL = RAV × AEP_Loss_Pct / 100; Hardening_ROI = (AAEL_Before − AAEL_After) × PV_Factor / Capex; Insurance_Gap = RAV × (1 − Coverage_Ratio); Adaptation_BCR = Σ(Loss_Avoided_t) / (1+r)^t / Hardening_Capex; SAIDI_Improvement = (SAIDI_Before − SAIDI_After) × Customer_Minutes_Value
standards: IPCC AR6 Chapter 6 — Adaptation Limits and Loss & Damage
Swiss Re Institute — Thermal Stress on Electricity Infrastructure 2023
calculationEngine.brief: US Winter Storm Uri (Feb 2021): ~4.5M Texas customers lost power for 3+ days; ~$100Bn economic losses; ERCOT grid failure linked to lack of weatherisation standards; led to Texas SB 3 weatherisation mandate and $800M hardening programme.

dataPoints.name: US utility storm costs (2022–23)
dataPoints.formula: Insurance and uninsured losses from weather events affecting electric utilities
dataPoints.interpretation: Average annual increase 8.5% since 2000; 2022 record year $165Bn total weather losses; utilities carrying increasing self-insured retention as commercial rates rise 20-40% pa.

dataPoints.name: Average insurance gap (T&D)
dataPoints.formula: Uninsured physical exposure as % of RAV
dataPoints.source: Lloyd's of London Infrastructure Risk Survey 2023
dataPoints.interpretation: Transmission assets typically 65-75% insured; distribution 55-65%; water treatment 58-75%; increasing climate risk driving insurers to reduce exposure in high-risk regions (Florida/Gulf Coast/California).

dataPoints.name: Grid hardening BCR
dataPoints.formula: Benefit-cost ratio for various hardening interventions
dataPoints.interpretation: ADMS deployment highest BCR at 4.4× (low capex, broad SAIDI benefit); underground cable conversion lowest BCR at 3.2× (high capex, comprehensive risk reduction).

/infrastructure-debt-utility-bonds.title: Infrastructure Debt & Utility Bond Analytics
/infrastructure-debt-utility-bonds.description: Utility bond universe of 24 instruments (green bonds, SLBs, transition bonds, first mortgage bonds, securitisations) across T&D/water/gas/electric/renewables sectors, OAS spread credit curve by rating (AA/A/BBB/BB), 16-quarter spread history with greenium tracking, ESG label framework comparison (ICMA GBP/SBP/CBI/Transition), covenant analysis (FFO/Debt, gearing, DSCR thresholds), and duration matching tool for ALM/pension/insurance liability management.

calculationEngine.formula: YTM = Σ(Coupon_t + Face_t) / (1+y)^t; OAS = YTM − Risk_Free_Rate_duration_matched; Modified_Duration = Σ(t × PV_CF_t) / (Bond_Price × (1+y)); FFO_Debt = (CFO + Interest) / Net_Debt; Greenium = Spread_Vanilla − Spread_Green (same issuer, similar tenor); DSCR = EBITDA / (Interest + Scheduled_Principal)
standards: Climate Bonds Initiative Utilities Sector Criteria
calculationEngine.brief: Enel SLB (€1.5Bn, 2032): coupon step-up 25bps if RE installed capacity KPI missed; at issuance spread 65bps vs vanilla Enel at 73bps — 8bps greenium; demonstrates SLB pricing incentive even for general-purpose proceeds.

dataPoints.name: Global green/SLB utility issuance (2023)
dataPoints.interpretation: Up 28% YoY; green bonds still largest at 68% of total; SLBs growing fastest at +45%; transition bonds nascent at <5%; CBI most recognised third-party standard.

dataPoints.name: Greenium persistence (utilities)
dataPoints.formula: Average spread saving vs same-issuer vanilla bonds
dataPoints.interpretation: Greenium widened in 2022 risk-off (compressed to ~4bps); rebounded 2023-24 as institutional ESG demand returns; largest greenium for AAA/AA issuers where scarcity premium applies.

dataPoints.name: Infrastructure debt illiquidity premium
dataPoints.interpretation: Direct infra debt fund average net return 6.8% vs public BBB utility 5.2%; premium compensates for illiquidity, complexity, and bilateral nature; Solvency II favourable capital treatment for long-dated IG infra debt.

userInteraction: Browse 24-bond universe with multi-axis filters (bond type, sector) to find instruments matching your investment mandate
Click any bond to load its full detail card (coupon, duration, FFO/Debt, gearing, use of proceeds, certification status)
Review Credit Spread Curve tab for AA/A/BBB/BB utility spread curves across 2–30yr tenors to assess relative value by rating
Use Duration Matching tab to input your target duration and find all bonds within ±2.5yr for ALM/liability-matching purposes
references: ICMA (2021) – Green Bond Principles, Social Bond Principles, Sustainability-Linked Bond Principles
Climate Bonds Initiative (2023) – Utilities Sector Criteria and Green Bond Market Summary
BIS (2022) – The Greenium and Market Pricing of Green Bonds

acronyms.def: Spread over risk-free rate adjusted for any embedded options; primary credit spread metric for fixed-income relative value
ICMA framework; coupon step-up/down linked to issuer-level ESG KPI; no use-of-proceeds restriction; distinguishes from green bonds
Spread compression for ESG-labelled bonds vs comparable vanilla bonds; typically 5–15bps for investment grade utility issuers

acronyms.full: Allowance for Funds Used During Construction
acronyms.def: Capitalised carrying cost of CWIP; equity AFUDC accretes earnings; debt AFUDC reduces interest expense
